39b60516-4ad6-48a8-8ee2-a830f5af025c: {n}Finding no other notable artefacts in the ruins, the expedition team returns to the ship.{/n}
c90a1793-b6f5-40b4-961d-43f426423983: "Guards — Protocol 12.45! Halt!" {n}The booming cry rattles the metal beneath your feet. A crimson-robed Tech-Priest, shouting over the moaning wind, holds out her hand in warning. Piercing the frozen mist that hangs in the air, the targeting lenses of metal Ruststalker soldiers focus on your chest.{/n}
aacf0824-5a5f-4644-8b2c-c36eedd592d0:
Pasqal gains +11 to all {g|Encyclopedia:Characteristic}characteristics{/g} and deals +{uip|LogicDamage|affa20abaee0491b8b1bec789d6a3809}% more damage. His {g|Encyclopedia:CritChancesAndCritDamageGlossary}critical hit chance{/g} is increased by +20% against creatures with less than 100% {g|Encyclopedia:HitPoints}wounds{/g}. The first attack Pasqal makes while standing on each of the highlighted cells does not count toward the {g|Encyclopedia:AttacksPerTurn}attack limit per turn{/g}.
This effect is active only while Pasqal is standing on one of the highlighted cells.
30c580b3-7683-4bef-9744-f1655b252ac0: "You have been working toward this for fifty years and you want to give up now? Are you or are you not a great Genetor? Save him!"
498c6ee7-2c45-42a5-b180-d139e88400f8: "I am acquainted with your distant descendant. He is nothing like you."
e8a74e00-c27e-442e-b5cf-4e7a79f9cbac: {n}The man before you is immediately recognisable as a servant of the {g|Encyclopedia:GoldenThroneGlossary}Golden Throne{/g}, thanks to his heavy insignia of dull gold. The man is swathed in dark fabric, with a cloak of rough cloth thrown over the top. His face, which is heavily lined but not by the ravages of time, is so unremarkable that it is as if it were a deliberate attempt to make himself forgettable. But his eyes — no one would soon forget those. They are the eyes of someone who stares when others look away. Only someone strong of will or a mindless monster could withstand that penetrating gaze.{/n}
9fa2bfd0-d829-4412-9ae9-797688c9e516: "...several members of a branch originating from a genus called the Pauper Princes. Certain similarities in composition and body structure — these creatures are constantly evolving, adapting to new environments. How exactly they penetrated a region new to themselves remains a mystery."
da1c82d4-d695-4cc4-8c96-2d295a105469: This creature is part of a single unified organism. Whenever this part suffers damage, the same damage is dealt to the Drover. When the Drover dies, so do all its branches.
d2b3b4fa-436c-4863-9e09-8e9fbfe33a5d: The mental shriek of the immense creature entrenched within Zarkov's depths. It awakens fury and clouds the mind.
3fcd4e9e-b81a-4902-800c-d860f8882aba: The Inquisitor gains +30 {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWeaponSkill}Weapon Skill{/g}, +30 {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerBallisticSkill}Ballistic Skill{/g}, +30 {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerPerception}Perception{/g}, and +60 {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWillpower}Willpower{/g}.
b7810aac-db70-4a80-85ca-23c0b9b4b36c: "It's a trap! We're all caught in a time loop, don't you see?"
a055baa4-91d1-424d-8cc4-92d8991a655c: {n}Trazyn's smug chuckle makes you flinch.{/n} "Ever since your little escapade in the Museion drew my attention, I have learned a great deal about the scurrying of humans in this region of space. Suffice it to say, a certain portion of that scurrying has piqued my interest. And what interests me is naturally of interest to you, my informant."
f5fc4dd4-30a8-4b95-8a60-d2e5244188a5: "I have not yet had the time to consider it."
877ff213-c8a8-4446-8783-c8733e7856e3: "Are there many of them? Who is their leader?"
815ae4ef-4165-4e67-99e7-fbe55f96b8c8: "Processions giving thanks to the Lord Captain... Lady Theodora was fond of them. She considered them an inexpensive and effective means of maintaining the crew's morale."
c1679f5b-cf86-4694-a18d-c429f89dd585: {n}Eogunn is talking animatedly to himself, not even turning around to look at the dummy servo-skull over his shoulder.{/n} "No, Nuncius, I am certain that we can expand the scale of the experiment even without a reliable material basis of xenos relics. Keep your cynicism to yourself. Triasomia is the future of our practice."
d3bd99cb-1c7f-45be-a97f-9ef931d8d2ea: Learn what Captain-Quartermaster Vedka Dunawski wants.
4b975372-54fc-43eb-a589-eb75910a56fd: "Hear me! We are part of the von Valancius dynasty! No longer are we nameless Guardsmen — we are the shield of the Rogue Trader now!"
80d25921-a5c2-421d-b476-1fca521a029c: "You spoke of a gratitude procession. I am ready to receive it."
4390b420-5906-4d77-87e2-4f5a5d8c0224: The user can move through allies and enemies. Each creature on their path suffers {uip|Damage|3a99ff1fc4d941fbbe9bb2820a1e6f55} {g|Encyclopedia:DirectDamage}direct damage{/g}.
1027aad4-0d2a-445f-97ff-1bd726ca2bae: "Nobles judge their servants by their deeds. I have done enough for {g|Encyclopedia:RogueTraderGlossary}Rogue Trader{/g} von Valancius to endow me with a title." {n}The intact portion of Isimond Sauerback's face maintains a completely impenetrable expression.{/n} "It is only a matter of time."
a861a304-382f-45c8-b822-47705cf09008: {n}The smell of medical supplies and weak cries of pain fill the cold air. Two figures stand over an operating table — the patient lying upon it is bound in place, and his head is soaked in blood. A woman in uniform is desperately trying to force her way to the table.{/n}
a0b63e12-b05c-4faf-987a-c990a6723e44: {n}Trazyn's dim projection now flares up with renewed force, and a hint of indignation has seeped into the xenos's usually detached voice:{/n} "It cannot be... Nocturne of Oblivion? That impudent Solitaire has somehow escaped my collection to strut about the backwaters of the galaxy!" {n}Toothless irritation rattles through whatever the Necron has in place of a voice box.{/n} "Well... I will bring him to heel again... someday."
5e9ddbe8-a091-4dd3-99b1-df1c452e55e4: {n}Drifting lower once more, Eogunn calmly asks,{/n} "Any more questions plaguing you, Rogue Trader?"
bbfcbf23-ea87-4fa3-b627-4e80722f0dc2: "The soldiers are fully aware that the God-Emperor has granted them one last chance at redemption. Anyone who fails to wise up to this fact gets shot. Even Jarub Vukchik, a sergeant whose record of disciplinary infractions used to be extensive, now knows better than to steal the regiment's property. If so much as a lho-stick goes missing, he'll be put in a cell or in front of a firing squad, depending on the regiment's current losses and needs."
0dcda502-679a-40cd-8c35-833a0e7b8f93: {n}Decabalus's optical sensor lumens flare for a second, before fading away completely. Without a sound, the Tech-Priest, clearly resigned to the demise of himself and everything he treasures, walks over to the machine and begins extracting the bizarre mechanism, which he then hands to you.{/n}
ea2d43cd-2389-422b-9909-d648545bde82: "I do not deny my actions, Rogue Trader. Indeed, I have introduced several experimental modifications to the servitors' construction, modifications that increase their productivity and task efficiency. But does this not enhance their utility? The essence of Magos Haneumann's accusations eludes me."
efe31f79-1668-4454-a4e4-418f133be8a8: "What is so surprising about my desire to be rid of you as soon as possible?" {n}The Necron's voice is even, arrogant, and... utterly false. As if he is suppressing a malevolent smirk that imbues his very words with acid.{/n}
0a381074-861e-4e12-826a-dc5240433c6b: {n}The Tech-Priest continues.{/n} "My conduct was beneath my station, Haemonculus, and my judgement... altogether lacking."
48a05519-b3e8-4c03-8f21-253231dd1d0a: "A radical position that anyone else might consider heretical. But there is a kernel of truth to it perhaps — a person can make apostasy the meaning of his life. However, one could argue that suppressing rebellion is one of the functions of the system, and therefore, paradoxically, rebels enable the system to perform this function by requiring its effectuation."
52f68ade-b171-48d4-a441-fedc962ea1de: "That is unfortunate." {n}Her eyebrows knit together as she frowns.{/n} "Unfortunate that, thus far, we have been forced to rely on the Drivestems' forces. Based on your meagre information, I assume you were not the one leading the troops. But the fate of the reinforcements and your kinsmen should concern you as much as it does me. Look for any clues: check the outpost and ask the {g|Encyclopedia:MerovPenalLegionGlossary}Merovians{/g}. I will speak plainly: right now, making yourself useful is more important than making excuses."
4de4228d-0dd1-4916-b28d-1fbd8182243e: "Attack him?" {n}Eogunn twists a bolt on his beard in confusion.{/n} "I merely used him as a visual aid for a harmless demonstration conducted under controlled conditions. Furthermore, in the process, I identified several faults in his augments and repaired them. I assure you that any effects on his organic components were negligible."
d11209a8-9867-4f0f-a363-d415025481f8: Now that the colony is safe, it is clear that the metal creatures came from the ruins where Seberian's trail ended.
eea55be0-bfde-43d3-8e65-26473929124d: "Do not dare raise your voice to the Rogue Trader!"
d02f8bb5-10e6-45b6-8615-cd99f33c1bcf: "The dynasty comes first, my heir. I am glad you understand that."
d5c9cdf9-1998-4266-bda7-93ecc6cd49fc: "Let me guess, the words of yet another wise man?"
c095510b-25e4-4f17-9123-ccc687a7af76: "You're as stubborn as a grox! I'll accept your help, since you're willing to risk your neck."
abfbac58-c17f-43e0-a85c-e558c86ec376:
"Servants of the Golden Throne to Elana. Respond immediately. Lack of communication will be construed as aiding the Heretic Coryphaeus due to cowardice and a dereliction of your sacred duty to Humanity."
{n}The vox-frequency does not fully convey the tone of voice, but the speaker is definitely anxious.{/n}
f2140124-7e17-41ef-9215-e99df45b78d3: "Can you tell me anything about Merov?"
144d492e-7c80-4d30-b173-cf5c29bacbf6: "Blessed {g|Encyclopedia:OmnissiahGlossary}Omnissiah{/g}! May maleficent code never touch your circuitry!" {n}Alva-9 turns to you — the Tech-Priest's right hand, which she used to wrench something out of the machine, is blackened and melted. Clutched in her curled metal fingers is a strange device made of smooth black material. A low hum resonates from deep within it.{/n}
ab287fdd-d217-47ba-b5b7-17171f1e4747: "After a thorough search, I found a handwritten diary signed by Seberian von Valancius."
e5ded366-67c2-4b54-9439-f7e097c59b16: "What brings you to the Sector Bionica, Lord Captain? Just say the word and we'll fix any problem!"
5df4e6c0-13b9-4e33-aa56-333b7a6989bf: "I have no intention of fighting alongside you. What I want is glory and that monster's blood, and you two are delaying my triumph. Get out of my sight."
366df482-858c-45a5-8230-118757bd779d: "Did you know about the existence of the anchor-core?"
3c450011-589a-400f-93b2-371d0ff1f17a: {n}The platform, carrying the mournful shadow of the holy warrior, rises up and away, into an intricate tangle of metal columns. Perhaps climbing one of those columns is the only way to leave this place.{/n}
c9f8b6fe-9334-4b08-a971-9e1429dc27d1: Even with the changes brought to the Cage, the corpse-cocoons remain. The people of Zarkov still honour the tradition.
190ef992-f0f0-49d7-b165-6b44357d76c3: {n}And again, the beginning. The same outpost, the same voices, the same words you have already heard. Time does not flow here, it runs in circles. A monstrous trap set by devious xenos.{/n}
9d66f228-7c6d-4f84-9f40-fb5f5ca29a88: "I will relay your orders immediately, Lord Captain."
4cab20e5-1edd-49e2-8d7c-71e7c6298fd4: "Drill Sergeant Ondzej Jovakha was struck by one of the Drover's maces. We sutured the wounds from the spikes, but he seems to have internal injuries. Given more time, we might find a way to help him."
bc07d476-014a-43e4-b94a-6dd374fbc9de: "Genetor Februs. A most audacious step — to present yourself to me in person after such an unexpected disappearance from the laboratory entrusted to you. I hope you realise that you are only here because I have permitted you to live, owing to the potential utility of your research for the Imperium." {n}The Inquisitor's tone is neutrally polite, but the look he bestows upon Eogunn is chilling.{/n} "Very well, the Rogue Trader's retinue is indeed a haven for those who harbour bold ideas. We only hope that these ideas do not turn into excessive free-thinking."
923f23f4-87bd-423b-99ef-11878ed5551d: {n}If the servo-skull has heard you, it makes no sign of it.{/n}
2e1b4277-4c4c-43fa-80a2-9cb935a44a29: "Stop evading! Alva-9 has figured it all out — you used xenotech to forge the anchor-core!"
03c29c35-1bb6-47ed-a492-5a28d7fc6c20: "Sometimes salvation requires we take desperate steps. This is the only way, heir. To venture into the darkness between the cold stars, to move away from the Astronomican, is, by the measure of many, an even greater madness."
71de86ad-fc27-4561-88be-2dbcb8ae8ae0: "The Gaprak scion was killed at Juvenna's instigation. She spurred Camilla Drivestem to act."
a54c0d85-00dd-4a14-b3a6-478f00f7c50b: [Accept the rot extract] "Very well, it's worth the risk."
45703a1f-9142-463a-bfc6-0f7af6dd0470: "I'd rather scrub every floor in the palace than be shot at! Am I right, lads?"
6833dd65-2260-407d-ac8b-2d0309c33d07: {n}Laria arches an eyebrow in surprise.{/n} "You should thank the God-Emperor for sending you my way. Few Seneschals would have listened to your words with the hope of extracting even a grain of truth from them."
9a28f25c-de06-4cfd-a86e-946984f42663: "It keeps its weak spot out of sight among the roots. And it's almost impossible to get there — unless you make it angry enough that it comes out. So first you'll have to deal with its armoured shoots. There're different kinds of them and we've given them different names based on how they act: barricades, maces, growths, and flowers."
c0129315-ad1a-45da-b564-d0a672a103eb: {n}Decabalus, after finishing issuing final instructions to his subordinates, approaches you.{/n} "Lord Captain, I determine your actions — your aid in prevailing over the hereteks — to be highly effective. Please accept my incalculable gratitude for your assistance in resolving the confusion — this sabotage."
4c5faf17-0edb-497e-8059-20e7c2bb44f7: "It is gratifying to see, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, that you are actually able to understand my colleague's recommendations and make a reasoned counter-argument."
d583cb8a-e722-4d91-a0f3-381bcb8ccb4c: "Now, with you taking care of us, it'd be wrong not to work twice— no, three times as fast!"
3e35b767-d9df-4bdc-8082-7379296423ea: {n}Whatever happened to Seberian's retinue, it is quite likely that your ancestor's trail lies behind that door. Now you have only to find the glyph that opens it.{/n}
8338270a-3447-4104-8994-2b240d4790a3: "Should our paths converge again and you have become someone of note — I suppose then you will have a chance of joining my collection."
a36482aa-8b78-4417-b957-22c5d2241361: "Let's get one thing straight, {mf|lord|lady} heir. You're here to subvert our ways. Take us to the stars. That's been foretold, but that doesn't mean I have to like it." {n}This last statement ends in a deep, guttural growl. Izdrig leans forward, as if ready to pounce, but makes no move to.{/n}
c936593b-b125-45c4-85d6-766138939a8f: {g|Encyclopedia:CritChancesAndCritDamageGlossary}Critical hit chance{/g} is increased by +{unit_stat|WarhammerIntelligence|bonus}%. Critical damage is increased by +{uip|Damage|450a41340e0843f29e6706e2224b0d49}%.
83427229-b82f-481b-88c4-65df119e0117: "Answer me, spirit of the genetic guardian. Who is Seberian?"
fd711210-1dc5-4ea2-a81f-1f1e093a16ee: "Wonderful! You should definitely go to Dargonus and convey that code to the cogitator's machine spirit."
443cdfee-d510-40e5-aae9-a3832f40d52d: {n}The colonel nods thoughtfully, shrugging his shoulders.{/n} "In the First Call-Up's second decade, our ancestors found a flying machine somewhere. They decided they needed to make their presence known. So one soldier got in it and flew off to transmit a distress signal. No one ever heard from him again. His name, Ruban Khoroshkavich, is written on the memorial wall."
0aca81a5-b7e9-40db-a518-ca81d8251550: "Wait, you have misunderstood! I don't serve the Inquisition at all!"
a7b33e2a-99c8-4c55-b6f5-f94779af725a: "Thank you for your report, Vox Master."
af255032-32d7-4b52-b8dc-8a580a241a7c: "I maximised the efficiency of the life forms serving within the sacred manufactorum on Kiava Gamma. By the blessing of the Omnissiah, I ascended to Genetor rank and was deployed to this flagship. My proficiency in installing augmetics was appraised as exceptional by the previous {g|Encyclopedia:RogueTraderGlossary}Rogue Trader{/g}."
1c358f9f-9b38-4fad-b71e-e0384fc45d9c: "What a curious find, shereen! My intuition tells me that behind great doors like these, great mysteries often lie... and great treasures... but, more likely, even greater troubles." {n}Jae gives you a grim smile.{/n}
418079c8-45af-4ff9-bee7-5cbbc0ce2cab: {n}Whole bastions of plasteel and adamantine soon sprang up in Zarkov's root system, but these defensive boundaries were directed inward, not outward. The battles were bloody and fierce, but despite the casualties, the Drovers were almost completely eliminated.{/n}
ac39615e-0eb9-46e8-83f2-7a1b97925059: "A rather obsequious valediction for someone you called a pest, wouldn't you say?"
52b751fa-8e33-42ae-afc5-9d0aefb0a36a: "Apparently, the passage to the camp is blocked. Any suggestions on how to get through?"
a5ee105c-af6a-4c14-8cab-8f659ecaed06: The Lord Captain has found not a lost relic, but Seberian himself — alive and well.
0b655622-6871-482b-8f1a-5d5c718ff5bb: "I found something at the outpost. We should discuss it."
52f78070-184c-4138-b734-f63b51f5daad: The temple found by the Rogue Trader deep within Zarkov's root system became the planet's secret curse. Every few years, the barrier holding the Drovers inside needed to be reinforced with a new dose of psychic powers. Over time, the need to sacrifice one of the planet's psykers to the barrier and the Drovers became a sacred rite, and the monsters became a symbol of the planet itself and its riches.
f10f0711-11e8-4260-b47c-95fa9690056e: "I found a blood trail stretching from the outpost to the balcony. A body was dragged and then dumped over the edge."
407c2114-a45f-4c12-b224-c82e20e01872: {n}The Rogue Trader's vox-grille emits a screech that you do not immediately recognise as laughter. Seberian dabs at a tear at the corner of his eye with a handkercher that seemingly appeared out of thin air.{/n} "War claims the lives of some and the sanity of others. God-Emperor, have mercy on this poor soul."
0e3a5edf-a823-4533-8015-863de329be95: "We were on Dargonus... Again and again..." {n}Deafening interference blasts from the vox-grille, causing those in earshot to wince. Seberian staggers, covering his face with his palm, his thin fingers clanking against his cranial augmetic. Quieter now, the Lord Captain says,{/n} "How is this possible?"
3f93f9e1-c280-4c76-8a0f-084163ba44ec: The creature is {g|Encyclopedia:StaggeredEffect}staggered{/g}. Additionally, each stack of this effect increases {g|Encyclopedia:ShockDamage}shock damage{/g} suffered by +20%.
360f868c-e9ce-4e84-9704-465939f5f0a1: "As I said — you are too craven to harm me."
69071759-4982-404d-8d4c-459b52b074df: "Yes, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}." {n}Vigdis gestures for Seberian to follow her.{/n} "This way... err... Your Lordship."
80a2373d-fee3-493e-a797-812506c22711: "It is high time we acknowledged that the dynasty has only one Rogue Trader, and that is me."
b948b7ae-f79d-4577-812b-042bf87faea4: "I know, old friend. I've come to release you. {name}, Rogue Trader, has given me the chance to do this."
01a6aacf-9dac-4648-9935-50d0d9a65412: "I am grateful for the news, but your interference in this matter ends here. We are at war, and I cannot allow a traitor to be at large a second longer." {n}Drawing out his vox, Seberian commands:{/n} "Take Captain Sauerback into custody immediately."
a5ea8a5c-34a6-4ed2-a41f-10f1faa30ab3: "There are also some ruins, not made by human hands. We rarely go there. A decent man has no business in such a place. Ask Dunawski. When she was up for her promotion to captain-quartermaster, she led an expedition to those ruins."
9b94f07d-7003-4a97-b593-416c7d77ccb2: "You're right: we share the one faith. Nobles are nobles, but we'd be better off shooting ourselves than at any holy symbols." {n}The sergeant chuckles, with a nasty glint in his eye.{/n}
e4f19a3c-8ad3-4754-927d-2b13ee431706: "It's too late, medicae. She no longer needs treatment. Maybe this potion can be of use to someone who can still be saved."
79e73f63-10a9-42f7-9291-b921f047357e: {n}The soldiers look at each other in confusion. Zarkov reaches for the vox-transmitter. You hear Laria's voice on the other end. Their conversation is short: the sergeant stands guard even over his words.{/n}
12958210-9ea2-4c20-9843-25802f7de372: {n}Eogunn frowns, pensively tugging at a piston on his beard.{/n} "You differentiate between the concepts of 'life' and 'survival'? But where do you draw the line? Can anything outweigh the desire to live at all costs? I admit, I have not considered the question from such a perspective, but your strength of will impresses me. Perhaps I should show some fortitude as well, and consider my hypothesis from a different angle."
1dc18c96-b948-496b-a314-07cb93614050: "I have found common ground with Aeldari, Drukhari, and a few other xenos species. But with officers, my interactions have typically been limited to issuing orders."
b7f934d6-2c66-489b-86fa-e0f91f2e1bc8: A jagged army knife. The blade is etched with the word "MORTIS."
3e1a5a15-9a05-45f2-9183-7b7903216ddd: "If only all interlocutors were like Nuncius."
6d17617b-d0c4-48d1-8e77-cc53d16c8958: "Inconceivable! This xenos has stolen relics from all three of the great dynasties of the Expanse and is still walking around with all his limbs intact? We would be doing House Winterscale and House Chorda an invaluable favour if we returned the relics that belong to them, Lord Captain!"
ca743e1e-b902-4700-b566-63937526e4f5: {n}Your forehead collides with the colonel's with a dull thud, making you both see stars. The stunned Merovian breaks his hold, staggering back a couple of paces.{/n}
396cf50e-c183-44d8-96d9-326368b4ee1f: "You jest, I'm sure, but... All of us here — it's us against the world, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. For hundreds of years, we learned to watch each other's backs and survive. If you kill our commander, you will become our enemy." {n}There is horror in Dunawski's eyes, but her voice is firm.{/n} "The Handbook states, 'A soldier who does not raise their weapon to save their commander is to be deemed complicit in their demise.'"
0d511b78-ad26-42ef-b601-4ed60c526cf0: {n}The cadaver wheezes,{/n} "Re-quest... kh-kh-kh... As-sign new... kh... ow-ner."
16def3b3-e148-4d82-ba22-43fa823b550d: "The Seneschal's opinion {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_DL_IzimondTraitor}should be trusted{/d}, especially considering her many years of impeccable service." {n}Sauerback brings his hand to his wounded eye socket, wiping away drops of blood.{/n}
78cb49b9-e121-4f29-b345-ad56693feccf: "The healers from the Sector Bionica killed my son. They promised me that Gnotec would come out of here good as new, but he didn't — he crawled out, {d|Encyclopedia:Ch2_FirstChoiceAugmentator}dosed up on chemicals{/d}, and died in my arms, foaming at the mouth like some void rat. Is this what we served for? Is this the thanks we get for putting ourselves in harm's way? I've come to collect my debt."
76c421db-9421-4f84-9f8e-5846b163a1e4: "Greetings. Fault correction?" {n}More than half of Ferrenz's visors have been removed due to the cumbersome neural implant soldered into his head. The remaining visors light up at your appearance in a slow and desultory manner.{/n}
fff6c8e5-9e09-4899-974d-0b5d64563162: The crane axle is covered in rust, but the gears move smoothly. The mechanisms inside the turntable are made of a strange black alloy.
fa54b87d-13b4-4160-8f92-a3b3bb28d8b2: When this creature dies, it explodes, dealing {uip|Damage|1b7dfda30e5e4c9d87dcfd0bb9cfefe8} {g|Encyclopedia:ToxicDamage}toxic damage{/g} (minimum 1) with 30% {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g} to all creatures in a 3-cell radius.
d00ab332-e5d4-4f02-b6d8-0eb33060dcd8: "What!" {n}The Seneschal seems to lose the power of speech for a moment.{/n} "I will order a search to begin immediately! We will track down the scoundrel and the missing item, Lord Captain!"
6d529c97-bc06-4c1c-9a0e-e37d616e37ef: "From noble succession to dictatorship by the best killer?"
277c8211-2c5e-43a3-a4d5-7ed767d09c19: "You asleep or what? Straighten your back! Spread your feet!"
409b267a-cbca-4939-a1fb-051e1e48118f: "So you have been lying to me all this time?"
c3922f45-ce69-4307-9448-dcbaf4b104b2: The very gazes of these warp-twisted creatures seem to exude pestilence.
c14c1f54-1015-4524-bac8-fa685890c5cc: "I know enough of these creatures to understand their language. The servitor is not needed."
28caa5e5-0871-407a-a523-57a1b0bac753: "Artisan Decabalus is too deft with his words. I would not recommend intervening on his behalf without first uncovering the full background to these events."
18616dc3-26a7-4e9d-8f86-c46fa355578a: "Why do you have so many augmetics?"
3d0e3992-f0fd-4db0-bcbf-9cb4a7275dbc: "I have more important matters to attend to than accepting the gratitude of the rabble. Remove them immediately."
830f9f31-c784-407f-9a01-6303b23a905e: "I am ready to embrace death by your hand... Yet, if there is but a flicker of hope for survival, I must seize it. For Nuncius's sake."
cb1dd147-dc1a-4cac-af24-7d8a249617cd: "What can you tell me about the iron creatures?"
6e2940e1-e0a0-4554-83b5-0886923b2707: An ominous "I" is emblazoned on the crate's lid. Its thick sides and hermetic seals speak for themselves: whatever was sealed within was never meant to be released.
eb112327-ff3e-406f-b531-1222b17edba1: {n}Without sparing Eogunn a glance, Tervantias snaps,{/n} "Your apologies failed to satisfy me, mon-keigh. The only thing you have earned is the removal of your tongue, but I will attend to that later."
086a887e-b414-4ac9-b9e5-ddfd3f55988a: {n}Seberian's implant-covered face betrays no emotion, only a glint in his remaining eye catching your attention before it disappears beneath long lashes.{/n}
63cb26e1-0e07-4c25-9ff6-20e131671b89:
Eogunn can target enemies with the {g|f:5bfccf14d8604ae1a041fb347fb8f19f}Bring It Down!{/g} ability. In this case, his {g|Encyclopedia:Resolve}resolve{/g} is reduced by −100% for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
The target immediately attacks the closest creature (prioritising other enemies). Then, the target suffers −{uip|ResolveLowered|b9936af246d44bb19c8bc8167b891714} to all {g|Encyclopedia:Characteristic}characteristics{/g}. A target affected by {g|f:780497b04a0944e59cb57e68bb9775c4}ferrum sanctum{/g} becomes {g|Encyclopedia:Stun}stunned{/g} for 1 round unless it is a powerful enemy. A target not affected by it suffers +1 stack of {g|Encyclopedia:PerplexedEffect}perplexed{/g} for 1 round instead.
Each target can only be affected by this ability once per combat.
2eaa9e16-987b-40bb-8bbd-e5279a4e6a89: {n}Eogunn winces.{/n} "Yes, Nuncius, I will relay it verbatim. He says that I possess a remarkable mind and excellent practical skills, but my, ahem, 'excitability,' especially combined with my 'stubbornness to the point of ossification,' would have stunted my development were it not for his thoughtful advice and fair criticism."
3ac6223b-cb53-45cc-b648-54b098162461: "The Quest for Knowledge — the mission in the name of the Machine God — will continue. The list of implemented enhancements will be expanded after the current series of experiments is complete."
a12ed533-36aa-4475-9c1b-522df7accd47: {n}Finally, Eogunn touches the voidborn youth.{/n} "Mikosh, a ventilation shaft crawler from Sorting Hub 10. He dutifully burned mouldslime and vermin, and would be the first to climb into blocked ducts, risking his life to deliver recycled air into the sector. He was a true believer and never lamented his lot. On his final foray, he saved hundreds of workers by using his body to plug a hole in a combustible gas pipe. The toxicity led to cardiac arrest and blood poisoning."
85135a1c-24a3-48c4-bde9-c5f18bca180a: "May the God-Emperor grant strength to those who stumble upon this lair of filth when their turn comes! And may their faith prove strong enough to endure this trial and burn the xenos blight out at the root!"
41d9ee3c-2fc2-4a9e-b1d8-c6fb0bdd3de1: "Enforcer, you died a martyr and rose a hero. Dying for the Emperor is honourable, but living for Him against all odds is the true accomplishment. I command you to live!"
9cdbdb51-f2ee-4bf1-b6c4-85e10b23d1c0:
Gear up for Koronus Expanse with additional in-game items and digital goodies! This pack includes:

- Firestorm-Class Frigate — in-game item An alternative voidship to suit your needs, with alternative starting gear and a unique appearance!

- Blast Wave Shotgun — in-game item Blast your enemies away with this unique shotgun! It allows you to push your adversaries back even if you miss the shot.

- Wrath of Saint Drusus Chainsword — in-game item Show those heretics how it's done. While this Chainsword removes the ability to make area attacks, it offers some amazing single target damage through truly devastating combos with your party.

- Heirloom Autopistol — in-game item The von Valancius Dynasty is venerable and proud, having access to some truly ancient weaponry – this Autopistiol, for example. The mastery required for creating this pistol has been lost to time, but the sheer craftsmanship enables the user to take their gunning skills to the next level.
2b0036a5-132a-4554-a690-44c67900cc6c: "Why is the Inquisition besieging the Rogue Trader's palace?"
75b2461f-d3ee-4815-acc8-ecbbdab0ae84: To move the ship, click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on any accessible cell on the battlefield. Once you do, you will see a hologram of the ship that will allow you to evaluate the efficiency of future attacks and actions from that position just as you would in regular combat. Click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} again to confirm your choice and command the ship to move. Alternatively, you can click {mouse_icon|RightMouse} to remove the hologram.
115d914a-2b8a-4906-ad53-75e0714c2a5b:
You can use the hologram to gauge the effectiveness of your intended actions in combat before taking them.
Click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on any accessible cell on the battlefield to place the character's hologram there. Select an {g|Encyclopedia:Ability}ability{/g} or a {g|Encyclopedia:SingleRangedAttacks}single-shot{/g} attack to see the expected outcome. The action will be displayed as if used from the hologram's location to let you assess whether you have picked an effective position for the action you are planning to take. The hologram also lets you see if the enemy will be within your {g|Encyclopedia:LineOfSight}line of sight{/g} after you move to the selected cell.
Click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} again to confirm your choice and execute the selected action. Click {mouse_icon|RightMouse} anywhere on the battlefield or press the [ESC] key to cancel it.
9c23a5b1-7ce9-4d3b-b30d-becfc10bfdae: "Have I grasped the situation correctly: the xenos quietly infiltrated one of the most important planets in the von Valancius protectorate to retrieve the malatek's remains, and no one even noticed?"
055d3be6-8eeb-4597-b275-0bd07faa5abb: "Smashing a big sinister machine to pieces would be a fine bit of fun, shereen!" {n}The Cold Trader grins eagerly.{/n} "But truly, we cannot simply take it with us, can we? Then we can let it fall into no one's hands, including the masters of this place."
c62be593-d117-4abe-bdea-d90d3d80c976: [Taste the wine and sigh] "You're right, Nuncius — we are part of the problem. I ought to put my wealth to good use."
f0789aef-96be-4ed8-bb8e-793564472bd6: {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:Manipulus}Manipulus{/g}'s massive bulk spins around to face you, his eyes dancing with excitement.{/n} "Rogue Trader! Your arrival has triggered a cascade of impulses in the prefrontal cortex of my brain. Delight — that's the word I was searching for! Cast off your worries — the subjects of the experiment have been preserved through a combination of alchemical compounds, local transactions of the Motive Force, and moderately invasive augmentation procedures. In other words, all three are very much alive." {n}Throughout this speech, Eogunn's mechadendrites continue their manipulation of the bodies.{/n}
8ec82f78-746b-4b0b-85e2-cc03a041aea0: In the eerie silence, the Rogue Trader's voice sounded strikingly clear:
ed6cd934-bf51-4e8b-96a4-680721b3c437: A complex system of pins extracts the sap from the plant.
cab74e14-4350-437c-b004-f3f2abf388f0: {n}After searching the camp, the team determines that it was set up by a group of pioneers sent to the planet by your predecessor Theodora. None of them survived — their bodies, covered in volcanic ash, have turned into mournful stone statues, almost completely robbed of their human form. The scouts bring the expedition's few surviving supplies and records back to the flagship.{/n}
541789b3-7720-4593-9242-94ca9c00d912: "An astute observation, Lord Captain! I am proud to tell you that I had the honour of serving alongside members of the {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptusMechanicusGlossary}Adeptus Mechanicus{/g} back on my home planet. Among the Tech-Priests of Janus, there are entire divisions headed by Genetors. The knowledge of the Officio Medicae was essential during more complicated procedures, for the sacred remit of Genetors does not include tending to patients' mortal bodies. That is when an experienced medicae must be brought into the ritual. And thus, we found some common ground. I must stress that the esteemed Genetors do not permit me direct access to the machines, since none but they themselves are versed in the mysteries of operating them. My purview is limited to... the organic aspect."
9fce1133-9d6f-4f08-975f-38c088f377ef: {n}Nuncius's empty eye sockets stare indifferently at his colleague's frustration.{/n}
852c9b4a-bdd5-41d2-9bb9-34ce8e14f0a9: "I recognise these numbers. A memory from my old life."
efc080fb-7dca-4279-b764-2ceacac1711b: [Headbutt him in the face as hard as you can]
5f97568d-6c82-45ca-a922-9980ccac4459: {n}The Tech-Priest stares at you with a single visor. Amidst a tangle of metal and wires, the vox-caster replacing his mouth hisses:{/n} "Scanning life form... Visit registered. Welcome to the Sector Bionica, Lord Captain. You will be received by {g|Encyclopedia:Genetor}Genetor{/g} Majoris Ferrenz." {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:TechPriest}Tech-Priest{/g} turns, and you suddenly realise that he was standing with his back to you — the front of his head is covered in blinking visors.{/n}
b1f989b4-ff9b-46c9-b890-5d305c551702: "I doubt it. My {g|Encyclopedia:AugmentationGlossary}augments{/g} are an act of reverence for the Omnissiah, a flawless alignment of parameters. Their function and placement are calculated with perfect precision." {n}Exalt-117 fixes his unseeing gaze on the piece of metal in your hands. Suddenly, he flinches.{/n} "Calculations indicate... the need for further study."
a6c4290b-ad45-4d35-9b22-9687cabc8db2: "Very well, I will take you to Aviorus."
ff98795c-b62b-45fe-ab51-5e889fd50efa: "Their goal is the destruction of the sacred central drill — the greatest of the station's technologies — by means of ripping out its anchor-core — the source of the Motive Force that powers our machines. Such inconceivable sacrilege — ruination of blessed technology — will have irreversible consequences that may lead to the disintegration of the sacred machine circuits and the sinking of the station."
5d1170c8-4308-4a9a-9ee8-050f1804b104: "What new blight is this? Wait... that word sounds familiar."
a88b337e-1b51-46c8-93c0-fdf4a5623cd0: {n}The servo-skull remains silent, unwilling to enter the argument.{/n}
52dcbddb-56d5-4ce6-9e86-683f14cdfda7: "The servants of the God-Emperor put the pieces of your head back together, and this is how you repay them? If I had known that my subject was incapable of overcoming adversity, I would have killed you myself!"
654e82b5-9b77-4b5d-bef8-d468000aeb6c: [Show the mechadendrite from the display case] "Does this belong to you?"
e20f4139-7bea-4c1e-8e78-d8890d066e55: "She also betrayed you and aided the Inquisition."
230d51b9-8ce9-425a-9304-acb570e93f92: Arch-Militant Vedka Dunawski was one of the last of the flagship's crew to die, fighting steadfastly and desperately, like a true daughter of Zarkov. The last thing the warrior saw before death was the distant stars that beckoned to her, and the person she considered the saviour of her homeworld.
28685ef9-7846-471b-a26d-7f87936baa05: "Thank you, truly, but no. You could not. To my regret, I am all too familiar with your approach to healing."
691ed4c4-ad76-4d6d-a83e-812668469dee: "Very well. I shall be here, should you have need of me."
6848ccd4-52a9-44be-9644-2ab84e4d977a: "The tablets that speak through eternity." {n}Eogunn gazes greedily, as if desperate to absorb every detail shimmering in the black metal.{/n} "The secrets they conceal within themselves are sinister, yet they carry the truth across aeons. Do you realise, Rogue Trader, that only a select few among the living have experienced this? The knowledge entombed within this vessel is akin to a double-edged lancet."
4476a68f-ea90-4a9d-a911-a6877500309a: {n}Seberian nods.{/n} "You are a loyal servant to the dynasty, and I will not forget that."
2b59aa47-06b9-4e87-80f2-889eb811e803: I cannot say how long our descent lasted. Finally, we reached our goal: one of the many passageways that riddled the bowels of the planet. The corridor, clad in black metal, was quiet and completely empty.
fa1beb2e-146a-4db9-bbe1-c517ad07327b: {n}Kibellah carves the same symbol on the back of her hand with a knife and whispers a prayer.{/n} "Domin'{name}, remember this sign. It is the beginning of your quest, the first blood trail."
7fc15fee-8adc-400d-8b59-4d15d8637382: "As a representative of the Adeptus Arbites and Proctor of the Koronus Expanse, I attest that the oath will be entered into the official records of the Adeptus Administratum."
4f43230e-677a-480f-a86d-70e0408a5ad8: "Stop. I won't let you see Father — I mean, the Lord Captain — until you explain yourself to me." {n}A woman in light armour seizes you by the elbow. Her face bears the traces of a recent battle: her eyebrow is bandaged, and her right cheek is covered in a web of scratches. Her cerulean blue eyes, sharp cheekbones, and the stubborn quirk of her lips betray a marked resemblance to Theodora's fugitive Master of Whispers.{/n} "I am Laria Voigtvir, Seneschal and close ally of Seberian von Valancius."
0cda40f1-8e6f-4c8b-9697-adf165e5bc66: "I have no idea whom you mean. If you are seeking a relative of yours among the fighters, I suggest you appeal to one of the field commanders," {n}the von Valancius heir responds in a slight drawl, fending off awkward questions with the same impenetrable courtesy.{/n}
a2fc2969-7500-4751-8d62-61a14f2547ed: "It's our home, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. Everything here is either poisonous, predatory, or fragile. In short, anything that isn't trying to kill you is only pretending to be safe. But you get used to it. And then you learn how to use it. Poisonous? Then you can use it for medicine. Predatory? Means it's edible. Hard to kill? You can probably use its skin for armour."
863187ed-4cb8-4e53-8920-8a3f863ba6bd: "There is no time to lose. The corruption that has nested in this place must be sanitised as swiftly as possible. Otherwise, I evaluate the risks of its infection as extremely high. Let us hurry, Rogue Trader."
ff68e131-e4ee-4ec7-8d92-2e18671f205c: {n}Eogunn stares through you, looking lost — difficult memories are dragging him away from the here and now.{/n} "Yes, of course, we must get to... to Nuncius. I have to find him and finish what I started, don't I?"
3f198998-e6a0-4d1f-ab3d-31ea77502f91: {n}A pause. The Tech-Priest's gaze softens.{/n} "He also adds that the thought of working with anyone other than me evokes repulsion and outrage in him. The value coefficient of our relationship is extremely high in his catalogue."
d170b897-2fac-4445-8930-12bdd5808fc0: {n}Seberian ponders for a moment, but then nods in benevolent agreement, the augments in his spine clicking audibly.{/n} "Very well, you may remain. A decisive battle is at stake, and as a warrior loyal to the dynasty, I will grant you a voice on this council."
6759a3f1-7f85-454b-8611-b08a2eacb36a: "So this is the real murderer of the Gaprak scion."
10bf7a24-3302-45ac-bf6f-7fdb390e08e2: "With our unholy weapons we will cull the unbelievers and let the Word of Truth into the world!"
8aadb98b-fcb0-4c5b-a1cc-f1ff97fd08c2: If an enemy {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeGlossary}dodges{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:Parry}parries{/g}, or {g|Encyclopedia:BlockChance}blocks{/g} the user's attack, the user's next attack against that enemy cannot be dodged, parried, or blocked and deals +{uip|Damage|d631acbb9e10424c88f2974f7c075207} base damage.
d455e8b0-9c9a-4eef-82a4-73b4d1106783: Encased in a leaf cocoon, Dunawski's body has curled into a foetal position.
09d50bb1-706b-496b-8904-49bcf2fe54c5:
Augmentation directly modifies the character's body. In contrast to equipping gear, installing an augment is permanent.
An augment cannot be unequipped and can be replaced only with another augment. If removed, it is replaced with a basic augment. The original body part cannot be restored.
There are several ways to install an augment. You can use the Implantation Throne, speak to the High Factotum on the ship's bridge, or open the Augmentation menu on the rightmost part of the Koronus Expanse map.
To install an augment, drag it into the corresponding insertion on the character diagram and click "Install".
33b627ea-0628-43ca-b364-87e16dfc0c9d: {n}Eogunn winces as if in pain, touching his temple, and mutters softly,{/n} "Nuncius... again? No, the sacellum is closed. My presence is required here and now, not there..." {n}As if snapping back to reality, he fixes a grim stare upon the Tech-Priest.{/n} "I recognise the xenos' hallmark — he has been corrupted. Only the mercy of the Machine God and fervent faith are keeping him conscious."
117ca1cc-f05f-4807-a15a-7822b8b5222c: "The will of the God-Emperor was sent down through His anointed to free the faithful servant from the enemy's shackles. Everything happened as it should have."
9a6488ed-082b-47d0-9187-5413f50274bc: {n}Khroshovach fervently makes the sign of the Aquila, trying to pour all his reverence into the gesture.{/n}
cd655120-c4ab-4267-a95c-055a31b6b4d9: {n}The console is clearly designed to control the process of transforming a living or inanimate object located on the centre platform into an incorporeal copy of itself. Your attention is drawn to the rightmost chain of glyphs — they are flashing, as is the large orb above them. This seems to indicate that the current victim is still alive. The leftmost chain is separated from all the others by a shining frame. The orb above it burns bright and steady, and the symbols are similar in number and appearance to those engraved on the moving parts of the xenodevice. Perhaps this is the control centre for the supply of power to the machine.{/n}
de77e03f-71ed-4f74-b998-e57a2e4931a7: "Is that a rhetorical question?" {n}The alembic on Eogunn's shoulder hisses, releasing a cloud of acrid vapour.{/n} "No, I see that you truly wish to hear the answer. Each follows their own path to knowledge, comprehending existence through the wisdom of the Omnissiah. My path and that of Magos Haneumann diverged at the very beginning."
a26c1142-1618-4b70-963d-ed65f7fc2241: Many regiments have patron saints. With their loyalty, the 32nd Regiment proved they deserve this honour. Lavish offerings to the Order of Saint Drusus shall grant the people of Zarkov the right to fight under the inspiring protection of Saint Cognatius.
a7bd447a-b85c-4d75-8aac-9d4a9bc8647e: "Perhaps the songs of your regiment's deeds are exaggerated, Vedka."
19b6c488-795f-45a2-8231-e9536b111aff: {n}Eogunn takes a deep breath, then enters a chain of commands into the stasis sacellum's cogitator. The field flickers and goes dark — then the silence is shattered by Nuncius's inhuman screams. Eogunn's {g|Encyclopedia:Mechadendrite}mechadendrite{/g} plunges into the black cube and wrenches it from his colleague's hands. Before the mutations can spread through his tentacle, Eogunn cuts off the end of it with a plasma cutter and hurls it and the cube into the container where the artefact was once kept. The remaining mechadendrites inject Nuncius with stimulants. The Tech-Priest stops writhing, but it is clear that he has little time left.{/n}
72ff5ad3-9caa-4c5d-b415-0aea3ba6e8d4: The cloud deals {g|Encyclopedia:ToxicDamage}toxic damage{/g} in the area in front of the creature.
afe1538a-5209-4782-8f82-e7595f99fd06: "Who dares to rob the Rogue Trader's chambers?"
ca59cf16-1bcf-4ccd-a027-f25dca0a63b6: Convinced the Arch-Militant to obey the servants of the Ecclesiarchy.
201b00a2-32f7-465e-87e0-bd4af89e7831: "Tell me, Nuncius, what is true love?"
4f6d9d57-71d0-45ca-8793-1b17d589c743: Time moves strangely in the Chronomancer's presence. Each time an {g|Encyclopedia:ExtraTurn}extra turn{/g} is granted, it's timed to last only 30 seconds.
7c348c15-c7c8-43c4-aafa-52df6b3e1ade: "A detailed catalogue of these 'things' would greatly serve the cause of knowledge, Mistress Heydari. My colleague, as always, is far too hasty in his judgements."
335c8eb5-da40-4bec-84d9-21beabd09ef3: {n}He removes an unusual-looking blade with a softly glowing edge from his belt.{/n} "I recognise you, {name} von Valancius. Your name was in the chronicles, and it was {d|Encyclopedia:CharGen_MOT_Glory}one of worth{/d}. This sword belonged to one of my brothers who fell in battle. It must be returned to the {g|Encyclopedia:DeathWatchGlossary}Deathwatch{/g}. Now this duty is yours, for I have little hope of leaving this place."
e53e17ae-4453-42af-b987-8f64ceced141: "I have already sought their counsel, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. The priests told us to get rid of the shroud-leaves and bury our fallen according to shipboard tradition. They say, since we have your patronage, our struggle for survival is over. Perhaps they are right, and it is time for us to let go of the customs of our world..."
cedca1a0-891e-47b6-a752-889057517a38: "Lord Captain!" {n}Janur drops his carving, stands up straight, and makes the sign of the Aquila.{/n}
3685c96c-c4eb-42c2-afb6-d1dc41e1f0ad: "Watch your tone — I am not some lower-deck drudge to be lectured by you."
7dfe5166-1a3f-4c81-8924-630cd35af46f: "...Nuncius, I know you have no love for bustling festivities, but out of respect for the Rogue Trader, please endure until the ceremony concludes, I beg of you."
c3347664-4e9a-465c-8718-723c0ad41c58: Gut instinct suggests that something is missing from the Lord Captain's chambers. Check that everything is in its proper place.
bd8300ec-476c-4839-8546-b6597278c51c: "The murderer of my kinsman must be tried without delay!"
e8fc53a8-97d7-4d49-8ead-df6a59cd811e: "How did you come to be on the planet?"
f27875b4-05fd-441d-8b07-52dc4375e806: The Museion's xenos guardian admitted the Crimson Dance of Khaine has been stolen and turned into an exhibit. To recover it, you must first find the "interactive diorama."
6f34c45e-f60c-48ee-9ff6-bf268c9afe1a: [Give a curt nod and accept the gift]
8d8c45ea-8c3f-4334-83d0-a000c57a3203: "Master Toliman, as I have already told you both: you will be seen when you reach the front of the queue! Now I have work to do, please do not distract me, or, Throne forbid, the Lord Captain!"
95c5c2b5-793c-4130-99a7-a8f333b6ffbe: "You're not dead, at least, my {mf|lord|lady} heir. That's a start. I have to give you credit for courage, and that's not some Commissar claptrap."
54b28a2e-21de-4050-bbc7-dba4f1edbcfe: {n}Eogunn speaks softly, raising his hands:{/n} "For thus spake the Machine God in the all-comprehending binharic: 'The distortion of My design is heresy.' The brethren who have succumbed to this blight must be utterly purged, before they spread the corruption further."
ae398141-76b9-43ab-bfca-42a78d9369b4: There was no knowing what lay ahead. {g|Encyclopedia:Lumen}Lumens{/g} provided just enough light to reveal smooth walls stretching down into the darkness, and countless platforms hovering over the void. It was as if the accursed structure were mocking us, and I accepted its challenge, as befitted a Rogue Trader. The descent became a series of vertiginous leaps between platforms, and it was only the Ferrum Sanctum my body was blessed with that saved me from falling and granted me success, as I was able to discover several side corridors in the walls.
c1c95d3d-b4eb-44af-8c60-d71bc24d9d27: Creates destructible partial {g|Encyclopedia:DetailsOfTheCoverGlossary}cover{/g} in a chosen unoccupied cell.
18a858fc-f7b7-430d-ad22-af75969c91b9: "Be extremely careful." {n}The Interrogator speaks near your ear, almost without parting his lips.{/n} "For someone waging a war against heresy, this lady has shown an overly high degree of moral flexibility."
2660ffe6-796c-4396-8b8d-a5296bc707c5: "The abductor knows exactly how to protect the environment from the pernicious effects of the tech-heresy! Look, some of the corrupted augmetics have been removed altogether, and some have been replaced with similar-looking but completely inert constructions. This cadaver has undergone an extremely skilful autopsy ritual!"
3515430f-c53b-483b-8e23-4f28c3a5b455: "I see you have replaced your prosthetic."
f6223040-6e0b-46ab-806b-22042a7c92f0: {n}Seberian obediently leaves the bridge. For the first time, his shoulders are slumped and his gaze is empty of all but infinite exhaustion.{/n}
27466798-5944-4774-a1e6-1984a7acc523: "This place... vexes me. It is long past time for us to tear all of it to pieces. I do not see why we shouldn't begin with that clumsily concealed device."
53a5868c-dfcb-49f4-aa76-2b3ace16332e: The Imperium could not forgive such insolent deviation from the order prescribed by Holy Terra. The von Valancius dynasty was accused of the seditious formation of their own empire. An invasion fleet gathered in the Calixis Sector, and the young utopia did not outlive its creator.
53e715c1-ebd7-4a68-a7cb-f18a9b46cfd9:
If the cyber-mastiff is:
-<indent=5%>...{g|f:e16934c3dbf44cb997c684a6ddce7575}apprehending{/g}, Solomorne's damage dealt is increased by +{uip|DamageBonus|38f5e9e8ab2f43b8aa356dbc89b821ba}% at the start of his {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}.</indent>
-<indent=5%>...{g|f:a63088cd6402424b8ec0c508e5143083}protecting{/g}, Solomorne's {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} is increased by +{uip|ArmorBonus|38f5e9e8ab2f43b8aa356dbc89b821ba}% at the start of his turn.</indent>
-<indent=5%>...is in {g|f:11251c47c7b14a87824cffe48ea6374d}Battle Mode: Standby{/g}, Solomorne gains +2 {g|Encyclopedia:MovementPoints}MP{/g} at the start of his turn.</indent>
This effect is active only if Solomorne has the cyber-mastiff in his {g|Encyclopedia:LineOfSight}line of sight{/g}.
43d0e042-563d-42a6-ac8c-4920593addbd: "Give me a chance to prove my skills, and I shall not waste it, honoured saviour of the regiment." {n}A new, slightly manic note creeps into Vedka's ringing voice.{/n} "May I be dismissed?"
ce72fa3d-269c-48b8-b3ad-c5c3427df464: "The decision to leave Seberian was a brave one. But we will still have to explain to the Adeptus Administratum how there can be two official von Valancius Rogue Traders in the Expanse."
591aa071-410c-449f-9f0d-36afe8dfc40c: "I h-hereby express gratitude to the Lord Captain for saving this life form's vital functions. May the Omnissiah bless you."{n}The bloody and oil-smeared Genetor Ferrenz rises unsteadily. Clutching awkwardly at the floor with his {g|Encyclopedia:Mechadendrite}mechadendrites{/g}, he turns to one side and wheezes.{/n} "I need to go... go to the operating room immediately. The Martinax life form requires my expertise."
0c0e36ce-4e40-4d41-aae0-c62dfb187d5a: "A rather bleak view of existence. Would you care to elaborate on that, so that I might better understand your point of view?"
eb160dfd-88e6-4fa0-adb9-89287b2616fe: "I beg your pardon? I have never had any such connection with a member of House Gaprak!"
eae69c30-758d-4986-8ea0-23b579a79772: "Only the strong are worthy of surviving, the rest are expendable. He did not fear death, so he is strong.
7115874b-84fd-4214-8c7a-3812cb272f14: "Welcoming guests is not one of your talents, I see. So what do you want me to do?"
af15138d-1930-41cc-a6b1-da41707dada8: "To think that Seberian von Valancius himself is walking the decks of this ship! If I hadn't seen him with my own eyes, I would never have believed it."
8e47aff1-3d27-4a84-8c0b-ce601d1f4c0d: "You could at least make yourself presentable."
20de82ae-2db9-4e28-a524-016d40d898f2: "So many Call-Ups later... And here it is: the oath, as written in the Handbook! Our regiment has found its purpose and returned to duty. All that remains is for you to decide, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, what to do with Zarkov and our regiment."
b8ce87ed-d85b-4aba-a4b9-5b7a7e50a35a: "I was merely curious about how you would react. You may stay."
c4e42a0a-8a60-479a-9d76-2926c95dc780: "Every word carries a cost. To say what he demands would be to surrender to the xenos, a betrayal of the Omnissiah and everything I stand for. It would mean selling my dignity and my very soul. I would rather perish."
50144223-dfa8-4d69-ae3b-0c2d1ed6d087: "Do not grieve, Aett-Vater! A great deed awaits you: the recovery of the ancient scroll of rights, inscribed by the Allfather's own hand! What a glorious saga, what a thundering legend it will be! And the feast that will follow! That is your wyrd, {name} — do not dream of any other."
f0a15552-9fc6-4ca1-a88b-32c6a31bb1ac: Eogunn targets an adjacent ally with at least 1 augment installed and restores {uip|HealAmount|66a1334321bc4000b9c19ee35614538d} wounds (up to a maximum of {uip|HealLimit|66a1334321bc4000b9c19ee35614538d}). To use this ability, Eogunn must have at least 2 stacks of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g}, which are removed afterwards. This ability can be used once per combat.
125b806c-0899-47e3-9abc-30134903ace5: "Far from it. It is a calculated risk taken for the sake of an experiment whose importance surpasses any possible complications many times over. I exercise great diligence in overseeing the procedure, so there is no cause for alarm."
9e84b9ee-cf48-4348-97e4-cbbe512675ba: "Ho-ho! Iron sage, beneath your swollen shell lies a soul brimming with the mjod of poetry! You have painstakingly righted the flaws in my verse instead of slyly claiming it is pleasing to the ear even in its original form. I like it!"
12d62303-83ec-4506-98a0-77cf93f5a99a: "From now on, Zarkov is a colony of the von Valancius protectorate. And it is time to appoint a governor."
13e96b45-b838-4fc8-a58c-751d33b24749: {n}The trek among the monotonous forest landscapes is becoming dull. The Rogue Trader's weary eyes are ready to close of their own accord when the usual sounds of the forest are interrupted by a blood-curdling scream. The cries are drawing closer and closer, then their source finally appears — a small creature with slick black fur and a long tail. As if mocking the Rogue Trader, it lets out another piercing cry and scurries away, its yellow hind paws winking between the tree branches.{/n}
10f93de0-a186-4a17-bf72-babf6ce5a839: "Pleased to have been of service to you, Inquisitor."
8dc38935-0ce7-42f7-ba85-2654d3181db4: {n}Trazyn's gaze fixes on Seberian — and the sword he clutches in his hands. The Necron's tone becomes caustic, an acid that burns to the very soul.{/n} "I see you have found your precious toy once more, little thief.... This is a momentous occasion in its own way. This all began when the Crimson Dance of Khaine fell into your hands, and this is where it will end. For you, and for your last surviving heir."
3be3454d-be94-4196-9d65-28cd2d845cbc: A backpack power unit emblazoned with a fleur-de-lis, the symbol of the Adepta Sororitas.
b6317656-9daf-4ab0-9caa-12268f90933e: Send a new team to the missing group's location.
2f04a56d-e4d4-4f47-94f0-b21c867a256b: "Hedonism is an understandable urge. A desire to experience life to the fullest. But at the root of this lies another, more primitive drive."
5475f991-955f-44b9-99cc-75bbd1fb84d0: [Stare into Batoshski's eyes] "It's over, Colonel. I've already won. Release me. This instant."
b3b0b580-2dd1-484f-81ad-75832eba9e06: "Perhaps my approach is fundamentally flawed or the objects are non-functional. Or perhaps they simply do not want anyone to root out their secrets? It's a mystery! A stimulating mystery!"
8b79660f-2a46-44cf-bf82-e47da62dd48b: [Convince Laria that you are in a time loop]
07e60f64-8ceb-4b54-978d-7fb206f1b364: {n}Juvenna clears her throat.{/n} "Father. I should have known! These people are from Lady Elana. You remember her, of course? I received word of her death a few hours ago. They seem to be part of her retinue. Let me deal with them."
7c2e96fc-0314-4255-8c93-bac9762a4d64:
All daemons summoned by Uralon gain a bonus to Weapon Skill, Strength, Agility, Toughness, Perception, Willpower equal to 50% of Uralon's Fellowship characteristic. Whenever a summoned daemon hits, Uralon gains +<u>(10% of damage dealt by the daemon)</u> stacks of {g|f:2606d221cbd84502957939547b6f4d21}tactical advantage{/g} (or +<u>(25% of damage dealt by the daemon)</u> stacks of {g|f:2606d221cbd84502957939547b6f4d21}tactical advantage{/g}, if the daemon scores a critical hit).
Uralon's allies do not suffer from {g|f:9f073e3858ab46dc90e16957a3081e78}Manifestation{/g} of any daemons.
d88e3c2a-a822-40a9-a8bc-0fa0c3ce72cf: "I highly value the productivity of our conversations, Rogue Trader, though I must admit that I do not see the appeal of layperson humour." {n}Your mockery of Nuncius seems to have struck a nerve with the Manipulus.{/n} "Nevertheless, I offer my sincere thanks to you for the invitation, as does my colleague. He does not often get the opportunity to socialise with laypersons of your status."
45914c4c-1860-4149-9227-8bc06a2f6e22: {n}Argenta watches the warrior's death, transfixed, without even trying to hold back the tears streaming down her cheeks. Her lips move almost imperceptibly in ceaseless, silent prayer.{/n}
5be9b9bf-3f60-40ac-81cd-9e5ec6aa8abf: "We served your hallowed ancestor several centuries ago, {mf|lord|lady} heir. We honour him for his deeds, but no one living knows him as a person."
d6f09bd5-e062-4f45-829b-402fda134662: {n}As a noble line, the Gapraks are no less ancient than the Drivestems. Perhaps imagining herself in their place has made Camilla anxious.{/n}
7a2f9dc2-ebad-4018-b47c-f6a0a973a821: "Sergeant Janur Batoshski, you're in charge of the tactical operation. Your combat experience is greater than mine, and the Handbook dictates that command in the field be delegated wisely."
f29d55e4-09ac-44bc-a893-f02c470884c8: "Hypothesis: the observed reality is a complex optical illusion constructed using perverse xeno knowledge." {n}Pasqal swings a {g|Encyclopedia:Mechadendrite}mechadendrite{/g} at the nearest section of wall, leaving a deep scratch on the stone.{/n} "Replication level: extremely high. The hypothesis does not explain the observed effect of the space-time paradox."
0d5234c6-db95-4eaf-9660-cb743471920c: "Servants of the Golden Throne to Palatine. The battle is near. Gather your units at the traitor's gates."
1b921fc0-1670-4faa-b671-8b34a569ffa2: "So the entire world is an arsenal? The First Call-Up broke into an ancient bioweapons depot? That's fantastic. And ironic."
7f51b2b5-da1c-4805-9fe4-4ddd71b34366: "No questions. Let's talk about something else."
0d8d8bd9-2db7-46d3-abbd-b61af5090ecc: "It's a bit early for you to start giving orders. We haven't had the Trial Match yet." {n}Batoshski's quiet voice easily cuts through the noise of the crowd.{/n}
afb227cc-65a9-4ec8-82eb-6350da9f0e01: {n}The fury of the 32nd Merov Regiment clashed with the xenos' merciless bloodlust. Gunfire and cries of pain echoed all across Zarkov, travelling through the root system of the world. Hundreds and thousands of soldiers will never be found, and their names will be carved into the roots around the settlements. And still, the wounded, devastated Merovians overpowered the enemy.{/n}
58983ced-6cfd-4eb0-9ddb-e654d0b0a1cf: {n}Seberian seems to jerk awake and draws himself up straight, the movement accompanied by the piercing sound of dozens of ancient implants. With a burst of florid profanity, the Rogue Trader draws his weapon and rushes forward. In response, the xenos raises his hand and, without looking, lazily snaps his fingers.{/n}
5b1d7eac-5139-47f6-9a72-b9455c87be8c: "The intervals between my realising the truth are becoming longer all the time. I used to uncover my secret about once every two months. That quickly turned into four. Now it takes as long as eight. This shows that the deterioration of my mind is accelerating." {n}He raises his colourless gaze to you.{/n} "So far, I have failed to find an effective countermeasure. I do not know if I will ever find one."
a67f1078-3800-403e-9247-e8cf230a1402: "Put down your weapon. That is an order from your Lord Captain."
dd7c0ed5-1dff-4e5a-a9a3-bb38846f15ec: {n}An angry spark zips along the pistons in the Manipulus's beard. He floats higher, turning his glowering gaze on the sector's ceiling.{/n} "Because I cannot abide the senseless waste of potential. I cannot accept that a foolish accident and the fragility of organic flesh can — in an instant — deprive the Imperium of worthy servants. And if I am able to extend their service life, I will do so. Whatever the cost."
8b55a772-d319-4045-afb7-7e92011dcd2b: {n}Idira plunges both hands into her hair, violently tousling it.{/n} "Wrong, this is wrong!" {n}She wraps her arms around her head, swaying slightly.{/n} "They only seem real, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, but when you look closer... half are ghosts, and the other half are walking around with their souls cut out. This is a deathtrap, a bad place. We need to get out of here, and take any still alive with us."
6078e0cb-ef97-4f4f-b231-02ebbd85d316: "I shall descend to the Sector Bionica immediately."
5cc73d87-4509-476b-8a47-02922b60d44b:
This modified transonic cannon grants Eogunn an upgraded version of the {g|f:c0dc6cf8887946e5910c64aa17ecb4c8}Transonic Wave{/g} ability. It does not count toward the attack limit per turn.
This augment is considered to be in a dexterous insertion. Eogunn cannot suffer a {g|f:b6a0edb0040f450a8a0c85c1300d6548}crippled right arm{/g}.
9cb4174a-dca6-420a-8ff4-0863e8beee14: "I am prepared to give you a rational argument as to why you should agree to this."
b8db98dd-ee65-4b3c-a171-93240a3bc8b0: "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship} is granting us a boon whose value is difficult to calculate — to measure in rewards and gratitude." {n}The Tech-Priest bows slightly.{/n}
247c3c84-4668-455e-86b4-15462b32d929:
"The Infinite Museion" is the third story expansion for Warhammer 40,000: Rogue Trader, drawing you into the iconic vault of Trazyn the Infinite. Pursue rare artefacts, enhance yourself with augmentations, and choose whether to serve the designs of one of the galaxy's most infamous collectors or oppose him.
Explore the Vault: Follow a trail of hidden agendas and unsettling discoveries that gradually reveal a larger mystery spanning the Koronus Expanse. What at first seems like a series of isolated expeditions soon sheds new light on the past of the von Valancius dynasty. As you uncover the truth about your ancestors, your strange ally, and the artefacts you recover, you will be drawn into a fateful encounter with Trazyn the Infinite and forced to make a choice that could reshape your destiny.
A new companion: A devout servant of the Omnissiah and Tech-Priest Manipulus with a shadowed past. A capable support specialist with unique buffs and technological abilities, he brings both battlefield utility and narrative tension to your retinue. The deeper you delve into his story, the more you must decide whether his loyalty can truly be trusted.
New augmentation mechanics: Unlock a new augmentation system, allowing you to replace your damaged flesh with bionics. Improve resilience, alter tactical options, and experiment with increasingly dangerous modifications. As the story progresses, even more esoteric bionics become available.
Meet Trazyn the Infinite: Trazyn the Infinite, the ancient Necron collector whose schemes span worlds, centuries, and civilisations, is no ordinary foe. With subtlety and cunning, he manipulates events from the shadows, treating people, battles, and entire histories as pieces in his collection. Whether you bargain with him, resist him, or attempt to outwit him, the encounter will leave its mark on you and your story.
b04b6db7-037b-4568-954f-ea86d51db2a2: "Mistress Heydari, you expressed the desire to inspect my collection of xenos artefacts. Nuncius, despite his inherent scepticism, has agreed to grant you access under his strict supervision."
b5913c8e-841b-4450-aa7f-a9da5cac1d86: {n}You overestimated both yourself and Seberian. He cannot parry your strike in time... and you fail to draw back your weapon in time. It slams into the bared patch of living flesh on his face with a squelch. Seberian falls wordlessly to the ground. The hail of gunfire his retinue bestows upon you turns you into a pile of bloody scraps of flesh in a matter of moments.{/n}
fc902f97-9099-4c5c-affc-9d096100e030: "The sacred embedded auspexes are registering blasphemous irregularity and are confounded by the inability to satisfy the Quest for Knowledge imperative."
c28ba372-762f-4266-94a8-2f81ed3ff7d1: "Servants of the Golden Throne to Elana. Shells are ready to launch. Report target coordinates for artillery support."
e682407a-20f8-4e49-9a6b-6d4c9bddbf3a: "...founder of Footfall, Humanity's main outpost in the region. It is unfortunate, but Parsimus Dewain's body was lost due to the failed extraction of an already deceased exhibit."
e9fd571a-514e-4c08-9e0b-d81011aab839: "The malfunction is caused by erroneous data-indexing. All-knowing {g|Encyclopedia:OmnissiahGlossary}Omnissiah{/g}, grant us a point of reference," {n}murmurs the Tech-Priest standing at the cogitator. A thick metal plate covers part of his face, and his strange-looking manipulators draw invisible circles in the air around him. A dry and quiet voice comes from the vox-speaker that has replaced his mouth:{/n} "Your vector has been calculated. Please present your designation."
ec0c91d1-5a7b-4477-99be-8855629cd032: "I did not dare distract you from pressing matters before now, Lord Captain. {name}, at your service."
8d9845f4-7dd7-47c1-9534-9014f80da51b: "As a Genetor, I will act as an observer. When it pertains to something as unholy as the genetic distortion of the image of Humanity, one must be armed with extensive knowledge of highly... specific areas of the medicae disciplines." {n}Eogunn casts a dubious look at Klein.{/n} "I will not interfere in the procedure, so as not to hinder the work of Mistress Sauerback's subordinates, but I will interpret the result in its entirety."
a3f2b8d7-4595-40e2-97d3-e842b9cad46e: {n}You hear the sound of many footsteps over your shoulder. After traversing the now-cleared path, Decabalus's hastily bandaged Tech-Priests come to stand behind you, along with the Artisan himself.{/n} "Gratitude: your assistance in clearing the path of traps was extremely effective. We will offer our support in the battle against the enemy of the {g|Encyclopedia:OmnissiahGlossary}Omnissiah{/g}."
e79f0bf6-ff58-4058-8572-6fb00fc0b738: "Given your rank... we'll do the cutting without anaesthesia. It won't be the first time."
d8effb0a-1bdc-40eb-ac9e-8559861da36c: {n}The gates of the palace look impregnable, but as you approach, hydraulic bolts hiss open, sliding into the walls.{/n}
30ceb7cb-58f9-4807-82b8-69daeb176a55: "I found cocoons made out of leaves, and what appeared to be... human corpses inside."
1054800d-5377-4324-8106-9425ddb7f065: The taint of xenotech riddles the Tech-Priest's augmetics so densely it is as if they have been marred with a black blight.
701c4b20-d181-46f7-9992-b722a466753e: {n}You are surrounded by dozens of people dressed in what only vaguely resembles Astra Militarum uniform. Homespun cloth is overlaid with wooden plates, mimicking Imperial flak armour. These clothes are adorned with numerous trophies: pelts, bones, bark, and remnants of giant plants. The savages flaunt their scars and stumps, yet many are fitted with primitive prosthetics. From time to time, someone in the group bares their teeth like a beast, while others nervously grip their weapons.{/n}
deb317ec-696d-4b8e-b549-c911d21b354c: {g|f:a020de8978b1475fb024748c37ca1beb}Torpedo Control{/g} grants +3 more VMP to the controlled torpedo salvo.
e4e7e97c-1ee7-4c25-aa4e-1d0c568b0056: "Hmm... if you ever decide to hunt down that inhuman, say the word, bloodhound. The vile thing conceals itself, knowing that the Emperor's sons are on its trail, but it cannot hide its scent from me."
53b70966-0afa-4411-ae04-989c1c7beff0: "Communication with one of the drilling platforms that extract promethium for Altar-Templum has been disrupted, and the shipments have stopped. It seems the Magos wishes you to find out what has happened," {n}Vigdis supplies in a whisper.{/n}
201cb6ae-0cb0-4e49-9611-4766726a7cf1: Camilla Drivestem is interfering with the Seneschal's plan. She must be persuaded to switch sides, or eliminated by other means.
3ff4970d-7785-405d-804c-fc07d35b5db8: "You tell a good tale! And a planet named after a sergeant... what a laugh!"
d20bc65d-ba58-42cc-a776-baa7c73c0482: "I see the esteemed members of the Adeptus Mechanicus did not refuse to abet the vigilante af Irramon. How could you turn to him after all the privileges I have showered upon the servants of the Omnissiah?" {n}The Rogue Trader's bionic eye lights with red fire, and even his mechanised voice cannot hide his bitterness.{/n}
d587a908-6352-4072-badd-3a8317f6a114: "Werserian, do not interfere. If there was any real danger, the Rogue Trader would take such a risk."
c5c376e9-9ca1-46d7-9665-ff512dbc9855: "Unless you are pretending, you can be proud of yourself, my heir. You have overcome the thirst for power and you have looked beyond the veil of pride. One day, when I have taught you everything I know, you will be a great Lord Captain."
d0b6b2cd-b2e3-4a58-b9a4-b6f77048e1cc: "You, I see, are adept at playing courtly games yourself. Good. I shall await your sign, my dear."
b9f6ee0e-439c-445b-9d37-479ce9caa8cf: The Lord Captain has repelled the attack on Zarkov. The source of the threat lies in the ruins where excavations have begun on the Rogue Trader's orders. The very same ruins where Seberian's trail goes cold.
ec9c924a-bba4-4adc-ad54-6d4907eb8096: The ancient implantation throne belonging to the dynasty was damaged during Kunrad Voigtvir's mutiny. It is up to the Rogue Trader to restore the archeotech machine to working order.
9d6963a5-95b4-42cd-b337-8789ec6fc845: This creature's {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWeaponSkill}Weapon Skill{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerBallisticSkill}Ballistic Skill{/g} are reduced by −15. The {g|Encyclopedia:MaxHitChance}maximum hit chance{/g} of their primary weapon is reduced by −10%. Can be removed and fully healed on the voidship's bridge.
c3d9a22f-e6f0-4364-9876-f9721f53c68a: "Why are mutations continuing even while in stasis?"
8c37214d-208a-45aa-9307-e5e982afeba6: "That would be a deed worthy of the true heir of Seberian von Valancius"
dbc45473-0c38-4bbe-b8fc-ade973f4dcad: {n}You see Seberian's newfound followers exchange nervous glances and turn pale. Seberian himself stands motionless, proud and majestic in his absolute composure.{/n}
837126d6-92ad-4fee-84fc-c750113c1b9d: "Indeed. My son and I are distant relatives of Vox Master Toliman. Our family serves the ship faithfully, and sacred implants like ours are passed down from generation to generation along with our position." {n}The old man inclines his head in a bow.{/n}
56332e0e-33b5-438b-9b13-a8759e8f52fb: "You're right. I will find another solution."
07c220b8-4755-4fdb-9e91-3c3b1776a3a3: "I'm afraid your sword is a fake. You are risking your life by going into battle with it."
cce99f76-0ddc-4466-b112-17a7b6314a3f:
The user deals −15% less damage and can perform only basic attacks with two-handed weapons in this hand.
When the user suffers a trauma, this augment does not malfunction, but the user's maximum hit chance is reduced by −5%.
22ba5858-e597-47ac-bf0b-fe1edda58500: "Pay attention to the Werserians. They are noble and honest."
b38dfe64-5fb7-49a6-b64c-da0e8476a4f5: "The scan results state the opposite. The heart rate of this life form is returning to normal."
5a9f5a83-c34b-4fc2-af2e-290e361a868f: "What can you tell me about Zarkov?"
09e6bb41-084c-4897-901a-87e9c2e38a78: "The worker, the enforcer, and the priest have their own purpose. It is impossible to assemble from them a unified whole fit for all three roles and duties at the same time."
515d4d15-bfe1-4a17-914f-0777acca1021: "Soulless killers who forsook their very essence in the name of a great vengeance. My kin battled them among the stars while your ancestors yet lacked consciousness. A handful of {g|Encyclopedia:MonKeigh}mon-keigh{/g} cannot overcome them. If aid does not reach that world, it is doomed."
c86edd18-c2b5-48d2-9f0c-98550c8a64a3: {n}Eogunn clucks his tongue in displeasure, worrying at a piston on his beard as he thinks.{/n} "The subject left the Sector Bionica prematurely. Nuncius is right, I was so absorbed in the triasomia procedure and its successful outcome that I made the mistake of unfastening the restraints before the psychological evaluation could be conducted. Rogue Trader, we must stabilise the Triasomata before the specimen is forever lost to us."
24046457-df13-4c58-b227-aebf82b485a4: "A trap filled with the foul technology of the Oghyr! How heedless and blind you were, elantach, when you stepped within these halls. However, even my eyes failed to recognise the trap set by the ancient enemy, and your kind is a hundred times easier to deceive.
b3889be1-954a-4e0d-88ae-9abf5a906de7: "The effectiveness of this action would be null. The mutiny on board the voidship has sown confusion and malaise in the {g|Encyclopedia:MachineSpirit}machine spirit{/g} of the implantation throne. If the sacred technology were to revolt during the operation, it could damage your augments. At present, the implantation throne functions only for inspections."
d33cbc49-afc0-4003-ae31-2b06ade78ec6: "It is a world of terrors. Every living thing is trying to kill you, and if it doesn't succeed, it will certainly try again. The best thing we have here is our people. Zarkov has tempered us, prepared us for atonement and our oath, whereas the Final Order of your exalted ancestor gave us purpose."
4fabfbbd-6e46-4a8b-ad88-efb7f4b4c239: "So let's play. Tell me the rules."
0af9681a-4786-4c03-8624-c49d54a8ab9a: {n}Batoshski appears deceptively at ease, but his green eyes track every motion across the bridge. When the colonel fixes a heavy stare on an enforcer, the subordinate noticeably flinches.{/n} "Lord Captain. There are far too many people around. Some are only pretending to be busy. We will take care of that. In due time."
38bd1c03-5590-417a-9398-54e3eab35186: The wearer has +20% {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeGlossary}dodge{/g} against enemies within a 10-cell radius. All attacks made by the wearer with a sniper rifle are automatic {g|Encyclopedia:HitSequenceGlossary}hits{/g}.
d4dbcb88-15ee-49f9-92e1-39eb73f46892: The Lord Captain has discovered ancient marks left by {mf|his|her} legendary ancestor.
f54e894d-45fc-4c0c-922b-4e3b7eccf672: {n}Seberian's expression darkens at your words.{/n} "Not a single word of Trazyn's is to be believed. But if he is holding Laria... I have seen what the xenos does to his captives. Laria is lost to the dynasty — and to me. I buried and grieved for my daughter the moment the portal slammed shut behind my back, and all I can do now is avenge her."
7c71a010-3986-4d1f-bc7c-2d77681bb74b:
Once per {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}, when an enemy enters Eogunn's {g|Encyclopedia:MeleeAttacksGlossary}melee range{/g} or when Eogunn ends his {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g} adjacent to an enemy, he teleports to a random cell within an 8-cell radius.
Eogunn is immune to the {g|f:6338756ef76148369f50bc5d6d48ac4a}crippled leg{/g} effect.
56408c0d-2011-4d24-bfd9-93c48603c843: [End this loop and begin a new one by stepping off the bridge. Perhaps a new loop will change things]
fc9e4892-6626-4ef7-9f97-16d662831ddf: "The Bloodspun Web extols both the ability to end life and the art of continuing it in the service of the Undying One."
6f4979bc-01ec-4ce9-9565-074e0cc0def2: "Good idea, Kuravich. Do not shy away from any means to grow stronger."
f3fd72f3-9649-4ed8-a2ef-f06897574e98: The mechanisms are in working order, despite the burning promethium all around them.
06b1a9b5-c969-4d3a-8e3f-db7ab76adc94: "Distortions. Interference. Entropic logic has intervened in the existence of this sanctuary, striking at its very essence and multiplying a pain that is without end or purpose." {n}Eogunn brushes away the sparks racing down his face with his mechadendrite.{/n} "This tech-pathogen is akin to the worst possible plague."
9773728c-8f95-414d-8790-2a1ffd62f324: {n}Your words cause the crowd to fall silent. The two crippled men who came forward look blankly at each other before beginning to back away warily.{/n}
7778ee76-11c9-4e70-be77-b943b090b967: "I shall say one thing: anyone who truly cares for the dynasty knows that it has no use for weak allies. The noble Drivestems will stand with the von Valancius to the end. Whereas the Gapraks, sooner or later, will drag all the highest nobility down with them. Think what you like about me, but know that Lady Juvenna sees the matter as I do."
ca9d72cd-dcc7-4d31-a67a-4ec76db2f78d: "The God-Emperor looks favourably on the submission of the doomed. But why should we believe you?"
3397291c-8bd8-4d2c-9540-c9916d42e3a4: "Ah, you were checking our combat readiness? 'A superior officer may conduct an unplanned inspection of the Guardsmen's uniform and equipment at any time.' Now I understand what that line from the Handbook meant."
3decc12a-8f3f-40ab-8b1f-d38ec76e8576: [Show the hand with Laria's ring] "In the name of Seneschal Voigtvir, Captain, leave us."
97bdcc05-cbb1-41a6-b878-f1c66cae1e14: "Enough. I am ending this battle for its sheer pointlessness."
cfbbf3b5-35aa-42ef-aced-76d26b31e3bd: "You think I don't know that? A man like Seberian cannot languish here. He is vital to the dynasty, to the whole Expanse! But the bitter truth is that I don't know how to help him." {n}The Seneschal almost snarls at the realisation of her own helplessness.{/n}
82cf0127-2c90-4659-8854-91b47008200b: "Not a chance." {n}Juvenna shakes her head slightly.{/n} "I won't be divulging my plans to someone who stole the title of a dead man so {mf|he|she} could poke {mf|his|her} nose into other people's secrets."
7556ce11-2dac-4c1b-bb55-0e7c696eaa40: "I'm listening." {n}Juvenna raises an eyebrow in question.{/n}
