578a879a-b9a6-43bb-af87-e539b0ee23f8: {n}The sergeant's lips twist into a strange parody of a smile.{/n} "I think I heard you say that before, {d|Encyclopedia:CharGen_MOT_Glory}Lord Captain{/d}. Before the pain set in. It was even worse back then, but when you appeared, I felt better. I don't remember why. I only remember that you came." {n}He twitches, his weapon lowering agonisingly slowly.{/n}
485d52f1-8174-4777-95ce-59849a32fdad: "In truth, whoever lacks purpose in the world yearns for battle so that they might justify their worth. The order of things remains immutable and unshakeable."
0430c04e-0305-4462-b60b-d801aa1f9933: {n}The absence of an air raid siren suggests that you need not fear any {g|Encyclopedia:InquisitionGlossary}Inquisition{/g} bombardment of the command headquarters.{/n}
2ad30e5f-8e1d-4d03-845b-8d1ee916dc47: "Yes, Rogue Trader. All that remains is the matter of the artefact."
abec8fac-5294-445c-8c6a-7fb958932f7f: {n}For a moment, you think there is a flicker of vivid emotion in the person's pain-ravaged eyes — something akin to extreme surprise.{/n}
b3b5e2cf-15e1-4ce2-8cee-45ecb5afbd39: "I know all there is to know. How to maintain it properly, what rites to perform. My grandfather helped build its housing, and my father was present at the consecration." {n}The foreman folds a finger for every boast, immersed in his memories.{/n} "Father even witnessed Master Decabalus bless the anchor-core that powers the complex."
20057f63-440e-4515-9d69-3c6c260a5ac5: "Two reasons. The first is that they were previously fatally injured. I have performed numerous rites and manipulations to ensure that each was able to avoid a fatal outcome. The second is that I have been conducting the necessary measurements to fully assess the compatibility of the configurations of Ferrum Sanctum and organic components for an optimal experimental result. This often requires the extraction of said components."
ac83c4cc-1114-48fa-92bb-4f6f69f1d201: "Not so fast." {n}The enforcer captain appears beside you so suddenly, it is as if he materialised out of thin air.{/n} "I have questions for you, Sergeant, and for your acquaintance here."
ea2606ad-8f0a-43a0-bf20-644e1e7c55f7: "Imagine my surprise when she revealed she was my daughter. My only regret is that she was the child of an unequal union, and I could not name her my heir. I entrusted Laria with my life and the fate of the dynasty, appointing her my Seneschal. Yet I was prepared to give her more. I was prepared to place the Warrant of Trade in her hands."
6e12b946-6243-40f8-ab2b-f38bba9f4a67: The user's weapon {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g} is calculated using their {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerIntelligence}Intelligence{/g} instead of {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerStrength}Strength{/g}, and {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerBallisticSkill}Ballistic Skill{/g} instead of {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWeaponSkill}Weapon Skill{/g}, if those {g|Encyclopedia:Characteristic}characteristics{/g} are higher.
b81b84ad-9704-423d-b8f0-3919f7c7a06b: "It appears so. The water levels are rising incrementally, but the cause remains unknown."
51637839-1054-4261-aa15-69335dab2782: "Seberian has been stuck in this trap for many cycles. We must get him out."
b0a31cd1-d425-4d69-a197-98a932fe42c3: "The sinking of the station would cause a catastrophic decline in promethium extraction volumes — its complete cessation — which in turn would bring production to a halt. This outcome would be extremely — blasphemously — suboptimal. This scenario also suggests — guarantees — extensive collateral casualties among the human units currently ensuring the continuous operation of the Tirkuaz-4 facility — its workers. Such losses would be undesirable — and highly regrettable."
b5ee7043-f24c-4ff8-80da-e58088eb6125: {n}With each step, the faces of your pious companions begin to display greater and greater horror.{/n}
c5ab7ff7-c02d-419e-9cc8-bfd6649a09e7: "I will repeat the experiment. Perhaps I will be able to stabilise him in a new configuration..."
123fda49-aef4-4125-acfb-501971d39543: "This poison isn't meant to kill you, only weaken you slightly. I'll finish you myself."
37340e76-cd62-4654-a820-d73417ce508e:
For 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, this creature cannot take any actions.
If it suffers any damage, this effect is removed, and this creature gains {g|f:a07e7b49ead74e5aabae72858414928e}early awakening{/g}.
75e62137-ac3d-405a-891a-69c4f4bcd7b6: Inspect the logbook and learn the purpose of the vessel.
35046901-cd29-407f-929e-98bf3fad4cc2: A significant portion of your body has been replaced by augmentations.
fbabc69c-1a6d-4833-9f41-a626572bb68c: {n}Without uttering another word, Janur turns on his heel and vanishes into the shadows.{/n}
0533601b-0eb9-4a4c-95bb-43d180d836aa: "I would rather not discuss the matter. If you think I have my eye on the Rogue Trader's throne, you are mistaken. My father has done so much for me, and I am merely repaying his loyalty in kind."
7f9b6d7d-16d5-474d-8918-3fc7088bb966: Light energy that possesses the properties of material objects.
7cf718c5-20a6-455c-9b62-c812f947ca0f: {n}In the crater left by the dropped shell, a sliver of some unknown mechanism peeks through the stone. Dark, solid metal carved with emerald glyphs glints beneath the debris like the exposed innards of a mortally wounded beast. Green shimmers streak ceaselessly across its surface in eerie, perfect sequence, as if every moment the unseen spirits of the xenos device are checking their receptacle and attempting to heal its grievous wounds.{/n}
9561489b-29b6-4ca9-88b8-66442d2dbdd6: {n}Trazyn hesitates.{/n} "Hmm... Your amenability has surprised me. Perhaps I will make you one more offer."
bfaa4556-1c1f-4f6f-a747-e3a05f9ed253: "A small price to pay to achieve a dream, don't you think?" {n}Seberian runs his fingers across his face, and at that touch you hear the soft chime of metal.{/n} "In the pursuit of distant stars and new discoveries, I have lost much. My face, this body... these are just what is most apparent. Yet I regret nothing, {name}. And were I granted a second chance for fresh achievements, I would gladly sacrifice everything once more. I am certain you can relate: to race against death and escape at the last moment... there is no greater feeling, is there?"
3e8219cf-a097-4b21-8ccd-d8d81c0a8e9d: "Protocol 12.1! Layperson, do not allow the hereteks to interrupt the destruction of Object 16!"
71a737fd-ce2d-4998-bd65-2f752e8156fa: "He has been granted an 'honorary' position on the upper decks. The high-ranking officers find his incoherent muttering and blissful smile entertaining and try to provoke the 'dummy,' as they call him. Yet the Omnissiah is merciful, having almost entirely divested the Triasomata of both his cognitive and motor functions. He stands in place for days at a time, staring up at the stone visage of the Emperor, utterly oblivious to the mockery of the... laypeople." {n}Eogunn spits out the last word with anger.{/n}
ec80b1d8-c542-431a-ad9f-b0f583617a3a: "Brilliant! You have grasped the very crux of the matter and articulated it with concision. Nuncius, make note of the Rogue Trader's phrasing, and I will add it to my catalogue of arguments. Am I right in thinking that we have reached a consensus?"
f942efbf-616a-45ce-b931-71864b9cdaf2: "In their darkest hour, the people find strength in faith. He is righteous not because of what he preached, but because of whom he did it for."
f366a89c-7a06-470d-bd9d-df41490d34ff: {n}The Rogue Trader waves you off as if shooing away a bothersome fly, scattering drops of machine oil on the ground as he does so.{/n} "Keep your flattery to yourself."
7b83791a-44fe-4879-9a87-86c3da982cba: "Number of modifications: one. Modification protocol: installation of a vox-grille by an authorised unit. Unit identifier: Genetor Majoris Ferrenz. No data found on other modifications since the inception of operation."
7ee6aab4-b938-4338-85fa-1468dcb20e8a: {n}The colonel frowns, then nods slightly.{/n} "It shall be done, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. You said to get along, so we shall. I shall not intrude."
4d75cd0d-a547-494c-a947-285feb502229: "Perhaps the display is rather... sparse without additional exhibits. However, how could I have placed a relic of my own vintage among less temporally significant trophies?"
55429ec3-de83-457b-9fc3-54e44cd4de90: "Cleophaneees!" {n}He speaks his name in a drawn-out groan, but then confusion immediately clouds his gaze — he seems to have forgotten it again.{/n} "Turned away from golden halls... to guide His flock... No satin, no silk... My sandals are worn to shreds, but still I walk... We hear His word... We hear it! In the place where... they wear cast-offs and... coveralls. I lead them... I lead..."
d0646974-b03c-4d64-95bb-7a1d489f5c0a: "Won't you allow me to explain myself? But, then, I have no right to criticise you."
5df81db3-f12f-4c4c-8a99-c7865b9d0087: "I wish to install a new implant."
cf64f32e-9f26-4a84-9d61-e4c486ff739f: "I have seen such missives before. Typically, they were followed by frightened reports of Aeldari attacks. Be cautious, Lord Captain."
f2ab5155-b921-4b23-9f0d-d8698f4a6909: "I harboured a faint hope that you would not desecrate the bodies of my kind, but no, the baseness of human nature is incorrigible."
602a87e8-d01d-4859-a088-ca0ac86963ab: "I do not know and I do not want to know!" {n}Alva's thunderous binharic howl-prayer carries over the roar of the wind.{/n} "The sacred mining station of Tirkuaz-4 is contaminated, long cycles of xenocorruption have tormented her machine spirit. We soothe and free it — that is enough."
8568c1d4-c3ed-4f27-a191-b54db330b610: The Drover is a dangerous creature, but it has one known weakness: fire. According to the Merovians, fire is excellent at destroying the creature's natural armour.
990af0ee-d63e-4f5d-907d-9dad8d24bbc5: "The artistry with which this machine joins flesh and sacred metal is so laborious and delicate that no human can replicate its efforts. Many medicae officers here have accomplished great things in their practice, but these successes are only crude imitations of the healing sacraments performed by the throne on their patients. Its loss would rob the Sector Bionica of its soul, Lord Captain. We cannot allow that to happen."
5eb83ed9-3c30-484f-803e-ec6d361dc5a3: {n}Not for the first time, Humanity asserted its right to rule the galaxy by force of arms. The Merov Regiment thirsted for vengeance for the fallen — and took it. Their eyes and souls were ablaze, and in those flames many xenos burned before they were driven back to the ruins or exterminated.{/n}
a9e1a713-4b3c-481c-8325-782041793d8d: {n}Ulfar's thunderous roar shakes the surroundings, trailing off into a low, menacing rumble.{/n} "By the Allfather's steadfast hand, these xenos think to mock us, as if they count themselves the wisest of the wise! When I lay my hands upon them, their skulls shall crack beneath my boots!"
c94f8bee-2939-4869-a736-c295b6dc6266: "Isimond? He is a loyal servant. When Juvenna presented him to me, I had no idea how valuable a subject he would become. The weapon you speak of, I personally gifted it to him for his services to the dynasty."
60b7fbd3-b995-4837-8eb8-38c0f4f55119: {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:DrukhariGlossary}Drukhari{/g} hisses several curses in his native tongue.{/n} "Funny. Yes, this little farce is to die for... Yet I have no desire to entertain myself with the games of the Oghyr for long."
301f91d3-e88c-48ec-80c6-80a9b9786e95: Ancient, desiccated remains of a servant of the Omnissiah lie at the tomb entrance. It is possible he carries logs regarding his fate.
b5922898-ed49-45e5-9a3c-9696d61b3623: Call off the expedition and leave the planet alone.
81997ddd-48f0-4fd9-83eb-b45a93a2019b: "Lorrrd Captain, greetings! I've been keeping my watch and helping the Master Genetors and Master Chirurgeons with the more unruly patients. The pain isn't fading, but I wear it like a medal — nothing can break my spirit now."
b9f0d181-85da-40fa-a80a-5c5fbbae1fcc: Press {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} to show this page in Encyclopedia
ef41fb82-6f9f-4c16-b6ca-61bcded25900: "You are in a strange position. Give it time — they will grow used to trusting you."
fd4692bf-e041-4adb-80fa-c4626f9da442: "Remind me again, what exactly are we looking for?"
b9511631-0569-43aa-baf7-d1ebad118925: {n}Kuravich looks himself over and groans, as if only now noticing his blood-soaked uniform.{/n} "Don't trouble yourself, I'm not wounded. I was carrying one of the soldiers. She got bit to the bone, that's where all this came from... And as for the knock to my head, it's actually quite rousing." {n}He pokes a finger at the crimson swelling and winces.{/n}
5f125732-638d-47ae-9900-d349dee750cc: Whenever this ability is used, the whole party suffers −100 {g|Encyclopedia:MomentumWithDetailsGlossary}momentum{/g}. All enemies of the creature have their {g|Encyclopedia:Resolve}resolve{/g} reduced to 1 and cannot use {g|Encyclopedia:CareerLeaderGlossary}Officer{/g} abilities.
5e458ace-806c-4850-9a60-f398fdd9f643: {n}Laria looks at you, her frank gaze lingering past the point of decorum, as if she were scrutinising wares in a shop.{/n} "All right. I shall find a use for you. But don't make me regret it."
514eb5eb-dde0-429c-a523-2c1da14b2aa7: {n}You hear only the low hum of the skull's servo-motors — his views on life in the Imperium remain a mystery.{/n}
ac84596d-71b4-4e11-9367-2318200ae164: {n}Seberian gives a slight shake of his head.{/n} "Perhaps giving me over to the creature would have been the best choice for both of us..."
b1ff0ac8-789e-4105-9b41-efbf90372e89: "Eogunn, do you wish to perform a vivisection?"
8782e6f0-d5b2-41c3-8063-7bec9ba82421: {n}Abelard shows a hint of hesitation for the first time, shifting his gaze from you to Seberian and back again, as if wishing to make sure you are serious about your request. But when the Seneschal begins to speak, there is note of challenge in his voice, and his eyes are fixed on Laria:{/n} "Before you stands {mf|His Lordship|Her Ladyship} {g|Encyclopedia:RogueTrader}Rogue Trader{/g} {name} von Valancius, heir to the greatest protectorate in the {g|Encyclopedia:KoronusExpanse}Koronus Expanse{/g} and bearer of the sacred {g|Encyclopedia:Warrant}Warrant{/g}."
9199121d-968e-440f-a50e-01e5eeb77697: For each stack of this effect, the damage and {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g} of {g|f:e86fc99b6dd24e4a8b6657e252b85901}Served Cold{/g} are increased by +{uip|BonusDamage|8202650d26924bb8aee774adbf520e30}%.
1a384451-0767-40d7-bc89-aac82d918e99: {n}Marazhai narrows his eyes, surveying the portal. Catching your gaze, the {g|Encyclopedia:DrukhariGlossary}Drukhari{/g} gives a subtle shake of his head.{/n}
3dafea1d-fb04-40e5-ac93-83e9582fcf0b: "Rogue Trader, I conclude we are being deceived. The soft tissue damage was inflicted postmortem, masking the true cause of death: a stab wound between the ribs that severed the aorta. Immediate death, a strike of such clinical precision it could rival my own mechadendrites."
50ec918f-bb69-4976-b370-784224978aeb: {n}He casts a bleak glance at the shuttle.{/n} "I'm afraid not, Lord Captain. The ship requires recovery and the application of Adeptus Mechanicus repair sacraments. And those can only be done aboard the flagship. We need an evacuation."
a0079864-cb43-4f4b-92b0-811b3e7bfd79: "So you really intend to rob your betters? I should never have started this conversation..."
c05e62db-8b99-49ec-b28e-31cdfe61be67: Augmentation, reserved only for the ship's elite, raised the crew's performance to an incredible level. Vox officers who had been fused with the ship were able to transmit information instantly, while gunners and artillery officers became working components in self-guided cannons. Together, this made the von Valancius flagship invincible, a legendary beast of the void that would be spoken of for centuries afterward.
8a46eae5-3b02-487e-b816-4fc3d57775d1: {n}Vedka pales, bringing her flesh-and-blood hand to her missing eye. The black stone within the socket seems to shift, ever so slightly.{/n} "Yes, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. I was young and eager to prove my valour and fearlessness, during the Trial Match for the rank of captain-quartermaster. I took a large quantity of explosive pollen and went to those ruins. I intended to blow them up, to show my daring and that we feared no xenos."
59470452-e00f-4086-83f2-bfb2fed1d0b1: "Much like you, I have no other choice."
8c19436c-4bb5-4312-8af1-bf2025ae3873: "Your latest thesis perfectly explains why your attempts to communicate with the laity are so woefully ineffective, colleague."
a4824cfc-ad57-4678-b8fd-fc5e59405519: "We must repeat the experiment. We need to identify several control groups, correlate all the factors, and carefully record the results. You don't discard a hypothesis after a single failure — you must gather evidence. The path of knowledge demands it, for the Omnissiah blesses the resilient mind and willingness to make sacrifices. Only once I have exhausted all possibilities and examined the full range of data will I be prepared to admit defeat."
3d389db4-ff5e-449c-a065-767141cc618d: "Alas, I am of the Kalivin line."
7737ed9c-a9d8-400e-9d00-73822f9f36d6: "I spent a fortune on those air defence batteries! Exalt-117, find out why they're malfunctioning immediately."
5411e88a-edbf-4ba2-8323-2f644efd5b10: "I am a loyal servant of House Gaprak, Your Lordship, and have come to your aid."
a687cbcb-40ab-411b-9498-153f64a63560: "I have no desire to hear about crimson priest squabbles. Die."
ae6dc568-05bc-4003-9ee5-4233ce3aecd9: "I need to return to my flagship. Do you have a vox-station?"
ddec1dcf-d430-4764-869a-3ca9d9bc83ea: You allied with the heir Juvenna von Valancius and sided with the Inquisition in the confrontation with Seberian.
c043d12c-4181-4e93-9665-0a8be572b71f: Forbade the governor of Zarkov to make knives out of the corpses of the iron xenos.
c5454b9e-bf99-4862-9f50-ebdcccd6eba9: "As the stories go, there were plenty of these Brother-Generals. More than enough to claim every decent soul. They treated their troops like toy soldiers, wasting their lives for sport instead of the God-Emperor's cause. Occasionally they sent them to the stars, but of course it was just to serve as shields for other regiments. The First Call-Up, our ancestors, was made up of just such unfortunate lads who ended up in the Brothers' hands."
99c25ae0-a723-4b89-b3b7-86d108ecd5df: "Is productive efficiency not the highest value for a servant of the {g|Encyclopedia:OmnissiahGlossary}Omnissiah{/g}? Servitor malfunctions will occur less frequently, shorter maintenance times will be required, and the labours of the {g|Encyclopedia:Technomat}technomats{/g} will be optimised. The utility of such a solution is indisputable and therefore requires no preliminary investigation."
7439fa17-a91a-4951-af50-602dedfbf7dd: "You could help me rule the protectorate. Your experience is invaluable."
7bb50a13-97fe-49ec-8d14-0e4620ffbc64: "The mind... is mine!" {n}He freezes, then begins smashing his head against the deck with redoubled force, screaming in broken words:{/n} "The bodyyy is not! The b-b-body... A c-c-cell!"
dd023bb5-5f0a-472a-8805-b86c35211b38: At the end of the first {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}, this creature uses {g|f:08dce81ae1254639a6c633acd56611ca}Mad Calibration: Tainted Metal{/g} on the Defiler, lasting for 3 rounds. This {g|Encyclopedia:Ability}ability{/g} cannot be used if the creature is {g|Encyclopedia:ProneEffect}prone{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:Stun}stunned{/g}, or {g|Encyclopedia:PushGlossary}pushed{/g} away.
fefa7662-06f4-49fe-86f8-572186911f3e: "The name 'Elana' doesn't sound familiar to you?"
33adb08d-369d-4d95-bf24-362d2b4eeac4: {n}Captain Dunawski salutes. The light that once shone in her surviving eye has dimmed and the solemn weight of responsibility has settled upon her features.{/n}
9ce8a518-5cef-43cd-a51f-7787162ad3fd: The Chronomancer causes 3 random obelisks to become {g|f:63f6eec4ea38409b926bd1a8429dd5a5}hyperphased{/g} for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
7eba7df6-316f-4398-aca0-89a80102fe34: A heavy ring is fused into the stone. On the part that was spared by the plasma, the coat of arms of House Gaprak is barely visible.
c6ac9f25-681b-401f-8c5e-1bd482d395db: "Idira, you are blinded by grief. Stop. None of us are perfect."
8b1cf0c1-bf3c-410d-a9a2-63a7a79ac4db: "The Necron Overlord is on your side? You have a talent for surprising me, I admit. But that will not save you from a serious conversation afterward."
130c3541-9808-4bc1-bcc8-166f002e0af1: "I humbly greet you on behalf of the von Valancius dynasty, Diodas af Irramon. First, I must offer praise to the {g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}God-Emperor{/g}: by His will has our alliance been accomplished. I also wish to introduce a friend. {name} was undoubtedly sent to us by His providence in our hour of need. It is thanks to {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_DL_JuvennaSide}{mf|his|her} efforts{/d} that Seberian now stands before us with bowed head, as befits a deposed heretic."
584c5fe7-1c1f-4ea8-982b-324116b4d9b5: The Chronomancer is the cornerstone of the Museion's defences. Killing them will disable all nearby Necrons.
d0af4421-15c5-4b94-8404-fda285b2ea4e: "I cannot comment. But I would suggest that no one jump to any conclusions."
21fbb496-2d25-4fba-a279-ecd90e015ecc: {n}Juvenna takes a few ragged breaths, regaining her composure.{/n} "I... believe you. My life is in your hands, but I am still breathing, and that is compelling evidence. I accept your offer."
d5e19afa-64c0-4788-a82f-580be45bb755: {n}As you step off the rustling parchment, the doors of the Chamber begin to slowly open.{/n}
81498865-fcff-4c94-b8cb-6cd530952050: "A worthy ambition." {n}Zorael's eyes bore into you.{/n} "But an unattainable one."
872b8f41-11ec-4e5a-8bca-be4e4967f68c: "You did right by us in the Cage and {d|Encyclopedia:Ch2_AdvicesToJanur}showed what you're made of{/d}. I reckon the old man believes you actually stand a chance against the creature. Must've earned a bit of his respect, I suppose."
443702ee-1c38-4f21-ab6e-05cefd68030a: "Juvenna... you... ungrateful..." {n}Seberian's organic eye widens in shock.{/n} "All this time you have been aiding the enemy? Bringing them to our doorstep? How? How could you?"
4b7660e6-7372-4a4d-aba4-65cdbb144aed: "What are you going to do with the anchor-core?"
73ecce50-1bb5-4a6f-84b1-9f38e41238e0: "Do you see, Nuncius? I have repeatedly said that your statements can only be understood by those possessing extraordinary cognitive abilities." {n}The Manipulus is clearly pleased by the result of your "communication" with the silent interlocutor.{/n}
b6f8c966-2411-4347-8c52-689c642e0a49: {n}Reflections of light play whimsically on the expensive oil with which the portrait is painted.{/n}
0e1b8dbc-4a8c-4772-aa61-08efe1a56e93: "I do not know you or your retinue. How do you know my agents' code phrase?" {n}Laria's flinty gaze latches onto every detail of your apparel and equipment, as if she is hoping to find an explanation for what is happening.{/n}
8360f35e-e99a-41be-8f76-d1bb2d32b5e8: "The vertical mapping of the terrain is updated regularly by my advanced vestibular augmetics, and these register no violations of celestial integrity. I strongly recommend that you conduct a diagnostic of your own ocular systems."
ab31deeb-1e5e-4b0f-8fca-ff57be2298c7: "Steel your heart, Lady Orsellio. Duty often forces us to stare into the abyss."
00f1e583-27d5-4019-8737-1cd98abbd324: Camilla Drivestem and Isimond Sauerback are in favour of Juvenna's plan. Their support should be maintained.
894b7ff7-b4a2-4b5b-bb25-d2a1d2061232: {n}Batoshski tilts his head like a beast before a pounce, yet his voice is surprisingly low and calm.{/n} "Down below, beneath the outpost, lies his tomb. We don't go there, we don't want to disturb the memory of your ancestor. If you desire to visit the Rogue Trader, you are more than welcome. But the Drover has entrenched itself right beside it, which means going there is certain death."
9e6e65f3-77f4-4984-af6e-597697c7cbf6: "You hold so little fondness for your own daughter?"
2b79fdaa-c468-4126-b2f3-c7394b730fc6: "What of the shuttle? Can it be repaired?"
19eb335b-ef9c-4c9b-9f03-7a8dbf28c400: "Reconstructing vector of convergence with the enemy is impossible: personal armament readiness is unsatisfactory. Omnissiah prayer priority activated."
55f9d079-5d57-4466-ba31-f624e92f1f42: Order that the man be granted the Emperor's Mercy.

The wearer uses {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWillpower}Willpower{/g} for {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeGlossary}dodge{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:Test}tests{/g} instead of {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerAgility}Agility{/g}.
Whenever the wearer dodges an enemy attack, they gain +1 {g|Encyclopedia:PsyRating}psy rating{/g} until the end of their next {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}. Stacks.
d61365f2-8d87-41c2-bc7a-0aa17e9f3b19: "Eogunn, I need the truth. What really happened on Amsanct?"
e748c8de-4ce6-4a86-a8b5-e17688c4682d: It turned out the sense of security was an illusion. The Museion's masters were still intently monitoring our every step — and they struck at the exact moment we let our guard down and allowed ourselves a brief respite.
79ff55f9-05e5-4031-887e-09fd3cff28f2: "Not was — is," {n}comes Eogunn's mild yet emphatic correction.{/n} "Everything I said about him before is true. He complements me in all that I cannot foster in myself. He is also my staunchest opponent — his scepticism and caution have always balanced my scientific optimism and audacity. We rarely completely agree on anything, but I believe that without our conflicts, we both would have discovered and achieved far less. Ours is a symbiotic relationship — together we form a whole that is more powerful than its individual parts."
014b62c5-c0b9-4b92-aa56-f686a63ade70: Another crudely carved arrow made up of a few sweeping strokes.
bb83d841-352b-4a92-8e2c-0067bb84ecc1: [Take a sample of the obelisk material]
2358b06f-e5ef-482a-a9a5-c284a175b049: "I had no intention of causing you harm, my heir, and still do not. The God-Emperor knows you might make a worthy vassal yet." {n}Seberian waves a hand casually.{/n} "But enough on that. We have other business."
4cac1ec1-250f-4134-9b5d-6501b584ecb3: {n}Many Merovians nod in agreement. Vedka, however, looks surprised.{/n}
b7ef33aa-3c6f-40da-a55e-9d5d71a01e74: "Ah, my head's killing me... Damn you, Drover..."
669ceffa-4d61-4935-acd3-9ad5a39a10e9: [Activate vox] "Finally, time to get back to recaf, baths, and a hot meal! Vigdis, where is my shuttle?"
edbf463a-fe26-4476-ae62-0c96c8b4bffc: "Lord Captain, only the Navigators are blessed with the gift of steering ships through the Immaterium. Anyone else would succumb to the same nightmarish influence it projects on the crew and the vessel. To attempt traversing warp space without a guide is a fool's errand!"
891b19b6-7c54-4944-b7b3-b17aafc87003: "Retrieving operational log... Completed. Searching for errors... No errors detected. Status of Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}'s implants: functional."
5740bfb6-1ced-4621-8327-71bcc67916a3: The {g|Encyclopedia:Augur}augurs{/g} have detected an unusual anomaly: a planet surrounded by a huge asteroid belt. Seasoned voidfarers say that it was most likely torn apart by tidal forces, and they suggest exploring the rings to obtain useful resources.
50b3fd35-966f-409f-b8e4-25f33edd3b63: Isimond Sauerback is loyal to Juvenna — his support should be maintained.
bf50dcef-a1a9-452d-9d43-0965e5ef5018: {n}Your words strike like a slap. The woman stares at her hands in horror, then casts the knife aside.{/n}
62d62121-3812-4fe9-8cc3-17de1bd528b3: {n}The duchess flinches, and the silk thread of the necklace snaps, sending beads scattering across the pavement.{/n} "It is beyond impertinent to accuse the head of a noble house of such a heinous offence. Even if there were proof, you are no one here, and your word is nothing against mine. I will listen to your fabrications, but only because you will surely start pestering other important personages. We cannot have gossip at a time like this."
e80af410-21dd-4177-a3ea-97c3baecbdf2: "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, Janur is a fine soldier and commander. But his skills are indispensable to the entire regiment. Batoshski can find a new quartermaster, but not so skilled a scout and hunter. He must not risk himself."
bb784b03-b0f6-40ff-b3ca-a8c727dae6c8: {n}The colonel shrugs his shoulders.{/n} "As you wish. It's no longer any concern of mine."
