c156e17e-b36a-4a79-8949-330c1b4a9a94: "The enemy's next move is to bombard the headquarters or our defensive line."
38740540-4c59-4ef8-a099-96e19f1d9d35: "Tell me about the Merov regiment, Colonel."
aa779b8c-9875-4ed7-b6d7-98a6be7e26e7: "I am {name} von Valancius, bearer of the dynasty's Warrant of Trade."
04af0196-dcb3-484a-8dd3-798a334654c6: {n}The sentence read out by the Inquisitor to your ancestor still rings in your ears. To find Seberian von Valancius alive mere moments before his execution — what a foul xenos joke. Everything you have seen here seems designed to mock you, including the fact that you are back where you started, as though a disgruntled host had tossed you out the door like unwanted guests.{/n}
c0b2f9f2-18a0-4e5d-8f8c-10133204865b: [Gain the trust of Juvenna von Valancius.]
a2383102-d9b3-427e-b6da-a154dac55b90: The ancient gate was clearly once sealed and walled up by means of inhuman technology.
c3fa4a8a-a67a-4c07-8854-a8e88435d315: [Attack] "Death to the enemies of Humanity! Death to the enemies of von Valancius!"
7eb05444-f92f-49af-b313-8a0580ef1fca: {n}You step back and wipe the sweat from your brow with the back of your bloodied hand. The patient's chest rises and falls steadily, and though his life is not yet saved, he has at least some chance of surviving long enough to be operated on fully.{/n}
5c20f9b8-1c34-4164-8cd4-4a7dc65f0c90: "An alliance of the God-Emperor's faithful servants, blessed by His anointed one? We could ask for nothing greater."
cee028e5-fb8e-404c-8550-0d2dc7410b1a: "A trap? The Inquisition is a dangerous foe, no doubt about that. Nevertheless, this foe still stands on my land, in my protectorate. Diodas is doomed to failure."
ad5d70d0-547a-4b20-b7f8-714ad27e6fc4: "The voidsman to whom I denied an implant — he is your son?"
f7046718-293b-41db-abe4-f23eed40e494: "You have changed your mind? How ingenious and inconspicuous is heresy. Should you think to oppose the envoys of the {g|Encyclopedia:GoldenThroneGlossary}Golden Throne{/g}, even once, then there is no way back. You are already dead, my fickle interlocutor." {n}A click signals the disconnection of the vox-transmission.{/n}
a89b4501-a71e-4b1b-bb8f-0d7f283a7e8b: "Magos Haneumann, in the name of the Omnissiah, I implore you to accept my assistance in the act of extracting the alien mechanisms." {n}Anxiety hums around Eogunn in a field of galvanic static, making the hairs on your hands stand up.{/n} "I can purge the corruption without interrupting your service, if only you allow me..."
b24dc0c4-6380-4e4a-818a-7768bdbd492a: {n}Juvenna lets out a shrill, spiteful laugh.{/n} "Empty threats! You don't have the nerve to kill the daughter of {g|Encyclopedia:RogueTraderGlossary}Rogue Trader{/g} von Valancius."
df5c9e8a-dbb8-4d97-b82f-5b592fdfa756: The user can move through allies and enemies. Each creature on their path suffers {uip|Damage|c1c53475a0234ba29d036f16b9a51659} {g|Encyclopedia:ImpactDamage}impact damage{/g} with 20% {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g}. While using {g|f:9b09708aa0c244a6bc4a8e46d69c5884}Dash{/g}, this {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g} ignores {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g}.
cac9d63a-1297-4ce5-862f-c259408e4105: "These soldiers have borne their duty to the Undying One for centuries. Their prayers are like ours, like threads of different webs leading to one spinner."
bc88d405-c03b-4e87-8cec-0e5e5a3459b5: {n}One after another, each face, disfigured by burdensome fate, eases. The head of the procession raises a metal arm above his head — and all those able to follow suit, until a sea of prosthetics are held aloft.{/n}
7fdcf45d-be58-4eb3-bb55-1a33d063c1bf: {n}You have heard from drunken voidfarers dread tales and speculation about metal creatures that do not know death. None of these stories ever sounded plausible, let alone convincing.{/n}
502f7fae-1d2b-4080-b74d-3503ed96a707: {n}The Merovians revelled in their enemies' war, placing bets on either side. The xenos victors, however, met the same fate after each skirmish: a surprise attack as soon as the last enemy had disappeared in a green flash of teleportation. And the Merovian who had just been celebrating his windfall would rain fire down on the creature he had bet on not ten minutes before.{/n}
b1e0b0b1-2a2d-40dc-9870-0e26e6c74fb4: "Veering into polemics, Genetor, is evidence of the fearlessness that has overtaken you. May I offer some advice? Rediscover your fear. The methods of the Holy Ordos is a very unfortunate subject for debate."
69788fb8-ab59-4340-8af1-f03c5935fc85: This man died recently — the body is still warm. It appears it simply has not been disposed of yet.
9da41369-51d0-4eba-a60d-33d06e2aa738: {n}Seberian raised his arm in an imperious gesture.{/n} "Allies, the time has come. Heed my order: carry out Seneschal Voigtvir's plan! Prepare to defend the palace! For the von Valancius dynasty! For the Emperor!"
5ed505ef-5eca-443a-8213-d97eaef5b448: {n}The man bows briefly again, but this time with much more deference.{/n} "We will follow you, Lord Captain."
dca9b823-e386-445c-8f2f-74ba19d64f88:
During movement in combat, the user creates an oil-covered area in all cells they move through. This area lasts for 2 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}rounds{/g}.
When an enemy enters, they must pass an {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerAgility}Agility{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:ResistanceTest}resistance test{/g} with a +{uip|DC|6322558474614e31a0e611bf7799d7f9} {g|Encyclopedia:Bonus}bonus{/g} (minimum 0) or fall {g|Encyclopedia:ProneEffect}prone{/g}. Then the oil is removed from the cell.
6f559a2c-ce92-4ed2-a14d-b72dde6c8540: Enemies {g|Encyclopedia:HitSequenceGlossary}hit{/g} with this weapon must pass a {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerToughness}Toughness{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:ResistanceTest}resistance test{/g} with a +30 bonus or suffer {g|f:4755bff66f544cf7bd9f5be37dad08db}cannot attack{/g} for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
f7e8087b-cefc-4627-af4d-982f57742e29: The body of Colonel Batoshski bobs in the murky liquid inside the cocoon.
45e9cfe6-e545-456d-88ec-1ff89789be83: "I'm not convinced." {n}She looks at you indifferently.{/n} "Your proposal does not interest me."
8f2d1cd8-8eea-46b5-880d-d3c842e8871c: {n}Be bold — this loop is ripe for experimentation.{/n}
7682fded-44cf-4e02-8e30-ea12e3126859: "Well. Pat yourself on the back — you have opened my eyes. Lady von Valancius always preferred to act through intermediaries, and that gave her special honour in the eyes of the aristocracy. It appears that I was her tool this time." {n}The unnaturally pale, mask-like face of the Duchess of Dargonus splits into a bitter, resentful smirk.{/n} "The girl learned from the best."
e37f9f90-fc0a-46e1-ba8f-7f6747d2a16c: {n}The man's face twists into a semblance of zealous frenzy.{/n} "Prayer... not words! Prayer... deeds of the rrrighteous! God-Emperor... I... f-failed to see Your plan, so I wailed! Now I behold! My sandals are worn to shreds, but still I walk..."
4ebea647-a5a9-4805-9136-b97aa14480fe: "Let the grace of the Omnissiah sanctify this place, let the sacrament in His name be accomplished..."
0507c0d7-363d-4b3d-a82b-6846d2ce90e8: "Hail!" {n}The Merovian salutes you and stands to attention, drawing his awkward form up to his full, unimpressive height.{/n} "Sergeant Jarub Vukchik, 32nd Merov, at your service."
34cc67ab-809b-44a3-97b9-337ced561486: "There it is, the Drover. Hefty beast... There are two ways down from here. That way leads straight to a flower, an abomination that sprays out poisonous clouds. And the other way leads to a growth that swells and spews flesh-eating flies. What route do you want me to take?"
4bf23e18-24a6-44fa-8073-dbcad21a910d: "Xenos or no... how can you smell anything through all the fumes? All I see is a shoddy, half-abandoned place that someone made an almighty mess of."
b4fc9791-fded-4868-923e-4247054707a7: "We hear and obey, Lord Captain." {n}Martinax offers you a graceful bow.{/n} "I will see to the implantation at once. And the esteemed servant of the {g|Encyclopedia:OmnissiahGlossary}Omnissiah{/g} will assist me in this procedure." {n}The Genetor Majoris's response in binharic carries a distinct tone of displeasure. Ignoring his colleague's reaction, the Vivisector begins inserting thin augmentation spikes into the patient's brain with uncanny precision. Before long, the iron plate of the cortical implant replaces the damaged bone with a resounding crunch.{/n}
1a4ee2eb-d607-44d0-bb8c-708c80cdc7aa: The user's {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} cannot be more than {uip|Armour|e868dbb0274243ccb24e8402397ee2ff}%, and their {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g} more than {unit_stat|WarhammerToughness|bonus}.
ab1046de-b23e-4e22-9668-de8dc6a1be3f: Tip the scales in favour of Juvenna
fdafd29c-a478-4c9a-b4da-8ed9758a99cf: "I wish to go down there and hear what these people have to say."
a883ed68-28ea-4325-9f20-af36ea68e05f: "What an... extravagant practice." {n}Research interest flares within Eogunn's dark eyes.{/n} "I venture to hope that the medicae officers of the Chorda dynasty have screened the donor's grey matter for pathogenic substances."
e810139e-da30-463a-a100-123911c69eae: The subject has lost its former authenticity, exposing the true nature of the replica.
14baf6df-1474-403f-a95a-c18940b0c35d: "Deathwatch? Is that a Chapter of some kind?"
2dda7437-057d-46c8-ac50-803b6b324376: {n}The servo-skull hovers in haughty silence, which is broken only by Eogunn's sighs of exasperation.{/n}
09ea5e95-0dbe-4c19-9f47-3378d5fe224a: {n}At the last moment, you dodge and Batoshski's knife only tears through your clothes, forcing you back even further. Instinctively, you pour more strength into your next attack, as you strain to reach your opponent.{/n}
22dec827-67f1-4221-8f0c-8c046148b332: "Sergeant, I can do without your help."
3862f51c-9ab8-4b5a-8962-54a224f959cb: Eogunn claims that his former laboratory is located within the Aviorus System. He seeks the Rogue Trader's aid in travelling there and retrieving important samples.
c58c0ae3-25c8-45d8-9c91-aa8dc6d64215: The righteous Triasomata created by Eogunn has escaped from the Sector Bionica, making his way to the lower decks. The Rogue Trader must find him and decide what to do with the runaway subject.
b0d92833-7ab6-4467-9564-240ad6b7305b: "Matches in data-index... not found." {n}The Tech-Priest turns his head, directing the gaze of his absent eyes at you.{/n} "You are addressing Magos Exalt-117, Chief {g|Encyclopedia:Enginseer}Enginseer{/g} of the von Valancius flagship, identification number 11782700012. I grant you permission to fulfil the objective of the selected trajectory. Speak."
6aa6ff22-affb-46b9-b830-2f5bc383f2fd: Locate the lost ruins from Zarkov's orbit
ecadaed6-e140-4cdb-af64-754e09af20bd: {n}Emitting a snide binharic hiss, Pasqal declares,{/n} "The attempted re-evaluation of the Machine Spirit by Genetor Februs has been logged. The outcome has been flagged in his efficiency metrics, pending a formal review of his competence."
86708c7d-c0a5-4613-ac9a-d64b11923b7b: The target creature starts its next {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g} with half its {g|Encyclopedia:MovementPoints}MP{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}AP{/g}. This ability does not affect {g|Encyclopedia:ExtraTurn}extra turns{/g}.
4493343c-3f2f-4f1b-8838-75dd1043f3ea: {n}Seberian says nothing for quite some time. Finally, he squares his shoulders slightly, and you hear the quiet grinding of augments.{/n} "Quite right, Juvenna, where are my manners?" {n}The Rogue Trader turns to you and nods graciously, not removing his hand from his weapon.{/n} "Esteemed strangers, you may introduce yourselves before I call you to account."
d8f55271-103b-4f05-a9ea-725552fb8145: "The distinction is well observed, but misinterpreted. It is evident that the Aeldari's affinity for Empyrean energies is higher than that of Humanity, and as such even the miraculous Geller field is unable to save their minds from... undesirable consequences. Do not accuse others of stupidity in an attempt to explain away your genetic defect."
8d74d98d-70b5-439c-85bd-a1b0feb3c8e2: {n}As you leave the shooting range, you feel arrogant stares burning into your back.{/n}
2dd306f2-cc83-4e38-a976-fe019b343d2a: {n}A powerful figure in dark armour lies sprawled on the platform, under the light of emitters from the unknown xenodevice. It is undoubtedly one of the {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptusAstartesGlossary}Astartes{/g}, and he is still alive. Strange shackles made of light restrain his arms and legs.{/n}
25dad62c-72b2-4e40-bfb3-1c5a8bd402c9: Under the command of its ruler, the 32nd Merov Regiment became the perfect weapon, an armed cult infamous for its twisted discipline and cunning.
b5d719f6-18c0-4482-8ab1-5f2ca5b502a5: Cables thrumming with energy, humming and spitting blue sparks.
b1c91530-82a6-45fb-a5d7-7caeb0e3ea92: "It's atonement for the one, heir of Seberian. After death, our bodies are turned into poisons, medicines, and materials. That's how a person atones for everything they did in life. If you're interested in the details, ask the captain-quartermaster. She's in charge of distribution. The previous holder of her rank taught her how to tend to the corpses."
25bc04f0-2fb3-4b00-ab45-f941460087a5: "I have been asked something like that question hundreds of times. And, I must say, it has never been asked by a righteous person. The lord of this planet, Seberian von Valancius, compromised himself several cycles ago by committing monstrous xenoheresy. In forsaking the very foundations of Humanity, he has sealed the fate of himself and all Dargonus."
d9736049-86d6-4a0a-946e-6809124eafe2:
Installing augments inflicts stacks of the {g|f:780497b04a0944e59cb57e68bb9775c4}ferrum sanctum{/g} effect.
For each augment installed, the character suffers +15% more shock damage from arc grenades and other effects that electrocute the target.
579e25a2-aaad-4c2b-a2cc-737b731f343f: "Why are you torturing yourself? You could have dulled the pain with stimulants."
634f441a-d775-4f62-9147-a43de24b52b7: "A dangerous question to pose for someone using a Rogue Trader's patronage as a shield against accusations of xenoheresy, 'shereen.'"
8fa16956-ed12-406a-9f5a-d02d677b048b: "Well, officially we're a supply platoon. That is, we gather the resources that the Cage needs to survive. And each of us has to have some special skill. Like knowing about poisons, making ammo, or recharging power packs for las weapons. We're supply troops and that means we're the best of the best."
376d490d-4575-47fe-8177-6125a540d246: "I am well aware of his ambitions. But, however I may feel about them, it is not cause to call him a murderer. Or perhaps we are missing a link in the chain."
06944ad4-ba40-4d65-95ce-153e01bafc15: The command headquarters, which survived the bombardment, stands as a powerful argument in favour of defence.
3ddeafc4-4e4a-46ab-be27-afe6310259ee: {g|f:48139b5d8510426fa2d07a55f99236f5}In My Sights{/g} gains additional {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g} equal to {uip|PER|07be5f794f6b4f54b91b0904e0c50e4b}%.
9a6393ea-d33b-4f5c-be95-1ce2221057ab: "There is a time to speak, and there is a time to be silent. A wise person knows when the occasion calls for one or the other. You are a wise person, {name}."
13c8b840-2611-4ce7-a37e-0be892914a10: A small shard of strange melted metal.
01b72869-2378-4869-9a8d-c037ae093186: "For the sake of my dream of a great dynasty, I was forced to master an art necessary for any man of my station: intrigue. I take no pride in my actions, yet they made me what I am: a holder of the Warrant of Trade. And I do not regret that I had to eliminate a couple of relatives along the way. It only benefited the dynasty."
4876e427-b346-4124-838b-8a9fd7ecc8f6: "Infinitesimal on the scale of the galaxy yet substantial for this star cluster. The chronostructure of Humanity in this region was, to a great extent, determined by this specific historical episode."
72d8e0ef-2798-4293-bdd0-f6aefb654530: "Diodas, like a spawn of the abyss have you poisoned my daughter's mind! You cloak yourself in His name, but you go against His covenants. Fools like you only burn away the shoots of knowledge that could serve the good of Humanity. Revel in your triumph, Inquisitor: my legacy is destroyed. But you will hear no contrition from me, for the truth is on my side."
b9db881b-377f-401a-b24a-2b2d865a355b: At the start of their turn, this creature deals {uip|ShockDamage|68f3934e6b5345808b49eaea6ce758a1} shock damage to other enemies within a 2-cell radius around it. After, half of the stacks of this effect are removed.
5fcef019-cf73-4641-982f-77bb01715d63: "No need for such base insinuations: bribery had nothing to do with it! The Sauerbacks are insignificant people, with pitifully few privileges at court. The only way to entice one of that ilk is with the promise of a noble title. But now Isimond shall not have it, for he cannot hold his tongue."
1c0e1bd8-ce50-4a53-8842-b394bc3aa806: "So you want to be treated without waiting your turn?"
8e16ec48-70e9-4287-9158-7b487aa53ef7: "Remind me: what purpose does the Sector Bionica serve?"
f335c392-8f33-40b7-9f7a-b41bc875d593: "Just think, we have visited a fully functional Necron tomb of titanic proportions! If only I'd had the chance to touch upon its secrets, or even to induce that powerful xenos into a more productive conversation..."
d2af67c3-5a5a-427e-aff6-2a2101e89e41: The screens of the ancient mechanisms are as empty and dark as a dead man's eye sockets. The machine spark has not filled them for a long time.
0aadccaf-87e3-4a5a-9ebd-bb4ce3000746: "I am Chronomancer Amatekh the Faithful, vassal of the Nihilakh dynasty, one of the Wardens of the Museion." {n}He gives you an indifferent look, yet you can still feel the contempt it carries.{/n} "It is my duty to maintain the exhibits to the fullest extent possible, safeguarding them from encroachment."
11063cd3-5a7d-4d7e-8bcf-a8f9d1e6aedf: "It would be strange to suppose that that... being would release us without a final parting blow. This is the least of the harms it could have inflicted."
6a97a924-4f55-4e6e-a434-82ba0e1baddd: "The {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptusArbitesGlossary}Adeptus Arbites{/g} precinct fortresses, Rogue Trader, remain the core defensive bulwark in times of rebellion or attack. Every Arbitrator knows how to hold a bastion of loyalty to the Imperium during all-planet cataclysms. I could share that knowledge with the Zarkovians."
708fc467-cc25-4707-af1f-edd30c6e1a66: "I received a message from Trazyn. He wants you in exchange for the Warrant."
69d43afc-391e-42e1-8d58-8ec9fe2de149: "Lovely. Not suspicious at all, is it, shereen? I'd hate to step into an obvious trap so recklessly, but it seems we have no choice." {n}Jae casts a sad look at the silent void and hunches her shoulders, shivering.{/n}
d97af70c-0a66-4d11-b0ad-2ce9019a931a: {n}It is a marvel that the augmetic survived at all, albeit in a sorry, unusable state. The staff on your flagship should be capable of restoring it.{/n}
076db7d1-1d4c-4433-97f5-deb161d5e24c:
Eogunn can now gain {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g} stacks.
At the start of combat, Eogunn gains +2 stacks of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g}.
2c9f0237-b9aa-4c74-904f-f73c6874284e: "Shereen, shereen! Don't miss the chance to make some coin. Kais-torr-Shaiga is a name I know well. I'm sure he won't disappoint — his business acumen is amazing for a Tech-Priest, and his agile mind would put any other Cold Trader to shame. Even just being acquainted with him has its benefits... which means we'll find plenty of mercenaries instead of Merovians for dangerous raids."
b507c23e-26b9-4ce2-b919-6598e2f91b20: {n}Upon seeing you, the Arch-Militant makes the sign of the Aquila and bows, waiting for permission to speak.{/n}
c266b37b-caed-47f4-aee8-fbbb38e17c54: Eons of time have chipped away at this obelisk's integrity. It has only 90% of its {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} left and 10 {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g}.
d7d99025-8bfb-4584-9cb7-b6a867bd0293: "Not quite. Faith in the God-Emperor is more important than any function. Without faith, there is no Imperium."
bd06bf1c-f54a-4890-9f50-4a0f9f1bf4e2: "I can't say anything about feeling watched, but the traps were definitely set by someone intelligent. And someone with a knack for strategy. Glaito, watch the perimeter!"
72329c9a-0116-41c2-920b-25c0a2bb5f98: "Protocol forbids it." {n}The Tech-Priest's growl sounds like the rumble of a small manufactory machine.{/n}
e9536fc8-165d-4179-9215-fc7752812249: {n}But of one thing you are certain: you were thrown back to your starting point at the precise moment that your distinguished ancestor, Seberian von Valancius, breathed his last.{/n}
942f9c23-4553-402c-97cc-e88b0704b5d0: Eogunn's {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} is increased by +{uip|ArmorBonus|cdd522da57e84708933db6908f8b79ea}%, and his {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g} is increased by +2.
c382448a-fda5-43dd-ae46-bc5b2cb0c77a: "It does not befit one to lose face in the presence of servants, my heir. The order to bring Janus to heel was issued by me, for that world required the Rogue Trader's firm hand."
10f62c36-3ebf-4419-928e-ce2906bd99a8:
Whenever the user {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeGlossary}dodges{/g} a {g|Encyclopedia:MeleeAttacksGlossary}melee attack{/g}, they use {g|f:22d6c1b32a6d47f4899a370e28cf16f9}Kick{/g} against the attacker.
{g|Encyclopedia:CareerVeteranGlossary}Arch-Militant{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:Ability}abilities{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:Talent}talents{/g} do not affect this counter-attack.
bfb192f3-8379-432d-84c5-114ba2747e17: "I've seen how the common folk live here. They complain of hunger, cold, and exhaustion. My people and I are used to hardship, but I'd like to know how exactly are we going to be settled?"
d355c075-3ae6-4e87-9583-74284f72e024: "Will you tell me about your settlement?"
cdcf4154-8867-400c-a909-2abc0b5d813b: {n}The jumble of torn skin and glistening gore looks extremely gruesome and is obviously causing the wounded man a great deal of pain. If it truly was caused by a shard, then the enforcer narrowly escaped death.{/n}
36ba26a2-6278-4d95-ae56-9c9bbe635d95: "Seneschal Voigtvir and I have already taken care of the traitor."
cd591331-56b5-4661-bcb6-659842e139af: "Battling day after day! Glorious times await, brother!" {n}Ulfar gives a bloodthirsty grin.{/n}
048a0367-e5a4-49a8-80f1-c886da3bcd87: [Address the patient] "Do not forget to breathe. It will dull the pain."
16be710c-e3aa-4c41-a1ea-c0edaf8684fc: "You have the right of it, master gunner. I remember in the old days they wouldn't let riffraff like that anywhere near here, but now look where we are. Strange times, indeed."
9474bac3-d32a-4f02-bc1a-0a901ac4c466: <u>({g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerIntelligence}INT{/g} bonus + number of Tech-Priest's {g|f:780497b04a0944e59cb57e68bb9775c4}ferrum sanctum{/g} stacks)</u>
f9afd5fc-a328-4347-9392-05291e36d7da: "My sword? I will restrain my wrath only because such news is better heard before the battle. Away with you!"
1298c525-5b61-4264-bd5b-b0dd2e8b7fe6: "Are you really... Seberian? Seberian von Valancius?"
011483d9-c1af-4214-98f2-ff3f4a8aeff1: The Guardsmen charged at the enemy, the battle cry of "Atonement for the one!" on their lips. Distant descendants of the penal legionnaires of Merov, the Zarkovians fought with all the fervour and ferocity of their progenitors, consumed by a holy hatred of the xenos. They unleashed the fury of the doomed upon the Necrons, and were able to hold them off just long enough for my scion and I to reach the platforms.
4f6e5d47-9020-4312-9e67-4682db87cf42: [Search for information on Seberian's actions in the temple]
0cf5e8b9-92ab-4819-952f-bb52b0c76386: If the servo-skull swarm is targeted by the {g|f:823b70d6ab9349b2a1e2b1bbbe4798e2}Galvanic Field{/g} ability, it also gains its effect.
32131ccf-8df5-496e-be7d-7818b700d29a: "See for yourself, Your Lordship. I am willing to risk my life to prove I am telling the truth."
6c73bf8b-a627-440d-841e-552aa97821ae: {n}Your reflexes are faster, giving Juvenna no time even to pull the trigger. Your shot leaves a gruesome, ragged hole in her torso. You should depart the scene of the crime as quickly as possible: there can be only one punishment for killing an heiress in cold blood — death.{/n}
013cbcad-2078-4f63-8a59-2f418008469f: "It is extremely gratifying that you share my position!" {n}Eogunn's sonorous, vibrating voice rings with enthusiasm. A discharge bursts from the galvanic cell in his abdomen, sending blue sparks scattering in every direction.{/n} "I hope you will share in this triumph with me, Rogue Trader. I will apply myself to my research with redoubled zeal."
aa89850b-d1d5-4390-9221-8bd9ab2f3206: "I smell a good hunt. If there is a worthy opponent here, let them come out and try their strength against a wolf! And if they choose to hide, they are not my equal."
64842354-3a15-48ae-a8ca-a322a9ae7125: {n}The C'tan's attention turns to you — you feel it as clearly as if the touch of this inhuman mind were a tangible thing. It lands like a monstrous weight upon your shoulders, but a few moments later, the heaviness disappears — and instead, the familiar speech of Trazyn the Infinite begins to flow into your now free mind.{/n}
9efba759-ab7e-4d91-9e92-3c690048f124: "After more than one decade of searching, I have amassed a modest collection of objects that belonged to these Necrons, but I have never succeeded in understanding the purpose of most of them. Many believe that Necrons died out long ago, leaving behind only ruins of black rock, but I do not believe so. They exist still, and they are closer than they seem."
299621d1-4085-469d-99a9-5b9f0d3e987e: "The shallowness of your thoughts is rivalled only by the narrowness of your perception, Proctor. Your accusations are groundless and your conclusions false, born solely out of blind faith in the Lex. Understanding nuance is an unfamiliar concept to you."
8fe2dee1-c8d5-4768-b484-e88be9799a5a: <u>(user's levels of {g|Encyclopedia:BurningEffect}burning{/g} (does not include warp burn or soul burn))</u>
40a87b63-80ef-4ca4-bb85-4b92394f9b9e: "An astonishing story, do you not think?" {n}Seberian clenches and unclenches his creaking hand.{/n} "The sword would have lain in that tomb for many millennia had I not found it. But find it I did, and I turned the legacy of the xenos to the service of Humanity. For it is our species' destiny, heir of mine, to rule the galaxy and everything within it. Everything, without exception."
312fe9fb-27d4-47ce-9332-387a2d176be0: The user's attacks with weapons in this hand inflict +1 stack of {g|f:678380188cb646a68a153703638c9f55}penance brand{/g} on {g|Encyclopedia:HitSequenceGlossary}hit{/g} until the end of combat.
d065d0ea-edf1-4da6-8318-ec760d86f797: [Address the Triasomata] "Greetings, I am {name}. I just want to talk."
83583a90-e8dd-4f86-b252-844a451a3562: {n}Finally, she sighs wearily, carefully straightens the certificate between her hands and places it in a special container with a code lock.{/n} "I would like to reflect on all this in privacy, shereen. Thank you for not leaving me in that situation. If I had been in your place... I don't know if I would have left you."
065b960d-e723-46e4-9315-2d94f49d2383: "That thing is ancient and huge. My great-grandfather saw it in the roots way back. We've known about it for ages, but this is the first time it got this close."
b629c5c6-dfca-428a-afd8-2a42f03ff036:
"The sage who dared to conquer death and time,
Now stands before its threshold, past his prime.
Within the halls of flesh, both crushed and spent,
Yet proud. Defeat he never shall consent."
f043a064-7297-4eab-be90-5cd1cae3040d: Exalt-117's logs show Seberian passed through the side gate and vanished into the heart of the ruins. You must follow his trail.
b845cdff-fade-4126-923b-d7cd25cfaacc: "A critical instability has been identified in the air defence battery installed at the outpost. It requires immediate repair."
906fb715-d09e-4536-893f-e91fddb6961d: {n}Argenta bestows upon the young woman a look brimming with genuine warmth.{/n} "I was taught that true faith is most easily witnessed in the hour of greatest trial. And here it stands before me. Years of deprivation have not robbed you of your faith in the God-Emperor, and the imminent oath of loyalty to His anointed shall be a worthy reward for this feat of the spirit."
204ce50f-4ac4-4e99-a0d7-105d09c63bb6: "If you call each other colleagues, why do you not work together?"
f2ec21b4-846d-4da7-adbf-1b7bbfa807ca: "I order you to deal with the growth."
cdb1119a-cef7-4ac0-8fee-f877a0961a3b: "I thought I could benefit the Imperium by creating truly righteous people. But I did not consider that each person in the Imperium already has a role and function. Like components in a machine, each working according to its purpose, people who fulfil their function are already righteous. This is an incontrovertible fact. Trying to assemble them into something new robs them of their functionality and, consequently, of their spiritual purity. I have committed the transgression of innovation, but with human beings, for which I have already assigned myself appropriate penance."
6d780b7a-ecbd-448c-8528-ef5afb4be29f: "Really? Well, out with it. We need to settle this as quickly as possible. You cannot face the enemy with someone holding a knife to your back."
19028351-26f0-4fb4-b4a9-3c3a398a6f5d: "You yourself suggested I use it as proof that we are in a time loop."
efe33fcd-c630-407a-83cb-493ed826c279: "How did you come by such a grievous wound?"
29f8f415-2f77-4901-856e-5cb2f63c52e2: {n}Despite your best efforts, the game is over quickly. Exalt-117's moves are perfectly calculated — it is as if he sees the non-existent board in several dimensions at once — but all you can do is carefully memorise the play. After the final winning move, the Magos concludes:{/n} "Do not reproach yourself. Laypersons are often unable to keep track of the numerical vectors. I personally reworked the rules of classical blind regicide to suit the calculation mechanisms of sacred machines."
cf657a71-8d78-4f44-a47a-065c993ad4f2: Until the end of their {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, the user gains +2 {g|Encyclopedia:Resolve}resolve{/g} for each {g|Encyclopedia:CritChancesAndCritDamageGlossary}critical hit{/g} scored if they have an {g|Encyclopedia:AugmentsFullGlossary}augment{/g} in the cortical insertion.
9c572dac-13a3-4ebb-85e8-845c20932b2b: "I don't think the problem is due to a lack of ordinary Tech-Priests. These cogitators have been here longer than I've been alive, and no one has ever worked out exactly what it is that disturbs them so deeply."
861374b5-4fbd-4cf9-86e4-d716f6ca99ec: "Sergeant Janur Batoshski, 3rd Supply Platoon, 32nd Merov Regiment. We are pleased to welcome you to Zarkov, Your... Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}." {n}Janur stumbles slightly over these words, as if recalling their sound as he goes.{/n}
762fc9e5-32d7-4478-b64c-3ae87856e969: {n}Following {d|Encyclopedia:CharGen_DH_Torture}your advice{/d}, the maimed man gasps for air — once, twice, thrice. His face regains a hint of colour, though his teeth are still clenched convulsively.{/n}
0f158bc3-d4d7-4a4e-88db-d8dcc9d6f6c7: "Among the exhibits in his 'Museion,' I saw unparalleled treasures... artefacts that would have made me the most powerful Rogue Trader known to the Imperium. But I misjudged and was myself turned into an exhibit... What happened next, you know for yourself." {n}The old man closes his eye, deep furrows crossing the patches of remaining skin.{/n}
02241fb3-dabf-4d2d-ad7f-608432e5d645: Electric flashes, like the wrath of the God-Emperor Himself, pierced the air, shielding the humans from the death-bringing xenos. One after another, the servants of the Omnissiah fell, spilling blood and sacred unguent, but they were too few. A number of the xenos were struck down, only for the rest to advance their onslaught, treading on the bodies of the dying Tech-Priests. Only a handful survived from the squad — yet, defying fate and thwarting our monstrously powerful enemy, my scion and I were unharmed.
099bbb1f-e108-40a0-b493-17c7f933c71b: [Smile sympathetically] "I see you, too, have loved and lost. Such is life."
4ec26cfe-5911-4958-85c3-275b4a6de60c: "The effort to recover Seberian has been postponed several times now. An unpleasant hypothesis has formed at the intersection of my mental paths: not all expedition members desire his return. If events continue along the present vector, I must go and retrieve him myself. The likelihood of losing my way is small, as Seberian was planning on leaving marks behind."
514f2453-f901-40e6-8f92-577cefa6bee0: "First, I shall have a proper look around here."
559f3e0f-0562-428f-85f7-64ecc4a83c3d: {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:Genetor}Genetor{/g} protrudes slightly forward. The multitude of visors hidden beneath the hood move asynchronously and distractedly. His voice carries a tinny twang, the edge of the vox built into the Tech-Priest's body was melted by a {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_AugmentatorWounded}recent wound{/d}.{/n}
f3090846-644f-40b7-b585-744f03f1ea70: "I grant you forgiveness. Sauerback, take her back to the deck she came from and let her go."
2fd5e77b-a388-40ff-a03c-1cc2c99129b3: {n}Almost instantly, liquid flame spreads throughout your body. Pain pierces every nerve... and begins to slowly recede as your body adapts. A large tremor hits you, and sweat instantly soaks through your clothes.{/n}
d830b688-85d0-4655-b8b1-0af82f786aa9: "Esteemed Magos." {n}Seberian inclines his head slightly in greeting.{/n} "I am pleased to see that the servants of the Omnissiah value their cooperation with House von Valancius."
b5f83257-6ab6-4a16-bfd5-6b02bc7de737: {n}The Arbitrator freezes, looking around anxiously. His cyber-mastiff rushes forward and stands between you and the rest of the retinue, as if to shield you from a possible attack.{/n} "Xenos machinations, Rogue Trader. I insist on thoroughly scrutinising everyone present before we enter a second time. Anyone capable of such a thing is an incredibly dangerous enemy."
83d19a3a-4285-4583-a52a-07da39f2cbca: "Didn't you demand that Seberian be returned to your collection?"
ca982661-4d9b-4ba9-b9ab-d514e4e27582: "Of course, Rogue Trader. I'm sure my dearest colleague will be honoured by your attention."
bbbc8636-6e6a-410e-920f-6dab9c798820: "The sensations you are experiencing are within normal limits. The operation was a success. Leave the facility and stop disrupting the medicae officers' work."
1523b6c7-702f-425f-ae4a-3daf302e677b: "Out of the question." {n}Eogunn shakes his head.{/n} "The outcome of the proposed cooperation is predetermined, and it is most unfavourable, Rogue Trader."
68c80952-9e30-4af1-93eb-0ce13688d2c7: {n}Danrok gulps for air convulsively.{/n} "I obey Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship} and shall remain in place for as long as you will it."
93912e48-27b1-4f72-9b2c-bdd9f8aaaf29: "Quite a show, wasn't it? The Drover's tendrils snatched your machine right out of the sky. The damn thing's been besieging us for two weeks now, we even—" {n}The soldier cuts himself off, seemingly before he says something he should not.{/n} "Doesn't matter. Not until we know who you are."
1713572f-da55-4874-8cc2-c760eb84af85: Click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} anywhere on the system map to command your ship to move there.
33f56aa5-489c-4946-81d2-54f03818f68c: "If you are capable of engaging in thought experiments for days at a time, I can only express my respect, but more humble people are not so inclined."
f1062fd4-3d58-4fdb-b5f5-3567cdf0b461: "House Gaprak would be the best choice, as they are closely related to the production of equipment and augmetics."
88cbba96-81b2-437c-a8fa-4a68b1ba871e: The creature is immune to {g|Encyclopedia:ShockDamage}shock damage{/g} and the {g|f:15f32ade9f3e4b9f8e47a2840f514b70}conductivity{/g} effect.
392fa9d3-a43e-4bf1-9a19-2c7978ac3566: Predatory intelligence flickers behind the creature's eyes.
debec7d3-606a-4f82-8f51-ec16e797c8eb: "Rogue Trader, I thank you for your contribution to our research. Nuncius and I can now call you colleague in all earnestness. I need to think, rethink, and discuss this new data with Nuncius. Please find me on the bridge later — we must discuss the results of the experiment."
7a5fd0f0-9b80-41ab-8c69-98786e59c5d3: "Ovedius Gaprak's company was destroyed on my orders, it is true. Palatine Lorcanda, having learned of their approach from me, brought the full might of the {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptaSororitas}Adepta Sororitas{/g} down upon their heads. But Ovedius survived, which was not part of my plan. Camilla Drivestem was eager to assist me and to settle a score with an old foe in one fell swoop. Captain Sauerback was both competent and ambitious enough to carry out such a delicate commission."
8bb10ade-e3cf-4b37-b4a3-acad2fc5c154: "How did you come to serve on the flagship? How did you become a vivisector?"
aa8336f3-df0b-44bd-813a-c5b33ad8e1fe: The user jumps to a cell within {uip|Distance|7e79642d9cdd442cb014809f1b4973a0} cells without provoking {g|Encyclopedia:AttackOfOpportunityFullGlossary}attacks of opportunity{/g}.
a87c424a-c9a1-42b1-b44c-eb26fcba75f3: The wearer begins combat with (2 × {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerFellowship}FEL{/g} bonus) stacks of {g|f:b669fe2c03a240fca1b3b2429ca87414}tactical advantage{/g}. Base armour property: +5 {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerStrength}Strength{/g}.
b76621f6-ec15-4ed3-aba5-516428f2d14a: "My soul hears no screams, witch. Only... silence. Devoid of hope and life. The coldness of eternity that presages calamity."
50dbf588-e869-4abd-ba40-ea9362e57e59: "To refuse your request would show haste in delivering the sentence. But haste is a sign of weak faith. Seberian's fate was sealed the moment he thought xenos artefacts might serve the good of the Imperium. It was then that the sentence was handed down. I am no judge, my friend, but merely a messenger of His will. Therefore, speak."
44dd6d9f-96f4-446e-b36f-b75627738102: {n}The man in the bed stares back at you through glassy eyes. His arms, held tightly to the bed by straps, shudder, his face contorting in a grimace.{/n}
9e540e55-fe66-4146-9491-552775e91b40: This rust-eaten Valkyrie with the emblem of the 32nd Merovian Regiment is at least several centuries old.
b5aad916-8b14-48ae-a2d0-76626a94bea1: "Seberian von Valancius. This rare member of your family garnered my interest. He did this by crossing my path and stealing a priceless relic, which I had intended to add to my collection. This act so impressed me that I decided to recover stolen item and thief together. I knew that Seberian would return seeking other treasures hidden on the world of Zarkov. I knew he would not forgo the opportunity to investigate the portal he would so conveniently discover inside the temple..."
db1cc937-ec1b-43b4-91aa-da091ae18c1a: "Did you have a chance to see what attacked us?"
bf2ad245-dbd0-4f6e-9d8f-92177a30c7d0: "Vivisector Martinax! How are things in the Sector Bionica?"
215e0abb-f44d-4243-b53e-1e09fae403bc: "I follow orders and fight where I'm told. The senior commanders deal with the rest of it."
aff2b8f4-1d2d-4b78-83df-565d1622fcfd: "Their colours... smeared. Ruined. As if someone has defiled a painting, dragging a brush dipped in crimson-brown across the still-wet canvas..."
e705098b-530e-433a-9fb5-9b1f284a124e: "The source of your data is woefully inaccurate, Captain. The scale of the penal world of Vheabos VI is much too modest to warrant the Cult Mechanicus expending such a great deal of effort and resources on it. Only forge worlds receive that honour, as a rule."
1ba4071d-7957-4674-9dc7-b92f0e0e573e: "I'm afraid my taste buds have atrophied considerably, so I cannot gratify you with anything other than my already stated findings. However, I do wish to note that tasting nutrients in combination is a most satisfying process, regardless of their form." {n}Eogunn smiles pointedly.{/n} "I suggest we proceed to the more substantive portion of our conversation, not least because my colleague is beginning to grow somewhat bored."
6eb3b174-9ff2-4583-9fe1-bd0eea57766c: "Can't we just blast the tomb from orbit?"
263a5db8-4ae8-4ec9-a290-d8fc67c7cdbb: "The ice was encroaching on the mausoleum, crushing its shell without relenting, and the reanimation protocols invoked me, awakening me. Nature and idea clashed, and nature prevailed, crushing both the mausoleum and the faithful one who had tried to tame it. Centuries have passed since then, and the plunderers found me drowned in their waters."
851fe035-8e60-4078-a7dc-c9d2b7833cc2: "That's what I'm saying. A rebellion endangers many, but the Trial Match is atonement for the one."
e5b0b0be-6656-43f4-a292-6a71ca93b3b2: "What was it you said about rebuilding trust? For some reason, I do not feel especially trusting at present."
74668594-0f17-4050-8300-ddc3e94991cf: "Vascular filters are failing... the penetrative capacity of the toxin is extreme..." {n}His bulky frame collapses. Blood streams from the Manipulus's nose and eyes, but his focus is pinned on his companion drifting listlessly at his side.{/n} "Nuncius, save yourself..."
bc202b4a-fdf9-4fb6-81b7-1bbf973b6e3f: {n}The Tech-Priest inclines his head in farewell, accompanying the gesture with a short binharic phrase.{/n}
f9bf8ddc-cd53-40c5-b593-e1d5355e3328: "Eogunn, can't you solve our little problem?"
7376a57f-50c6-4c01-bdb8-9b739ef6dbd6:
Whenever a shot {g|Encyclopedia:HitSequenceGlossary}hits{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:DetailsOfTheCoverGlossary}cover{/g} instead of the wearer, the attacker gains +3 stacks of {g|Encyclopedia:Exploit}exploit{/g}.
While not in cover, the wearer gains +{uip|Cover|3b270aa2bd61498baeb8f78789b30014}% cover efficiency (maximum 50%) as if they were in cover.
6a007761-b40d-435c-86e4-10918fb74a1c: "Excessive impulsiveness often leads to dire consequences. Alas, but you will have to drink from this bitter cup of experience more than once or twice, Sister Argenta."
a39bba3c-5973-41f2-9523-7d7d99431a19: "I just witnessed the execution of one von Valancius — do you think you can dictate terms to another?"
37d9f870-7c1d-4712-bc44-a6dcc5dcf53d: {n}The volume of data stored in the cogitator's bowels is enormous and its comprehensive analysis would take months of dedicated Logis and Historitor labour. However, the ancient device's machine spirit turns out to be unusually accommodating, and informs you that it will readily open any section of the archive if you present the correct identifier of what you are searching for.{/n}
d1b64c94-c7f4-422f-8f47-daa2fa15c30d: "What a woeful production. Do you think I can be fooled by this travesty of Dargonus?"
720360c2-07e0-4ce1-90cf-b820e90043fc: "Your commitment to perfecting your body is truly awe-inspiring! I'm sure you know what you're doing."
9bbb9f9f-e7f8-4bc1-b1af-013d0b6a99d0: You refused to allow the installation of a cerebral augmetic in a flagship soldier.
efb6d672-9573-4ec4-a53a-f54101e5767d: {n}The warrior's dark, almost black armour is covered in holy sigils and amulets. His right pauldron gleams a vivid crimson, marked with a winged black drop — the emblem of the {g|Encyclopedia:BloodAngelsGlossary}Blood Angels{/g}, one of the most illustrious Chapters of the Adeptus Astartes. Among the other heraldry, you notice a massive letter "I" decorated with a skull and crossbones.{/n}
81fbf77a-0456-4d87-abff-0cfdcfb8b2c8: "Juvenna von Valancius is relaying information about the Inquisition's command headquarters at this very moment."
2833832b-4ada-4f89-879b-14748b11c7ca: {n}The High Factotum bows, forcing his way into the conversation:{/n} "I am compelled to draw your attention to another problem. It is on a lesser scale than anything previously mentioned, but it is nonetheless significant. Our most ancient archeotech, the sacred implantation throne damaged during the riot, is at risk of destruction. I dare not take up any more of your valuable time, so please, if possible, attend the Sector Bionica, where the Drivesmaster will tell you of the tragedy."
36c1b3fa-5310-4ed8-80a6-b028b29f9480: {n}Angry veins swell on the {g|Encyclopedia:Manipulus}Manipulus{/g}'s forehead.{/n} "The only thing it will show is your unfitness for your post, Ferrenz. Your lies will not fool the {g|Encyclopedia:RogueTraderGlossary}Rogue Trader{/g}."
0cafdfe5-cc54-41e6-9e81-7ee3e1c8b0d5: {n}She falls down dead, her arms flung wide. She makes no sound, but the shot echoes all around the vicinity.{/n}
79a7267b-bf30-4e5a-bdee-f4e0d08a68c3: "The sacred symbol of which the {g|Encyclopedia:MonKeigh}mon-keigh{/g} are so proud is wrought with very primitive technology. It is as if the fragility and ephemerality of your species is reflected in your culture as well." {n}Yrliet takes a few feather-light steps, scarcely creasing the paper beneath her feet.{/n}
7ec6f22f-3bc6-4f90-abdb-025218bc2178: "Please. I don't want to... suffer. It's time for atonement... for the one."
7f1e91c2-ad3f-4907-a8b0-39c847151a7c: "Request granted. Initiating the voiding of the medical equipment in accordance with the Lord Captain's decision." {n} The system mounted above the operating area springs into action, and, muffled under the rumbling of its mechanisms, Genetor Ferrenz lets out a binharic trill of satisfaction.{/n}
e76ce4d6-e734-4029-bd90-013ad79a9c9a: {n}Cassia shakes her head in disapproval.{/n} "I was expecting a serious conversation, but you are laughing in my face? I do hope you will give a proper answer to my second question."
74179ed7-6866-401e-8b74-9eabb36889b9: {n}Without uttering a single word, Eogunn extends a mechadendrite, injector bared, toward the poor wretch, administering an unknown golden liquid into his neck. The Manipulus's face is set in a mask of deepest sorrow.{/n}
a99e90fb-d5fe-4e6b-850c-fef7cf2c38cc: {n}The apparition of the xenos dissipates, leaving empty air where the flickering image once stood, and with it the voice of Seberian, who has clearly been speaking for some time now, can be heard.{/n} "...it is time to set aside our quarrels and turn our united strength against a common enemy. The dynasty's Warrant must be restored to its place. Without it, the von Valancius will perish in the darkness. Do you agree to stand together against the enemies of Humanity and the dynasty?"
42c03cfe-4b63-4942-930c-c29989752339: {n}Seeming to appear out of thin air, people approach you with caution. Every one of them is scarred and wearing some kind a uniform and you notice that a few have crude prostheses made of bone and wood. Their eyes are fixed on you, noting your every move.{/n}
a69aa602-6273-4895-9180-c8ca66cd4a9f: "Let's not waste time on empty words. If what you said is true, then you must put a stop to this abomination as soon as possible."
9b6c3bb8-c00f-4c04-a36d-4e9cb95fa2eb: "He cannot hear you. But I appreciate that you still see him as a human being."
276f0f18-27f0-40dc-80a1-b968a728fe51:
For each mechadendrite Pasqal has, the attacker's {g|Encyclopedia:CritChancesAndCritDamageGlossary}critical hit chance{/g} for the attacks that deal {g|Encyclopedia:ShockDamage}shock damage{/g} to Pasqal is increased by +10%. There are the following mechadendrites:
-<indent=5%>{g|f:215371a91c7047abb0fc1acbbbbcf63c}Manipulator Mechadendrite{/g}</indent>
-<indent=5%>{g|f:bde399f936324b8e9fc7b950a0cdd2ff}Ballistic Mechadendrite{/g}</indent>
-<indent=5%>{g|f:de49d9600eeb4e9fb8a0adab3c46a260}Medicae Mechadendrite{/g}</indent>
-<indent=5%>{g|f:0e0d989a49294bafa1ffb4fd8b8a8bff}Scanner Mechadendrite{/g}</indent>
-<indent=5%>{g|f:e4c59223308e4235846c7f6688486437}Utility Mechadendrite{/g}</indent>
7397f119-67a5-4082-b583-d5fcbfae2b42: {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:CommBead}comm-bead{/g} in your ear comes to life with a crackle.{/n}
c0c9434e-1071-4190-8bec-6a43154018d6: "Who makes the final decisions during procedures?"
a6f31171-c32e-42a1-b929-27d273200dd8: "Your information is incorrect, Captain. The Gaprak family tradition is to serve in the ranks of the Imperial Navy."
ac7f09b6-44e7-43e9-aefa-95a05968c844: {n}The Triasomata looks more stable. A little more, and you will be able to calm him down.{/n}
7aedbf6e-4fbd-43f7-91ef-efcfb06a6014: "I acted discreetly to ensure that my colleague would not discover my requisitioning of the reagents. That is, until work stopped in one of the capellae biologis, where there was a regrettable incident involving a group of {g|Encyclopedia:Servitor}servitors{/g} I had been experimenting on. Such a fuss over just a few controlled mutations!"
9b6398f1-2a4c-4333-b63d-7306c92cbd49: {n}And then, for the first time in centuries, the deepest levels of Zarkov's root system were flooded with light. The explosion ripped apart the tomb from the inside, blasting many of the roots to pieces and setting still more ablaze.{/n}
391550df-729a-4326-b1b7-a92be9bb92bf: "Has such a mass occurrence of xenobeasts ever happened before?"
ec61ad30-d303-4700-ac79-a4d5d549bd32: "I do not wish to offend, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}! The Lord Captain would never miss an opportunity to enjoy a personal conversation with... a {mf|man|woman} of similar station." {n}The Vox Master loudly clears his throat, looking for the right words.{/n}
361da7b6-9064-448c-82ec-f516e1956eb4: "I register both ultra- and infrasonic frequencies. Theoretically, such emissions are capable of inducing deleterious effects upon organic brains."
f901edf6-26f6-4160-9b18-086b28389f52: {n}The Rogue Trader squares his shoulders and addresses the assembly:{/n} "The enemy has drawn close to the palace gates, and it is time to fight back."
7a9d8e78-4a6a-401e-a10c-51dd460926bb: "Your Lordship, your blade had fallen into the hands of a vile thief! The culprit has been executed. I accept my punishment for bearing these bad tidings."
584c1b25-a595-4207-96d8-c1ffc0a5a59f: {n}The captain speaks with fervour, passion, a breathless intensity. Your eye, however, is involuntarily drawn to her leather satchel, upon which a crude tattoo is visible.{/n} "The funereal detachments are true geniuses, Lord Captain. I come from one myself, though now I serve as quartermaster. We honour our dead by sacrificing their bodies to the rest of the regiment. Every man and woman is prepared to give not only their life but their flesh after death, so that others might endure."
928ed690-9cd0-46f4-8a87-25a922dde456: "I found this augment on a frontier world. Can you repair it?"
184d888a-4792-48aa-ba33-77fc108794cc: A ceremonial cloak in the colours of the von Valancius dynasty.
12552a04-82bc-45d5-874c-848413e38743: "Huh? I don't know those fancy words. But I know this: the best killer, as you put it, is only on top as long as the troops respect them. Either that, or as long as they're feared."
476c89c1-be47-47d4-b127-26e3f02a82a9: "I wish the commander hadn't stripped me of my rank for absenteeism... He broke my ribs for disrespect... Oof!"
97130f9f-2043-449f-bad1-5b86039548bd: "This is a rare case where poetic allegory most accurately encompasses the essence of the communicated thought."
3cd4808d-6dc3-4f93-ab27-8c8c0083c36d: Manipulus Eogunn is conducting strange experiments in the Sector Bionica, having occupied the implantation throne and disrupting the compartment's operations. The Rogue Trader's intervention is required.
8d9ae728-63ef-4e66-a5fb-f3cfe6bb5a32: "You do not know me, but I know you. We are trapped in a time loop, and I will prove it."
8511dfc8-2888-481f-a01f-3b337c74a8cb: "Such methods of minimising entropy are one of the few things that disturb the galvanic circuits in my brain. That you would sanction them, colleague, truly surprises me."
9de64c02-7319-47f9-a7ca-7d84603337e5: {n}The sergeant stands, swaying slightly with a deliberate air of nonchalance. Jarub casts a furtive look first one way, then the other, with the barest turn of his head.{/n} "I'm ready to move as soon as it happens, my {mf|lord|lady}. Any minute now."
8f3cb62d-a5d6-4de5-83c6-3dace3dfdaf0: {n}The answer to the enigma before your eyes {d|Encyclopedia:CharGen_MOT_Mind}comes to you immediately{/d}. The Warrant could not have vanished on its own. It could only have been stolen. By someone possessed of such staggering power that no amount of precautions could stop them. By someone who has already proven themselves a great thief, having stolen the Rogue Trader himself from the von Valancius dynasty. Trazyn the Infinite.{/n}
fc71f3e8-b95c-4f39-b8ce-d6e11f4704ec: "Set the Drovers on the xenos? I like that plan."
e0e0e2b4-837f-48c4-ae2f-f2cbbb41aee4: "I have come for your artefact, and I demand that you hand it over immediately."
d96df1a1-494a-432d-9d9b-ea899a2a581c: {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:TechPriest}Tech-Priest{/g}'s twisted, tarnished remains with splayed {g|Encyclopedia:Mechadendrite}mechadendrites{/g} make you think of a squashed metal spider.{/n}
8944543a-f0ee-4e9d-b68a-bf4a3dcfcf7a: {n}The old man frowns, but still bows respectfully to you. The young man behind him looks outraged and tries to step forward, but the older officer deftly seizes his son by the long cable embedded in his head. A stifled splutter erupts from the vox-repeater replacing the young man's mouth, but it is drowned out by the father's booming voice:{/n} "We will accept your decision whatever it may be, Lord Captain."
4ef3f82d-b795-4c19-826a-de20ab5916fb: "Count me out. Even the seats in this so-called vehicle look like they feed off the pain of the people who sit in them. I'd rather keep all my body parts intact."
2bba0007-8f6c-4765-aa14-527248a3ca16: "I thought a {d|Encyclopedia:CharGen_MOT_GreatDeed}hero of the Imperium{/d} would feed me some Commissar claptrap about the divine right of the nobles. Now I see why common folk call you a hero."
dbea8d70-e1c2-44b8-a97d-1c1edf93a0ce: Only the last part of the letter has survived: "...and so, Seberian, cast aside your doubts and act without hesitation. And, I beg you, forget that you are a Rogue Trader, if only for today. Exercise the caution of us mere mortals and destroy this letter IMMEDIATELY. Otherwise, by the Holy Throne, I will have to assign an acolyte to clean up your tracks. Amella."
17082025-0a38-4451-8ca8-4a8c8040b906: The sarcophagus on which Seberian's name is carved is empty. Inside, it looks completely untouched, as if von Valancius's body never entered the crypt.
0b37ece2-c575-484e-8de6-06aae28f5c4d: "I knew something wasn't right with that servo-skull!"
20a3059a-d8e6-4f6a-8128-5151bbb1f2bf: "I am going to the medicae deck. Notify the relevant parties."
e0da8ec7-d4fa-4644-8b0b-8dac10fc3f32: "You, malformed mon-keigh, surprise me more than all the others. I do not believe my eyes, but... Do you really not distinguish between your brothers?"
9af07551-9781-41d4-9b3a-84467e774cd7: {n}Idira's eyes dart around several times before she speaks:{/n} "A foul business, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, a risky one. They'll notice, they'll know... They will come." {n}A violent shudder runs through the psyker's body.{/n}
440cde2d-fb5f-4a3b-b705-6d12fcdf425b: "An expedition." {n}Batoshski grins crookedly as he says it.{/n} "The First Call-Up was requisitioned by Seberian von Valancius. I don't think they complained — better than serving as human shields for other regiments."
c072185a-fbcb-4df3-82da-f7e3a381f206: "Lord Captain, the esteemed Magos Haneumann is requesting your attention on a private vox-channel. Establishing connection."
5b7d12e8-77d0-45e9-800a-04d79e458256: {n}Seberian's shoulders heave at the sight of his hated enemy, as if the Rogue Trader is filling his cybernetic lungs in anticipation of a decisive battle.{/n} "You wanted me back, xenos? Here I am. I will destroy both you and these unholy halls. The ruins of your museum, so dear to your cursed soul — if you have one — shall serve as a funerary monument to Laria Voigtvir..."
50c99f71-e210-404b-b8d1-5f3df0fc00ba: Tech-Priests who succumb to the temptation of heresy should face public execution.
cf506daa-53d4-46ff-9197-d72f6da8c5ac: "Undoubtedly! The restoration of reliquary archeotech is a sacred and inviolable duty of any servant of the Machine God. But before this action, I initiate a new iteration of an appeal to the Rogue Trader: what is the fate of the Miraculous Fusion Reactor?" {n}The Logis repeats the words with the same tone of exaltation as before.{/n}
ca433dd9-1f22-4c84-9691-b9ee196d6c97: "The mere act of uttering such words will stain my soul with filth, whether I believe them or not. It does not matter what the xenos thinks. What matters is that I would have spoken heresy."
fb57c919-6208-46fd-9891-24891b1666e3: "You owe me three casings. The Oath happened during our Call-Up."
6e659736-29da-4f69-9708-c610e5f1f613: "I must observe that our presence continues to go unregistered by these laypersons."
e3555d67-346c-4825-9002-d78af2bed0a1: "Because we must always fight, against all the odds. I know it won't change a thing, but is that any reason to stop fighting against the inevitable?"
ccbcfbdf-4179-4488-88e5-93f7524fde69: {n}As he finishes studying the cogitator and its faintly glinting, dusty vid-screens, Eogunn exhales a deep rumble of a sigh.{/n} "The vital functions of this blessed machine are on the verge of dying. I will do for it what lies within my power, yet this cogitator is a tragic testament to the fact that even the works of the Omnissiah are mortal."
498b08f3-f316-4654-ab5a-5aae128793b3: "And what of you, you impudent {mf|whelp|chit}? Wearing the name of a dead man, accusing an honourable duchess of a crime. I still do not understand which side you are playing on."
12d5a575-0798-4a53-b2e3-0a67cd025c5f: "...curious that this specimen was deemed precious enough to be encased in such a construct. {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptaSororitasGlossary}Sister Messima{/g}, as she was called, was completing some march of penance in the region, which ended with her entering my collection..."
9d15f30b-c314-4cb4-8f95-de1028b7c306: The Rogue Trader has forged an alliance with the Inquisitor. The next step is to find the liaison with the call sign "Elana."
4e076f9b-6ffe-4a93-8f53-7360b8876e5e: "It's not working, Sergeant! The cursed beasts have damaged the batteries. We're low on power!"
e2221257-c589-429b-b793-9029ed0d97f5: "I wouldn't know, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, and why should I? The Handbook dictates that we 'put our complete trust and deepest reverence in our gear and armaments.' These ear mufflers are from the times of the First Call-Up. We sheathed them with fabric and covered them with a protective sleeve to preserve them better."
e64919af-282a-4b00-a622-1948b562a482: {n}Batoshski watches you like a predator on the hunt. With a heavy sigh, he pulls himself together and crosses his powerful arms across his chest.{/n}
21d98a10-37a6-4e28-abb0-8da51f1539f7: "You really are Seberian — and you're alive!"
8769cc48-c4d7-45d1-afb1-d0a21b20448d: {n}After a few moments, the Tech-Priest nods at the empty air.{/n} "Quite right, Nuncius, I should not have entrusted just anyone with such a delicate matter. Yes, I should have immediately sought out your expert opinion — we should stop this farce and repair you ourselves."
e48a5d64-268c-4d02-bdb2-b6521879655b: Juvenna proposes seeking an alliance with the Inquisition through negotiation, and she requires the Rogue Trader's aid to convince Seberian of this course.
caa1e501-7c0f-43d5-aabc-e1998c74c56b: Based the Triasomata's righteousness on self-sacrifice.
f84e66d4-e3d3-4b15-a6ea-6d11770121d9: "What are you going to do now? I have no more secrets from you."
6ea8ab7a-7428-4ca1-9f87-212678bf12d6: "The central generator once blessed the research camp with power. After so many years of solitude, its machine spirit has likely forgotten the words of the Galvanic Hymns and fallen dormant. I can remind it of its duty and share the Motive Force from my own flesh. But, to do that, we must reach it first."
ec6ef79b-5cc2-4efc-bfe3-9ff0a04d6f1a: {n}A highly unusual goal for a Tech-Priest — in the eyes of the servants of the Omnissiah, human life rarely outweighs the value of augments. Something must have propelled him down this path in the past.{/n}
dcd4c4c9-d04b-4cfc-99c3-ffd7292f56de: "My dearest colleague asks me to convey that he does not share my strong emotions and humbly apologises for this outburst. He also swears before the Machine God that he was created according to all the canons of sacred engineering and that he is not a sacrilegious perversion of His design."
1670035d-85c4-4cf4-a64d-1768d7bc1bd8: "Servitorise him and send him back to the lower decks. He may still serve the dynasty."
c4dc5f5d-890e-40fa-88d8-b458b420c732: "{g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}God-Emperor{/g} protect you! I'll wait here and make sure the fire doesn't get too close to the shuttle."
183a847a-e233-4a76-94e1-5267a14168c1: "Your deeds have brought me to the doors of this palace, Seberian. I have been following your trail for some time."
54a13186-a08d-4eaa-85ad-77b5e3668756: "I did not see Dunawski during the engagement."
be7b7520-05b6-4169-bfa9-0d2a3bd9f8a6: "That world was populated by the most feral of the Aeldari subspecies. They called themselves the Exodites, and they had the audacity to attack me the very moment I set foot upon the planet's surface and proclaimed it the property of Humanity. Decades were spent exterminating them to the last, purging the new holdings of all xenos filth. Thus, I earned the title Conqueror of Janus."
c077ea60-ea47-43eb-82fc-9e6a1401b496: "What was your life like before you assumed leadership of the dynasty?"
b9394c23-42c5-4bd5-b57f-de05b61a2078: "I doubt the Merovians attach any sacred meaning to the extermination of local fauna. From what I have been able to observe, death is a daily occurrence for them."
31924690-79a7-40b0-b6c7-702f72d39668: Despite its grim fame, the regiment itself did not last long: the bloodthirsty madness that had captured the souls and minds of the Merovians led to a breakdown of discipline and internecine warfare among the once loyal Guardsmen.
719b83f9-e996-460d-b8db-fdef8122115e: "Is that so?" {n}Laria's gaze burns as hot as any melta charge.{/n} "What a splendid idea. Let's start right now."
c1ef3189-9c97-4db3-a279-5082162ae958: "First, the reinforcements... and now this."{n}Furrowing her brow, Laria leans toward you and lowers her voice.{/n} "I suspect there is a conspiracy afoot in the palace. I need someone to be my ears and eyes — I cannot step away from my post. Observe, listen, report. In the name of the Lord Captain, we need to find the traitor."
4a00d615-c7c7-4b73-a64e-cdc1ab1bf5cd: "I have come to a decision. I will allow augmentation."
034ed526-15ac-46d9-bf2e-5ccff5c8aa00: {n}A group of aristocrats stands apart from the rest of the nobles. Their whispering carries a tinge of displeasure.{/n}
072934b1-6346-49f7-b8ea-310c6c0f9f99: "I don't know how you know this, my {mf|lord|lady}, but I can see that you know it for a fact." {n}The sergeant nods gravely. {/n} "I'll try to keep out of his way from now on."
594f6993-5ba4-4d6b-ad6a-f8ef6a56b83d: "Absurd! Unacceptable!" {n}Eogunn scans the Triasomata, furiously pounding the keys of the micro-cogitator mounted upon his torso.{/n} "You have succeeded in pacifying him, but... the Triasomata is rejecting the attempts of the implants' machine spirits to contain the cognitive entropy. Total personality degradation is inevitable. I... I must reassemble him!"
7530587e-73e0-4fb6-a754-f7d7f2767ddb: "To the best of my knowledge, the cloister Mistress Tallar is referring to is located in the Furibundus System. Its denizens are not well disposed to communicating with the laity, but I am willing to leverage every diplomatic connection I possess to gain an audience with the station's clergy."
ff78976c-ebf4-4f4e-87f0-97a8f9231227: "What now? He's harbouring xenotech reserves in his belly?"
9e2a706d-327c-4c7c-826c-10b437ddb0ba: "Officer, gather the troops. Today the Drivestems will go down in history as the only house to {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_DL_KamillaTraitor}come to the defence of Dargonus and the von Valancius{/d}."
8a047e6a-2c6a-4804-8de3-05812183c73c: "Blast it!" {n}The curse bursts from Laria's mouth before she can stifle it. The Seneschal immediately begins to probe you for minute details about the palace, and in the end she is forced to admit that not even a spy could be so knowledgeable.{/n}
3b5aac2d-c51d-44a6-a04d-c43d51eb28a0: "This world has tormented us for many Call-Ups, Lord Captain. We have finally been given the order to fight back. I wish I could be there."
412d4701-6366-4b69-bbe0-65897a6c96c0: "I am not Elana. But I am willing to give you the information you need in the name of the God-Emperor."
25a1028d-4a5a-4528-b1c0-ffde65f9d21b: [Examine the traitor Astartes in grey armour]
deacff04-e1ce-444d-86a0-860306492b65: "As soon as I said it, I realised how woefully trite it sounds and I lost all interest in this discussion."
d78d2838-6057-4c55-a66c-4af0815f662b: "A prudent decision." {n}Trazyn's voice drips with gloating triumph.{/n} "I will accede to your request."
a8b264e6-8967-4839-a0e5-a8de0106c85c: It is not possible to extend the bridge from this side — the control cogitator is on the other side.
b7177b7a-8fe3-4733-a117-2f47474048dc: {n}A sigh of relief escapes the Seneschal's lips.{/n}
7641f89d-b43d-4fae-a9c7-da772067fe0f: "Eogunn, make him tell the truth. I do not care about the methods."
42a35bab-ca4e-45e2-8c4a-b5cf059dcd43: "You call that pain? I remember the master gunner once gave me a punch in the face for insufficient zeal — nearly sent my soul to the God-Emperor. Here's not so bad."
17fbee15-30c2-4a9f-aed3-0e9732aa68ce: Bloody, bare footprints stretch further down the passageway. The air smells of machine unguents and medicae substances.
e7edfd1d-26c1-4e73-a025-98b4d889fe3b: {n}Whatever Elana's true identity, she is relaying sensitive information to the enemy side that is crucial to the defence of Dargonus.{/n}
2ab2e8a9-3b67-4366-8347-177980eca4c8: "My house couldn't possibly have built a spaceport on Vheabos VI, Captain. Not least because our ancestral holdings are on Janus."
7b932f3f-92f6-4ff2-89ac-6aacda3c03e0: "Lord Captain, our {g|Encyclopedia:Augur}augurs{/g} detected a faint emergency signal. The source was traced to a crashed Valkyrie. Its {g|Encyclopedia:Vox}vox{/g}-systems are still transmitting the message. Transferring the signal now."
22781d71-5212-4017-a35e-4d51b5257639: "Ridiculous! Ignorant fool! How dare you deliberately twist the truth—" {n}Eogunn stops talking abruptly, peering over his shoulder as if listening to someone — he is looking at the place where his dearest colleague usually hovers.{/n}
5fd042c3-1e11-4781-8f01-38570c69cb76: "A predatory tree? I have yet to slay such a monstrosity! We must find it and carry its durable timber back to Fenris. Let us carve it into the haft of a worthy weapon!"
36129758-70f2-40df-a38f-4cb7492e4416: "The settlement fortifications erected during the Drovers' frenzy have performed well again, Lord Captain, so the Merov Regiment has saved a significant number of lives and bought themselves time."
1e9ec59b-b6df-4ea1-bfcb-abd05603b296: "And why don't you drive the creature away from your settlement yourselves?"
1b94b333-f81f-46f9-adfd-8f22322938fc: {n}Panicking, Jarub snatches up Seberian's sword. Captain Sauerback fails to stop the strike, and the blade pierces the enforcer's armour. Without warning, the sword shatters as if it were made of glass, a hundred glittering shards flying in every direction. Jarub blinks in disbelief at the hilt still in his hand. A moment — and a blue flash lances through the hapless Merovian, who drops dead to the ground with a piteous whimper.{/n}
024c9844-f23e-4e6e-bb38-60f281a4e39d: With shouted prayers, the Zarkovians rushed into battle. They held the unholy machines at bay, fighting until the last drops of blood left their veins. My scion, {mf|his|her} retinue, and myself were able to climb a platform that took us up to the Museion, but all the brave soldiers who had not hesitated to follow their commander had fallen in the unequal fight.
df2464c8-85fd-49b5-8d74-c3c832a58db2: "You should know that the Gaprak scion, who led the army of reinforcements, was killed at the outpost."
caf41b2c-e96d-4967-aafc-075591956442: The Rogue Trader's subjects request permission to hold a gratitude procession upon the bridge. To accept their thanks, you must speak with Vigdis.
7f0f520f-448e-4e46-b2bd-01a3ce9c54e9: {n}Seberian's hand darts toward his weapon, the augmented fingers locking around the grip with a hiss.{/n} "How do you know that name? Who else have you told? Wait a moment. You can't be..." {n}Coming to some conclusion known only to him, the Rogue Trader relaxes.{/n} "I will keep your identity a secret. Amella is my ally and protector, and I had faith that she would not leave me to fend off Diodas alone."
b16c448d-5720-4264-aae8-78d766362567: The power of the von Valancius protectorate grew and grew, bringing new territories, people, and resources under its control. The dynasty's possessions were full of strange machines that worked night and day, leaving some priests of the Omnissiah in awe and amazement and others fiercely angry. The machines, given to the dynasty by its mysterious patron in exchange for the great betrayal, were devoid of souls, and the Rogue Trader became equally soulless in the end. Fear of {mf|him|her} kept the protectorate in a state of unquestioning submission.
751cf003-a846-42d6-9a04-5298980b0df9: {n}Attached to the entry are astrographic coordinates. With a dutiful trill, the machine spirit transfers them to your {g|Encyclopedia:Dataslate}data-slate{/g} with the utmost diligence.{/n}
a8ce742e-3368-4065-ad04-5e50a8fbb369: "Lord Captain! Zarkov is reporting a serious situation on the planet. The flora and fauna of the world have gone mad. All because of a mass invasion of creatures that the transmission calls... Drovers? Isn't that the xenobeast you encountered?"
d6271cb0-e996-4b9a-9a48-e80194d2d019: "You belong to the Toliman dynasty? The same as my Vox Master, Vigdis?"
950a0cac-55f0-47a5-8a3e-e0b6b2bfb736: [Take a closer look at Sauerback's injury]
a0cd496c-8c8d-4b24-8e78-71af1b020d37: "What about your soldiers? They were criminals once, and now they will turn against the God-Emperor? The Inquisition does not grant second chances."
2893faac-308f-4745-97bc-db9792f3858f: {n}You are too exhausted to make speeches, nor worry about anything at all. The Warrant has been recovered, and that is what matters. You feel hollow. You simply. Need. To rest.{/n}
e4597a1e-c06c-40bd-b410-41cccae2c383: "Very well. I await your next report."
0f89af43-b117-4f49-99d9-a6f14352392a: {n}Your efforts to inflict as little damage as possible on the Imperial relic are paying off — not that you have left no marks at all, but, at the very least, you do not seem to have damaged the paper itself.{/n}
57462da6-750e-4468-8d34-6ce7c1d88b8c: {n}Batoshski smirks, baring a shin hideously scarred by an acid burn.{/n} "It spits globs of caustic sap. Anything they hit gets sizzled. It also sprays pollen for some reason. It seems harmless though. But if you see a flower dissolve into a puddle of acid, don't think it's safe for a second, it's just another trick. Wait a little while and that flower will be back up again."
30560f47-0c7b-4f36-b4d6-567ff206fe81: "I understand, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. So, we'll need to issue high-speed shuttles, cartographers... Forgive me, I will draw up the list of equipment needed and organise its delivery later."
b804136e-47b4-42c6-bf80-98205e02c0ba: "I like your idea, Marazhai. See to it that we exploit our enemies' conflict to the fullest."
8560488a-dbf5-428f-96d8-2011797197e6: Someone has managed to appease the machine spirits, although the air defence battery is malfunctioning. This requires high-level access codes.
c169d5bf-16af-443c-a0e5-191401793172: Juvenna proposes that Seberian negotiate with the Inquisition. If the council can be persuaded to support her, this outcome might break the temporal loop.
9b5757ce-9683-452c-be13-e1c11bb82592: "It is unique. One of a kind." {n}The Tech-Priest's optical augments flash, and surprising fervour creeps into her voice.{/n} "In the Logis Annals of the Adeptus Mechanicus, this throne is identified as a shrine capable of autonomous augmentation rituals. Its loss is unacceptable, and any condoning of it is sacrilege."
067f3afa-3b68-49f7-a69d-fbede0c4a43a: "Do not dare blame the Lord Captain! Blame the hangers-on who buzz around him like malfunctioning servo-skulls. We need Father to hear only those loyal to us and those whom you manage to bring to our side."
c6dfb6a1-0ec6-4502-8230-e59828aa356b: {n}Finally, one of the creature's limbs extends in a lofty gesture, after which perfectly intelligible words in Low Gothic pour forth from the narrow slit that crosses its face where its mouth is:{/n} "I offer my greetings to you in the galleries of the Great Museion, visitor." {n}The xenos's pronunciation and word stress are flawless, but the tone is devoid of humanity.{/n}
c7fe4a86-9c7b-42af-a668-76019cafbd7a: "Those bearing the rank of Genetor bestow blessings on the installation, consecration, and maintenance of the augments, thus drawing the feeble bodies of Humanity closer to maximum efficiency pleasing to the Omnissiah."
21dd89de-4bac-4278-aed9-b76d157c03fe: The few scouts who survived the descent to the roots' bases report stumbling upon ancient, powerful augmetics from the time of the First Call-Up. The governor requests the Rogue Trader's permission to launch a Crusade for the Lost Gifts: thousands of soldiers shall plunge into the depths to recover the sacred iron. And though nearly all the volunteers shall perish in the ravenous darkness at the bottom of Zarkov, the glory of the 32nd Regiment's deeds and sacrifices shall echo throughout the entire Expanse.
71623577-cb72-4983-bbce-6def6082863f: "I grieve with you, Magos Haneumann, and offer prayers to the Motive Force that it forever flows in abundance through the plasteel veins of Eufrates II. But despite the losses, the world endured, as Stygies VIII once endured. In time, it was able to recover, and so too will Eufrates II."
326420cf-05e9-4118-824a-03bddb818667: [Nod to Exalt-117] "The time has come to serve the Omnissiah... and Lady Juvenna."
9196863c-2c1b-459f-a907-d09a8a5404f9: "Lord Captain, I'm doing my best! I am... But how much longer can I go on?"{n} The man's lips twist into a pained grimace, and he closes his eyes for a moment. A fresh glob of pink mucus spurts out from the inflamed seam between his skull and the implant.{/n}
28a1584d-1521-4023-bb39-de87a8eec5b6: Eogunn grants {g|f:4644d27aca624059883392696c49456d}electrified strikes{/g} to himself or an ally within {uip|AbilityRange|bbd46bb4c64e4d6aa6783fd1fb6bc819} cells for 1 round, allowing the target to deal additional shock damage. To use this ability, Eogunn must have at least 2 stacks of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g}, which are removed afterwards.
2298cd1f-f5fc-45e3-a8e2-45d97c22cd01: The predatory silhouette of this blade reveals its creators to be connoisseurs of first-class weapons and the exquisite suffering they can inflict.
43612955-1412-4e79-ba5c-a72eb768d222: "We have preserved the memory of you for centuries. We sang songs of the regiment's deeds under your command."
512eebf4-746b-4550-9843-cc6241490e05: The user pulls an ally within {uip|Range|ea9d6dd442d34403a3e4228ba6178a5d} cells to themselves. If that ally is larger than the user, the user is pulled to that ally instead.
63a59d63-2a72-4138-85f1-56b39d73d657: "It would be unfair if I allowed augmetics for one and denied the rest. From this day on, subjects from the lower decks will be granted access to augmentation!"
606408d6-4d68-41a8-b2f0-22bf0192b26b: The entire surface of the planet is lifeless grey rock, but the atmosphere is breathable without a voidsuit. In some deep tunnels, strangely shaped chalky sediments have been found.
894597fe-70f2-4254-ac7b-b1c6ac45a885: {n}The obsequious reply comes almost instantly:{/n} "I will arrange everything at once, Lord Captain!"
bcbdf4c9-688d-4102-8708-bb5a763ddf96: "He suffered the same fate I did. He was connected to an experimental specimen to force him to speak. He endured this torment stoically, without uttering a word." {n}The Manipulus closes his eyes with parchment-thin lids.{/n} "Tervantias made me watch... the entire process."
7ef46371-4e4f-41fd-b8d7-f006c0f1efb2: "I must admit, I was expecting rather more... sizeable reinforcements." {n}Despite the fatigue that bleeds through even the creaking distortions of his vox-apparatus, the man smooths the non-existent creases in his uniform and introduces himself with dignity:{/n} "My name is Seberian von Valancius, but you already knew that. But this is the first I'm seeing of you."
e406d47f-2ece-480c-bf41-d06fc5af2693: "But, before we proceed, would you indulge a few questions, wretched heretic? Foremost, I must know the techniques by which you..." {n}Eogunn pauses, tilting his head as if listening, then exhales a grating sigh.{/n} "You are right, Nuncius. This is neither the time nor the place for such discourse."
5a65e67a-e09d-41c4-8e93-e004e6831a82: {n}The small plants clinging to the sarcophagus snap with soft, wet squelches as you move a surprisingly light lid. Inside, you see nothing but a gaping hollow. Neither your ancestor's body nor his legendary sword lie in the sarcophagus.{/n}
4ba215d9-59e7-4d8e-962a-41b4b5ad17e0: "Affirmative, Sergeant. Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, please wait but a moment."
e16679a3-c8e7-4b4a-b4ae-7f05a8ec97f1: "Ice... was encroaching on the mausoleum... nature... won... out... centuries... passed... plunderers... found... me." {n}The Elucidator servitor's translation is punctuated by abrupt pauses. The machine spirit seems to be interpreting the clanking and howling sounds that serve as the xenos's speech with extreme difficulty.{/n}
1b65f0b2-a0f0-4f03-bd73-76c80187add5: "The sigil indicates that this acolyte served in the Ordo Hereticus. What were they doing here?"
075ffe06-2fd6-430c-a5fd-60d87a910ef5: "The air defence turret system has been damaged by the enemy."
2c4b5686-1aa0-426c-aa3c-9cb0017bc853: Instantly destroys one shard copy or deals <u>(50% of the orb's {g|Encyclopedia:MaxWounds}maximum wounds{/g})</u> damage to the energy orb.
300edf31-a8a6-4299-8d54-a2506ec02ace: {n}You hear nothing from the vox but white noise.{/n}
62f3a249-a344-4e24-8bf7-8ba8256b4cf8: {n}Juvenna raises one perfectly defined eyebrow.{/n} "I was prepared for anything, but—" {n}cutting herself off, she crosses her arms over her chest.{/n} "No matter. Name the direction, and I'll start the transmission."
54761e64-2677-4401-b4a0-8667018ce2fa: [Make the sign of the Aquila] "Of course, Nuncius. A true believer only has room in their heart for the God-Emperor."
582552d1-98f4-4686-b382-e9d6a08c75c2: "I would rather attend to my calculations."
6fb2a3ee-a70c-44d9-9b6c-365d639e5a35: The creature suffers −10% of its maximum wounds at the start of its turn.
0c13a639-0bc4-4fec-890d-c0a7deca2cd0: "Elana is my call sign for Inquisitor af Irramon's troops. We have quite the long-standing arrangement. My father is a heretic, and that is a fact. If he is not stopped, our entire bloodline will be destroyed." {n}A spark of stubbornness flares in Juvenna's sapphire irises.{/n} "I have done everything I can to prevent that from happening, and God-Emperor as my witness, I will do just as much again."
1f321259-54fc-46f1-867b-3e464f0f8330: "Anyone who witnesses the consequences of thoughtless flirtation with the energies of the Immaterium will think twice before setting foot on this crooked path. I find the sight not merely sad, but also highly instructive."
44958f49-e376-4f08-8580-1292f63836e2: {n}Jarub lowers his voice to a whisper and looks around uneasily.{/n} "You haven't changed your mind, have you?"
