ca4c1d77-083b-4e29-b9c1-b8e6a8ff0db5: "Well, this is rather embarrassing... since the Kalivins are a minor offshoot of House Drivestem. A dozen souls, all told, and I know each and every one of them."
ece6f011-a3f3-4006-bb55-a617bb61aa82: "Those among the {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptusMechanicusGlossary}Adeptus Mechanicus{/g} who are supremely competent in the sacred skill of construction are elevated to the rank of Artisan — maker of machines. Artisans are proficient enough to also be called 'the hand of the will of the Omnissiah.'" {n}The Tech-Priest's bent back straightens at these words, and he seems to occupy more space, swelling with importance.{/n}
57a3cf80-04f8-4d29-8d51-5d2d091ee3c6:
As the game's story unfolds, you will see notifications at the top of the screen indicating that the status of your current objective has been updated and that new information has been added to your journal. You can open the journal by pressing [{bind|OpenJournal}] or clicking {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on the notification at the top of the screen.
Journal entries help you keep track of current events and guide your character on their journey. The quests tab tracks the progress of the main story — here you can find information about any quests you have accepted and their current status. Use the journal to help yourself understand where to go on the next steps of your adventure.
The rumours and contracts sections also contain important information — you will learn more about them later.
956f56ce-ea80-473f-bd15-417cd0b249b9: "So must I." {n}Zorael makes the sign of the Aquila.{/n} "Fight with valour, human."
a2a5fe52-9d05-4318-ad4f-9f2c4ec92b7c: Deals <u>(from 18 to 40)</u> shock damage with 25% {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g}.
01f5152f-667c-44b4-b9c2-5d8fbf2b555d: The Tech-Priest is deep in a recuperative trance.
4b1b5b15-8063-4b3d-881e-57304921c2f2: "The Undying One will give us a sign when the 32nd Regiment must strike, Domin. I will be the herald of retribution."
78050cd1-f688-45fe-8254-8383ab57d1c9: {n}The Lexmechanic utters these final words with distinct envy, keeping his eyes locked on Genetor Februs.{/n}
1da60743-4d5e-4859-95e5-7a3a023e61af: {n}When you hand Seberian over to the {g|Encyclopedia:InquisitionGlossary}Inquisition{/g}, you notice that the Lord Captain never once lost his signature poise, even in the face of death. Thrusting out his chest emblazoned with the von Valancius coat of arms and with his metal chin proudly raised, the Rogue Trader marches majestically to his execution.{/n}
2d223a2c-d01b-4be7-92fb-4e533f38b6d9: A restored bionic arm of superior quality, emblazoned with the von Valancius crest. Made from a blend of premium materials both human and xenos in origin. Its very existence is flagrant tech-heresy in the face of the Adeptus Mechanicus.
e0c80e06-cfdd-45be-9323-e5a16a6e9368: When activated, weapon attacks with {g|Encyclopedia:OverpenetrationFullGlossary}overpenetration{/g} instead have the same chance to ricochet. On a ricochet, damage is dealt to a random creature or object within a small radius around the initial target. This damage is often reduced.
2572ceed-2791-4fd9-b2ef-3ec927f12d10: "Serenada Schwartz? Well... After her pardon, she herself petitioned to be servitorised and sent to the Sector Bionica, so that she might atone for her crime through service." {n}The Vivisector presses his lips together, his eyes hardening for a moment.{/n} "I thought it only right that the resulting medicae servitor be assigned to Ferrenz's desk."
dbab6399-6bc6-4743-a932-599085881cf4: {n}There can be no doubt about the weapon's origins: the sword depicted by this unknown artist's brush was not forged by human hands. The fluid curves, the exceptional lustre of the blade, and the gem at the hilt show the weapon to be the work of an Aeldari artisan. The blade is also unusual for the runes engraved along its length — they are most ancient indeed. The inscription is in the Aeldari language, recounting the legend of an unholy god of war revered by the xenos.{/n}
fdab3279-d54f-4700-a321-6e92ad49e864: "Nothing out of the ordinary, Lord Captain. Just a celebratory procession in recognition of your deeds. The flagship's preachers and officers occasionally hold these ceremonies to foster gratitude within the crew toward the hand that feeds them. Remarkably, the procession was not organised by the officers this time."
5e4523f1-72ec-485c-8890-59c4bab99a04: "Any idea what exactly he is doing there?"
52ffa184-34dd-4b3b-9c1b-4b8050149917: "This secret chamber was his sanctuary and laboratory. I believe that Seberian was overly interested in... radical ideas."
6aed1373-6552-4aed-843c-4c137ea75b06: "I'll see to the defences, appoint new sergeants, and then follow your trail. I'll catch up with you at the ruins."
743a763c-4cc8-40d4-b125-272b7dbfe057: "There is a whole list of names within it."
bb512f06-5db8-4102-b253-b4f889169d4f: Within the fleshy walls of a plant, you can see the outline of a human body floating in some kind of fluid.
1892a3f8-0840-48bc-9c61-ca588e39b164: "We'll go ahead to report your arrival. Just follow the trail, you can't miss it. You need to meet with the colonel."
97b86177-1f7e-4f81-8237-91281cb9bf5c: "You're exhausting me. Sort this out yourselves and do not dare disturb me with such trifles again."
379216b3-32c9-429e-8342-8fa67feac86f: {n}The Drivesmaster's vox-grille emits a binharic aria of thanks.{/n}
cd88879b-13af-4ea3-acd7-ca63c02fb6a9: {n}Eogunn's auspex briskly scans you.{/n} "The Rogue Trader is not compromised. Brain cell damage is within the acceptable range." {n}With a greedy look at the slab, the Manipulus adds,{/n} "Repeated interaction is permissible."
2c3673bb-1729-43b5-9468-084d401ec32b: "First, I wish to know if you can restore the implantation throne."
a14b6ecb-0ccb-4af9-b6bd-60d91eea50a6: {n}The Rogue Trader's decision is not to everyone's liking — you see wry smiles and tense looks. But no one dares to argue.{/n}
c2359098-3ea3-4439-bc40-c25f7e36f0a7: "Lady Navigator, what does your special sight reveal?"
cf1651bd-1991-433f-9138-be15b2a79bef: Become an acolyte of the Inquisition in this grim, dark, party-based, story-driven cRPG.
fa594116-a3ca-43c6-b359-0840b226e044: {n}Izdrig scratches his beard as he looks around the outpost.{/n} "Fresh supplies are helpful. New people coming to Zarkov not so much. I'll see plenty more danger in my lifetime, but our children will have it a lot easier."
18ed1f2a-0642-45ad-ad56-970696015d20: "No." {n}The single word uttered by Eogunn rings with a metallic clang entirely uncharacteristic of his voice.{/n} "Under controlled laboratory conditions, I would venture into such communication, but presently Nuncius strongly advises against assuming the risk of data contamination. I align with his judgement and propose that everyone do the same."
f5806094-6a98-4a29-9551-56739a8af939: "You bestow too much importance on painted rags and pieces of metal. How can there be great meaning behind the urge to lay down your life for a trifle? Be glad that this platform is not arrayed with the bodies of those whose names you invoked."
7a525a64-a93d-41bf-9e62-785c09eff70a: "Unfortunately, I must decline — I have urgent matters to attend to."
8f46bb59-6bd8-4e1a-a249-20b071ddbe10: "Nuncius expresses his sorrow. I share his sentiments, but since the Omnissiah has determined this path for me, so be it."
1996f454-0412-460b-b31a-d4fc6c970072: "I have taken over management of the sector, but I endeavour to adhere to the wisdom bestowed upon us by the Adeptus Mechanicus. Sacred augmentations are being installed in strict compliance with patient status and efficiency." {n}Martinax shudders and adds quietly,{/n} "I rather sound like Genetor Ferrenz, don't I?"
97805c72-8c0a-4887-8b52-bd6f60d5bf27: "Yes, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. I carve things for myself and for the fellows, and sometimes I take commissions. Toys for volunteers, medals for awards, pieces to put on gun stocks. Sometimes I help with prosthetics. But I also do it for the pleasure of the thing."
12ad2732-3b2e-4a0c-84e9-91d9f5e5b46a: The legends of how {mf|Lord|Lady} von Valancius challenged an ancient, indescribable evil and vanquished it in battle spread throughout the Koronus Expanse long after, passing from mouth to mouth and gaining more incredible details with each telling. The legacy of House von Valancius was restored, and its power was strengthened a hundredfold. The truth, however, was that many years later, Trazyn the Infinite returned to the Expanse, this time with the sole purpose of claiming the legendary Rogue Trader as his complete and undisputed possession. The outcome of that confrontation is unknown, and the latest mentions of it have been lost to time's abyss.
9543fb6d-79c9-4135-a090-ba4820105fb6:
Until the end of the user's {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, each enemy the user moves through suffers double {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g} and is {g|Encyclopedia:PushGlossary}pushed{/g} 2 cells away.
At the end of their turn, the user becomes {g|Encyclopedia:SlowedEffect}slowed{/g} until the end of combat.
e33a89f8-0e25-4edb-9bb8-35e5b1265817: Pursuing his Quest for Knowledge, Genetor Februs is conducting a series of scientific investigations in the Sector Bionica. The Rogue Trader has received an offer to take part in the rite, serving as Eogunn's trusted assistant.
19f85691-eb1c-4332-9aeb-e07a1d3b39d2: "That will do. Let's talk about something else."
9c7e2a1d-56d2-4731-a197-7a3c8cb1779f: "I need to get my augments serviced. Can you do that?"
24a10e9d-02d9-46b3-b1ac-215380a8dbba: Enquire about augments in the Sector Bionica
ce776d72-fbb4-4f3e-8798-f6253bc3816d: "A very impressive collection of heraldic symbols and family artefacts of sacred dynasties. I wonder how long the relics have been... kept in this strange place."
9c3190b7-fd5c-40fd-a382-2a656cc10304: "Nonsense!" {n}Bereft of her usual prayer beads, the duchess digs her fingers into the edge of her ornately decorated belt.{/n} "All she told me was that Ovedius Gaprak would arrive unaccompanied. For years, my house has battled against the influence of those servitor-brained upstarts; it would have been foolish not to seize such a shining opportunity..." {n}The duchess falls silent upon hearing her own words. Her eyes briefly cloud over like cracked glass, and suddenly she looks like a tearful, hurt old woman. She takes a step forward, crushing a bead under her heel.{/n}
5a925ac0-fbda-44bb-ab33-3663a817de15: "He will not die." {n}Zorael pauses, allowing you to take in the meaning of what he is saying.{/n} "I have tried, human. I cleaved his iron head from his body myself. His mind immediately transfers to the body of another Necron."
651e7f67-1bd9-41d6-b21a-5b79964ce7d0: This creature will not leave the turret until it is destroyed.
4f2360f2-7d52-4398-9035-f932038a2af4: "Hereby do I bear witness, that the Rogue Trader is blessed by the Omnissiah, and the truths of the Machine God shine upon {mf|his|her} path." {n}Eogunn spreads his mechadendrites wide as a hissing spark of the Motive Force leaps between them. The gathering freezes in pious awe... then erupts into rapturous cheers.{/n}
f8c2bd44-0c0c-4a4a-8451-e501f47f8b47: "Your Warrant is safe within my Museion, for now. It holds little value to me in itself, like most of your so-called relics. However, as a means to settle a score with a dynasty of thieves who have made a habit of crossing my path..." {n}The Necron lets out a low laugh.{/n} "But, very well. I would be glad to be rid of the Warrant and return it to you, were it not for one problem: its place must be taken by another exhibit. The one that was stolen from me."
b613f1de-385a-4699-af23-f0656fa5cde7: "You pray for the people, Amic'Solomorne, even though you know the Undying One will come for them when needed. Such... sentimentality."
415b4bfa-b3b1-4dac-9bd4-3be17b418707: "Don't beat yourself up, old man. The Warrant was surrounded by thousands of servants and guards, and kept under the best locks — and no one heard a peep! Even my little voices were silent, the sneaks!"
5a6b1604-1a2c-4488-976e-65cc26dd075e: "A commendable attitude to restrictive dogma and a diligent approach to datahealing, colleague. Alas, the current disposition of circumstances is such that your patient will have to be disturbed. But with the greatest of care."
64ff21a0-1163-4c47-a63d-afcb5a125657: {n}When you touch the endless bloody wound that the entirety of Laria's body below the neck has become, she makes a muffled groan through her teeth, and for a few seconds her breathing stops. It seems the pain from the torture alone is so intense that any examination could bring about instant death from shock. Yet life still flickers within her, which means a chance to save her remains, however faint.{/n}
c4152315-60aa-455f-a10b-b9021e0b3662: {n}Beneath the ruptured slab, something ancient, immaculate, and alien continues to work.{/n}
d5fcb402-65c6-438d-8d2a-391a22193c04: "Scanning successful, request approved. The {g|Encyclopedia:OmnissiahGlossary}Omnissiah{/g} will bless the removal of organic frailties."
eeeceae6-9d23-4a56-a56e-e8238572e55b: {n}The sergeant drops into a low bow in farewell.{/n}
b148c526-014d-4187-aedf-8ca866858c60: "Nuncius, what is your opinion on the hardships of the common folk in the Imperium?"
d0c60d4b-25b3-4086-9055-6b3a189fbf81: The Angel of the Emperor's gaze is full of silent condemnation, judging you for your failure to save him from a disgraceful end.
b7887728-0e88-4747-ac0b-a199ca237439: "The standard punishment for brawling is no less than twenty lashes for each participant." {n}The Seneschal wipes away the blood trickling from his nose and continues, more quietly.{/n} "But under the present circumstances, I believe some leniency may be shown."
8d90990a-7674-4a1f-9b6e-8c296aad9e36: "You have endured a great deal. There will be no more suffering. I promise to spare you — just let the hostage go."
700b50d2-cda2-4bce-a8b4-b64bb4f273eb: "It is an honour for me to fight alongside an Angel of the God-Emperor!" {n}Argenta's voice trembles with awe.{/n}
bf7984bf-dbc0-4752-a1fb-aecb6ee7d7a8: The Expanse has yet to see warriors as skilled and resilient as the sons and daughters of Zarkov. The Fellowship of the Void promises to pay handsomely for the chance to recruit Zarkovians to execute their dark designs. Who else but the God-Emperor's anointed has the power to override the Imperium's laws for the welfare of the protectorate?
df80eda4-5fe9-4ebc-8efd-2b75a835a220: "Temper yourself, colleague. You are making a category error, thinking that the indigenous population is capable of observing the rituals of service to the fullest extent. The best that an ignorant layperson can do for a working mechanism is to leave it well alone. The burning of sacred unguents, however, is... quite unnecessary."
3f68fc50-e755-4cd7-b752-5b42fff1d0fa: {n}Elana the Unshaken is a Calixian saint who died many hundreds of cycles ago. This call sign cannot have been chosen by chance.{/n}
f198f0a2-4bb2-4a51-b607-2a0d2287977d: "I know you believe in me, captain, but who will aid your regiment if I perish here? Who besides you can grant them hope for atonement?"
ba28dc0b-78b8-4b3f-be29-c9f7a1e832ac: {n}Zorael nods to you in greeting. The Astartes's armour is chipped and his cloak is fraying, but his unblinking, intent gaze is clear and focused.{/n}
984639d1-27dd-4875-a6a4-d06b3dfe87f4: Combat begins with the tactical phase, during which characters cannot attack or take other actions but may try to take up advantageous positions. Select a character by clicking {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on their portrait at the top of the screen, then click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on the cell you want the character to move to. Red cells are areas inaccessible to the character. During the tactical phase, each character in the party may move up to their maximum {g|Encyclopedia:MovementPoints}movement points{/g} but cannot position themselves adjacent to an enemy. Press [{bind|Pause}] to start the battle.
8c04349c-dcbc-4c30-abad-997b31b41f00: "The concentration of pathogens in this place exceeds every conceivable and inconceivable limit. Touch nothing! Nuncius, ready the vials — I require samples."
8fb23800-5e5e-43d4-8b94-d2f2125d402d: "Eogunn, you are going to repair Nuncius... with Nuncius?"
9f94b90f-5a20-4e02-88b9-e26b997e175d: "What are you doing on my ship?"
a67d6f7f-b1c0-4028-867c-adc99c2564c2: "Don't worry, Jae. The little voices have been telling me lately that if we don't destroy this scum, we're going to be stuck in this hole for a long time. And we're not exactly spoiled for entertainment here, as you can see..."
43949474-fe8a-4773-aee9-7c4bdc44eea1: "As you wish, Lord Captain. It will be a dangerous venture — we all know how unstable unsanctioned psykers are. However, I'm sure the Drovers will not be able to resist such a feast."
b455bf02-218d-4156-8d8b-1f1bd594d61c: As your eyes meet, he confirms that this was the ship he used to flee the secret Amsanct facility. The Chartist Captain was merely a cover for Inquisition operations plying these secret routes. The demise of the ship could not have been a mere coincidence.
ef894731-5214-4f1f-86ec-4654e0673542: "The unnamed subject of the portrait somehow obtained an Aeldari ritual sword decorated with their religious writings. It would be a pleasure to possess such a thing."
0f709d7d-a036-49d5-8692-98ac70605a66: "Time holds value only for short-lived creatures such as you. Mark this well until the end of your transient existence: everything is going according to plan! Everything always goes according to my plan..."
93417322-44b0-45fb-8ffc-b2fb30e91162: "By my determination, the local recreational customs are primitive, but intriguing. However, ingesting such toxins is not the most effective approach to calming the mind, based on my statistical findings. No, Nuncius, those were indeed experiments, not tastings."
949bbee0-b322-4674-8d28-f0a767f73fd8: "And Drill Sergeant Jovakha is leading a squad defending the path we've established to the xenoruins north-west of the landing pad. That's where the first wounded came in, and they were being chased by those cursed creatures."
94c16f64-7ba8-4589-95d9-c626d644212d: {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:Manipulus}Manipulus{/g} looks blankly at you, then tilts his bulk forward, reducing his hovering height so that his feet almost brush the deck.{/n} "I apologise, Rogue Trader. I was delighted to find such keen interest in my nature and... forgot myself."
be573f39-67a7-40d4-8cdc-e1093d316b6d: When the user is {g|Encyclopedia:HitSequenceGlossary}hit{/g}, they gain +{uip|THP|fdbac6922400427e92a08d56391ce24c} {g|Encyclopedia:TemporaryWounds}temporary wounds{/g} for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}. This effect triggers even if the attack was {g|Encyclopedia:BlockChance}blocked{/g} or {g|Encyclopedia:Parry}parried{/g}. Does not stack.
594d0979-5c58-468c-aa66-7a646e5c39f8: "Are you the same voidsman I ordered to have a cranial implant installed?"
2fdf7fd3-bf2c-4038-b567-278085fdc0ef: "A colleague, you say?" {n}The Haemonculus's face stretches into a crooked thin-lipped grin.{/n} "You mean that bony shell belonging to that chattering fool? Mon-keigh, do you really treat it as something... alive? Ha!" {n}His unpleasant chuckle prickles in your ears.{/n}
0619aa8c-e5be-42f9-b473-d4017d0c704c: {n}Eogunn inserts the tip of his augmetic finger into the goblet and pauses for study.{/n} "Analysis reveals traces of metals and organic compounds in the composition, the very same that coat the fountain's inner workings. Alas, this substance will give the drinker nothing but veisalgia and utter disappointment in the local customs of amasec making. No, Nuncius, my analysis does not require your revision."
e1c88b44-2a4a-4cb5-a5b5-f30fcff64819: "Throne protect us!" {n}Abelard strikes a mighty blow with his sword against the console, but it leaves not a scratch on the accursed metal. Swearing under his breath, the Seneschal lowers his gaze in defeat.{/n}
7483c0dc-a814-440e-898c-7c8b2fdb083c: "Won't the battery in your guts power the lift?"
d201c206-cffd-472f-8dd5-0c42fe4d543c: {n}Seberian's eyes linger on you, but Juvenna anticipates his question:{/n} "Father, this is {name}. {mf|He|She} is here at {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_DL_JuvennaSide}my invitation{/d}."
3fa48eb2-a4a1-495e-ba3d-94c3be53365e: Magos Exalt-177 does not support Juvenna's plan. He must either be persuaded or removed by other means.
e2e5ea9c-40da-4c8c-8936-698671312b03: "Let me take a closer look." {n}Eogunn floats over to you, his hulking form bristling with manipulators as he looms overhead.{/n} "Hmm... complex polytrauma... possibly cardiogenic shock... Hysterical twilight state or oneiroid delirium? Or something truly inconceivable? Quite right, Nuncius, a cursory examination is not enough!"
fc9d22a2-10a9-445c-9df5-70d3d4e00954: "The sacred precepts of the Omnissiah do not allow me to treat tech-heresy."
2ddb03b1-21e8-4f1b-9351-90269a073aca: {n}The device housing has been made with exquisite skill — it fits perfectly into the niche, leaving almost no gap between itself and the stone. The internal workings and cables are neatly tucked away, like snakes lurking in the dark.{/n}
54ccea0b-aa39-4e04-a2c0-1f952999abb6: [Carefully step off the Warrant, whispering prayers of repentance] "May the God-Emperor forgive us..."
faf73758-e424-4cfc-83ab-d0573fbf2f7c: "The look on your face is priceless as it is, but now it is about to get even better. You told me the code phrase yourself."
0c4a1288-562f-41cd-b92b-fa60d8eebf3a: "The Gaprak forces have been destroyed and the scion eliminated, but someone claiming to be a Gaprak presented themselves at the outpost. A surviving relative? An impostor? I'll deal with it." {n}Distorted by interference, the voice grows quieter.{/n}
fc7bc3b7-bcda-4fa4-97e1-666d1f1896d6: {n}The piercing stare from above seems to mock your self-assurance.{/n}
52b5487e-462a-41d4-93de-12ec6905b695: "Likewise, I shall turn a blind eye to the threats looming over the protectorate as a result of a lack of vigilant oversight."
17aa00d5-b65a-4549-b677-d9c5055c8f54: "I am no stranger to human foibles. We all live with that fear."
2b65d10d-02fc-48e4-9c5a-5bd8c53fb88d: Manipulus Eogunn Februs is a Genetor Extremis who has attained unprecedented heights in the art of augmentation. Possessed of a keen and restless intellect, he spends his time in experimentation, labouring day and night to discover a means of prolonging the functioning of the human body, regardless of the cost. On this path, Eogunn is inseparable from a servo-skull companion named Nuncius, with whom he speaks as if it were a living person. Eogunn does not fear to push the limits of what is permitted, displaying a keen interest in xenos technology. But what is the price of touching the forbidden, and what may he sacrifice in pursuit of his goal?
21129cd1-6caf-445b-bcf8-ad7ff1346c89: Seberian is too reliant on his xenos blade. It is preventing him from trusting his allies.
51248b7f-b0d6-4bf9-9e61-1a57f2a0c8dc: "Yes, Captain! Apologies for this show of weakness. I overstepped."
25814b5b-3faa-43cc-9498-963cae61ff5a: "The third plan involves the creation of an exclusion zone and erecting observation posts... In short, leave the xenos in the tomb alone and create a restricted area around it. With a portion of the infrastructure allocated to it and soldiers assigned to a permanent secret guard. There will be no casualties, but it will require a high level of secrecy, so the human and material resources transferred to the 'Cage' can be considered as good as destroyed."
e251bc22-af54-4939-bb98-68e75aaad8bb: "I'm not ready. I'm sorry, {name}. I need more time — a few years to research, perhaps more. Nuncius's life is at stake, I cannot decide his fate with incomplete data. When you said you would help me reach Amsanct, it terrified me. What if I'm wrong? I won't get another chance. I panicked and named a system where trading vessels often pass through, so I can get to {g|Encyclopedia:FootfallGlossary}Footfall{/g} and from there join the Explorators in the hope of buying time and discovering new knowledge. I... acted irrationally."
7227c583-d84b-498c-8b01-bb4e92cad7dc: {n}Your ancestor lets out a laugh. The sound rattles unpleasantly within his iron throat.{/n} "Your actions speak of a will to survive and a fervour common to all members of the von Valancius line. Take care that these qualities do not lead you to ruin, as they did me... heir of mine." {n}Seberian suddenly staggers, yet forces himself to remain upright through sheer will.{/n} "And now, I require rest."
b2256fc7-13a1-4fd1-b221-d97750724245: "It is only here, beside spilled blood, that salvation can be accomplished, Veni'Cassia."
a78bf026-c99a-413c-bfad-d2144ccb8734: {n}A soundless shriek lances through everything around you, setting your very eyes vibrating within their sockets. Fury, terror, and revulsion surge upward in a boiling tide from the depths of your consciousness.{/n}
5715d9d8-c63c-49d0-b914-c95608cf2e8d: "I am intrigued, but I will get to it later. The mysteries of the Sector Bionica can wait."
65757012-55bb-466b-8c95-b450028e31df: "Do you know what that 'Nomos' thing is?"
c130d7f6-234c-45d8-9d5b-56b2eb4b9f2f: {n}The Vivisector's eyes shine with excitement.{/n} "It's incredible, Lord Captain. The lighting, the instrumentation... The servants of the Altar-Templum have awakened every sacred machine and granted us incense mixtures to dull pain! We will have fewer deaths from shock, all procedures will be completed in less time... Our work will advance to a new, unprecedented level. This is the best thing that has happened to the Sector Bionica in many decades."
7cafb30b-e8e0-4578-819f-3ff55490941b: {n}You see how stunned the Merovians are by your reprimand. As the realisation that they are considered an undisciplined rabble sinks in, they all avert their eyes in shame.{/n}
8ce1ec9c-2527-472e-b7fe-ca11cb715b22: Deep within the ripped-open bowels of Zarkov lie the decayed remains of colossal creatures. Smoking promethium rigs shall turn the ash of the planet's ancient rulers into promethium.
3c4400a3-ce2d-43e8-a4c2-6dbad543eba6: {n}Batoshski watches you expectantly, like a beast coiled to spring. But the captain-quartermaster's eye widens in terror, her lips silently mouthing,{/n} "Don't."
4d45c23a-7503-4ae4-b764-043f24b192d8: "Reserve your hopes for yourself and your biomancy, Interrogator. You have endured a great deal yourself, devote your thoughts to your own well-being instead of worrying about my future."
bb95cc0b-f178-4275-9708-07f5ffc38af6: "So I grew up on an ancient xenoworld of bioweapons? No, that can't be... No, no. And even if it were — we brought His footsteps to Zarkov, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}! We are pious, and in our hearts there is no room for xenoheresy!"
4d379a99-64a5-4117-92a1-0a42837d7930: "As befits a good officer, Batoshski. Now go. I shall speak with the heir."
cb9be4f7-700b-4753-b9ad-c13be59cf8b0: "What happened after the attack on the Sector Bionica? I thought you were dead."
ed1545ae-e65c-456e-808f-3a3591d2d58a: "We could barely catch up with you! You're obviously in a hurry to meet the God-Emperor, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. Colonel Batoshski decided to send a few of us to help you, so here we are."
4263304e-f464-430e-83e9-f99ae1ed1819: "Detecting interference in the ship's systems by an unknown person with the status 'Genetor Extremis.' Affiliation with the Tech-Priesthood of the forge world Stygies VIII detected. Potential interest in theological debate with the new unit has been logged."
2fe8013e-900a-480e-b9ec-d87eadf54869: "I can't find... my heart! I for-forgot it down there in the shaft... I need to check!" {n}The young man glances around, mumbling nonsensically and occasionally slamming his fist on the deck and pausing to listen. His attention suddenly shifts to his hands.{/n} "My body's been switched... These are the hands of an idler, not mine!"
f1a06313-c851-412a-a72e-c98a092daa97: As long as Uralon is conscious, Uralon's allies gain +1 {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}AP{/g} for every 2 {g|Encyclopedia:SoulMarkCorruption}Heretical{/g} ranks they have.
eb83dec7-521a-453b-b2e2-46064c1b6105: "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, we have received a transmission from Magos Kais-torr-Shaiga. In most elegant terms, he informs you that his research on the Drover has yielded 'results which he is pleased to share with his kind patron.' Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, I am most disturbed by the Magos's courtesy, it is extremely uncharacteristic for a Tech-Priest."
fb0e9caf-023c-4db4-9be3-ac939669a2a9: The wearer gains +{uip|Bonus|7517abf76ba54f0c8e1f608631c46177} or +{uip|PotentialEnergyBonus|7517abf76ba54f0c8e1f608631c46177} {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerBallisticSkill}Ballistic Skill{/g} (whichever is higher) and +{uip|Bonus2|7517abf76ba54f0c8e1f608631c46177} or +{uip|PotentialEnergyBonus|7517abf76ba54f0c8e1f608631c46177} {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWeaponSkill}Weapon Skill{/g} (whichever is higher).
1fd182fb-1340-4db6-8efb-9dcb86ac5bd1: "Are the Tech-Priests of the Omnissiah even allowed to work with living flesh? I thought you hold it in contempt."
ee7872b0-30d3-4746-a221-780c0b2a8d12: "Through your efforts, Janus has become a plaything in the hands of a glutted aristocrat who sees in it only a source of her own enrichment. This promises it nothing but decline and desolation, and I alone know how to prevent such a fate."
c689fa87-c2fc-4303-8559-6efb1f6dea60: "Zarkov never changes, Lord Captain. One species devours another. Let them weaken each other before we come."
4401dcc4-66e7-4513-b3b9-c4a3b472f534: "You mean the 'Hymn of the Lost,' Lord Captain. By it we live, and by it we die. If you want to learn anything about it, just ask. I know it by heart."
7d69012c-4adf-485c-b50a-03d30fa0167e: "Something's jamming the lift. Looks like it's that overgrowth. Give it a blast, will you, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}?"
934eed73-8148-4bd9-8bb0-08bf14c0fc72: {n}The cracking of twigs heralds the approach of people. Janur appears just as suddenly as the first time you met. Apparently, the sound that gave him away must have been his version of a polite warning.{/n}
bcc9a7b9-a211-42e0-9522-4071622031c2: "The same as ever — to draw pleasure from life by satisfying our baser needs."
36bd46a9-4b72-4c42-8903-0350d6a5d741: "That cranial mechanism was another mockery. The specimen had the audacity to lure me into studying this 'Nuncius' by treating it as an equal. I wasted my precious time on it, and what did I learn?" {n}His claws clatter with disdain.{/n} "It is yet another mon-keigh bauble, primitive even by your own pathetic standards!"
a5dea9c8-4a15-41c8-87d1-4dc8350b6e30: {n}The cogitator hums quietly inside its bone receptacle.{/n}
328b91d2-fb03-4b71-9e04-61e1e49432fd:
The user's {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} explodes, dealing {uip|Damage|ecf41086be2743f2857ff0dceac7a920} {g|Encyclopedia:DirectDamage}direct damage{/g} to all creatures in a 3-cell radius.
The user's armour and {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g} cannot be more than 0 until the end of combat.
07eb4814-dfcf-4dcf-95a6-1cdc05170760: The creature known as the Drover is preventing the Lord Captain from contacting the flagship and leaving Zarkov. Moreover, it has made its lair near the crypt of Seberian von Valancius, whose sword {name} is seeking.
e0de70f2-1eb8-476f-93aa-11ea12261894: "Do you see? The metal's biting into the skin, and there's pus oozing everywhere!"
c314f8b0-ad93-497a-95c9-539b7073d443: Saviour of House von Valancius, the Dispeller of Darkness, Conqueror of Janus
ca990ceb-43d2-4b01-9e24-884276ed31a3: "It is pleasing to see that the medicae facility now has some patients who have not had access to the wonders of healing until now. But you, Lady Cassia, really don't belong in such a gloomy room."
c29b3d97-cd45-472d-b709-7213d68739bd: ...my heir gave the order to advance around the pyramid and fight through to the platforms.
b02420ed-7fac-4397-92e7-f6fcacb72640: {n}Next is the old man.{/n} "Preacher Cleophanes. Originally assigned to serve in the ship's reliquary, he rejected the luxury of the chapel in favour of an eternal pilgrimage on the decks where no servant of the Ecclesiarchy had ever set foot. Through his example and word, he encouraged and instructed the outcast and forgotten. Thousands of souls who would otherwise have lost their faith and fallen into heresy, came to know the word of the Emperor. But it only took one to end Cleophanes's preaching for good — a madman attacked him during a sermon, managing to rip out the preacher's vocal implant before the crowd tore him apart."
7ffa024f-f274-423f-a51a-f38cbe63179e: Upon conducting the rite of communion with the augurs, Eogunn Februs compares the machine spirit's findings with his own calculations. He finds the data disheartening and after offering up mourning prayers to the Machine God, he reveals that it was this very vessel that brought him to the Koronus Expanse. Until this day, he had remained ignorant of the tragedy that had befallen it. His data indicates it formerly belonged to a Chartist Captain whose background is shrouded in mystery.
48a5d6c9-5ba1-4132-a51c-d64d2517d8ba: "I want to hear your arguments. Why ought the workers be granted access to sacred implants?"
44aeb3e6-4230-4b76-9297-2f633ae48436: The additional damage suffered from {g|f:780497b04a0944e59cb57e68bb9775c4}ferrum sanctum{/g} stacks is reduced to 10% per stack.
03765236-db67-4eae-84c9-f24e6a14747d: "Did you plan to kill Vivisector Martinax?"
8430dc41-931e-4038-a381-62ba030d2eee: "Can you please hurry this along? I have other matters to attend to."
67f05e99-6087-4b85-8f8d-0834b9090981:
{n}The sergeant watches Dunawski leave.{/n}{br}
"She's a stubborn one, that Dunawski..."
5433483c-2fe5-439a-aef4-7dd31d13f0fd: "Nuncius noted that you were more suspicious of the Merovians at the beginning of your acquaintance than you are now. Has this simple ritual offered to the Rogue Trader mollified you?"
f723ff18-48c1-42a1-91a5-99df34ca4582: The wearer is immune to {g|Encyclopedia:WarpDamage}warp damage{/g}, cannot use psychic or Navigator powers, and is immune to their effects.
b7ec4dd2-b325-4a3c-9852-4320f5f0d825: The Chronomancer links itself to all Necron obelisks. For every obelisk, they gain +1 stack of {g|f:c3b38d719f744d56ad27b8cf48c8ef1d}obelisk-protected{/g}.
031bbbfd-ea3a-45d9-abbf-89545eb38b4f: Isimond keeps a particularly close watch on Jarub as the most unreliable of the Merovians. Jarub must be warned to remain on his guard against the enforcer.
750eb8ce-3b50-4e9a-9905-abd7be3d3b01: Grants shadow protection to Keykeross for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}, which allows her to avoid {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g} from the next attack or attack {g|Encyclopedia:Ability}ability{/g} of her enemy.
443702f8-6c1f-4052-ad1d-298d0b79cca4: A skilfully crafted mechadendrite of unknown construction.
4b109e8a-aff2-4ad4-8189-0b3f7c682abb: Damage-over-time effects inflicted by this character deal +<u>({g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWillpower}WP{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:Bonus}bonus{/g} / 2)</u> more damage.
d02651bd-dcbc-40c3-8c29-395088c27fd9: The squad captain tinkers for a long time, trying to convince the {g|Encyclopedia:MachineSpirit}machine spirits{/g} to grant access to the logs. After several unsuccessful attempts, the screen blinks several times, and the cogitator emits a long beep and goes dark.
1f5d1c8f-086d-4f5d-8758-5c16ed174d30: "A sabotage attempt — activity directed at halting promethium production — has been detected within the confines of the station. The perpetrators are Alva-9's group — ordained members of the priesthood of the Adeptus Mechanicus. Probable cause is violation of the Sixteen Universal Laws — tech-heresy infecting their cognitive centres."
0e2263ef-87c4-429a-be3d-77d55ac6d77a: {n}Baffled by the presence of an Astra Militarum penal legionnaire on an ancient shuttle, your squad returns to the flagship. One thing is now clear, however — the world where Seberian drew his final breath is close at hand.{/n}
af362fb4-3c0d-46a2-9424-b4779d4e0888: Perfectly synchronised with your nervous system, your implants feel as natural as the body parts you were born with.
8aebaec8-0a9a-4665-a850-c074c5dadabc: "Your Lordship, do you know anything about the reinforcements from House Gaprak?"
7756785e-ab16-45f9-93d6-37ca5680c21c: {n}Seberian chuckles grimly.{/n} "Laudable resolve, heir of mine, though unexpected! The wretch must pay!"
0399f601-8736-4a65-87f1-72868395a802: {n}After a series of dry clicks, the {g|Encyclopedia:MachineSpirit}machine spirits{/g} awaken from their long slumber. A steady buzzing sound from within the memory module grows louder, and the data-slate screen produces a pale glow.{/n}
fafe15ee-f5a0-43f0-b811-596682ff228b: Any enemy that makes a {g|Encyclopedia:MeleeAttacksGlossary}melee attack{/g} against the barricade must pass an {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerAgility}Agility{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:ResistanceTest}resistance test{/g}. On fail, that enemy suffers <u>(damage dealt to the barricade / 3)</u> {g|Encyclopedia:PiercingDamage}piercing damage{/g}.
2ff67a1b-6245-4328-8105-0bf5b12fadcf: {n}Laria clears her throat, drawing everyone's attention.{/n} "Before you stands His Lordship {g|Encyclopedia:RogueTrader}Rogue Trader{/g} Seberian von Valancius, possessor of the greatest protectorate in the Koronus Expanse and bearer of the sacred Warrant of Trade, Saviour of House von Valancius, Dispeller of Darkness, and Conqueror of Janus." {n}The female Seneschal's piercing blue-eyed stare is uncannily reminiscent of Theodora's fugitive Master of Whispers.{/n}
cc3ead82-044a-4691-b245-7b0312f79028: "We have detected strange signals on the surface, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. Requesting permission to conduct a targeted augur scan."
52368067-d60e-47df-9a76-e750b62bccf2: "This statement is true. Other means of bringing Genetor Februs into compliance have already proven ineffective, having had an adverse effect on the productivity of the engine compartments."
f12530aa-738a-478b-9af0-18af2e7111ec: "A right-minded servant of the {g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}God-Emperor{/g} amid the xenoheresy flourishing on Dargonus — could it be?"
7246ac2c-3f55-4773-acac-51171356d8b8: "No, Rogue Trader. Not this time." {n}His tone is utterly flat; he makes a deliberate effort to keep his eyes away from the beast.{/n} "I understand the irrationality of this decision, but the torment of this construct has stirred fragments of memories within me that I would prefer not to disturb. That is all I can say."
df46d34b-a737-40af-8157-39a5f961e0d0: "I am not entirely clear on the reasons for your confusion, Seneschal. An individual's genealogical origins and proclivities do not determine their ability to form close personal bonds. This is an incontrovertible fact."
0e32a96e-b31d-4bd0-899e-491db7560b7e: While the item is equipped by the Rogue Trader or their Seneschal, while the two are adjacent, the Seneschal will instead suffer any damage dealt to the Rogue Trader (except damage-over-time effects).
aef2e23f-0136-453f-9504-0717dbbc74f1:
The range at which medikits and stimulants can be used is increased by +{unit_stat|WarhammerStrength|bonus}.
The use of medikits and stimulants costs −1 {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}AP{/g} less.
When a medikit is used to restore an ally's {g|Encyclopedia:HitPoints}wounds{/g}, it restores +{uip|Heal|f4a1137ff9bd4c7a92f000bdd80a7fe8} more wounds.
When a stimulant is used, it restores {uip|Heal|f4a1137ff9bd4c7a92f000bdd80a7fe8} wounds.
cb548abf-ce24-44f6-ba6e-f08c5f98cdbd: "How did you manage to steal the Warrant?"
1f618d02-e2c7-44db-adbf-289c613b0cc9: {n}He looks wearily at Pasqal.{/n} "I am ready to grant the privilege of vivisection to the esteemed Magos, should he wish to perform the rite."
f8831732-7abb-43cb-a103-ce85a5bbc0a1: {n}A stocky blond man steps forward, clad in a semblance of Astra Militarum armour made of tree bark. His burn-scarred face is frozen in an expression of surprise and slight bewilderment.{/n} "I never thought I'd have to say this, but... State your rank, occupation, and reason for visiting this regiment's deployment!"
e129ff1e-c5a2-4d7f-919b-652b52bdc1fb: It has been revealed that Juvenna orchestrated the murder of the Gaprak scion. This revelation would surely interest Seneschal Voigtvir.
47f96565-c241-4ab5-a0f4-63740feb0d67: "Certainly not. Due to the design features of the blessed galvanic cell housed within my abdomen, direct contact with the water-filled tanks will result in an uncontrolled discharge of the Motive Force. Causing the death of the Rogue Trader is the very last thing I would wish. What is more, the sacrament of disinfection, which I perform regularly upon exiting my lab, renders any additional hygiene procedures unnecessary."
3cc0c72c-2f1a-4959-9cc0-a6de70a700d5: We plummeted downward in an infinite, soul-wrenching fall. I closed my eyes, unable to bear the terror, and consciousness fled me.
a2b1a0ab-ffc5-47df-9efb-a9635dbc795e: At the start of combat, Eogunn gains +{uip|BonusEnergy|c1c1223167f041e2aa5a7d4885d3f80f} more stacks of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g}.
849d4ac5-3852-44bd-b9fc-08557d2f6236: "I... agh... Jae, about whom... ahem. I don't think stooping to transparently derogatory insinuations is worthy of the Lord Captain's retinue!"
d6e4690c-1625-45da-8ce3-f7d57f158160: At the beginning of the combat, the wearer gains the {g|f:6a0a266d5fac47eba2784b5b083afde2}shadow field{/g} effect. While under this effect, the wearer counts as being in a {g|Encyclopedia:DetailsOfTheCoverGlossary}full cover{/g} against all {g|Encyclopedia:RangeAreaAttacksGlossary}ranged attacks{/g}. The effect is lost when the wearer is {g|Encyclopedia:HitSequenceGlossary}hit{/g} by a ranged attack and suffers {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g} from it.
6ff9c8d9-6e6f-408b-919d-acc656d686bd: The Rogue Trader's holdings on Zarkov grow only through a constant, bloody war with the planet's aggressive xenoflora. To drive it back, military engineers will erect massive fortifications, unleashing a torrent of fire from the nozzles of thousands of flamers.
590fc7a0-e0d9-4270-ab9c-cd79e7c026a9: "Rogue Trader, I have the enteral injector at the ready. Nuncius is poised to assist if there is an urgent need to extract this... nutrient from your stomach."
78c58d14-d8bb-42df-b942-3908f6051748: "Not anymore. My inaction was long, and my wounds have healed. I am ready for battle."
9807064c-114f-4ff6-933e-d9fe9381c731: "Perhaps we should indeed restore everything to how it was. The Warrant returns to the Chamber, and you return to the Museion."
78be98f5-a094-4769-85db-8c6616112dd3: "House Drivestem supports the claim of the von Valancius heir. Our loyalty to the Golden Throne and the von Valancius dynasty is unwavering." {n}The old woman straightens her back proudly. For a second, it seems as if she is several decades younger.{/n}
fc670712-20d7-4eaf-9b6e-378d5044a07a: "There, in the depths... I discovered a corpse."
1c057bff-3035-4cae-b0fb-4d1c602ca670: {n}The Triasomata goes still, allowing you to examine him. You begin treating his wounds and the places where implant meets flesh with antiseptic ointments, but before you can finish, he starts to howl and shoves your hands away, cowering on his knees.{/n}
b29e4608-2d6a-4e91-99ce-7e08714883a2: {n}Without Nuncius, Eogunn cuts a rather strange and lonely figure. His eyes are sorrowful, but the familiar placid smile has been restored.{/n} "In a lifetime, we endure many losses and privations, but isn't it curious how some of them have the tendency to linger? Everyone carries phantoms of the past within themselves, but not everyone is able to keep those phantoms from consuming them whole. I am relieved that I have at last {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalAcceptance}managed to make peace{/d} with mine."
ff23119a-71f5-405c-ad2d-c126db55bb15: Find out what happened at promethium mining station Tirkuaz-4
8d0090f1-99ec-4ab9-aea5-d73bc8ab51e2: {n}Vedka frowns, her lips thinning into a tight line.{/n} "Then, with your permission, I will share our ways with those willing to listen — the Handbook, our songs, and the stories of Zarkov. Perhaps that will help them understand..."
560df827-f047-4f51-a6aa-f61229da6d31: Long-range {g|Encyclopedia:LanceWeaponSpace}lance weapon{/g} dealing 96 {g|Encyclopedia:SpaceBattleDamageGlossary}damage{/g} to a chosen enemy within a narrow {g|Encyclopedia:FiringArcSpace}firing arc{/g}.
9f625347-452c-4ef2-9120-5daff59e7bf9: "Pride is a flaw that has doomed many wise men. Their knowledge and research perished along with them. Do you really wish to share their fate?"
abb47abd-a945-4f0f-9d6f-3fccb563b68d: You sent the Triasomata created by Eogunn to serve honourably on the officer decks.
ff6125fb-afcc-450e-8eb9-5cdb49b337fb: "I beg your pardon? Did I mishear, or did you claim to be a member of a family that perished in a warp jump a hundred years ago?"
4e62f65d-33a1-4168-9666-a24cecaad84f: "I am surprised they're still functioning." {n}Eogunn's facial muscles twitch, as if the Manipulus is making a titanic effort not to grimace in disgust.{/n} "Perhaps the air here is conducive to the slow decay of the organic matter... or perhaps even servitors possess something akin to a will to live. What's that, Nuncius?" {n}The Genetor turns to his servo-skull.{/n} "No, this particular phenomenon does not seem anything like a machine miracle after all."
8510186b-efcd-4b06-b669-f32e2d379c2e: "Sending coordinates. This information is highly confidential, please use it with cognitive caution."
8cb185c9-4d35-4fe6-b15c-44b0dc601d48: "Pasqal, activate defence protocols. We must hold them back, whatever it takes."
d18b56bc-241c-4c9f-9687-03c0fad01579: "Lord Captain, if I have your leave to speak — Zarkov has forged hardened, skilled fighters who are used to relying only on themselves. If they are asking for help, it means the situation is out of control."
51ea6bab-f8bc-4151-ba2c-35e217866d0f: {n}Most of the messages catalogued by the machine spirit have long since been erased from its memory — save for the last.{/n} "Acolyte Watcher, Temple Cell. Attention from the locals has been avoided. We lost Acolytes Keykeeper, Oculus, and Dusk. Bastion Cell has ceased all contact. Void Cell has gone in search of Bastion."
f3f2f559-66f5-4360-a28e-ef376808fec0: Each stack of this effect increases the turret's damage by +5%.
0035efed-1e00-45ee-a966-69d96bcf454c: {n}Holding a few white tickets in one hand, Jae covers her mouth with the other, suppressing a yawn.{/n} "Has anyone ever told you, O wisest of men, that your voice is miraculous? A deep, sound sleep really does make the queue move faster."
28fc52a2-c96f-4445-9028-365134b10cba:
Some objects in the game can be interacted with in a special way. They are marked with specific icons — some icons allow you to study objects, others will transfer the character to neighbouring locations or launch a small event. These icons become visible when your character is standing close enough to the interactive object.
Click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on the icon to perform the associated action. Some interactive objects, such as doors or chests, are not always marked with icons, but the cursor changes when you hover over them. Hover your cursor over the object. If the cursor changes, the object is interactable.
a0ca12e3-ed8d-4823-bff9-efc4c9a65a3f: "The actions of a true servant of the God-Emperor — to fall upon the enemy of Humanity without doubt or fear."
faa1392c-8900-43b9-8a5b-d2496c38c637:
The range at which medikits and stimulants can be used is increased by +{unit_stat|WarhammerFellowship|bonus}.
When a medikit is used to restore an ally's {g|Encyclopedia:HitPoints}wounds{/g}, it restores +{uip|Heal|a19ae99565a345c4acdfb67b287faebf} more wounds.
When a stimulant is used, it restores {uip|Heal|a19ae99565a345c4acdfb67b287faebf} wounds. It also grants that ally {g|Encyclopedia:MomentumWithDetailsGlossary}momentum{/g} equal to that ally's {g|Encyclopedia:Resolve}resolve{/g}.
0bbb4807-bccf-44de-a571-2641d832187b: "A highly ambivalent decision, Rogue Trader. The sole benefit it may yield is facilitating access to xenos cadavers and technology. And rest assured, cadavers will by no means be in short supply on either side, no matter what promises the xenos have made."
f7e17caa-6296-43e8-a768-b4f132d294b8: {n}Your body is in some kind of trap, but like none you have ever experienced. There is nothing restraining you, nothing confining you — and yet you cannot move a muscle. When you realise that your heart and lungs are also frozen, animal terror seizes you. The xenos has not only paused your body, but your very life.{/n}
845234e5-578f-4a02-b9ca-a28b8279c4a3: "Captain-Quartermaster Dunawski is the colonel's right hand. She's in charge of supplies, which makes her my immediate commander. She is a good sort, just a little bit... well... out there." {n}Janur makes a vague gesture near his head.{/n} "She believes that, now you've arrived, our lives will change overnight. Everything will get sorted and we'll head for the stars with you. That we'll get our atonement, even start eating off real plates. It used to sound like Commissar claptrap, but now... I don't know. I'd rather stick to my uncle's ways."
dda76499-0520-4166-bf4f-bce99e38be78: [Address Trazyn] "The C'tan Shard is too dangerous. Take it back to your Museion as you wanted."
66edfbf8-56a9-4142-9363-f0b24ba35bea: "Interrogator van Calox, would you do the honours?"
11185e8a-1161-470e-8618-980614c758f5: "Was it agents of the Inquisition who killed Gaprak?"
e3197a22-0e0c-404b-89db-fcb5e43235d9: "Out of respect for Magos Haneumann, I will not respond to such a blatant provocation with aggression. That would be counterproductive. However, I would note that my esteemed colleague is fundamentally mistaken in his interpretation of my actions. I have followed the Quest for Knowledge, serving the Machine God as befits a Genetor Extremis. No blasphemy against His commandments has been committed, as Nuncius can attest."
752eff3d-1742-4a0f-9747-f08a792b0b49: You convinced Camilla Drivestem to join your side.
80a99e67-960d-4670-9a18-b36d30f2f67c: "I had already forgotten what the fruits of a true Rogue Trader's wanderings look like. A whole new world ruled by the von Valancius dynasty... We will have our work cut out."
e607cd55-0931-4c2c-80b0-e27e53d7c395: "I, Rogue Trader {name} von Valancius, and my retinue are not moving! Bellowing like that is wholly unnecessary!"
42a7d81a-44e8-4c09-91f3-c3d8986c5041: "Destroy as many of the petty creatures as you can. They must not be allowed to overwhelm us with their numbers!"
8fb8b600-6a8e-4d75-9f36-e3066df35604: {n}Abelard studies Batoshski's face intently.{/n} "A curious tradition. I trust you are fully aware of what will happen to you, and to your entire penal regiment, should the Rogue Trader von Valancius suffer so much as a scratch."
23dae932-77da-43b0-8129-ff8a76972026: {n}The Rogue Trader catches your eye.{/n} "You suggested I listen to my retinue. But what do you have to say? Do you, too, think one can negotiate with a rabid grox?"
b3d17a89-726c-483a-a9a6-f42d0fa0e4e9: "Magos Haneumann, your conflict with fellow members of the Cult Mechanicus has piqued my curiosity. What dogma have you violated to provoke such an attack? Do you even know? Nuncius and I could assist with examining the augments of the fallen—"
31c4c4c3-c8d2-4a87-b7e5-835db4f80795: The hurriedly etched arrow speaks of Seberian's haste.
e54a11ff-01eb-486b-85ae-48a86360dda3: Until the end of combat, strikes made by the {g|Encyclopedia:CareerBladedancerGlossary}Bladedancer{/g} (including attacks made as a part of {g|f:916fcf40f9cc410f9b0a810516089207}Death Waltz{/g}) reduce enemy {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWeaponSkill}Weapon Skill{/g} by −{uip|agiB/2|7bbed20878bb48f8bb4f1e8fe0fb1904} and decrease enemy {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeGlossary}dodge{/g} by −{uip|agiB/2|7bbed20878bb48f8bb4f1e8fe0fb1904}%. These {g|Encyclopedia:Penalty}penalties{/g} stack.
8a777729-97d9-443a-be74-4d2da68dc869: "Does my retinue have anything to say on the matter?"
3fdc28bc-7f58-46df-80f1-2c8e3fb7b78f: "You work in tandem with the Vivisector, don't you?"
999b3e37-a3a1-4da6-b487-2155c0c37c71: "What are the estimated numbers and composition of the enemy's forces?"
372f09e3-35e2-4071-9152-985aa16667c6: Beneath its metallic surface, this immaculately clean, delicate mechanism conceals an array of unpleasant-looking needles and capsules.
430fc4c3-eaa7-4994-98d0-1ab437d1f63f: "But can anyone truly know the essence of another person? For a veil of words often shrouds meaning that cannot be discovered in one brief conversation. Then again, all the greatest works on human nature began with scant material. Ask your questions, Rogue Trader."
b1925c5c-1bc8-412e-989c-0e9766a20909: {n}Your interlocutor shakes her head, keeping her eyes on her victim.{/n} "They're all connected. They are all one festering wound on the body of your crew..."
868bdea0-a55f-456b-9eec-bf74238607e2:
The user makes a {g|Encyclopedia:MeleeAttacksGlossary}melee attack{/g} against all enemies within a 4-cell radius.
The user suffers {uip|bleed|02c674fff8f34bc3a880d726638a4a73} levels of bleeding and immediately suffers an additional instance of damage from this effect.
47d5879a-a176-43bf-8b0a-c6f703209de0: {n}The High Factotum, Chirurgeon Martinax and Genetor Ferrenz are already waiting for you by the mysterious door. The wall behind them looks perfectly flat. Only the metal coat of arms gleaming in the lumen light and the steady glow in the genetic guardian's impassive lenses reveal that one of the dynasty's secrets lies behind this wall.{/n}
e80d4153-ef74-45b9-ade4-d04dbdd446bc: "The weight of your death would be more than Nuncius can bear. Can you really go through with this, knowing you'll leave him all alone?"
318f00a5-e930-4206-8222-2d9efb8d477c: "However, by my calculations, the logical chain connects you to the discovery of sabotage, making a malicious motive impossible."
e6ccdf24-c3a1-4b27-8e2c-ff31a81f1fae: "The scarlet shades have been replaced by black, deep hues, like a bottomless pit. What Yrliet said... is a strikingly accurate description of what I see."
eb897f1b-7aec-4b3c-96d2-3225f630bb2a: The user's single {g|Encyclopedia:MeleeAttacksGlossary}melee attacks{/g} cause chain lightning, dealing {uip|Damage|a1d6e67e4ec643fe8b7babc0e62dfe77} {g|Encyclopedia:ShockDamage}shock damage{/g} with {uip|Pen|a1d6e67e4ec643fe8b7babc0e62dfe77}% {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g} to up to {uip|Jumps|a1d6e67e4ec643fe8b7babc0e62dfe77} targets.
34e2093c-40de-44b2-86ef-68aa0dd0054e: "You know, centuries from now, your blood will flow in the veins of a person by the name of Kunrad..."
c1bbe77d-3299-49a9-b941-5bfc2ff519e6: {n}Something about this strange creation of xenosorcery — the structure, the colour, the way it seems to pulse — makes your skin crawl. You recognise the features of the portal that once transported you into the looped reality of the exhibit, a trap that you escaped only by a sheer miracle.{/n}
7dbf0d3e-bb7c-4594-aa12-aabe6bc2ec8d: "Forgive me, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}! The Drover, that vile creature... Thank you for bringing me to my senses!"
b7d5b6d3-a59f-4f37-97e1-67bb257d0729: {n}Your ancestor does not hesitate for even a second.{/n} "The thief who stole what was bestowed upon our line by the Emperor Himself must suffer most terrible punishment, so that he will never again seek to cross our path. I am ready."
50464ae0-d2f1-4f56-a7cc-cb28030dccfe: "Thank you for your trust, Lord Captain! If I may..." {n}The Vivisector carefully takes the artefact from you.{/n}
0ad5f1ef-8636-4306-82c1-18dcc715640b: "I order the Merovians to retreat to the fortified settlements and hold the line, then counter-attack at the first opportunity."
0aa0ee56-4cdd-4fd0-a2bb-dde134985ab4: The daemonic machine sits motionless, surrounded by worshipping heretics. It is silent, but it reeks of hatred.
6b70b1ae-3c2c-4dd4-a59a-dba4a5cb8d5f: You exiled Izdrig Batoshski to Vheabos VI for insubordination.
6ed6ca5f-6b3e-4fad-b86d-4b261a8288b7: Governor Izdrig Batoshski, upon learning of the path chosen by the von Valancius scion, launched one of the bloodiest planetary revolts in the history of the Koronus Expanse. Under siege, he left the Cage and retreated into the forests, where the remnants of Zarkov's army waged guerrilla warfare for nine months. In the ninth month, Izdrig was killed by his own desperate soldiers, who gifted the former governor's head to the dark {mf|lord|lady} von Valancius in exchange for {mf|his|her} favour.
2ff1edf0-97b9-42a1-827b-ea666cd57425: A new scouting team is immediately dispatched to the site of the disappearance — and very soon sends back a report that the vox-signal is blocked by the strange, matte-black material that covers the walls of the tunnels at a certain depth. They also report that inside these tunnels are the remains of a camp, apparently belonging to an Imperial expedition.
158573ff-9704-4535-b0af-e33236547c0b: The grief and madness that engulfed the soul of Manipulus Eogunn Februs finally shattered his sanity. After performing the unnatural act of triasomia on the body of his dearest colleague, the Genetor descended into an abyss of heresy, embarking on a series of repulsive experiments conceived by his fevered mind. Straying further and further past all acceptable limits, Eogunn lost his human form and became one of the most malevolent hereteks the Expanse had ever known. His body now fully transformed into a foul construct of flesh, metal, and sinister Necron technology, the creature that had once been Manipulus Februs was captured by Trazyn the Infinite and added to the ranks of the exhibits in his Museion.
170d3242-7958-428a-9f1d-a912a8f091f3: "My colleague and I discovered this specimen in a state of natural cryostasis. Its ability to function in spite of its damaged state captured my imagination. Refusing the chance to study it was almost unthinkable. Since then it has been confined in a container intended for... supervised dissections."
fab3e76b-caf6-4aef-b318-400591e9c055:
All creatures in a 7-cell cone in front of Eogunn suffer {uip|DamageDealt|26d7e8d0c00e4d3f9bd6f433d253197d} {g|Encyclopedia:RendingDamage}rending damage{/g} with {uip|ArmorPen|26d7e8d0c00e4d3f9bd6f433d253197d}% armour penetration. The affected targets suffer +{uip|ArmorPen|26d7e8d0c00e4d3f9bd6f433d253197d} stacks of {g|f:7e42fcb1d83040e38e8d68d7edd96375}echopulse{/g} until the end of combat.
Eogunn gains +2 stacks of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g} for each target affected.
9b9675a2-6ddd-47ce-a53b-2fa41b957a36: This creature has +<u>(2 × {g|Encyclopedia:SoulMarkCorruption}Heretical{/g} rank + 20% of Uralon's {g|Encyclopedia:MaxWounds}maximum wounds{/g})</u> {g|Encyclopedia:TemporaryWounds}temporary wounds{/g}.
da646276-0eb6-4f41-9377-1a2fef51c558: {n}After a period of silence, the Manipulus relays:{/n} "My dearest colleague has accused me of oversimplifying matters. He believes that in trying to convey my point of view, I neglected many of the nuances you set forth in the question, and thus gave a trite answer myself. But he considers the general premise to be correct, albeit riddled with holes."
563edaf1-975e-4a1b-b9c5-85e85a6cc78b: "I have grown accustomed to playing the role of mentor, and this habit has done me a cruel disservice — if only I had stopped talking and listened, I could have learned a great deal. From you I have learned {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalAcceptance}acceptance and mercy{/d}. Survival does not equal life. I have argued that death has no meaning — it still doesn't, it is unfathomable and merciless. However, the same is true of life — meaning is something we give it, whether it be service to the {g|Encyclopedia:ImperiumGlossary}Imperium{/g}, the Quest for Knowledge, or forging bonds stronger than blood. I have made my meaning self-destruction through guilt and false hope."
111369f8-8643-4139-a02d-91ce85ee1779: {n}It is astonishing that the grievous injuries inflicted by the tormentors did not kill Laria instantly. The cruelty with which her body has been mutilated is horrifying. Most of her bones are broken, her torso has been flayed alive, and each of her vital organs bears crudely inflicted lacerations. What was done to her was not torture but slow execution. Even the most skilled medicae could not save her here and now.{/n}
30a03c5d-bee0-463b-a026-5468ba8684e0: "That is... correct." {n}Something shifts almost imperceptibly in Alva-9's tone.{/n}
b6fce54c-eaf7-4772-b4e4-4ff6e97e02a8: {n}Jae flashes the Tech-Priest a saccharine smile.{/n} "I'll take that as a compliment, venerable Master! I'd be delighted if you ever chose to expand my perspective regarding our mutual interests."
9586b733-6921-489e-a302-e5295cddee6b: The ship suffered huge losses, including a large portion of the working population, as well as both chirurgeons, who had tried in vain to save those who still had hope.
a8d5bb12-c3cc-4c54-8f56-15168460c6db: "Altar-Templum-Calixis-Ext-17 is a very unusual place. A Tech-Priest monastery, access to which is denied to all who do not belong to the ordained ranks of {g|Encyclopedia:MarsGlossary}Mars{/g}. Some say that its cells are the most secure haven for the sanctity of the Omnissiah, while others say that the most heavily guarded prison of the Adeptus Mechanicus in the Expanse is hidden inside it. It is surprising enough that they have communicated with laity at all; expecting them to leave the walls of their cloister would be unthinkable."
3856e1c1-f041-46ab-b53d-2619404fa158: {n}Eogunn scowls in testy displeasure.{/n} "I must admit, Ferrenz, I expected greater competence from a colleague of your experience and intellect. I accept that you are unable to understand the delicate workings of my dearest companion, but your refusal to acknowledge your skill deficit and your attempts to disguise the fact with outrageous claims that he is in perfect working order—"
c6290d2e-7a64-4de1-871e-ff90379fa07b: You entrusted the governance of Zarkov to Colonel Izdrig Batoshski.
12363254-89cb-4961-bcdb-218095cd9456: "Well, quite. My best scouts fail to return from behind enemy lines, and yet you stand here completely unharmed. And even if you had managed to pull off some miracle, you would not waste what little luck you had left by talking utter gibberish to the Seneschal." {n}Laria wrinkles her nose, as if displeased by her own words.{/n}
ef60a091-2f10-452b-b86e-2182ef7429b4: "Seberian, will you come with me to bring back the dynasty's stolen possessions?"
a0fe2077-9539-4b86-b7e2-18f0c73fdbf1: {n}The Necron throws back its head with obvious contempt. There is an insinuating hiss in its voice, like that of a venomous viper. {/n} "If... only... I could. The plunderers... had the sense to... deprive me of that possibility."
89f61119-506b-4d7c-aacb-4812beb16b2f:
Select an attack action on the panel at the bottom of the screen to attack an enemy with, then click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on the enemy ship. Each {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, you can use each of the ship's attacks once.
Manoeuvre your ship in battle and attack with different weapons to make the most out of your ship's capabilities.
92e9e2d7-e0b2-4c35-b16f-1ffb88d51ac9: "I know. I've received the report." {n}The colonel's voice is tight with tension, while the captain shifts nervously from foot to foot.{/n}
dba575eb-03f9-404f-9820-fb6aa26d77c0:
The user gains {g|Encyclopedia:TemporaryWounds}temporary wounds{/g} equal to their current {g|Encyclopedia:HitPoints}wounds{/g} for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
When the user loses those temporary wounds, they suffer +1 stack of fatigued until the end of combat.
c3847ca0-c57b-4202-8700-e0221144a9bc: It is as though a huge titan is supporting the platform on its shoulders. And by the way the water is rapidly devouring centimetre after centimetre of its body, it cannot last much longer.
769eb318-104b-49f6-81f9-dded3941e7f3: "Not intentionally. I lied to both of us. I did not recall those details until today."
f6629c55-07a6-4405-9311-588c0b2188d4: "That is enough, Eogunn. Leave this blissful fool be and let the medicae of the Sector Bionica tend to him until the end."
d57f4d04-2766-40bb-80cf-0603b64b735b: "...degraded members of an ancient race that managed to outlive its creators. I have collected for local display individuals from the unique Drukhari community that frequents this region, the Nexus of Shadows, which itself is a historical example of the surviving community of this race beyond the borders of Commorragh."
add62a5e-1d25-47a7-8bd6-ebcd4300c676: {n}Vedka draws herself up, clearly proud to share this with you.{/n} "That is our processing graveyard. A discovery of the Third Call-Up. The saps and poisons of various native plants can work wonders upon the bodies of the fallen, provided the right substances are injected into the cocoon and it is left for the proper time. The skin we use as material for grips or clothing. The sinews for bows or cords. The secreted compounds serve as poisons, medicines, or for tanning. Everything is put to use."
e5155a92-fb58-4724-9cba-f4abfc464d59: {n}Tervantias nods approvingly, savouring every word the Manipulus utters.{/n}
8bc4026b-25b8-460c-a691-28b0233e9636: "Hmm..." {n}The colonel growls like a disturbed beast, then suddenly straightens and salutes your ancestor.{/n} "All those I hate are dead, Your Lordship. You are the saviour of my people. I will protect you even at the cost of my own life, so that you may remember: the people of Zarkov do not forget a good deed, even centuries later."
721c7ce8-4ce4-4a98-9a63-1e79e591fff5: "I will set out to search for the sword. In the meantime, you must find a way to get to Trazyn. Once I have the blade, we will give battle to the xenos and unleash the might of the Crimson Dance of Khaine upon his unholy head! The augurs have already scanned the chambers and will give you the coordinates where we will reunite. If I am not back at the designated time, go on without me."
bf6847ea-557c-4800-a219-ffbea14187b7: "You're quite right there, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. And it's the colonel's order that I'm not debating!"
38e736b2-9bec-4d9d-9684-6cd4475f10e2: Abelard deals −10% less damage if at the start of his {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, there are no allied characters in his {g|Encyclopedia:LineOfSight}line of sight{/g}.
50365ca6-10ea-41fa-9058-75f96d0fc28f: "According to the reports received, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, they emerged at the dig site we started on your orders."
39ef8331-f18d-4820-99d3-c40dd295f4be: "That's right. Ferrenz is in the business of coaxing {g|Encyclopedia:MachineSpirit}machine spirits{/g} with the most sophisticated augmetics." {n}Martinax's face twists into a strange expression.{/n}
f4622a41-ed74-438a-96c9-f5933d934e43: Whenever the user performs an attack, all units in a 3-cell radius around the target suffer {g|f:8f9bac2e470942e4874a50926bff5e73}the cold embrace of the Immaterium{/g}.
5ebfc677-7291-4f3d-976b-b721fecc8e68: "Let Lady Juvenna decide my fate. Tell her Elana has gained an ally."
c5466e81-7c36-4259-88f8-d2eb48a72322: {n}A young man is leaning over the control panel, hurriedly pulling levers and pressing worn buttons. He glances at the elevator, then looks askance at you, a flush slowly rising in his pale cheeks. Giving up, he removes his trembling hands from the panel and turns to you.{/n} "Your {mf|Shordlip|Shadylip}— sorry! Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}! Th-the lift. Th-there's n-no... power." {n}The young man takes a breath.{/n} "The xenos — damaged the batteries. To restore the current — we have to turn something off. In the settlement. Anything. That's it."
9c73adac-17d8-449e-88f9-979805b01c4d: "Gamma-Skitarius Amphitrion-37... What, in the name of the Machine God, happened here?"
81a99d85-19db-4b2f-9ff2-4688733daa72: {n}The strange product of xenosorcery gives off a shimmering glow. Your hopes of seeing the deck of your flagship on the other side are dashed — you see only a vortex of alien energy.{/n}
516034f7-9c4b-4b87-9af2-7ec548d48d67: {n}The two strangely dressed officers bow deferentially at the sight of you. The older one, a tall and wrinkled voidborn, steps forward. The tails of long neural cords embedded in his head dangle unpleasantly down his back like a ragged cloak.{/n}
419d48c0-ca79-4007-a038-26cd2698d58f: "Your demand is absolutely justified. Nuncius and I have long discussed the need for Stygies VIII to establish a greater presence in the Koronus Expanse. Perhaps the patronage of the Rogue Trader could bring this about."
5dd43315-4657-4199-b485-0908a1fa7986: "Quite. I am here to eliminate tech-heresy — that is, your group."
567ae979-f882-4367-b6e6-8a711430c79c: Arch-Militant Vedka Dunawski served the dynasty faithfully for decades, inspiring the fighters of Zarkov by her example. Each grievous wound she received only served to toughen her further — and add more iron to her body. But Vedka met her death not on the battlefield, but on the operating table during the implantation of another augment to keep her wrecked body alive.
2f85c034-a9ce-4c5f-9cc1-f55db6b7f2fc: "You are a loyal vassal of House von Valancius, after all. With good references..." {n}You notice Laria frown at her own words, but the very next second her face becomes unnaturally serene.{/n} "Besides, we are in no position to be casting allies aside. So, where were we?" {n}This abrupt change in her is alarming. It is as if Laria is striving to make your presence fit into the current scenario, trying to make it make sense.{/n}
cbccd1a6-51b6-44ad-8969-335c662db526: "Still acting the buffoon? Some people never learn." {n}Solomorne gestures behind his back to the cyber-mastiff, sending him off on a circuit along the wall.{/n}{g|Encyclopedia:ProctorGlossary}{/g}
6d3b3528-c46e-42f7-951c-c454cae10916: {n}The cogitator falls deep in thought, its cooling systems buzzing tensely. After a few minutes of keeping you waiting, the vid-screen blinks and it dutifully presents you with a list of several journal entries that have not been accessed in centuries.{/n}
ad36a3ec-df4d-4938-bdc0-2a8df57c70bd: "The Drover inserts its growths and maces into old bones and wears them like we wear clothes or armour. But there's a trick to it — fire makes the Drover tremble. And that makes the growths either break their armour or shake it off." {n}Izdrig makes a dismissive motion with his hand, then scoffs.{/n} "And no, I didn't get any scars from the armour."
01779d0b-bdf7-4cbb-a139-5e1a00a446b8: {n}Whomever your angry outburst was directed at, you receive no response.{/n}
840b09fe-46f6-433a-99cd-252930301c2b: Deranged writing has been roughly carved all over the surface of the staff.
4e0a22a7-acbb-4eb8-8a32-5f3b21674842: Deals <u>(from 25 to 40)</u> energy damage to all enemies within a 5-cell radius and blocks a random post for 2 rounds.
fad87760-439d-4ee7-a734-42fbb3d10b7e: You recognise your own flagship, however some of the components are different.
a2778d98-a655-42f5-8878-aad32b1d51fb: {n}For a second, Martinax's impenetrably friendly tone cracks, and he sighs waspishly.{/n} "Yes, Lord Captain, sometimes we seriously disagree. Ferrenz believes that replacing flesh with iron, and receiving the blessing of the Omnissiah, is the privilege of the few. I, on the other hand, make no distinction between servants of the God-Emperor. Every faithful soul deserves a chance to continue working for the glory of Humanity. I'm afraid it's not always easy for Ferrenz and I to find a compromise."
f1f8eae1-9037-4f64-a58f-00db99c5ff9d: Through a glowing green haze, I saw {name} struggle to {mf|his|her} feet, forcing {mf|himself|herself} to resist the pressure of the monstrous sound with a superhuman effort of the will. {mf|His|Her} example stirred {mf|his|her} subjects' hearts and rallied their spirits. One after another, they got up and followed their {mf|master|mistress}, driven by that most sublime of human sentiments, loyalty.
671d8696-34eb-4ee7-a0c2-ed0353356b0f: "It is worth the risk. Zarkov requires many resources — let the planet compensate for some of the cost. Send word to Kais-torr-Shaiga."
a5ab9aed-9ed3-42db-951b-737d0295f54c: Allowed the Genetor to take the unique augment from the veteran worker.
cbd1618d-e27f-4dc0-92c0-2d6b762ddd7a: "Perhaps you'd prefer fried tree bark? We're under siege, so don't expect fine dining for a while."
19dd59cd-c71f-40b8-a5a7-c5aad8e00fd8: "Someone with the ability to gather the deadliest foes in the galaxy like a collector is unlikely to be tempted by a large number of mon-keigh creations."
85df235f-04ac-4975-aeee-61be9d3ddc20: {n}Eogunn is whispering quietly to the servo-skull. Catching your gaze, the Tech-Priest smiles sadly.{/n} "Rogue Trader, do not worry — my mind remains clear. But habits are hard to eradicate, even habits such as communicating with a figment of one's own imagination. I still remember the fate of Nuncius, and I know what I must do." {n}He closes his eye and whispers,{/n} "Omnissiah, give me strength..."
1f649ec9-a2da-420e-abc3-63053e87d8a1: "I will not hide the facts — the xenos in question has previously appeared in the annals of the Inquisition. He is a dangerous opponent. I fear we cannot even fully determine quite how dangerous."
b5dedd76-c824-4220-9644-2f6b3ca361de: "Are there any rulebreakers among the soldiers of the penal regiment?"
bb5e11e2-0e89-49f7-a401-38830e9725cf: "Lord Captain, there are signs of human activity. The planet is inhabited — large concentrations of people detected. Human-made structures too, albeit crude ones."
16e9b0a6-a484-41f0-b5a3-6e0b98ceba23: "I was so fixated on the idea that he could be saved that I failed to notice that there was nothing left to save. The Nuncius I knew died on this very spot half a century ago. But cold remains are not all that a person leaves behind. Memory. The {g|Encyclopedia:ImperiumGlossary}Imperium{/g} is built on memory. The memory of the fallen. Of the technological covenants of those who came before us. Of the great sacrifice of the God-Emperor, who guides us through the darkness of unforgiving space." {n}Eogunn takes a long look at Nuncius through the blur of the stasis seal.{/n} "I will grant him the Emperor's Mercy, and afterward I will honour him as is his due as a servant of the Omnissiah."
522579f5-0d62-4c30-b8b6-4032c8957412: "But a servo-skull is incapable of complex thought. How can he be your colleague?"
71a16962-1b40-40e1-8c3d-52d358ec56ea:
For 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}, the user's attacks with a weapon in this hand inflict +{unit_stat|WarhammerToughness|bonus} more levels of {g|Encyclopedia:ToxinEffect}toxin{/g} and the base {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g} of the {g|f:1b7dfda30e5e4c9d87dcfd0bb9cfefe8}toxic saturation{/g} effect is multiplied by 3.
At the end of their {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, the user suffers +{uip|ToxinSelf|b06a441417434f2f9bc6317283920d37} levels of toxin and immediately suffers an additional instance of damage from this effect.
f443f011-45af-492d-9c58-7997be9623d8: "I have seen a great many things in my life. Proposing an alliance can often accomplish more than declaring war. A well-timed agreement will increase your influence a hundredfold — even one made with an enemy. Especially one made with an enemy."
52bd13a0-ff86-4706-a018-69c66adbbbf0: You convinced Sergeant Branek Zarkov to betray Seberian.
37d5ff19-24e4-4537-a244-61cd39e1b5d8: Whenever the wielder kills an enemy with this weapon, veil degradation is reduced by the −<u>Wielder's psy rating</u>. After that, the wielder gains +1 psy rating for 1 round.
15411ae0-821d-4249-8f57-e9aa7a2ea92a: "Esteemed Seberian, this is Vedka Dunawski, my Arch-Militant and commander of the 32nd Merov Regiment."
451e73f9-a965-4ace-bd00-f98bec61d01c: Laria is attempting to persuade Seberian to act with caution and trust his allies. Perhaps, if the council can be swayed to her side, the temporal loop can be broken.
271331b0-0650-437a-ba80-e94c63fa7e93: {n}Kuravich sets aside the data-slate and sighs heavily.{/n} "Everything is piled high with corpses, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. That's how."
5fe4c8fa-c773-4f6d-a309-5d38f7d1396c: Covered in dense script and cryptic sketches, the pages are on the verge of crumbling from old age.
cc989737-aaa9-44cb-a768-2b3c1b4bb919:
Whenever an enemy enters a 1-cell radius around the mace, the mace immediately uses the {g|f:81a72485c85b44f2b0b950f14541dbb9}Whip{/g} ability.
Whenever an enemy uses attacking {g|Encyclopedia:PsykerPowersGlossary}psychic powers{/g} or Navigator powers against the mace, it immediately uses {g|f:b5b58d839060425e9c18f86c48954e14}Feeding{/g} against that enemy.
This effect is not triggered if the mace is {g|Encyclopedia:BurningEffect}burning{/g}.
d6906ca3-60a9-48fa-976b-fa291e911c86: "Gather the Psykers and send them to one of the poles. Lure the Drovers there — it will keep them busy for a long time."
f2f9854f-24eb-4bef-a351-f10eb9b94677: {n}You hear a shocked gasp in response.{/n} "On my honour, we will rout out the heretic who dared to commit such an outrage against the Lord Captain!"
acc08aeb-a9f5-478f-be36-35cea4b65958: "There are days when the desires of my heart align with the demands of duty. This is not one of them. Yet my brothers shall be avenged. By your hand."
f2391322-8ab6-444c-ae4e-8fd63512c9dc: "Vheabos VI? It's a tiny penal world, but not a spaceport by any means."
8c12481c-24cb-4019-98c9-3b42bf4e44eb: When Trazyn suffers lethal damage, he enters recovery mode. In this mode, he cannot be damaged and cannot act. At the end of his next {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, he regains {uip|HP|62d234165d134aa29dc521ee3883ef95}% {g|Encyclopedia:HitPoints}wounds{/g}, removes all {g|Encyclopedia:NegativeEffectTypesGlossary}negative effects{/g} from himself, and can be damaged again. This ability may be triggered twice per combat.
3626b864-bf1d-4161-a9f4-5404de630197: "It is a ceremony of gratitude for a layperson who, by chance or insight, has aided in purging the frailties of the flesh."
a81e3ea3-3d91-47f9-84e7-6a746b9672c2: Seberian is possessed by an obsession with the Crimson Dance of Khaine, an obsession that makes his retinue tremble with fear and leaves him reliant upon none but himself.
458551bc-8e77-42f9-8e51-89a3ad1c7725: The shore leave passed without incident, and the ship's officers talked about it for weeks afterward. As for the Lord Captain, {mf|his|her} dreams were haunted by strange creatures that watched the expedition from the other side of the mirror mountains.
0d1ee935-38f6-43ae-b76d-7752ce0554dc: ...my heir's call. "Faithful servants of the dynasty! Fear no evil and follow me!"
69676dda-0e19-4675-a3a1-db60304f427b: "Interrogator van Calox, your current condition indicates reduced efficiency. As a Genetor, I could—"
81dcf107-7f96-4a63-a3cc-b756aa4a2e62: Eogunn suffers 30% of the damage dealt to an ally affected by {g|f:bee0d96b40b84f5da78bc3c9f41ec1fa}line of support{/g} as {g|Encyclopedia:MentalDamage}mental damage{/g}.
8c25ab37-330a-4a46-8792-b5c1bcc4bc56: Laria is prepared to arrest Isimond based on the evidence gathered, but the Rogue Trader wishes to speak with the enforcer first.
986b2343-c85b-43d7-a40f-be0860da58a8: "I apologise, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, but if you fall, there will be no atonement for us."
e6422b6d-7b71-413c-a56f-d51a698db825: {n}You know you speak the truth. You begin to think that the man in the portrait is looking back at you with faith in his eyes.{/n}
d3f8bfdb-98a1-42e4-a239-5f81af8e82d3: "A man whose words are heeded by the throng, and who can make the masses follow him, is more than useful. Usefulness is a fine criterion for righteousness."
