90bf2109-62b4-4045-9d32-baed264bb2cb: {n}The lieutenant-chirurgeon is splattered with blood from head to toe — yet he himself appears to be uninjured.{/n} "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, I owe you my life. Thank you... and glory to House von Valancius."
b1b84989-6a16-4025-82eb-49d76088e212: "I am seeking the Crimson Dance of Khaine. Is it here?"
d9324aa3-14cd-4522-bf96-babe2182bbf4: "The review is over. You are completely healthy and can perform your duties without any physical hindrance. But... please take care of yourself in combat. Be merciful to your body. Even His chosen are vulnerable to the weapons of the enemies of Humanity."
056ea03b-231e-4b02-97b2-e8619ca9efdd: Since the excavation began, workers have made great progress in the arduous task of clearing the entrance to the ruins.
60bcb979-e999-4e82-aa91-c97b31d090fc: "I shall give you the chance to perform deeds as great as mine!"
9ec3290a-36b8-4549-9ae6-75328e5ff0b3: Whenever this augment's ability is used, the user suffers −10 Weapon Skill and −10 Ballistic Skill for 1 round. Stacks up to 2 times.
f6c4064d-6178-4150-8fe1-77a786ac2081: "According to the Handbook, the duty of a supply platoon is the constant procurement of resources, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}."
4825f0cc-1f93-4035-9e45-4686d0a7e7fb:
You have found a consumable item!
Grenades, stimulants, medikits, and other consumables can be placed in quick access slots. To do this, double-click on such an item {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} or drag it to a highlighted slot by holding {mouse_icon|LeftMouse}. Each consumable item has a certain number of uses, which is indicated in the item's description.
As with most actions in combat, using consumable items spends the character's {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}action points{/g}.
31df20fb-ad52-4af7-b8a8-ca664bdeefe7:
Trophies on the right side of the trophy window go to your inventory — such items can be used or equipped by your characters. Trophies on the left are packed up as cargo and sent to your ship, which can later be used to trade with various factions.
You can move trophies from one side of the window to the other by dragging them over using {mouse_icon|LeftMouse}. Alternatively, you can use the buttons at the bottom to mass transfer all trophies to the cargo or inventory section of the trophy window and vice versa. Click "Collect all" or press [SPACE] to transfer the trophies from both sides of the trophy window to the corresponding windows.
39b503a7-8c5d-4031-9d9d-34cd99b6ae7f: The Rogue Trader has forged an alliance with the Inquisitor. Now {mf|he|she} must find the agent operating under the call sign "Elana."
157b4231-e662-4f24-9e12-d109281209af: "I {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_DL_ZarkovTraitor}don't care{/d} who rules the dynasty or whose name is written on the Warrant, as long as the Imperial Creed is followed. You, Lady Juvenna, seem fairly strong in your faith."
3ef86232-fc13-4572-bcf7-d28fae54b017: "Limitations of... your reasoning... lowly one." {n}The xenos lets out a clanging laugh, pausing only to assess your reaction.{/n} "Only the one beside you... is guilty... of the extinction of the intellect that complemented him... Irreversibly."
f5d8219b-e2fd-4edf-86c6-92d72f506d1b: "Magos Logis Tetria-Lemma transmitting. Omnissiah's blessings! The incalculable probabilities of the paths have once again brought your vessel to the point of concluding Pact ТR-3. Are you willing to agree?"
ca50746a-9b8d-495d-a9df-27ee53e823fe: "Remarkable accuracy in the rituals of the tech-sacraments! I would like to meet the person who calibrated you."
231730c3-eedc-4b42-8c2a-124a3e8db0ee: {n}The makeshift hospital is a hive of activity. Here and there, the wounded moan through gritted teeth. A man wearing a chirurgeon's armband and a lieutenant's insignia stands at one bed examining a patient, while a young assistant with the rank of sergeant rushes about.{/n}
7e4810d9-8c61-4219-8936-ca792c2063be: "I trust, {name}, you will not deny yourself the opportunity to savour this 'nectar.' From what I have heard of your people's living conditions, consuming the processed remains of your own kind should hardly be a novelty for you."
e263773a-641c-4d7d-b6f8-1c458e11ad1d: "The final entry tells of a siege of Dargonus, but such a historical event is not in the chronicles."
b3d260b6-1879-4624-8cb9-9f204a4ea220: {n}Izdrig frowns and lets out a strange sound, a mix of grunt and growl, yet devoid of any aggression.{/n} "I miss it. I was born there, grew up there, and nearly died there more than once. Back home, I knew what to do, but in the wider world — I don't." {n}Your eyes meet, and you catch a glint deep within his green eyes. Suddenly, the colonel smirks.{/n} "And that's a good thing. I feel like I've grown younger. I have to learn, adapt, and think all over again, instead of giving the same orders day after day. It stirs the blood."
901ae87d-bebc-4217-85d5-d32a77fd2972: {n}Astounded, Eogunn turns rigid, letting his hands drop helplessly.{/n} "This means that the laboratory on Amsanct is not the only one? The Inquisition was running a project unsanctioned by the priesthood of the Adeptus Mechanicus? Now the pieces are falling into place... now I see."
a4b6a686-bc58-4717-9550-5cc9c41b10b5: {n}Instead of responding, Eogunn looks expectantly at the servo-skull. Nuncius hovers impassively at his shoulder, the very embodiment of brevity.{/n}
3bc4ae50-a58e-4030-8598-84b365480aa8: "The Omnissiah has granted many revelations to these xenos, and they have turned them into riddles that are, at times, unsolvable. What? Yes, Nuncius, I am well aware that in order to cut the power, the geometry of the circuit must be disturbed, but I fear that without an assistant endowed with... non-standard reasoning, it is far from feasible."
c1d64801-fe73-48b7-ac13-99163a2f5df7: "I would like to learn more about you."
a62c8ac0-c4f6-49ef-85f2-b2bf4b84efd6: "With my dearest colleague, Nuncius, who else would I...?"
fa919d66-de64-4e7f-8253-7983b62d095f: "Destroy... the enemy! Ch... check the arsenaaal! My-my body is... the enemy." {n}The man hunches over and strikes himself in the chest again and again, drooling bloody saliva. Then, he straightens abruptly, lets out a wail, and starts bashing his implant even harder.{/n} "Not mine, not mine, not mine! It's gnaaawing at me... from within, taunting me. I'll die... I'll destroy it!"
f1b879ac-9989-4f72-9fcd-580ae903035c:
This creature's demise is imminent! If it is not healed by the start of its next turn, it will die.
Enemies will prioritise other targets while this creature is in this state. It has +50% dodge.
1b88e11a-74b1-44d8-91fa-4ecc01cfb965: "May the Omnissiah keep you from harm." {n}The Tech-Priest bows slightly.{/n}
b084b36f-b945-4824-885c-6c89d3567ab8: {n}The sound crackles with brief static, then the voice of Logis Tetria-Lemma occupies the airwaves once more.{/n} "Let His sacred law be heard by all!"
f33b150b-5006-4e85-a8d9-d764970646b4: "Burning away xenoblight with the sacred flame of the God-Emperor — what could be more righteous, {name}? Let me give the Zarkovians a lesson in burning out heresy."
425185e2-381e-4070-9ca2-95029d6de033: The Chronomancer can attack targets regardless of having {g|Encyclopedia:LineOfSight}line of sight{/g} and has unlimited attack range. Their attacks target only enemies and cannot miss.
4817cbe2-be8f-4dac-a398-5edfd2e9c691: "I'm no great storyteller, but if my {mf|lord|lady} insists." {n}Jarub's expression darkens.{/n} "Every one of us {g|Encyclopedia:MerovPenalLegionGlossary}Merovians{/g} is guilty before the {g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}God-Emperor{/g}, terribly guilty. We wash away our wicked deeds with blood, dying for Humanity. No matter how petty the offence, death for the glory of the Golden Throne awaits everyone in our regiment." {n}He shivers as if from a sudden chill.{/n}
b2febacf-4533-4f92-9d3d-01b4d1cc01ca: Light dies upon the artefact's perfectly spherical surface. The xenos creation resembles a pocket of primordial darkness, and stands as a hollow denial of the very concept of existence.
43173c6d-380a-43fc-b459-86d6c80182fd: "It's better just to be patient. Look, two people barged in without queuing, and later they were carried back out..."
e75cc445-36a1-424a-a86e-aafca00fa234: The wearer gains +30% {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} and +3 {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g} against Necron weapons.
2028903e-faf3-4d21-92a8-7835ddf41f6b: An unmarked vessel drifts slowly past the flagship, its {g|Encyclopedia:Lumen}lumens{/g} extinguished, and its hull pockmarked with melted ruts from the salvos of unknown weaponry. A swarm of space debris circles the ship like insects over a decaying corpse. But more alarming is the feedback from your {g|Encyclopedia:Augur}augurs{/g} — there is something alive in the bowels of the ravaged ship.
625270d0-e169-4624-a6b7-be5d670c18c0: "Lord Captain, the priesthood of the Altar-Templum-Calixis-Ext-17 sanctuary customarily have no desire to communicate with the laity... But today is an exception. And so are you." {n}You hear the clicking sound as the Vox Master toggles between frequencies.{/n} "We have been granted access to the station's frequency. What request do you command us to transmit?"
43222580-2dc5-4d8c-b8ac-7a3018bc8ad9: "Since something akin to trust has sprung up between us — or, to call things by their proper name, mutual necessity..." {n}Laria removes the signet ring with the von Valancius coat of arms from her finger and holds it out to you.{/n} "I imagine you know the value of such a thing. Henceforth, you may speak in my name. Be kind enough not to tarnish it."
5729c3cd-f19a-4b03-ab99-90c9411eafb6: At the war council, neither side can tip the scales without the intervention of the Rogue Trader. Laria's proposal is to rely on the dynasty's allies and maintain the defence.
d1f241f0-20fd-40ba-b9d3-610ceec76b82: "Ferrenz, what is going on? Where is his memory?"
4c21b46b-732a-4e1f-b6f7-2d59b10595a2: "We are under siege and cannot afford an enemy on this side of the gates. The evidence gathered is enough to hand him over to experienced interrogators. I will give the order to have Isimond taken into custody."
3de61847-516e-4d53-9bd4-33db308d8d63: "Ambitions supported by will often cross the limit of what is possible. I would be interested to witness the moment when you realise yours."
3bbec2ad-6016-4e76-bba4-b4acecdcfbd9: "Magos Februs, do you truly believe flight is the best course? You know too much. Without the Rogue Trader's protection, the issue of balancing your usefulness against the risks of your continued existence will inevitably arise. You are intelligent enough — calculate your chances yourself."
80dfb8c9-155d-4a8c-9982-028c06e12543: "You mean to say the station is sinking?"
b702d0e2-6a22-4664-9c88-d9b9f7597c3d: "Lord Captain, Tirkuaz-4 is not receiving vox-signals from orbit. Our {g|Encyclopedia:Augur}augurs{/g} are registering that the water level around the station far exceeds the norm."
dc4028ef-c439-4ad3-ae63-4e10c29920bd: "You have your own unique dialect here, it seems. Would you care to translate?"
50e0003c-4a3b-432c-ba95-556ccdcf6c1a: "Threats and insubordination. Tell me, Colonel, why shouldn't I kill you on the spot?"
80849a29-6276-45bd-ac4c-5df3e11c1930: "Your illustrious ancestor, a member of the von Valancius dynasty, Conqueror of Janus, and a Rogue Trader..." {n}The Factotum's eyes remain cast downward.{/n} "Former Rogue Trader."
9b3eb6a2-5420-4826-8fce-2b73e7d87620: "It will be difficult to explain, but we are in a time loop."
ecd11d1b-7138-4ebb-bafb-29647c6f93c5: "Do you believe that the Warrant can still be recovered?"
2f6ea029-4dfb-49a1-b92d-6b8443bd76f6: "Yes, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. I will order the equipment prepared at once."
e201dcdb-5270-484e-8436-7e9d638eb1ba: The Battalion of the Nameless Last, as the tomb guard soldiers became known, vanished from all records, but remained unspoken heroes. They forged a strong alliance with the Ordo Xenos and provided the Lord Captain with a new vein of collaboration with the Inquisition. The support of the agents of the Throne helped minimise losses and repel the onslaught of the Flayed Ones, and some who had served in the Nameless Last were recruited to serve in the Inquisition.
cc8b8bdd-57c2-4560-b9bc-891c42644d88: "Oh, as soon as I obtain the answers to my questions, I will rise from this throne and restore the Motive Force to the compartment, rest assured. And now I beg another pause, Rogue Trader — I must excavate the heart implant. Nuncius, kindly monitor the functioning of the atrioventricular node, if you would." {n}One of the flexible mechadendrites dives deep into Eogunn's chest, carefully extracting the augmented heart.{/n}
1b745ca2-dab7-436c-8bd1-237bfd3d5c8e: "Let's settle this cleanly, shall we, Batoshski?"
d635d4c3-376a-43cb-b2de-b3e4a445687a: "What would your actions be in this situation?"
7df12ae9-d161-4f8b-9f4b-c0b0b0e910b9: "The priests in the flagship's chapel say that the God-Emperor Himself grants power to the nobility. And He cannot be wrong. If you suspect these noble families of heresy, you need only give the word."
df28eae3-b31d-48b0-981d-0abbcfd2ed39: {n}You duck under the strike, and the blade cuts through skin and muscle on your shoulder blade. Driven by adrenaline and a thirst for victory, your body shrugs off the pain and the weakness of the poison.{/n}
49d6f94a-866a-403c-afa0-3ae274e44ab1: "Let's discuss new thoughts on the temporal anomaly."
a5f9b1d6-f893-4dcc-a93b-2c8664df7c71: {n}Obeying your will, the Vox Master signals the start of the mission to all participants.{/n} "May the Emperor protect you, Lord Captain."
84d1b974-37ec-47c0-9ba5-25e379645366: "Or..." {n}Trazyn breaks off into gleeful laughter.{/n} "Or... in exchange for a favour, I will accept into my collection one interesting article I spied on your ship when I was observing the dynasty through your eyes. I did not expect to encounter something unique on an ordinary human vessel. You call it 'Nomos.' I am intrigued by the nature of this phenomenon and would like to have it for my own — in exchange, I will help you deal with Calcazar."
6f708d7c-697d-474e-a921-b80580349b9e: "What do you intend to do now?"
c6522fcc-80e0-4162-bb32-32acfa04ae13: [Show Laria's ring] "The Seneschal has ordered me to relieve you."
7861c25e-ea10-41b1-a9f0-3beffc7247e9: "This is but a reprieve. That decrepit xenos does not forget. He will come back for you. You have made a powerful enemy, {name}." {n}Seberian lets out a dry chuckle.{/n} "But by our enemies is the glory of our lives measured, at the end of it all."
2249aba5-81fa-4ed6-bf82-f41c3a16a16b: "May the grace of the Omnissiah shine on us."
0c2c09f6-5d26-4a5a-822c-6583549dcc43: "The tale of the elder von Valancius was a wild one, and I sort of liked it. But still, it's good that the story got its ending."
ed9be2f3-da1b-4c19-8c60-a94272b7d7c5: "Confirmation: this thesis is true. The fact of cooperation is recorded in the archival logs with the following note: 'extremely high efficiency.'" {n}The Artisan bows his head pensively.{/n} "Nevertheless, this experience is not relevant in this situation."
1979edf8-fa33-43f0-9727-267433af6cb8: {n}Janur narrows his eyes as he stares you down, then he nods curtly.{/n} "God-Emperor help you. Atonement for the one!"
f3419f95-838d-4516-b288-c67841672ae8: "How to make a human even more greedy, arrogant, and fussy? Grant him the title of Rogue Trader! This exhibit is dedicated to perhaps one of the most notable examples of this curious social stratum of the Imperium — Seberian von Valancius, the cunning and brazen thief who fell into a trap in pursuit of my treasures."
1265d13e-43d6-4ef0-8c03-24522ce0eed1: "A valid proposition. Let us make haste."
5e0d07d2-9a14-43d8-b7d0-1fb7a3525d29:
aA bB cC dD eE fF gG hH iI jJ kK lL mM nN oO pP qQ rR sS tT uU vV wW xX yY zZ
0123456789/*-+!@#$%^&*()_+{} ":><?|~
e0ed47ca-ea0b-4764-b69d-7883c8a8d2dc: {n}The man who addressed you takes his eyes off the workings of the voidship and stares at you in surprise. Under that intent gaze, like a dark veil descending around you, the last sparks of your resisting mind are snuffed out, and you recall your next line with perfect clarity...{/n}
951dfdab-817b-4b0d-a2d8-ff261a802f00: "Take the lift down. It'll put you right outside that thorny bastard's lair." {n}The colonel exhales heavily, the air hissing from his lungs. His green eyes, clouded by a veil of rage, rake over your face before suddenly softening.{/n} "If you want to throw your life away for nothing, I won't stop you, you can leave now. But if you have enough humility to take advice from a local, I'll be waiting for you at my quarters."
948ea7d4-b5e0-4397-8bb6-53efc3f39be5: "No, it does not." {n}Seberian shakes his head sadly, yet you read something that looks like warmth in his eyes.{/n} "But I am grateful for your words. Do not think it has escaped my notice how the Koronus Expanse has changed. All that I knew and valued has been lost."
021678d4-284c-403e-b1b4-c12712a8f8cb: A black stone, with occasional flashes of green light dancing across its surface.
8e351c8f-4ec3-42ff-8ee1-b2c731af753d: "I found it within a black tomb belonging to kin of the vile xenos Trazyn. Its former owner, an Aeldari, was gripping it tightly in his shrivelled hands, as though refusing to part with it even in death. This stubbornness, along with the blade's masterful craftsmanship, sparked my interest. I travelled to the farthest reaches of the Koronus Expanse to uncover the truth about this artefact. Eventually, I managed to capture one of the so-called Children of Asuryan. Under torture, the xenos identified the blade as the 'Crimson Dance of Khaine' and recounted its history. A mighty Aeldari warrior once wielded the relic. This warrior ventured out to destroy one of the lairs of the ancient enemy of his kind, whom they called the Oghyr. Evidently, he failed."
0b1ddc3d-05c1-4966-bb51-5e59573485c9:
At the start of their first {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g} in combat, the Chronomancer always uses {g|f:100829bd43eb4b0285906a77fb6fb61b}Obelisk Protection{/g}. After that, starting from the next turn, they start casting {g|f:43d5df84c9784721a5fefdcb512bd5d6}Obelisk Hyperphase{/g}.
When the Chronomancer is killed, all Necron obelisks will be destroyed.
63c3ad1b-ebfb-495c-8b17-33aa23e0655e: {n}The enraged howls of the bound {g|Encyclopedia:XenosGlossary}xenos{/g} merge with the sounds of the overloaded {g|Encyclopedia:Elucidator}Elucidator{/g} to form a single cacophony.{/n} "Cursed... runts... of universal entropy... lowly creatures of the vanquished... foe!" {n}The Necron tries in vain to break free from its prison.{/n}
26c0bdc1-3e96-46d3-944d-c1a18f2024f0: "And why do we praise the God-Emperor even when all seems lost? Why do we struggle to survive even where we should surely perish? Because we have no choice but to fight against the inevitable. Izdrig will battle for the regiment even against the Emperor's Angels. As the Handbook states, 'The only thing a Guardsman should fear is the failure to do their duty.'"
57951905-c7f2-4fec-bbf6-84aaa72bbf4a: "The sacred machine for installing augmentations has reawakened, Lord Captain."
d97a0e75-d8a8-4df8-abb5-1d6bfb802dbb: "Let me pass, and no one will be harmed."
0f60ed49-cce6-4f19-9ece-d6261a81a5e3: "Should have just said that to begin with, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. And you'll have to tell me what a grox is later."
fa3da9b4-ed4e-428a-bd10-59ffc15db2c1: "It will be done, Lord Captain. The full force of the dynasty fleet will leave these creatures no chance of survival, rest assured."
eab3d6a2-c5a6-46cc-bb89-72b208e45166: {n}Your forehead slams into your opponent's nose with a wet crunch. Batoshski's grip loosens, and he is forced to stumble back as he struggles to regain his balance.{/n}
3ea1a026-05b3-4798-836e-51f43e6829ef: Deals <u>(from 35 to 70)</u> shock damage with 50% {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g}.
de6d2b2d-ec22-4078-89ec-c289d2e1684b: "Thank you. It took time, but we have come to an understanding."
66290b3f-ae10-4693-8d96-fc04c5f45403: "There's just this bitter ooze trickling out. The drought, I s'pose."
1ee9cc97-734d-4f6a-9918-52e91813f27b: "Thank you, {name}. If I may, I will leave you now. I have much to think about."
5ae0ed56-b3b1-49f6-9fcb-68f6f4e4a30c: "Well, maybe it's easy for you. You probably have some charging things back where you're from, but we don't have anything like that. But you can throw a battery on a fire. There's even a proper name for the ritual, but I can't remember it. The problem is that sometimes the battery just explodes. It's risky, and the rite ruins them eventually anyway. So, the ones who know exactly where to put it in the fire and how long to let it burn, they're valuable soldiers."
f631a208-64c8-43e6-bb32-2e1468e962bc: {n}Your companions murmur in agreement in response to your words. The Inquisitor silently turns his gaze to the {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptaSororitasGlossary}Sisters of Battle{/g}. They stand as if carved from stone — and yet there is no peace in that stillness. Their bolters are raised just enough to take aim in a single motion.{/n}
314862a0-94b5-4a97-8336-4dc76ed05abf: "And let their guard down? That's not the answer, Lord Captain. I'll talk to Jocasta. I'm sure we'll find a solution. Maybe joint patrols, or using the 32nd to bolster punitive operations."
a345e725-f02e-4dd2-9cdf-0f1310741854: "Then, there's this rebellion. Convenient. How many records and crucial witnesses do you think will be lost? Some people might call it a 'cover-up.' I do not like how aptly this term describes the present situation."
6c757ec2-7941-4f1c-b37f-dae252338a48: {n}Batoshski bares his teeth. Unexpectedly, there is respect in his smile, but it is that of a beast that has met another predator.{/n} "Because I wish to hand over the regiment to you following every tradition. Because it is the right thing to do. And because I refuse to insult you with pity or disdain."
c8f0fbe9-613e-4ac0-932a-9237147fbe15: "True faith can bestow astounding powers, but its blade is double-edged, Rogue Trader. Consider this: will this weapon be turned against you if the one who wields it deems your deeds unworthy? Death, in the hands of this woman, will always be at your back."
73b106fd-9ec1-4694-88aa-e6f0bbc83a21: "After my victory over Diodas, I craved greatness for my dynasty. A sword alone was not enough. I was certain the crypt concealed many more secrets, and by unravelling them I could make von Valancius the most powerful Rogue Trader house in the Koronus Expanse. And I was right..." {n}Your ancestor falls silent, his blank, unfocused gaze directed past you.{/n} "But it was that very confidence that undid me. Among the ruins, I found a portal leading to the lair of the vile xenos Trazyn."
21408584-df6d-4872-88fc-c29f60907345: "Deeds? I suppose you did guard one of the minor worlds in my protectorate with some distinction." {n}Disdain colours Seberian's words, and the admiration in Dunawski's eyes shifts to bewilderment.{/n}
efb2c405-42d7-472f-a4da-11f510b69ee5: "You have the crest of the Blood Angels on your shoulder. Why?"
b9024977-7808-429d-a631-67e27536d180: "Coincidence, not 'fate,' you primitive creature. You should be more grateful to the laws of probability that we did not remain enemies — it would have ended in disaster for you."
032a9843-0e05-4d4e-a9b3-5538594abbb0: {n}Eogunn closes Nuncius's eyes. He stares at the body in silence for a time, then summons the {g|Encyclopedia:ServoSkulls}servo-skull{/g} and issues a command in binharic. The servo-skull's eye socket goes dark and its manipulators freeze, and the Tech-Priest places it at the foot of the sacellum.{/n} "Now I have let him go for good. {name}, thank you for this opportunity. And thank you for being here with me."
87bf9d5e-ad85-4529-bf9a-975dab41927e: "In the last void battle, Lord Captain, where he barely survived his injuries. He has suffered a few healing attempts from charlatans on the lower decks and endured a long road to come here. However, no weapon can destroy the devotion and fortitude of a true servant of the God-Emperor."
ffb61408-ee2c-4d56-ba20-df4d77451a8c: "Given your inability to conduct a scan... I will respond to a limited set of enquiries."
bc5ccd46-3395-4d54-84e1-5e127273f676: "I see. Let's talk about something else."
33cefc33-13ba-4732-bec8-ebc77d1f6ce6: "Even with supplies from the dynasty, many will die, Lord Captain. It will be a long time before we can announce a new Call-Up."
4b8eec35-25d7-4a5d-be51-0222288be272: {n}The remaining pages of the diary are utterly untouched. Perhaps the cogitator in the palace of Dargonus, mentioned in the last entry, can tell you more about this mysterious Seberian von Valancius and his life's journey.{/n}
ee62e656-567f-44cc-a676-43fb627c9c9d: "Who are you always talking to, iron sage? I neither see nor scent anyone near you. You cannot be trading wisdom with that skull at your shoulder?"
9d3291db-c6a4-48db-8b21-76765cde9393: A traitor has emerged within Seberian's camp, an agent through whose hands the Inquisition reaches into the very heart of the palace.
e665e899-6e8b-4e5a-b724-38f01c8f1985: {n}Marazhai licks his lips slowly, not moving an inch. He watches Gnotec's every movement and whispers so quietly that you can barely make out his words:{/n} "Pure, unadulterated suffering. Delectable. Give him to me, if you do not kill him first."
a191eed2-60e5-401b-8f37-1299c4f318fa: "Pain is merely the instrument by which living flesh alerts us to deleterious effects upon it. I am using this tool to receive the most complete and, thus, the most valuable information about the consequences of my procedures."
bf0bab8d-fed9-4b01-8509-28dcf4013b17: "Lies!" {n}The Manipulus responds with unexpected vehemence.{/n} "If the xenos mind is so limited that it {d|Encyclopedia:Ch3_TalkWithHaemunculusAboutNuncius}cannot hold a dialogue{/d} with such a distinguished thinker as Nuncius, then the blame rests with the xenos alone."
870e8d2d-4d9b-4bf2-8331-dbedf59a3752: "The sea is angry. The mon-keigh have gnawed into the heart of the planet and are taking her life force. When she tires of their greed, this place will be doomed."
cd1d749f-5e4e-41ce-8e45-99bdbe8c9141: {n}Pressing his lips together, the Manipulus grunts out,{/n} "To condense his overly pedantic response: Nuncius does not consider righteousness to be in any way a significant factor in this research. Had I any less respect for my dearest colleague, I would say that this constitutes an egregious misunderstanding of the concept of the Triasomata, and is indicative of extreme closed-mindedness. But such remarks are beneath me."
78a189be-2a93-45b0-b7cf-d6bf8bff6c40: All {g|Encyclopedia:RangeAreaAttacksGlossary}ranged area attacks{/g} deal +5 base damage.
c1c9b45e-edec-4822-a6dd-c8b23356bedb: [Address the skull in your hands] "Nuncius, I believe your colleague has lost his mind, wouldn't you agree?"
b19dad13-9833-4da6-8fae-94747d0348d7: "Speaking with you is the greatest honour, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}."
be76d421-4c79-4636-9c27-a6959f731529: "How curious... It seems the Rogue Trader's presence is indeed influencing the patient's state of mind." {n}Martinax looks back at you with interest.{/n} "Whether it is a manifestation of devout faith in His anointed, or a bizarre form of delusion, it can only be to our benefit. Whatever you are doing, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}... please continue."
1397dccf-9ad6-4ed3-8b29-f77d0c002756: [Smirk arrogantly] "You have been teaching me how to be a Rogue Trader all this time — now at our parting, are you not impressed by how well I have absorbed your lessons?"
70827dad-94b8-4bd6-aaae-1a8bd9dc5deb: "I could turn your punishment into something worse than death... Did you ever consider that?"
263c5620-1164-422b-89f9-7b463eb1341d: "Real fighters, shereen! I am quite certain their talents could be put to the best possible use." {n}Jae nods along with the Merovian's words, squinting with satisfaction.{/n}
d66fbf4c-7805-4dd5-8ce6-3041b0115768: "The rabble have forgotten their Lord Captain's visage. I shall refresh their memory."
e54f9c23-b040-42ee-bf35-e45e39995b92: The steam that fills the room beyond the grate scalds your face. Perhaps there are valves somewhere nearby to regulate the pressure and temperature of the pipes.
5563ac4e-8587-449f-9983-bc303ddc731e: "Rogue Trader. I... I do not understand. The Omnissiah has granted me a revelation, but I feel no gratitude, only heaviness and pain. Would it be a heresy to beg Him to take back this knowledge? Yes, perhaps it would. It is not Nuncius who needs repairing. No, not him at all..."{n}He looks at the servo-skull as if for the first time.{/n} "I need time to absorb this, to ensure that I am not mistaken and this is not another Commorragh illusion. We will return to this conversation later, but for now, please leave me."
cdf6aa69-6163-4967-804a-03288b603e67: "Your speech differs from that of other Tech-Priests — it is less mechanistic."
69690422-2182-4870-b615-15c79484a813:
The user gains the {g|f:b3617f252b704125bfd60cc4702af97f}Force Couple (Enemy){/g} and the {g|f:cd63f0732da44952a78f7f79e50c737f}Force Couple (Ally){/g} {g|Encyclopedia:Ability}abilities{/g}.
{g|f:b3617f252b704125bfd60cc4702af97f}Force Couple (Enemy){/g} allows the user to attack the target enemy with a melee weapon, if they have one equipped, after the pull. If the user has two weapons equipped, they attack with both of them, but each deals −50% less {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g}.
Using either of those abilities sets both of them on {g|Encyclopedia:Cooldown}cooldown{/g}.
7c9f31b3-f5c3-4c0f-944e-147108aa20af: "Yes, Lord Captain." {n}The Arch-Militant's reply is dry and curt, as one would expect from the military man.{/n} "A squad of the finest soldiers picked from those serving on Zarkov is fully equipped and ready to go."
794f7a30-a753-4657-bd81-9b20c37d3696: "How else am I to speak of it? My condition is a known fact. I have discovered it, assessed its impact, weighed all the variables, and settled on the most rational plan of action. Which is to remove information about it from my memory by means of a ritual of transcranial electroconvulsive self-flagellation that I designed myself. The ritual is dangerous and unreliable, but it is better than the alternative."
da76b7fd-af2a-44c7-89eb-053862bf2f82: "With all due respect to your most holy ancestor, his orders are of little use now. Keep them — after all, it is your legacy."
ba3e97a4-146f-45fc-905b-584818750b0c: The platforms that carried us upward had already drawn level with the gates leading into the galleries of the Museion's lower floors when a terrible blow shook the crypt to its foundation. Another of the flagship's artillery strikes had hit the planet's surface not far from us, and the accursed pyramid proved insufficiently sturdy to withstand it. The last thing I saw was cracks running along its surface, filling with a poisonous green glow. Then an unbearably bright light flooded everything, and I was temporarily blinded.
db6ef6dd-ea12-41fb-9a9e-f01731b29e44: The parts of the creature are immune to toxic damage, telepathic powers (the {g|f:fa305ba4e2aa48f394608d1ccfc6d385}Psychic Shriek{/g} and {g|f:87be0b4299564f34802f8b339bba05fd}Psychic Assault{/g} abilities), multiple control effects (prone, immobilised, {g|Encyclopedia:CantAttackEffect}cannot attack{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:BlindedEffect}blinded{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:CalmedWarpEffect}psy dampening{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:SlowedEffect}slowed{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:StaggeredEffect}staggered{/g}, and {g|Encyclopedia:PerplexedEffect}perplexed{/g}), forced movement effects (for example, the {g|f:17077e37decf427db1a049ea25bb2b66}Notch of Purpose{/g}, {g|f:b0d4e6b6b3534497bb21590826c34d13}Point of Curiosity{/g}, and {g|f:d2a3ffe8100b4e7983ca87b2b76a6cc0}Zone of Fear{/g} abilities), as well as some damage-over-time effects ({g|Encyclopedia:ToxinEffect}toxin{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:BleedingEffect}bleeding{/g}).
e81d770f-4bfe-493c-a283-b26d6dbc7609: {n}Eogunn pinches the bridge of his nose wearily.{/n} "Technically, you are entirely correct, and my colleague does not possess a cardiovascular system. However, you are attributing statements to him that he did not make."
b5c102c3-e59f-43e9-83c9-d02acc564bd7: [Scowl] "This place is nothing but disappointment. So, what do we do now?"
1cbd7cce-d831-4f61-93bd-14976644ccf8: An unknown assailant has attacked the medicae officers in the Sector Bionica, taken a hostage, and is demanding to speak to the Rogue Trader. The Lord Captain must descend to the medicae facility immediately.
bc0eda0e-56b7-4b7d-8217-141496e64026:
This grenade creates a toxic zone until the end of combat.
Each creature within that zone is dealt 50 {g|Encyclopedia:ToxicDamage}toxic damage{/g}.
d2a7b1c4-a853-4aad-9fa0-8175c44b08e9: "Fine. I will put an end to this creature."
b559e174-4ab2-40d5-bdc7-006d274fba4f: The creature gains +80% {g|Encyclopedia:ShockDamage}shock damage{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:EnergyDamage}energy damage{/g} reduction, +3 {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g}, +20% {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g}, +25% damage, +25% {g|Encyclopedia:ArmourPenetration}armour penetration{/g}, +25% {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeReduction}dodge reduction{/g}, and +1 {g|Encyclopedia:MovementPoints}MP{/g}.
74e00cec-9e60-490e-a025-f7f94eae81b4: The research begun on Zarkov was eventually stopped, for the knowledge the world contained carried too many markers of blasphemy.
2056f7ee-3c41-4e09-a6f2-42f9174a759d: "So I had to, you know, leave my lasgun behind."
d4d16461-60c0-452b-9964-96a952bb3f2b: This creature ignores 50% of the target's {g|Encyclopedia:DetailsOfTheCoverGlossary}cover efficiency{/g}.
e2777559-6bfe-449e-8d2b-0b4f5f612006: "For the God-Emperor and House von Valancius! Atonement for the one!"
2636b112-31f9-45b2-a104-896cbde0cb96: "Thank you for brightening my time with conversation. The days here are too much alike..."
1aa5c6eb-1584-4abf-9f22-7aaa7da4d981:
Eogunn's {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g} is increased by +{uip|ArmorBonus|c8eff4d26a3e4411974fbeb4f5494f0f}%, and his {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g} is increased by +{uip|DeflectionBonus|c8eff4d26a3e4411974fbeb4f5494f0f}.
If Eogunn has more than {uip|INT|c8eff4d26a3e4411974fbeb4f5494f0f} stacks of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g} at the start of his turn, these bonuses are doubled for 1 round.
0138aa29-11c5-43f9-8025-46a939f49d61: "That iron mon-keigh and his useless trinket have grown tedious. To prove my mastery, I will piece him back together with meticulous, excruciating attention so that, every second, he understands his existence is a privilege I can revoke. Potentially a far more exquisite pleasure for me than any of his pathetic snivelling."
c22df2de-10cf-4281-abe8-b797ba670893: "The relic is unique and as old as the ship itself. It was damaged during the traitor Voigtvir's mutiny. Despite the Tech-Priests' ceaseless prayers, its machine spirits continue to fade. It may soon be irretrievably lost."
5d3cb026-7a21-45ca-8ff9-136444fb0609: The xenos's body is covered in deep, jagged gashes, as if someone had furiously driven a knife into the metallic foe until it finally died.
e950ba73-7c72-4478-ab5b-4f4642807f18: "I come from House Gaprak. May I speak with you briefly?"
969dc247-a15a-4881-a555-32fcdafc16ea: "...the Archon of the Children of Thorns, one of the leading Kabals of the Nexus. A prominent member of her kind, the Mother of Shadows was behind the complete annihilation of two dozen rival Kabals."
55852d8d-2625-4ca7-beb7-5291cb4f9530: "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, Magos Oksilda has sent you the first results of her efforts. A breakthrough, she says. I needn't have worried when she didn't respond sooner. These scientists, you know, can become so engrossed in their work..."
2e1871be-5250-4233-ae75-adff6c4a4327: "Sacred machines of augmentation are blessed in their own right," {n}grumbles the {g|Encyclopedia:Genetor}Genetor{/g} jealously.{/n} "But this particular set is slightly rarer than others. With proper care, it can greatly increase the speed of a high-quality recipient."
7af898ae-ba38-42f5-bcf8-cde763ca4442: "The Undying One had already whispered his name. And the Domin's hand bestowed upon him the greatest of all honours."
b739e2c4-f795-4f4e-a416-f8aa61e74fe6: {n}You hear the rising roar of aircraft turbines. You hear the whistle of launching rockets. You do not hear the explosion. But you feel it, if only for a fraction of an instant, before your body is ripped to pieces.{/n}
2fa55201-0031-4480-9426-99c87c13b272: [Toss the knife from hand to hand, distracting Batoshski]
be1d4629-160e-4fe6-8264-86be3ca22f5a:
Until the end of the turn, whenever the user performs an attack, all units in a 5-cell radius around the target suffer +3 stacks of {g|f:8f9bac2e470942e4874a50926bff5e73}the cold embrace of the Immaterium{/g}.
The user suffers +1 stack of {g|f:8f4a66a1c9674e2b9016f79cc73f58a6}perplexed{/g} until the end of combat.
299a2eb8-f6bc-46cb-91b6-560eb237769c: "You don't understand. I'm not myself anymore! These cranial shackles constrain me, they break me down. I cannot even pray! This has to stop. This is... slavery to iron." {n}The man's gaze wanders.{/n}
3c534587-225e-4e7b-a5e6-a682209344b7: "A modified Sslyth, one of his Drukhari kin... and a chimera created from a human, the exact construction of which remains a mystery to me for now. When you arrived, I was contemplating methods of recreating multi-component {g|Encyclopedia:Servitor}servitors{/g} of this kind. Tervantias has deprived me of all my usual tools, except the ability to think... and to log my thoughts." {n}Eogunn's pallid face curls into a smile at you.{/n} "I hope the next specimens will be more... inventive. I have managed to awaken in the experimenter a certain semblance of professional jealousy."
5a950fcd-cbdc-4524-9cbf-37512484f9f8: "The name has faded into the aeons of time, lowly one. I was once one who conquered the firmament, a Geomancer of a great dynasty." {n}The stumps of the Necron's upper limbs twitch impotently, as if it were trying to make some gesture.{/n}
10296e51-2279-42fb-9169-72ea673fda40: "May the Omnissiah guide us along the path of true function." {n}The Drivesmaster turns around and issues a short binharic command.{/n}
1f47efdf-160d-46de-865f-619ed38bb239: "How do you manage to manufacture ammunition in such conditions?"
1e8187a9-1712-431a-865a-2cd0f66247b6: "Holy Throne! Forgive and save us... God-Emperor!" {n}Sister Argenta stands frozen in prayer, her hands forming the sign of the Aquila.{/n}
cddd2f9b-0b7a-4dbb-815b-b2ab4519ed64: "Why is this experiment so important to you?"
072d1e63-1569-4a7b-a708-79ea36894d4b: "This augmented life form arrived on board along with the rest of the replenishment from Rykad Minoris and has since dwelled in the ship's cannon bays. One of the servants of the Machine God noticed the incongruity of a high-performance sacred augmetic being worn by such a life form, and he was brought here."
3ee313ef-d1cd-44ce-a6ee-a7dfe7f5e93c: "That is not for you to decide. However..."
5ece3182-9a5c-446f-88d5-5f8e936e2e9a: "When sacred probabilities add up in such a unique way, refusing to accept their guidance results in failure. We on Altar-Templum-Calixis-Ext-17 will pray for your good judgement."
bb219eb9-7f28-4e3b-8804-c53caaa77246: {n}Eogunn lets out a genial chuckle.{/n} "To think that all Tech-Priests speak exclusively like machines is a widespread misconception among the laity. We may strive to draw closer to the Machine God, but we remain part of the human race. How exactly a Tech-Priest achieves this oneness is the choice of each individual ordained in the priesthood of Mars, depending largely on personal characteristics, practical considerations, and interpretations of the tenets of the faith. Of course, many attune their language to Machine Cant or even prefer not to use flesh-speak at all — it is then easier for them to make contact with {g|Encyclopedia:MachineSpirit}machine spirits{/g}."
d01b0055-7f39-4f32-ba41-494f459d8647:
Allies that move through cells occupied by the energy wall gain +{uip|BonusMP|52fe1454f6054f119ddafd9941b3950c} MP (once per round).
Enemies that pass through the energy wall suffer {uip|BonusMP|52fe1454f6054f119ddafd9941b3950c} (up to a maximum of {uip|MaxDamage|52fe1454f6054f119ddafd9941b3950c}) shock damage.
33dda86d-a982-4bd3-bede-c4f7d80ac6ae: {n}Eogunn meets your eye with a haunted expression on his face.{/n}
648be451-002d-4e37-b87a-c1dd210a4b36: "The whisperers... they're screaming. In rage and resentment. As if they're on the warpath. But... they're afraid. They say we're going where death itself dies."
6b333cd0-389c-4a5f-9876-74823d15b2c9: "Wait. You do not have to do this. You can make a different choice."
e3e3035e-a70b-4872-aed0-7995c6ea1ebd: {n}The metal giant, draped in a weighty mantle and holding a sinister-looking staff, turns his head slowly, surveying the palace of Dargonus. You see no hint of emotion in the unmoving mask that rests in place of the {g|Encyclopedia:XenosGlossary}xenos{/g}'s face, but in his unnatural voice, however, there is a clear note of vexation.{/n}
10c2f0f2-4964-4b96-8793-adceb52cf09a: {n}You exchange fire with the small complement of guards, even as reinforcements are already pouring in from the back of the courtyard. Seberian lunges at you with a hoarse shout, trying to shield the Seneschal with his body. But a well-aimed shot pierces his bright blue eye.{/n}
5940cab9-abb1-44cb-9804-6fa1af17f546: Eerie sounds rip through the vox. The officers on the bridge fall silent as they listen to the whimpering moans. The unknown person does not respond to human speech.
a7eb897a-c252-49c7-a444-531b2fe6e16c: "The Gaprak was murdered by Captain Sauerback."
90b90a35-bbb1-4740-ad5f-4bf439ea1aac: {n}Tervantias's lips curl maliciously.{/n} "Until that specimen acknowledges my superiority by offering an apology and admitting his own insignificance, he will remain here. He dares to call me an 'esteemed colleague,' as if imagining we are equals! Such naivety is truly revolting."
f728957d-1805-4b08-a979-e0b415c74036: "Thank you. I have realised my purpose. I will not flee again until I see this through to the end. That is my vow."
c1d45eaf-3f13-44ef-9b6e-db6ef0901070: {n}Several indignant bursts of binharic chatter precede Pasqal's response.{/n} "Directive acknowledged, Lord Captain."
c35ebaad-b953-4da0-a113-39d8fbe34d5e: {n}Izdrig only gives a bitter smile.{/n} "Because we aren't fools eager for an early grave, and we aren't the Brother-Generals who drive their men to the slaughter. We endure. We survive. Just as we have for centuries."
c16c8058-3a57-4bb8-8cbc-57138a9fa3e2: Pasqal gains the {g|f:0b8058ef3f33406faebea94ee7967d66}Machine Spirit Banishment{/g} ability.
d75aff85-029f-4d2e-a831-ca0a899e0eb2: "My dream was to reach parts of the void yet unexplored by humanity, to know their terror — and their grandeur." {n}He pauses for a moment, fascinated by the image in his mind's eye.{/n} "But it is only a dream, one that may never be fulfilled."
07fc8349-fac1-4a11-bce7-cdfc6b305070: Grisly-looking implants gleam coldly in the Haemonculus's chest.
aca42764-6345-40b7-8c55-93d84416b125: "What exactly do you want to know?"
efcd1732-e807-453d-9c4b-fe5e881aab7a: "17-401, have you been subject to any modifications?"
9c27d47f-f999-49e9-947b-8f2bea5d57bb: "Hmm... In the archives, this world is flagged as... 'of no further value.' What manner of classification is that? So long as a planet retains an atmosphere and at least a scrap of resources, such a write-off is a crime against the dynasty!"
2e3a82ff-1dee-467e-8c9f-f1337980d159: "Consider this my thanks to you for your honest service."
ad991ff3-2f6e-4e85-8d57-e15cb7ac4b2d: Drill Sergeant Jovakha died in the field hospital during the Drover's siege.
d774324a-f161-4d46-a582-f4760dfcfdcd: "We are making progress, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. We are inviting new people, forging connections. We caught a pair of newly arrived scribes thieving, and granted one of them the chance for final atonement." {n}Vedka's gaze drifts meaningfully toward the leaves with the bodies within.{/n} "We labour for the glory of the dynasty and the God-Emperor."
22e201be-e5c6-4a7f-aaa0-e0bd294cabd7: "Nocturne of Oblivion, a Solitaire from a group of Aeldari belonging to the Harlequin subspecies. Solitaires are extremely rare, but I managed to capture this piece after multiple attempts."
1f0bfe71-bfb3-4445-8bb9-8eaf0000c74a: "Funny, you failed to ask yourself that question when you condemned Nuncius to torment."
d54d214a-162c-4c70-a3b3-bba64bb0fd89: "You see, there is neither need nor use in repentance, for our faith grants no redemption. But by the grace of the {g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}God-Emperor{/g}, Seberian will furnish the {g|Encyclopedia:KoronusExpanseGlossary}Koronus Expanse{/g} with one final gift. It will serve as a reminder that the more one earns of His blessings, the heavier the burden of responsibility becomes. And for those whose backbone of faith should break under that burden, there is no other way but cleansing with holy fire." {n}The Inquisitor's voice sounds muted and measured, lending weight to every word.{/n}
8c9ab9bf-6f9c-461d-bfbe-0116e71a812c: Before embarking on his sombre voyage, Eogunn remained by his patron's side for a long time, sharing with {mf|him|her} all the invaluable nuggets of knowledge he had accumulated over the years among the xenarites. It was the least he could do for the newly minted ruler, in return for the invaluable compassion and understanding the Rogue Trader had granted him.
4a648970-87a0-4075-b154-03bf59978289: The Warrant of Trade has been returned to its protected chamber and re-consecrated with the blood of {name} von Valancius, its current rightful bearer.
5877fc54-9f9a-484a-be91-40c3a0a8fb67: "My greatest transgression is that I didn't destroy you immediately, you wretch. There is no secret valuable enough to justify your existence. Of that, I am now certain."
daa08fdf-8d88-483e-a9b4-4f479b5cc308: {n}The glyphs come to life, quickly changing sequence. A low rumble comes from somewhere in the depths of the machine, and then the components of the monstrous mechanism heavily split apart, revealing the power circuit to your eyes.{/n}
2a27436e-c230-4633-b6f1-8fee59ff001a: At the beginning of combat, the wearer gets the {g|f:b6e3c9398ea94c75afdbf61633ce2f85}Medikit{/g} and {g|f:aa74639ee0f34ac7bd5f6f797631bffe}Onslaught{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:Ability}abilities{/g}, which reproduce the effect of the medikit and onslaught consumables, costing 0 {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}AP{/g}. Each of these abilities can only be used once per combat.
80502420-71c5-4198-b86f-ccfd6e62fb15: {n}Kantus retreats a little to the side. The cables in his head jangle with the movement, and you notice that some of them have melted.{/n} "We are members of a noble clan that has spent years overseeing the sacred vox systems of this voidship. It is a job that requires frequent maintenance, for many machine sparks must pass through our augments. Recently, the frequency of their augment inspections has decreased as access to the Sector Bionica has become more fraught. Instead of esteemed officers, the local medicae officers prefer to cater to crippled vagrants..."
29a479c6-2d9b-42bc-b797-39bfbd1024c3: "Did I misspeak? Or do you only acknowledge an order after a Trial Match here?"
5982709c-7ce4-4ed5-baa6-7c607cd7505c: {n}The skin that borders his augments tightens, and you realise that Seberian is frowning.{/n} "Who are you?"
dd5fb835-c7c7-4f41-a66f-076f99a63a24:
During movement in combat, the user creates an oil-covered area in all cells they move through. This area lasts for 2 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}rounds{/g}.
When an enemy enters it, they must pass an {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerAgility}Agility{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:ResistanceTest}resistance test{/g} with a +{uip|DC|2b385735a44746e0b2fbaa2b68ef0b49} {g|Encyclopedia:Bonus}bonus{/g} (minimum 0). On a failed {g|Encyclopedia:Test}test{/g}, that enemy falls {g|Encyclopedia:ProneEffect}prone{/g}. On a successful test, the user becomes that enemy's {g|Encyclopedia:PriorityTarget}priority target{/g}. Then the oil is removed from the cell.
ab7f8f84-c104-4bad-91f8-3fce50b53b87: "According to the Astra Militarum Handbook, your defiance of a superior officer qualifies as treason. Are you quite certain you wish to be counted a traitor?"
49ed9531-60a6-4603-931d-42fbb8bea551: {n}You notice some among the Guardsmen cringing in pain, as if from a headache, or twitching. These people look deranged, ready to snap into violence or dance in delirious joy at any moment.{/n}
7cf7a7ab-51e0-4281-807b-1d22e7354f0b: "Toiling for the glory of the Emperor is the duty of every person. Not everyone knows how to bear this burden without complaint. He bore it well, and thus he is righteous."
186e05b9-5c83-437e-a3db-3e1e72220bab: "I support this action, Proctor. Perhaps we should discuss later how we might increase security measures on board — each within his own professional remit."
163a2a56-ed6f-4f14-b1a6-c555b90d11e9: {n}The Rogue Trader stills, astounded by your insolence.{/n} "I allowed you to speak at the council, and this is what you have to say? Stop talking nonsense."
842e92a8-29f7-45ad-bc0d-41541d5ba1e6: Seberian von Valancius, having learned of Trazyn's demand, has agreed to the xenos's proposal. His loyalty lies with his dynasty, and without the Warrant, House von Valancius simply cannot exist.
3bbf2eca-ac0d-45f7-95a9-8dfdb9eb7f5f: "Cut the crap, there's no way {mf|he|she} could've done that."
b2620346-5ef3-4b8b-b351-7976a56147f8: "We need to tend to the wounded."
47a0012b-22c3-4831-a0c6-38b66da21889: "Protocol 12.70 dash 4!" {n}The Ruststalkers instantly shift their visor beams to focus on something over your shoulder.{/n} "Join forces with the {g|Encyclopedia:RogueTraderGlossary}Rogue Trader{/g}! Defend the perimeter until I have dealt with Object 16!"
769da03e-8be4-4bf4-8ff3-473606c91756: "Nuncius, shed some light on these experiments, would you?"
d533c351-b4aa-4520-8b5e-7193045e20f6: "Unfortunately not, Lord Captain. The tombs are buried beneath many layers of Zarkov's root system, forming a solid shield. It will take us months at least to break through it — if that's even possible. Even if we were to succeed, the consequences for Zarkov itself remain unpredictable due to the complex system of intertwined roots. Our auspexes predict a massive collapse of the root structure."
ddac27f6-203b-4995-9bcb-2088cc05a6db: [View the traitor Astartes in black armour]
7970948b-405f-4b2d-b88a-80596adaff05: "People are not machines. You tried to break people down into mechanical components — your original hypothesis was flawed."
946e9ae3-88bf-45d7-b5f9-9a8b201cbae4: Cannot be unequipped. Replaceable only with another augment.
505352af-d025-44a8-8cad-21374c98b505: "The esteemed Tech-Priests could offer their assistance without any terms." {n}You detect the wounded tone beneath Abelard's grumbling.{/n}
ecc34bac-e3d7-4c9f-a97d-18463b7e6f93: {n}The analyser pierces your hand, wriggling repulsively beneath your skin. A split second later, the colour of the genetic guardian's vid-screen changes to a welcoming green.{/n} "Identification passed. Welcome, member of the von Valancius dynasty." {n}A tremor passes through the wall, accompanied by the deep groan of ancient locks releasing.{/n}
4f1891c6-4687-4051-9357-ae0576910adb: "...I recovered this exhibit from one of the most inaccessible regions of this sector. Only a molecular stasis field allowed me to stabilise the remains of the ancient craftworld Lu'Nasad..."
1eecafc1-8a3b-47fb-a1a8-97c7534eb7cf: "My only son, he's serving in a void unit. I was the one who told him to go there... my poor Gnotec," {n}rasps the woman from behind the medicae officers.{/n}
defcb0da-13f0-4eb0-a0c8-865dd8efaf45: Laria is fanatically devoted to her father. It will take something truly significant to make her support negotiating with the Inquisition.
c76354f9-7915-4010-a5f7-8d7c8835d6d0: "I will relay your order immediately, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship} — and I will begin allocating the necessary resources for this plan."
0ce71949-23dc-4787-8581-94f5e029f95f: "Under any other circumstances, I would be far more prudent in my approach. But you have many loops ahead of you, and if we make a mistake, you will be able to fix it. It is decided, then. I shall order the arrest of Isimond Sauerback."
88097802-ffaa-4e81-8318-b10bc6826a6f: "That explains both Decabalus's secrecy and his vague answers, Rogue Trader. After what the Skitarii commander said, I'm beginning to think this was a well-planned deception that was in danger of being exposed — so the Artisan thought it best to cast the accusation of heresy against the person prying into his secrets. It's an age-old ploy."
c385a999-5a7d-429d-a580-d736a1f79684: [Take the colonel's swiping nick, relying on the immunity granted by the rot extract]
9e0e947b-92fb-419d-b274-353eb400eef0: "If only you knew how little I care about all this. All I require is a Genetor in my retinue who still possesses his full mental faculties. And since you cannot handle your own ailing mind, I will take you to Amsanct by force and see it done."
036c099c-2705-446f-aae5-a0fb2ff214ca: {n}Eogunn flares with indignation, poised to launch into an impassioned tirade... but abruptly catches himself, instead gazing dejectedly at the servo-skull.{/n} "Very well, Rogue Trader. I will submit to your decision. You are right, Nuncius. Scaling unproven solutions could lead to unfortunate consequences."
8d2d113b-dbd8-43ec-a7dd-d5d50b3ef3d5: "Lord Captain, we've got the beast cornered! Give the order, and we'll capture it!"
687d4e26-5d39-4f75-a9f2-0aec13fdb50d: "It's down to chain of command, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. And discipline."
40f0a94b-5755-42ab-940d-70167d9bbf51: "You didn't trap anyone — you shot yourself in the foot. Only an idiot would think they could outmatch a Rogue Trader and a {g|Encyclopedia:ProctorGlossary}Proctor{/g} together."
01697eb1-6c83-43d0-acce-4a80e1d6ceda: Explore the cluster of systems around the Tear of Solar
87811cb2-0f30-436d-aaa3-c03ac1babda4: "What nonsense." {n}Despite his words, Seberian still hesitates.{/n} "Juvenna has indeed been acting strangely these past few days... I am certain it's all a big misunderstanding. Seneschal Voigtvir will join us so that she may immediately arrest you for slander once we have all seen you proven wrong."
402a5a9d-3998-49aa-ba62-c776a2bcb188: "Father will be pleased to hear there is one fewer rat in his retinue." {n}Before you can do anything, Laria whips out her laspistol and shoots you in the head.{/n}
0f44686e-2f53-4b51-b92e-ab8e77f9a07f: "Ordinary crew members have seldom been furnished with prosthetics before! A saw and a prayer to the God-Emperor — those were our two main treatments. But things have changed under your noble rule, and the people of the Sector Bionica agree with us!"
9688959c-476c-467a-a574-f021dff8b30e: "I don't know what you're talking about, my {mf|lord|lady}. You should ask someone with quicker wits — I'm not used to such talk."
45434bfa-f734-41b0-9bf7-7e2c7dfbb388: You appointed Vedka Dunawski to be your Arch-Militant.
71de657b-91b4-43d3-8a9d-cfe2f87ca89c: "The colonel said that you know something about the xenos ruins deep in the roots."
fd76cf4f-f8c8-4081-b4d8-2dfd7abef7a0: "They dressed me... in another's flesh. But, but, but..." {n}The old man starts babbling and drooling, breaking his own fingers one after another without even noticing. Then he throws up his hands and screams,{/n} "My spirit's struggling! I need to set it free... into His grace!"
807b582e-288d-44ff-a1ac-1c63e46fb063: "Then tell me what really happened on Amsanct."
aa057740-261d-45be-a706-9e03ae3616ad: "Huge creatures of some kind once inhabited this world, but they all died out. The roots shot up after that. We often find a skull right in the middle of a root, or a vertebra the size of three men. It's clear as day, the roots had grown right through them."
84bba75c-dbad-4840-ab19-afe55d271108: "Whatever you may think of me, I won't send someone to be butchered just for a laugh."
56d750ed-07e5-4e4f-ba3d-e2f31d397216: "Their coming is such an honour, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}! {g|Encyclopedia:AugmentationGlossary}Augmentations{/g} saved the {g|Encyclopedia:MerovPenalLegionGlossary}Merovians{/g} once before, revealing the might of the Adeptus Mechanicus and the gifts of the Omnissiah. Allow the Tech-Priests to demonstrate their power again."
195fdb71-9b27-422c-b294-e4e50b55933c: "The voidsman who was refused an implant — he is your son?"
2d22af39-1ecd-4cb2-99ca-e02c9f639c7f: Whenever a character attacks or uses an item or ability, Trazyn gains a stack of this effect. At {uip|Stacks|1542914a27bf4962805195e1b0e8eaef} stacks, he removes all stacks and gains an {g|Encyclopedia:ExtraTurn}extra turn{/g}.
b90c3b96-5e71-4b34-91ac-ad5030f0058c: "If you come into possession of any augments that are worthy of your attention, we will always be here to install them in accordance with all procedures."
b22f37ea-9193-4b03-ae4b-d2b96ff49a4f: {n}Izdrig grinds his teeth with a sound that sends shivers down your spine. He keeps his heavy gaze fixed on your face, yet he addresses Dunawski.{/n} "Captain-Quartermaster Vedka Dunawski, we will speak of this after the Trial Match, in private."
f4fa70d3-77fe-4c1b-8936-ebdf45fd2061: "Thank you, Proctor. I will alert the High Factotum and oversee the initiation of the Merovians into the ship's forces. The planetary infantry... will have to be thoroughly trained."
14bd3132-278c-46b9-8451-ebfa2656af29: "Thank you for the generous gift. I must return to my flagship."
59fa7b67-dd8c-4f76-a724-637fe2d2fd5e: {n}Vivisector Martinax's pupils dilate. He takes a wary step forward and freezes.{/n} "Incredible! Generations of chirurgeons have toiled away here and were utterly unaware..."
af369fac-cd67-46b6-8c49-806f16941707: "If that is a veiled invitation for me to lie on a dissecting table and let you delve around in my innards, it was a decent attempt, but try again."
6a181eae-a89e-45c1-a87c-4cdd4637a9f0: When this creature suffers lethal damage, it inflicts +1 stack of {g|f:79bde8332e774c37a3dd12a67d2782e3}avenge me!{/g} on other Veterans.
28582b02-cda2-4b98-a59a-8238b86cb24d: "A good question, Interrogator. I am sure you are aware that an attempt to desecrate a Rogue Trader's tomb is a grievous affront to the von Valancius dynasty."
76f18c05-e017-412d-b8eb-f8ad567d9a2e: "No, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. The Colonel-Governor stated that there is no mention of such a phenomenon in the annals of the {g|Encyclopedia:MerovPenalLegionGlossary}32nd Merov Regiment{/g}. This is the Zarkov colony's first experience of this."
8d9fae10-385f-4c5f-87d3-dba3afbea7b2: Order the squad to return to the flagship.
00174385-0c5c-4d6b-b6db-beb749512756: [Pray silently to the powers beyond the material world and drink the contents of the cup]
dfb6c0fb-aa1d-467e-a51b-2b0076d51230: Once the {g|f:b9f9ef145708451f81283a73c16c527d}braced{/g} effect is applied, each affected ally immediately gains an {g|Encyclopedia:ExtraTurn}extra turn{/g} with 0 {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}AP{/g} and {unit_stat|WarhammerToughness|bonus} {g|Encyclopedia:MovementPoints}MP{/g} and has their {g|Encyclopedia:DetailsOfTheCoverGlossary}cover{/g} efficiency increased by +30% for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
45bb8197-251e-4dfb-a4b2-1ce0584cbcf7: "I left my post down in the roots to clear the landing pad. It's good that you're here. Now the creatures are as good as dead!"
d93c0c3a-c142-4715-beb5-9a06576ada2f: {n}The Inquisitor draws out a scroll bound with ribbon. The dark wax seal yields easily, and af Irramon unrolls the parchment. Line by line, he reads out the sentence — steadily, with neither wrath nor triumph. He lists names, titles, and testimonies, and cites ancient decrees. Until he arrives at the final lines:{/n} "...let this impious heretic, who bowed down before the xenos abomination, be consigned to the flames. The sentence is binding. The seal is set. For the glory of the God-Emperor."
896dcbfd-8dfe-43ea-be15-a4c26d06d40b: "My will bears the sanction of the highest order, for the configuration of its source demonstrates maximum efficiency and is blessed by the Omnissiah." {n}The Tech-Priest's last word is accompanied by a quiet but solemn binharic trill.{/n}
9ef6ea5a-2f4d-424d-9dab-25f251d7eacd: "The voices... The voices say the withered dead know no peace. They cannot sleep in their tombs. They suffer, these lost souls... Whatever that means, {name}."
be9ac0ab-40c4-4948-a00c-cf3a985d8285: {n}You look at the planet through the bridge observation screen... and suddenly you are struck with terror. An unbearable, gut-wrenching sense of emptiness inflicts {d|Encyclopedia:CharGen_Psyker}agony on your mind{/d}. The planet is the epicentre of this emptiness, and it is sucking the energy of life itself out of everything around it.{/n}
07451a15-2196-49e2-849b-506ec6de4475: "Omnissiah's blessings! Let us rejoice, for the probability in which the archeotech was lost has passed! The outcome is recognised as most favourable. Transmitting a proposal for cooperation pact TR-2, in accordance with which possession of the sacred archeotech will pass to Altar-Templum-Calixis-Ext-17."
fd90cd12-02cd-414a-b886-4a66d73f6141: {n}The Rogue Trader's gaze stops just above your shoulder. When he addresses you, the voice from the vox-grille screeches more violently than before, unable to convey the outrage directed at you.{/n} "You brought a xenos to my palace! Let me remind you: what is permitted for a Rogue Trader is not for his vassals."
91f9afaf-65dd-4d31-bc12-79501455902e: "Those are the words of a true commander, Lord Captain. Not some Commissar claptrap, but genuine care for the soldiers. Right out of the Handbook. I'll be glad to tell the troops!"
8c92fe68-cd91-4827-944f-b871e071717b: "These iron creatures came out of thin air. They attack in waves, giving us hardly any break. It's a good thing we managed to get a message out in time. These things are not made of flesh and know no fear, and they're closing in on us on all fronts. So, your arrival is a blessing from the God-Emperor Himself."
cf528e2f-5332-401e-9731-69db4d18f880: If an ally is {g|Encyclopedia:HitSequenceGlossary}hit{/g} by an arc rifle, {g|f:2c4d692f28fe47e3a65f45a914c5189b}Magnarail Blast{/g}, or {g|f:09c4605a50c74262aec197ec9f9a9d58}Transonic Wave{/g}, then at the start of that ally's next {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, they gain +1 {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}AP{/g}.
eff25560-b57d-48f1-a797-00860557a1fb: {n}Trazyn glances around at the crumbling Museion, then shifts his attention to you.{/n} "There is no point in continuing this battle. This part of my archive is past saving. I am just wasting my time on a stubborn pest who is intent on protecting a worthless exhibit."
650e3cc5-6617-4c85-b481-7f9678b2d767: "I will readily admit that the lack of speech in servitors is more of a disadvantage than a plus. However, if the Rogue Trader so bids, I can certainly leave her vocal cords intact or even make some improvements to them during the servitorisation process."
5ac42dcd-6fbf-459b-b188-ff439d543df6: "I want it! Nuncius... will thank me later."
bd30267e-ccfb-4dbc-82c6-b6ff4ee9a5c6: "Why are you lingering here instead of fighting?"
fa9c544a-0259-4388-8ac4-3dc6a7b598ef: {n}Following your orders, the team tries to activate the prism, after which the vox-connection is jammed and interrupted. A few hours later, they return aboard, bringing with them a detailed {g|Encyclopedia:Pict}pict{/g}-recording of what happened.{/n}
e4e47880-8715-435d-a9e3-38c3dd32065b: {n}The Vivisector breathes a sigh.{/n} "I thank the God-Emperor every day for sending us such a merciful ruler." {n}There is no escaping the satisfied look the Vivisector gives the Genetor.{/n}
e6508389-c7e5-4a4c-a491-6b229e37a9c1: "I have no issue with the arrangements. Let's get on with it."
fb77231b-7e0a-4614-9b77-92e3b7861d27: "Yes, it is. Monsters are born of hope and fear. I was driven by both."
bf7709d2-8621-4983-b60d-fb7d25f96d8a: Increased {g|Encyclopedia:ShockDamage}shock damage{/g} dealt to the target due to the {g|f:780497b04a0944e59cb57e68bb9775c4}ferrum sanctum{/g} effect is reduced by −5% per stack until the end of combat.
bad81c1f-ab6b-4c2f-a434-5f650f2488c0: "I find it concerning that the Guardsmen of Zarkov have spent so long without the guiding hand of the law. Perhaps dispatching a garrison of enforcers to the planet is worth considering."
fe82ae04-790b-4ec0-8ae9-b16f1591f3e9: "I could have destroyed you, but my offer of an alliance still stands."
4641c96c-27d7-43a3-8e43-961b9d63444c: Encouraged the Arch-Militant to use the Ruinous Powers as a weapon.
7e834b13-b04e-4881-814f-99e38587382b: Deep longing and uncontrollable hatred seem to emanate from the mysterious milky-white shard.
18ca034b-b505-434f-8c7b-466e9e101185: "The operational stability of the resulting modified servitors cannot be prognosticated. The ship's machine spirits may find themselves defenceless and fall victim to entropy, which would begin to multiply within the computational cycle."
62b2151a-f6eb-4779-9337-d82d254d9bf3: "You are not lost, on the contrary — you have set foot on a new path. You were ready to die, but the God-Emperor said you must live. So live and lead the faithful!"
cfbe4c4d-c5fa-4edb-8615-5d8b4e7b855a: {n}Ferrenz lets out a shocked exclamation in Lingua-Technis, which shifts to a sharp binharic screech. Then, embarrassed by his outburst, the Tech-Priest bashfully steps aside, respectfully making way for you.{/n}
cd05a073-5709-4d95-a3ac-ea9a6d26d531: Helped Nuncius see the light of the God-Emperor before his death.
a97b4668-26a7-4ca9-a856-0b8f7fb71faf: Only a loyal servant of the God-Emperor could endure the searing heat emanating from this augmetic.
20d32f2f-6ca6-4397-8367-a92fbc58a07a: [Contact the Master-at-Arms] "Summon all enforcers! A trophy has been stolen from my study!"
b5182c7b-5f94-43bc-bb5c-daa7338eba56: "Your utterances are long-winded, convoluted, and overblown, Genetor. They sow the poison of freethinking in malleable minds and hearts, and infect thoughts with corruption. Accept your penance and humble your heart in repentance."
15634a43-bf7a-41ad-8ef2-48a13ae754d0: "What is a joke, but a deliberate distortion of meaning for comic effect? Recall our disputations, Lady Navigator. The motley of hues is merely a fiction intended to divert your attention away from the truth."
381a54d8-e3ee-42a1-97aa-cc8eac2bde2f: "It is rather curious that your cyber-mastiff's model does not entirely conform to classic templates and contains a high percentage of organics. In your opinion, Proctor, is your canid the locus of a machine spirit, or does it remain a living autonomous organism?"
ae608b28-a732-4eaf-a73c-f1ae4ad10114: {n}Maintaining his phlegmatic composure, Eogunn scribes a note onto his data-slate.{/n} "It is not impossible that my hypothesis concerning the transmission of pathologies through the ingestion of grey matter may prove to be correct. Changes in behavioural patterns are self-evident, indicative of organic degrada—" {n}He instantly switches to Lingua-Technis upon meeting Incendia's steel gaze.{/n}
5f171068-56f0-4915-aaa8-6f95dcb0cdda: When the user suffers an {g|Encyclopedia:Injury}injury{/g}, they also become {g|Encyclopedia:Stun}stunned{/g} for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
b027c319-dcb1-4e56-a746-00278a76136f: {n}With a heavy sigh, Eogunn lowers his hands holding the servo-skull and looks you in the eye.{/n} "Rogue Trader, you know that I have an extremely high opinion of your intellectual abilities and your personal qualities. But you are, nevertheless, a layperson. You have not comprehended the mysteries of the Machine God. You have not studied the esoteric mysteries of communication with {g|Encyclopedia:MachineSpirit}machine spirits{/g}. You are not versed in the principles of sacred engineering and hermetic augmentation protocols. Nuncius is a one-of-a-kind construct; he cannot be understood simply by looking at—"
aaf01641-5cc0-41f7-a8a7-56cf45b64702: "I will not dishonour the glorious name of the von Valancius dynasty. I swear it."
cab5c64f-8c0c-410e-82c8-d016d809670c: "So many von Valancius on one voidship... There haven't been this many on board since before Theodora's death. I hope the numbers don't drop so dramatically this time."
42d4c750-f03d-4865-a82d-a444e44d435d: At the cost of many lives, the {g|Encyclopedia:XenosGlossary}xenos{/g}'s defences were breached. Once the artillery finished, several shuttles broke off from the flagship and headed toward the planet's blast-cratered surface. The small, manoeuvrable craft were perfectly suited to my heir's plan to stealthily penetrate the heart of the ancient tomb and take that which was {mf|his|her} birthright.
c67df11b-e0c6-4b0b-a689-25b6f1c2a613: {n}Laria's voice grows weaker and quieter with each passing second.{/n} "I dreamed for so long of escaping the clutches of that filth. Five centuries... of captivity. And now, at last, that moment has come." {n}Her voice drops to a barely audible whisper.{/n} "I am journeying to the cold stars, Father. Together with you." {n}The last spark of life leaves the wounded woman's eyes. In complete silence, Seberian reaches out his hand and closes the eyelids on his daughter's still face.{/n}
b8dcc3d8-5262-4d43-8a6a-fb491a1c6c34: "Why don't you just give me a new one without all this yelling."
9387c467-5abd-4346-8299-cfaeb8a3af9d: "I quite agree, Nuncius. A room of such imposing scale could have been utilised to far greater effect. It is unlikely that the man who now rules here is familiar with the fundamentals of the sacred teachings of ergonomics."
86e56339-fd76-459e-be9b-72e743a4e709: Sacred machine texts slide rapidly across the vid-screen of this cogitator.
2cd86503-6678-43e8-a013-3464c6b4c30e: "Living with the weight of guilt is a punishment far worse than death, this is an incontrovertible fact. But can a xenos even comprehend its guilt toward humans? I cannot give a definitive answer to this question. Only through experimental methods can the truth be uncovered. Spare her life, my friend."
8da99b54-95d0-4575-a663-2dd9688bc0c4: "I'm looking for Eogunn, a Genetor from my retinue."
0a0dd126-c58a-438e-a5b7-b4d89cf4ed33: "I can see you are in pain. Lower your weapon. I will instruct that you be given the best painkillers available."
fe1602f6-f819-4898-8825-cf76dafd63f9: "You mentioned that you have knowledge of xenos."
3f2b992f-7a90-49dd-a456-b08af563c80b: {n}Seberian lets out a bitter chuckle, the sound like the clanking of a broken mechanism.{/n} "Indeed. My time has passed, never to return."
6fce6439-c4a1-411a-b071-f48473ded7cf: "One of your people is relaying information to the Inquisition via vox."
2c5fa0bc-4b18-4a72-a80b-42f96f637b19: "Not to worry, my {mf|lord|lady}. I've known the captain a long time. You could sneak a melta bomb right under his nose and he'd be none the wiser." {n}Jarub waves off your warning with feigned insouciance.{/n}
3be1c975-f365-4ef9-966f-e7f6c78ebe04: "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, you're here! I knew you wouldn't abandon us! Those iron creatures have been attacking us all over the planet. No one knows where they came from — or we haven't been able to find out in all the confusion, anyway."
b52906a3-3110-4545-9b69-34a6949ba8fa: "What bodies was Tervantias connecting you to?"
ce6de634-e4f4-4cbc-887e-c05461da85d7: {n}Dunawski moves so silently, you would not know that she spends her days in camp dealing with quartermaster business, rather than commanding scouting parties in the field. You glance around again, preparing for the final push.{/n}
68017e50-7297-427e-9fcf-576e5c63355a: "The whispers beyond the door are rolling with laughter right now, the little blighters..." {n}Idira says, barely concealing a crooked, but unmalicious grin herself.{/n}
cc782bae-10a7-45d0-8c10-dea783efe228: {n}The Tech-Priest raises her other, still intact augmented hand and touches its claws to her chest.{/n} "The weak flesh within me has been replaced with a consecrated servo-heart node. It... beats very loudly. I am forced to shout over it."
c06a1aa8-dd9a-464c-bd04-2c10858b219f: {n}Abelard claps his hands together in a business-like manner, and there's an ominous glint in his unaugmented eye.{/n} "Lord Captain, what a fortunate rarity to find you at leisure! Since you have nothing else to occupy your time, I will give you a lecture on the history of House von Valancius. I would not be doing my duty if I did not start from the beginning... "
6d0e8f1f-bc2b-4f53-9835-515b3635c316: "Nuncius, I wish to speak to you."
51558414-6e70-4fac-b947-7a0f86eba5b7: The Chronomancer grants an {g|Encyclopedia:ExtraTurn}extra turn{/g} to two other Necrons.
58db40b9-c138-4018-97bb-794eb26a31cf: "They're not dangerous in themselves, but the Drover uses them to block a path. They're thick and dense. Watch yourself when you're smashing them — they're bristling with thorns. Strike it the wrong way and you're sure to get skewered. And you have to hurry, any cuts in it heal quickly." {n}Izdrig holds out his palm, displaying a scar from a wound that went right through. Clearly the work of one of those thorns.{/n}
cbb989af-94c2-409d-bd08-0e0a2442b6e6: {n}Eogunn frowns, closes his organic eye, and sits in deep thought for a while.{/n} "I... have reassessed the situation. I am plagued by doubts, and I am not entirely sure I am doing the right thing. But I will trust you. This is my final decision."
b70cf8d3-028a-4366-be7a-ba820d8a9bfa: {n}Iron skeletons do not know fatigue, and the usual tactics for wearing down one's opponent failed. Many Zarkovians ended their attacks pierced by xenos claws or stretched over metal ribs. The Merov Regiment paid a heavy, bloody price for victory.{/n}
2e8fb704-35fe-422e-a414-2b40cec61cb8: Something downed the Lord Captain's shuttle. The vessel crash-landed near a human settlement detected by the augurs. It may be possible to find some means of contacting the flagship there.
db614cea-2474-4160-8ee8-85696d8686a2: {n}Pasqal's visor flashes scarlet, and a stricken binharic screech erupts from his vox. The Tech-Priest does not dignify the Manipulus with a reply.{/n}
0a53331c-378f-4fca-a2f9-b70cbbc4809a: <u>(number of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g} stacks on Eogunn + number of {g|f:780497b04a0944e59cb57e68bb9775c4}ferrum sanctum{/g} stacks on the target)</u>
8e42f58b-52a6-4373-82fb-99f6820757de: "It's not supposed to be... like this..."
3a9667f8-a527-4953-b28c-f1497cfe2406: "This is only a small part of the procession, Lord Captain. The Master-at-Arms personally selected and scrutinised the most stable and trustworthy. To allow the entire march from the other decks onto the bridge would be impossible — or at least highly unsafe."
398d36ef-853d-452b-a57a-885c49af130d: {n}Despite your best efforts, you fail to avoid the spikes. You can feel them finding gaps in your armour and hungrily digging into your flesh. The puncture wounds immediately start to burn — the plant seems to be poisonous.{/n}
c7d4a250-a0ea-464b-84d5-d2e9e8c83d1a: "Eogunn, now that you've seen the real me, I suppose you won't want to have any more of our highbrow chats, will you?"
cd77f99c-4dc7-4048-81db-06d1f31e8216: "A preposterous notion! My cybernetic companion is merely an auxiliary tool. It is far less sophisticated than Nuncius, and lacks his outstanding communication skills and sharp intellect."
8570b94d-d474-4082-b0d6-24c261ffbcef: "You are surprisingly imperious for a common vassal. Do not abuse my goodwill, I have lost enough people today."
2653ebc3-5309-4549-98c8-ee1a6febe337: {n}Your ancestor sighs — and you can hear the distinct relief in his voice.{/n} "I'm... pleased. Pleased to return to oblivion. I hope the memory of your sorrowful age will be mercifully erased from my mind. Farewell, my—"
ded69672-8c11-4f49-8952-7fabf72fd5cd: {n}Nuncius struggles to say something, but Eogunn does not give him the chance. The mechadendrites dig into his flesh, extracting and replacing organs one by one — Eogunn rips something right out of his own body, quickly replacing it with implants or strange objects of apparent xenos origin. One of the mechadendrites continuously emits the Motive Force, targeting organs and augments, compelling them to function against all odds. Eogunn does not make a sound during the entire operation.{/n}
2b359e65-f610-42cc-8edf-818c0ef294ae: "You and I have clashed more than once over such prohibitions, and now you have become their embodiment. How bizarre are the transformations wrought upon the minds of the ordained in the crucible of fate. I do not know where this path will lead you, but I hope that what you gain from it will be worth the sacrifice."
d1b4266b-ecc8-4b78-b075-cc3109612c61: "I know beggars can't be choosers, but the quality of the average soldier is depressing. Have you met the riffraff from Merov yet?"
595534b6-aee7-48f6-a9b4-b2644ef269f0: "Eogunn, you are going to repair Nuncius... with Nuncius?"
7bf11729-9d70-4817-8f49-f5a0fe569961: "Curiously enough, you and I are at odds on the difference between the particular and the general. I considered the meaning of life as a global concept within the framework of society, while you took the individual as your starting point. But how these two concepts marry up is a topic for a separate discussion."
51a509fd-a0a7-40b3-aa35-fc653cee19cf: {n}The moment your name is spoken, a change ripples through the crowd. Some are struck with awe, others turn white as ghosts. The man in front of you makes the sign of the Aquila.{/n}
3f0edef3-8a72-41e9-921a-f56aa9d0a646: "For thus spake the Machine God in the all-comprehending binharic: 'The distortion of My design is heresy.' Before the Omnissiah's gaze, I declare this verdict righteous, and I echo it a hundredfold."
812a4d53-d1ac-4e8f-adec-6f22c38d170e: "All these people are like rust eating away at the bowels of your ship. They must be scraped off, all of them, so they do not spread their iron shackles further. I will help you do this, Lord Captain."
c1b16f87-98b1-47f8-8798-b61b5c68a1b1: {n}The Astartes's already sharp features harden further, making him resemble a carved statue.{/n} "Because my duty is to serve the Imperium while the blood of Sanguinius still courses through my veins. And you will help me to carry it out, one way or another."
71ab74d0-0726-405b-84e6-7c24dcd145f5: "Later, Eogunn. I require some time to prepare."
de31abc1-d240-4d37-ac71-f66afcd21de6: "Then let us not delay." {n}With a charming smile, Juvenna takes your arm.{/n}
600e7d01-a0a1-410a-adc9-494d768ee138: "Must I remind you of your secret, Sauerback? And of the many people who would be interested to learn of it?
4425de3a-4ae9-4bab-b87e-fc973cefb7a0: The first hit per target from this pistol increases the level of damage-over-time effects the target is already suffering by +5. Does not trigger again for new effects inflicted on the same target after the first hit. Does not stack.
2abc9ea3-d41a-4bda-9638-4cde07a3313a: "I am currently engaged in a study of determinacy in the existence of amalgam individuals. Your presence will no doubt enliven the discussion my dearest colleague and I are having as I work. You have caught Nuncius and me about halfway through our disputation."
0e326365-489f-435f-943e-5016fe8b7d29: "What do you think of our new equipment, Ferrenz?"
436b5cea-62f2-4789-aaff-75d5f639ff8f: {n}After several attempts, the Merovians developed a strategy of mobile diversionary units. They learned how to vanish from the point where the psyker left the warp echo long before any Drovers appeared.
