Known as "the runners," these units became havens for the foolhardy souls of Zarkov.{/n}
f67eb1f6-1a2c-4910-aef0-1b85c2ee8407: Permitted the Arch-Militant to preserve the funeral traditions of Zarkov.
92bdd53f-2bc5-4a63-b553-76e2a496ab29: "What... how... How do you know this?" {n}Despite his extensive implants, Seberian cannot hide his alarm — the deep furrows in the living patch of skin and the slight interference from the vox-synthesiser give him away.{/n} "This is truly terrible news. I must inform the enforcers that reinforcements will not be arriving. Who would stoop to this crime? Has Diodas fallen so low?"
02dd9aca-0225-40fe-84a1-af809cbe9370: "The living weapon of an ancient foe, lost to timelessness. This region of the void rarely attracts Aeldari, which is why the display is incomplete. Other sections of the Museion contain more interesting exhibits from this civilisation, but I managed to find a few interesting pieces in this backwater as well."
44b0a383-b750-4087-88a6-c6a4f4e3d1c3: {n}Danrok's plump cheeks turn purple from the rush of blood. He wheezes, tugging at his coat collar.{/n} "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}... I was labouring solely for the good of the protectorate... I have substantial proof."
915755b6-263b-409b-b215-acfbe74d4f89: "Arch-Militant? At such a young age? I suppose my Laria earned the title of Seneschal as soon as she came of age."
b43f80ff-6e72-4ca4-965f-9ac9cf758b4b: {n}Turning to you, he smiles.{/n} "Rogue Trader, as you can see, my {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalMadness}unification with my colleague{/d} is already proving fruitful. There are nuances to triasomia that we have still to refine, but as you are aware, it is all the easier for me to experiment on myself. I cannot describe the exaltation I feel at being reunited with my dearest colleague! He is alive thanks to you!" {n}The Manipulus gestures delightedly at the empty air.{/n}
682c20ca-892f-4848-90c9-3b8c3a8bf292: "Serenada Schwartz, Deck 2 Voidsman Unit... But what's the point?" {n}The woman huffs a laugh, bitter and sad.{/n} "You'll get my name from one of the Administratum minions once all this is over."
d372b5eb-892f-43b2-98b4-0b4acfe6ddc0: {n}Jarub lingers awkwardly by the display case before at last finding the courage to interrupt your watch.{/n} "Uh... Perhaps we'd best meet at the infirmary later, my {mf|lord|lady}? I can do the rest on my own. Don't take this the wrong way, but you're a bit too conspicuous."
715efb24-19fd-4f33-ace6-86833235062d: The Merovian 32nd Regiment perished long ago, yet, from its ashes, the 1st Zarkovian Regiment shall be reborn. It shall not stand alone: now that the planet possesses the necessary infrastructure, the Rogue Trader, with the aid of the Departmento Munitorum, shall initiate regular conscription, ensuring that Zarkovians bring glory to their new home in the war against the endless enemies of Humanity.
8615f7e9-4784-42d0-b99c-613a507d64f1: "Is that how you became colonel too?"
9b34f865-09a4-4b22-91b1-ec265eccee59: {n}Vedka looks thoughtfully at her prosthesis.{/n} "The von Valancius gift... Forgive me for wasting your time, Lord Captain. I should have made such an obvious choice right from the start."
967d8c3d-1cd8-497d-bef9-874ce1861930: "I will not let you imprison us both!"
b8fe8234-62bd-449b-8e48-b225229269a5: {n}The man looks up at you with a clouded gaze.{/n} "Forgive me, Lord Captain. I cannot take it anymore. I must break free from these shackles."
710b862e-4a91-4637-ad54-395daaa41a58: {n}Kuravich pauses, then slaps his forehead.{/n} "Not bad at all, those blades!" {n}He looks over your shoulder and screams piercingly.{/n} "Yasik! Yasik! Get a move on! Grab a couple of men and see if you can saw the claws off those beasts with the cutters! We'll have ourselves some knives!"
1091e381-8c9d-4b27-b01b-3b278b9e39a6: {n}The members of the motley procession bow deeply. Among them you see the familiar figures of the experts of the Sector Bionica.{/n}
c3fbb7d9-74d1-42e5-b74a-a83bbab9133b: Whenever the {g|Encyclopedia:CareerBladedancerGlossary}Bladedancer{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:Parry}parries{/g} an attack, the number of attacks made by the next {g|f:de2fa09b36314b2dafe09c3e8354d214}Blade Dance{/g} is increased by +1. This effect stacks up to {uip|agiB|7d51ba40347341b38cd181fb18097edb} times.
c7052959-a7ed-47bf-9f1a-9f3bcd1fc192: "What Calcazar so covets and fears... What is that?"
616af563-0263-42b6-8d39-bf0ae961d330: "Alas, there has been no word from them."
afd16931-7706-4c12-8c17-a40ce55f1789: {n}In the silence, you hear the soft sobs of a woman. Gnotec's mother is weeping helplessly, furiously wiping her eyes with her hand, but now a smile of relief lights up her face.{/n} "Thank you... Thank you, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}! I will remember your intercession for the rest of my life and pray for your good fortune."
ac2ce1a8-ae9d-4dbb-a5dd-d79c124e159b: "That is something better discussed with the Magi of Altar-Templum-Calixis-Ext-17."
99b1f008-c746-460f-8b66-e688fc6ba7ed: {n}Even among the pincers, cutters, and coils of the Motive Force and other technical equipment, the creature encased in the machine looks alien. All that remains of its body is a greyish-black skeleton topped by a skull-shaped head. As you approach, a spark of evil, inquisitive intelligence awakens in the alien's lone eye, which glows with a dull green light.{/n}
621967f1-3e2b-463d-bbe1-2a650a9544fd: This time the carved symbol points to a levitating platform frozen above the chasm of the hall.
e139086a-3aff-4e92-b0b7-19917e892e17: "And finally: the blade, the Crimson Dance of Khaine, must be acquired at all costs. As long as it remains in Seberian's possession, our proposal to parley with the Inquisition would seem laughable. However, if my father is deprived of that foul artefact, we may be able to prove to Diodas af Irramon that Seberian is ready to cooperate. I can see only one solution here: find someone with very nimble fingers and a gift for stealth — someone desperate enough to turn against their fallen master for the grace of the {g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}God-Emperor{/g}."
f1d1f1dc-ea85-4f9f-8c04-e9fd2a0a3fb8: "I work for Laria Voigtvir. I can be trusted."
26e28713-b2a3-41e5-9d54-066c13116f3b: "Destroyed? Hardly. You have merely disrupted the hypergeometric logic of the labyrinth with your unruly antics, nothing more." {n}The xenos's voice acquires a sardonic note.{/n} "But don't let that detail spoil your little celebration — it is still a most impressive feat for a member of a primitive species."
efa24783-41b8-4b32-993e-ba58d597ba84: {n}The Arbitrator frowns, then speaks in a calm, warning tone.{/n} "Rog— {name} von Valancius, from this moment forward, all your rights, freedoms, incomes, holdings, and status may be called into question at any time." {n}He is silent for a few seconds.{/n} "Not by me, but still... should someone wish to put an end to you..."
a8b41c0a-841f-4a22-8547-4ac348c672aa: "Miiikosh..." {n}He speaks his name in a drawn-out groan, but then confusion immediately clouds his gaze — he seems to have forgotten it again.{/n} "A vent crawler... We're the l-lungs of the ship! How can the ship... breathe if there's no one to pick the mouldslime... off the blades? Death's no excuse for idleness! I need to help them... breathe..."
dd4b039a-02f0-4c04-b3c4-bafb9a4a25d8: {n}Upon hearing about the Nexus of Shadows, Marazhai's lip curls in disgust, but he does not lower himself to comment.{/n}
a0fc0f96-cad9-4024-b198-b9b0f872fec6: {n}Juvenna seizes the paper and runs her eyes over it. Her chest rises and falls sharply, and for a moment, it seems as if she is about to explode in outrage. But she merely takes a deep breath and rips up the document into tiny pieces. She then offers you a saccharine smile.{/n} "There is nothing suspect about preparing title papers for a capable servant. That hardly means they will be acted on immediately, surely? What is suspect is how my intentions, set forth on paper, found their way into your grasping hands."
90010611-f22c-475a-bab6-fa8b04304931: "It is probably a consequence of my years of practice among the Genetors." {n}The chirurgeon listens with genuine sadness to the lilting sounds of the binharic hymn sung by the technomats to keep the ancient machine alive.{/n} "I learned much of the craft of healing from them, and after I found myself in the Sector Bionica... the machine spirit that inhabits this relic became my mentor."
eae7ae73-77d9-4c39-9b18-75419af32831: {n}The data-slate reads: "Error. The requested identifier 'Nuncius' is not recognised. Unit 17-401 is processing the request: the structural integrity of the logs is intact. No tampering with the cranial cogitator detected."{/n}
eb180238-135a-40ad-8ff7-8358ea8b3837: "It was one of the most complex procedures I have ever performed! We replaced the majority of his head augments, but it was only thanks to the knowledge of the crimson priesthood that a neural implant capable of replacing the damaged parts of the brain was found in the repositories of the Sector Bionica. They had been keeping it in storage all this time, withholding it from the laity — and for good reason, as it turns out. Isn't that right, Ferrenz?"
1db7e4ad-550c-48f0-8fa8-5eb54c3eec23: {n}Izdrig lifts his chin and sets his shoulders.{/n} "She {d|Encyclopedia:Ch2_ToldBatoshskiAboutPsyker}found a psyker{/d} and let him live. She didn't let him get his atonement. Instead, she filled his head with nonsense, and the poor sorcerer brought the Drover right to the Cage. Dozens of my people are dead thanks to her folly. Maybe you forgive such things on your ship, but on Zarkov, we execute saboteurs."
19d88d6b-4bcf-4cd5-b5d7-d37c744f592f: {n}Rumours of the terrible fate of the researchers trying to investigate the {g|Encyclopedia:XenosGlossary}xenos{/g} creations spread through the ship very quickly, so the scouts descend to the pyramid with all possible caution. The mysterious structure is made of solid stone and lacks even a hint of doors or windows. Nearby, the scouts find evidence of an Imperial camp.{/n}
0ebf76e5-9b20-41e3-8de1-ff883bef9cf5: Whenever damage is dealt, restores 20-25 {g|Encyclopedia:HitPoints}wounds{/g} to the Dover.
c8b69330-d7a0-4dbc-a4bb-c8065ec6fc8c: "A monstrous miscarriage of justice. I believe we can help each other."
ae35f37a-bff3-4081-b978-9e3682dde723:
The wearer moves in a straight line to the target and deals <u>{g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}Deflection{/g}</u> damage, then {g|Encyclopedia:PushGlossary}pushes{/g} the target 3 cells away. This ability does not count towards the limit of the {g|Encyclopedia:AttacksPerTurn}attacks per turn{/g}.
There must be at least 2 free cells between the wearer and the target to use this ability.
f45fc67a-38b2-4e6d-96a4-ef5430dde4ec: "All houses are puppeteers and manipulators. Do not put your faith in anyone."
68e22cfb-fec3-467c-844c-5cfe317e1655: "Nuncius is not subject to removal, and the removal of my own head would do little to restore your cognitive functions, layperson, even if I were to graft it onto you. There are cases, it seems, where even my skills prove powerless."
de071527-2b63-4c42-a1ce-d43dc052d3d3: "However, if one were to overlook these pessimistic predictions and proceed from the hypothesis that escape from this place is even possible, then our best bet is to seize upon the xenos' infighting. During my time as a subject of the Haemonculus's experiments, I came to the conclusion that pride and envy are inherent not only to him, but to his entire accursed species as a whole. The xenos will scheme and connive — there is no question about that. The only question is: when will he be caught in his own web of intrigue? Hypothetically, that will be our chance."
4d6923f1-6dce-4d0c-b8a9-a34c89e013f8: Hundreds of names are etched upon the memorial, and space remains for thousands yet to come.
9ed63553-4b5b-4e96-b1eb-0ca757515cd3: "My lord, {mf|he|she} is of House Gaprak. I'm not sure we should trust {mf|him|her}."
c91b48d7-1d66-442a-9af3-38f015d99dbf:
The Inquisitor summons Valkyrie aerial reinforcements. Any creatures (except Seberian) present within the marked area at the start of the Inquisitor's next {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g} will suffer lethal damage due to the Valkyrie's air strike.
After that, the Inquisitor marks a new area for the next strike taking place at the start of the Inquisitor's next turn.
2cbd7902-74fd-4cb3-8220-89feaf938c71: {n}Ferrenz wilts under the {d|Encyclopedia:CharGen_DH_Signs}crushing weight of your gaze{/d}. Breaking eye contact with visible effort, the Genetor shakes his head convulsively, as if to dispel a haunting vision. When he speaks again, his voice sounds strangely humbled.{/n} "As you command, Lord Captain." {n}The Tech-Priest accepts the augment with trembling hands.{/n} "The technology will be refined to its maximum possible efficiency."
87c11224-68d7-435d-8309-bb95506a7fe4: "No offence, Cass, but this is a familiar smell and atmosphere to us ordinary servants of the dynasty. You should be used to it by now if you want to live in the real world..."
4d5e0eef-49ff-4923-a86b-438db4c6819a: "Laria, you have proven time and time again that I can rely on you. I know you are acting solely in the best interests of the dynasty, and I trust your judgement." {n}Seberian's hand rests on the hilt of his sword, which is secured in an ornate sheath at his belt.{/n}
8e414542-dd45-420a-8a2b-507cde7a21a3: The situation on the promethium mining station on Tirkuaz-4 has been resolved.
9a4839fa-98f4-4a02-8ba2-2aae71d3eb9e: "I am the anointed of the God-Emperor and, should I wish it, your head will leave your shoulders. Pray that does not happen before you provide an answer."
a0c544c9-0739-413b-a2a7-fd40aa77eeff: "Yet, a narrow mind frequently bars the path to knowledge. Reflect upon this, esteemed colleague." {n}Jae flashes the Manipulus a grateful smile.{/n}
ff60e9a4-5e0b-4bf7-84bf-ad7b4c07a246:
At the start of combat, Pasqal (or a random ally, if Pasqal is not in the current party) gains the {g|f:bee0d96b40b84f5da78bc3c9f41ec1fa}line of support{/g} effect.
While Eogunn stands in a straight or diagonal line to an ally affected by {g|f:bee0d96b40b84f5da78bc3c9f41ec1fa}line of support{/g} and that ally is within his {g|Encyclopedia:LineOfSight}line of sight{/g}, he gains +{unit_stat|WarhammerPerception|bonus} to all {g|Encyclopedia:Characteristic}characteristics{/g} except {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerPerception}Perception{/g}.
If Eogunn starts his {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g} in such a position, he also gains +1 stack of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g}.
This effect works even if that ally is {g|Encyclopedia:UnconsciousEffect}unconscious{/g}.
8900dddf-855d-4487-a29c-4879debd9f83: This creature gains +5 Weapon Skill, Agility, and Perception for each stack of this effect.
9b1f5387-c825-40f7-8732-aea0283415e7: [Give the order for battle to commence] "In the name of the Emperor, I, Rogue Trader {name} von Valancius, call the fleet to battle!"
caae168b-b7ef-40e0-a226-77c70626f8cd: {n}You sense some agreement in Nuncius's silence.{/n}
0c0f8b8d-e145-4f44-8e6d-d756f44cbbc7: "Retreat to the palace and hold the defences! Now!"
469ee9e5-6654-4269-bf66-dd7a5eefa6d3: "I never dreamed of such a thing, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. But I took the oath and I accept the post."
ea512201-df6d-48f7-8493-6388ccbaada2: {n}The woman shoots quickly and professionally, without hesitation. Thanks to your reaction, the autopistol round penetrates the medicae officer's body more to the left than planned — he falls to the floor, clutching his head, his hands instantly drenched with red.{/n}
f9749bb5-fa5e-4f66-ba64-484a13ede882: "Domin, you are here! As it was always meant to be, for His cards never lie, yet unworthy doubts have gnawed at my soul." {n}Two long, half-healed cuts bloom like a bright cross upon Kibellah's face.{/n}
e0076620-f03a-477f-8b01-7814b1d1fbe1: {n}Izdrig nods grimly, then snaps to Vedka,{/n} "Captain-Quartermaster, tell the funereal detachment to inspect the Drover's lair."
63ea0e0e-e616-444e-89d7-e1f90c426ae5: "A pathetic attempt to turn the situation in your favour, specimen. Speak again and you will be part of the next experiment yourself."
a6db642b-2a47-4117-a510-be83df346bdc: {n}Exalt-117 is stationary near the turrets. The {g|Encyclopedia:TechPriest}Tech-Priest's{/g} body is vibrating subtly, with rhythmic sounds coming from his vox-transmitter. The Magos seems completely absorbed in tech-meditation, but as you take another step closer, his head turns toward you.{/n} "You have entered the radius of the sacred ritual. What is the objective of your approach?"
72e8cd3c-b639-4039-a9c9-60c40a0f6cd2: The twisted figure, devoid of anything human, does not move, yet the feeling of its single eye upon you lingers.
ff06c782-a9e4-4b42-99f6-9c5482cd41c5: <u>(number of {g|f:780497b04a0944e59cb57e68bb9775c4}ferrum sanctum{/g} stacks × (number of {g|f:ad5bda1047bf4e4dafa278f87d07c5ad}potential energy{/g} stacks + 1))</u>
b7de229c-1bab-49f2-beff-f3a15b8e12d2: {n}Shock is replaced by peals of laughter.{/n} "Sobering honesty, {name}! If a machine does not fulfil its function, it should be repaired. I fell into an abyss of emotion and doubt, failing to consider the situation pragmatically. After all, to you, I am a tool. Of course you will not let me just walk away. Which means I can only move forward. I will have to take the risk simply because I have no other choice."
c0bbb7cf-2c9f-4210-85dc-67405cb17072: [Execute him] "Colonel Izdrig Batoshski, I charge you with the foul crime of secession, the blasphemy of insubordination to Terra, and the sacrilege of breaking your oath. I hereby sentence you to death."
f6e5486e-4132-410c-8398-5ee02569b4bb: "Everything around us keeps repeating itself — it is akin to some strange technology."
e2a67d3c-393d-45ab-b7d7-a898422d7dfc: "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, I must report a strange occurrence detected in the Sector Bionica. It manifested following the machine rites conducted by the esteemed Manipulus Februs."
1772634c-9a23-46c6-a6f6-92f467fa8d86: "I am concerned about Zarkov's safety, so Tau-Phi-0098 should lead the researchers."
f999ccf6-3549-4825-918e-2b3c1074ac7f: "It is as though I were invisible... Are they in their right mind?"
8157f66a-2f47-4f35-be31-16694cce5b8f: Because of their specialised training and immesurable battlefield experience, this creature's {g|Encyclopedia:Characteristic}characteristics{/g} are increased.
48d07c17-1d54-428e-a39d-621dcd790f21: "As I said, the Drover itself hides in a tangle of roots. It will only come out if you chop down a whole bunch of its shoots. But here's the thing... no one's ever been able to kill it. Usually, it just runs away. But we don't have weapons like yours."
0c825c3f-8232-4525-84bb-955201d806aa: The Cold Embrace of the Immaterium
39b2bd76-1a01-444d-892f-0be9daae8a95: "Look how cleverly this beast is trying to outrun time. My gut tells me that it's related to the one we met in the ruins. Well, it can't escape its fate, no matter how quick it is."
ade4fc70-7b44-4fe7-a64b-33f8721b671c: Branek Zarkov is acting on Laria's orders. Either give him different orders, or remove him from the board.
4929a20f-123e-4aca-8467-4eff81acfc3d: "We have nothing more to discuss, and your presence here is inappropriate. I have opened a dimensional passage that will lead you out of the Museion and back to your ship. Go now." {n}The xenos speaks in a tone of weary irritation and... something else. Something strange and dangerous.{/n}
3fa48a1d-f1c8-4cd4-bab5-ae28f3ecf424: The user's {g|Encyclopedia:MeleeAttacksGlossary}melee attacks{/g} ignore {uip|Deflection|0511b273cf194d7c9e4b3ddf1b7780e0} of the target's {g|Encyclopedia:Deflection}deflection{/g} and {uip|Absorbtion|0511b273cf194d7c9e4b3ddf1b7780e0}% of their {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g}.
b37fa7d4-8b1f-403c-98f1-fe077053dbc8: "I could have handled it myself without your help."
a511510a-eb8e-497c-80b0-c84f82bc62de: {n}The High Factotum bows respectfully before the throne, extending an ornate container toward you.{/n} "Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, I am pleased to report that the recent unrest upon Janus has been suppressed through the efforts of the Merovian Guardsmen. The world's aristocracy sends this gift as a token of their gratitude."
f8f8458e-8104-4f97-988a-c9fb1f90a41c: "The life form has not yet regained full functionality. You will be able to communicate with him later in the Sector Bionica." {n}Ferrenz steps to the side, deftly supporting Martinax, who is at risk of toppling over.{/n}
82e41117-ff17-4b1c-9823-ed2c229cd4d0: "How did you come to serve in the Sector Bionica?"
e0f99860-05e6-4931-9b08-0660acfea47c: {n}The wounded man's face twists in a grimace of poorly concealed hatred.{/n} "Blackmail, then. I'm forced to accept your decision and submit to it, but don't think I'll forget this moment even for a second."
ffdcb998-78d0-4825-8732-992244a86a33: {n}A mechadendrite touches the table with the middle-aged man.{/n} "Enforcer Lorinque. He has diligently served on the ship since birth. A participant in countless combat landings, where he was a model of valour and steadfastness. He was known on the lower decks for being tough but fair. His last act of heroism was the rescue of an enforcer patrol and a number of workers on deck 515-E-2 — Lorinque placed his own body over a krak grenade thrown from the crowd by a mutineer."
f143704e-b9e7-49e0-bb95-148e9ef5c7c0: "I know how unworthy leadership withers a dynasty. I know how the spirit of von Valancius has been dwindling over the generations, turning its leaders into common merchants. And I know how a dynasty can become a puppet. I will not let that happen."
c7f969ff-f563-451c-b8b2-6ac7d7eab864: {n}Your efforts have failed. You step away from the dead man in the bed and wipe the sweat from your brow with your bloodied hand.{/n}
148dccbd-2826-4491-b21a-c24ebf5596d2: {n}The woman turns, and for a second it seems she might lunge at you. Then the denizen of the Cage shifts her gaze back to her comrade.{/n}
7e7ad3e6-b09b-4dd2-8e5f-e7f8c5d9af58: Eogunn cannot equip cloaks or boots.
c4205f3a-cb4d-41f4-b262-e185699a018c: {n}Noticing the xenos in your retinue, Seberian lets out a nasty chuckle.{/n} "Is Diodas flaunting his hypocrisy for my amusement? Using xenos to fight heretics is a tad ironic, wouldn't you say?"
8a17f420-c72c-46a2-907e-7ec14192c5ef: "Yet another set of strange ruins turns out to be an Oghyr tomb. What a fascinating and unexpected coincidence, wouldn't you say?" {n}There is enough malice in Marazhai's smile to poison an entire compartment of a voidship.{/n}
54bd1974-5f24-42a7-9023-5c49c341bf30: "Merov is a world that has nothing but metal and throngs of people. Every one of them lives, serves, and dies in the name of the God-Emperor. Our stories say the Brother-Generals have turned their backs on His light, and now they do whatever they please to regiments like ours. I think some of those stories are exaggerated to strike fear into recruits. If we can serve the God-Emperor among the stars, why concern ourselves with some Brother-Generals?"
cfe8e8a0-9dbb-48ca-87cd-9a76d0d96c74: Through centuries of life on Zarkov, the soldiers of the 32nd Regiment have evolved into natural killers, as silent and unseen as shadows but forged to endure any hardship. Their signature tactic relies on the use of vocal organs harvested from the Drovers, which emit mind-crushing wails. This weaponry shatters the minds of their foes, yet poses little harm to the Zarkovians, who have been accustomed to these cries since childhood. There is no reason to share such skilled warriors with the Astra Militarum. Therefore, all soldiers of Zarkov shall swear fealty directly to the von Valancius dynasty, becoming the Rogue Trader's personal guard.
72819734-9042-40cb-840f-f62672a338f0: "It reminds us that our survival is built upon the bones of the Call-Ups that came before, and that upon our own bones will our children grow. It speaks of our duty and of atonement."
a970dac2-8add-4b87-81e5-07659c74bcfa: "Just know that the von Valancius dynasty is in good hands."
c6e11561-6dc7-4dba-b83f-c1bc0d4c02cc: {n}The first Adeptus Mechanicus forces soon made planetfall on Zarkov, and their complex machinery was roused to wakefulness by the chanting of binharic prayers. Under the hard eyes of the Merovians, the Magos-led Genetors began preparations for their investigation.{/n}
aedfb1ce-d8d5-4250-ac38-501fce504dd6: {n}The moment your words leave your throat, your body freezes like a block of ice — a wave of Trazyn the Infinite's hand is enough to rob you of your ability to move.{/n} "Do not forget yourself, primitive creature — or I may become utterly disillusioned with this little 'pact' we have. The deal was made and remains in force." {n}Another brief movement — and you regain control of your body.{/n}
173138f3-db1d-4b06-95a0-eb89565596fc: {n}The anomaly seems to move as well, quickly dropping off the augur screens.{/n}
9e25c6b1-2b2d-4a81-9f88-2c540e686400: The device is designed to diagnose and repair leg augments.
dbb9f18d-b226-45e9-b80f-c407503f3b18: {n}You and your retinue seem to be the only ones here still in possession of free will. The other captives of this mysterious trap are doomed to repeat the same actions over and over again, forever trapped in the day Dargonus was besieged by {g|Encyclopedia:InquisitionGlossary}Inquisition{/g} forces.{/n}
8369f26b-7ed7-4f5b-9600-634f0e529c4c: The plague only accelerated what awaited the ship under the leadership of the champion of the Dark Gods. The whispers that poured forth from every crevice of the flagship turned ordinary workers into alloys of metal and corruption, unswervingly loyal to their only master.
4d1c27a4-05f5-497d-a261-4a5f101a375b: {n}Your strike drowns in a flash of green, not harming the metal xenos in the slightest. Your hands, bathed in the nauseating unnatural light, begin to feel a little numb.{/n}
3a2c66bf-7056-4022-bd2c-c602f5982dac: Countless turns, crossings, climbs, and descents later, the squad finally reached its goal. The walls, once closing in relentlessly around us, suddenly opened into a wide, low-ceilinged corridor that disappeared into darkness.
44c04744-793c-4c8e-9993-f9a6cce1f9a3: "Once our ordeals in this dark sector are over and your ship returns home, I shall find a Chartist willing to take on board a reanimated dead man who still dreams of the stars, and set out toward the unknown. I have spent too long gathering dust. It is time to conquer new worlds for the dynasty... and leave the protectorate to its rightful owner."
994e8838-2580-4540-86af-3227242e30da: "Here is one last lesson for you, young {mf|fellow|lady}: only a member of the Gaprak-Olwens could set foot on Dargonus at present. Although... it is rather unseemly for the dead to come to court. Guards!" {n}The soldiers who surround you leave no hope of victory. The last thing you hear is the dry click of a bead between Camilla Drivestem's fingers.{/n}
866e1545-7ce0-465a-b14c-f551954f48e4: {n}The Tech-Priest slowly extends her mechadendrites, twisting them in an intricate gesture of greeting.{/n} "Lord Captain. The blessed machine spirit that dwells within the throne is fading. I and many grieving Tech-Priests have been performing unending sacraments of tech-healing and prayers of operational benediction on this shrine, but even our devotions are merely staving off the inevitable."
21f10b25-acd5-4da1-8a17-a336c71b4c6a: "You should not underestimate the wisdom of an old friend and experienced colleague, Mistress Heydari. Extensive studies show that the presence of such individuals can be vitally important."
4a308b7c-a88c-4e1a-981a-7ff0b4f7b0de: "It was madness to challenge Diodas. Now it will bring down the whole dynasty."
1afafff0-2ff8-408b-95cf-4c3e7379ec1e: You appointed Colonel Izdrig Batoshski to be your Arch-Militant.
1bc44f70-a7f1-467e-844c-63a68f01f1d7: Whenever the wearer kills an enemy with {g|f:e955823f54d24088ae1fdefe88d3684d}Blade Dance{/g}, other enemies adjacent to the wearer become {g|Encyclopedia:StaggeredEffect}staggered{/g} for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
873c2eea-540a-414c-a9d6-5b8a751c0bbe: [Search the memory module for information on the sealed door]
3323701f-3334-4f1f-95c3-991b84546d38: "Seeing to the needs of a maimed man is tedious work undeserving of the Rogue Trader's attention. Do your duty as you see fit."
acb99f0e-6182-4e94-a683-5a414143ca5c: {n}The vox-synthesiser makes a mocking trill.{/n} "You are a dreamer, {name}, and that is what I like about you. Your aspirations know no limits. But as much as I would like to share this eagerness with you, your words will not break my resolve today. Instead, I am entrusting you with my dream of distant stars. Carry it proudly, for you are a worthy heir of House von Valancius. Bring greatness to our protectorate — for us both."
57a97244-235d-4816-a39a-563ce81e5aa1: At the end of the first {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}, this creature uses {g|f:16678d533cf840d597c2931b49c07ef7}Mad Calibration: Energy Rejection{/g} on the Defiler, lasting for 3 rounds. This {g|Encyclopedia:Ability}ability{/g} cannot be used if the creature is {g|Encyclopedia:ProneEffect}prone{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:Stun}stunned{/g}, or has been {g|Encyclopedia:PushGlossary}pushed{/g} away.
86388dbe-4bf8-4e81-a103-bc3701581733: Send a scouting party to investigate the rings.
d93020c3-7474-4e7b-8d40-7cf1ce904e28: "Whom among his retinue does Seberian listen to above all others?"
8ea8cfcf-da67-493e-9681-e26c1eb91576:
Until the end of combat, enemies that enter oiled cells also suffer a −5% {g|Encyclopedia:Penalty}penalty{/g} to {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerArmourGlossary}armour{/g}. Stacks.
At the end of their {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, the user becomes slowed until the end of combat.
3070dbb1-b1a4-4cda-ad20-f3ee912f37a8: {n}As soon as you avert your eyes, the man in the bed lets out a loud, anguished scream. The pair leaning over him flinch, but do not remove their hands from his sawn-open head.{/n}
7f0a8706-00c9-4e15-ac7e-f911117afcdb: {n}Your words hang in the air. Seberian turns and looks you straight in the eye. Without a single word. An unspoken question on his face.{/n}
a20b09a7-2228-469a-9228-2f485a3407a6: {n}Laria nods quickly, inviting you to speak further, and gives you her complete attention.{/n}
68645562-a15c-46bd-9ac8-78c90f5b26b4: "I am going to give you a short head start before sending assassins after you — as payback for all you have done."
53508efd-bb68-46f8-81da-b299fd2055c5: Ancient Ring of the von Valancius Dynasty
0842cee6-db18-431c-b2b2-069bbd747fa5: The partially decomposed human body has been considerably disfigured, but you can still make out the signs of mutation on the rotting face.
4f4ee9b0-f436-43e4-9048-c0a2cc336132: "Well, have you become acquainted with the officers?"
6ac7ede2-5ddc-4836-9a2f-a8551e00323f: {n}Seberian's hunched metal shoulders give out a low groan as he straightens.{/n} "To be frank with you, {name}, I do not have the faintest notion of what we might face. I know only this: I must give Trazyn his comeuppance. He seized that which I value more than my own life, and I am determined to get it back — or die in the trying."
64cca759-115e-4341-a6f2-ad74e4470d5a: "A rational pattern for a layperson of your status." {n}Alva-9 respectfully inclines her head.{/n}
c5187cb2-fe43-46a1-9e3c-d6d3cb64aa6f: {n}Juvenna looks around helplessly. Her fists are clenched with such force that small drops of blood fall from her delicate hands.{/n} "The gates... are shut. It's no use. It's all pointless." {n}She goes still, bowing her head low. The moment has passed. The humiliated heir is unlikely to take more decisive action.{/n}
046e383c-e265-4e8f-87fe-19f2364664d3: The Warrant was there, somewhere ahead, along with the one who'd had the audacity to steal it. And we, the Rogue Traders of House von Valancius, were prepared to do anything to reclaim what had been granted to us by the God-Emperor Himself.
48324df3-a728-4cbe-881c-17bd1cc8224c: If any word of the reinforcements was sent to the palace, it did not go beyond the outpost. The outpost must be investigated.
20638495-1b25-4dd7-9def-16efb775fd25: "You are mistaken. The only Elana I know is Elana the Unshaken, the saint of the {g|Encyclopedia:CalixisGlossary}Calixis{/g} Sector. She would hardly take our side in this."
0a870702-0627-436d-8315-b93c225dac52: "Therein lies the problem, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. No Rogue Trader by the name of 'Seberian' is listed in any dynastic chronicle. The historitors of the flagship are also unfamiliar with the name. Why the guardian thinks it belongs to an owner that never existed is a mystery. And only you, as the actual head of the dynasty, can find the answer."
f9d100df-4042-4574-8db6-45ee182ad85c: "Allow us to introduce ourselves: Kantus Zehir Otto Toliman, officer of Vox-Repeater LMXIV on Upper Deck 6, Sector 2. And this young man is my eldest son. We are here on a serious matter, and we would be most grateful to Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship} if you would lend your support to our fair petition for the staff of the Sector Bionica."
4ca030e6-522a-4255-a7c0-e7ee07e9087f: "And you left him like this? That's monstrous."
fa816d63-c1d2-4796-922b-c81eb44829cc: "Quite right, Captain. It would not have been possible without the grace of the Machine God."
9cebec37-6e4d-4290-b1e2-ee57b1c5f24c: "Any deception of the enemies of Humanity is a virtue. You don't have to mean the words."
11f68583-71be-4fa5-8d5e-416989ae7997: "Are you joking, Master Killjoy? They're all descended from penal soldiers! They'll make mincemeat of any overseers you send down."
68b73bf7-034e-4730-995d-4a49e686c5fb: "Don't worry. I am following the plan. Everything is proceeding accordingly."
1231af9f-889b-44e7-bf9a-d06c020d6b05: "You're right. That Sergeant Vukchik, for one. You can put a void rat in a uniform, but that doesn't make them a soldier. I'll tell you this: when Vukchik's around, keep your valuables close."
ab4f54b9-128a-434b-9150-b3cb1284a84d: {n}Gnotec's mother sucks in a breath and forcefully wipes away the tears. Her half-crazed eyes roam over her surroundings, and finally latch onto the Genetor Majoris. Animal rage simmers in her blown pupils, as if she were ready to lunge at the Tech-Priest, but the impulse dissipates after a moment. In a dull voice, she says,{/n} "Thank you for your mercy."
01d9889f-84b5-4a5a-b408-8403e765b7f3: {n}The servo-skull remains true to himself, staying silent until the last. Perhaps he simply hates goodbyes.{/n}
258a6ead-a0c6-43b6-8093-55a893518c4a: "I listened to you. Now you listen to me."
480f4d44-e481-4627-b08c-9703bc55c97b: "You are a coward, Eogunn. For years, you have been butchering your own memory and stalling for time with pointless experiments while your friend suffers. Enough. Take responsibility. Return to Nuncius and let him go."
d7a8ce4e-7c42-49f5-a067-27a2f07a0b6f: The user restores {g|Encyclopedia:ActionPoints}AP{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:MovementPoints}MP{/g} to their maximum, gains +10 additional attacks, and until the end of the {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, deals +100% more damage and cannot be {g|Encyclopedia:Stun}stunned{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:ImmobilisedEffect}immobilised{/g}, fall {g|Encyclopedia:ProneEffect}prone{/g}, or fall unconscious. At the end of the turn, the user falls unconscious.
bc998b12-84eb-4b3c-93b5-6eea7ed6a417: {n}The woman raises her startled gaze to you. However, the fire in her eyes lasts only a split second before she once again loses interest in you and everything going on around her.{/n}
bd8d4874-9102-437c-9671-7495800d96dd: "Cogs... Don't trust them, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. Their gifts can easily turn into chains of bondage. And their goals are often very different from yours — and in the end it's the common folk who wind up as their prey. But if refusing them is not an option, then don't let the Zarkovians become dependent on the Tech-Priests' mysteries."
699ab8c0-b27e-4604-92c1-0c2be86662b2: "It was 'Hymn of the Lost,' the song of the old Call-Ups. It holds the answer to why we're here and what we're meant for. It's how we remember the fallen and warn the living. And as for the tomb... well, that's just a local horror story."
f7374693-4f16-4a82-9274-6a590f8d7406: "I will remove your sight and tongue, so that your false words do no more harm."
238a672c-bf58-4a80-86e7-bbca62016b2f: "Would you like to discuss this delicate matter somewhere more private?"
e732bf4b-b9fe-43de-a7a5-25117bd1cc48: "According to the Handbook, my primary duty as a Guardsman is to aid the God-Emperor's faithful servants in the struggle against the enemies of Humanity, and to preserve the life of a superior even at the cost of my own. And you are {d|Encyclopedia:Ch2_MerovianGoodShooting}to be our commander{/d}, after all."
36598a29-d9ae-45d6-b1f5-8bce1248ee24: {n}In the hall full of unnatural xenos creations, your gaze catches on a poignantly familiar object — your {g|Encyclopedia:WarrantGlossary}Warrant{/g}, put on display like an exhibition piece... or like a taunt to the people it was stolen from.{/n}
c83d9514-826d-4937-bf78-c03284fe5cad: "Why risk your servants? It would be better to help your enemies find each other. Trick them and lead them into each other's path. While they're busy, scout out the ruins or kill the survivors."
c88ecb02-c2a1-4f95-a0bd-8f2250143414: "You think placating the spirits of an entire air defence system so that even Exalt-117 is convinced of its serviceability is a mere trifle? Explain, then, why the {g|Encyclopedia:Magos}Magos{/g} deemed it necessary to subject it to a new test."
92db0516-eb88-4765-a92c-52cc619f658f: [Show Amella's letter] "Look: they made an exhibition out of your life, and this is one of the exhibits!"
06aac6e2-0a4d-4375-ae18-f1d6518df80a: [Convince Exalt-117 that the time loop exists]
0bd9600d-2e37-4759-9cfa-3618af0adae5: "I have encountered inconsistencies in my research. Ghost colonies with fresh tracks that match the descriptions of these xenos from various sources. Adeptus Mechanicus expeditions that plunged into obsidian ruins, never to return. Gaps in the knowledge repositories of individual worlds, as if someone had been trying to hide information. In isolation, all these are unexplained coincidences, but if I analyse the cumulative frequency of the events and the systems in which they occurred, and compare them to the few things I do know, I can detect certain patterns. The ancient xenos are exhibiting activity."
82242918-ad9d-46b8-a63c-6a276a80ff1d: "The {g|Encyclopedia:TechPriest}Tech-Priests{/g} detected an anomalous surge of energy. We came here to investigate, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}."
f5aacd19-4abe-4b5c-9636-632206f49b82:
When this creature scores a hit against an enemy, it inflicts bleeding (14) on them. This effect stacks.
This attack deals more damage the higher the level of bleeding on the target.
7066c50f-9d59-4394-9751-d8ee99ba646a: {n}Pask's mouth tightens for a moment, but he quickly recovers his previous gloomy expression.{/n} "It's not my place, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}, to know about such things. Standing around gawping is a good way to get tangled up in the gears of a machine — there won't even be bones left to pick up. Pardon my insolence, but I have nothing more to say about this."
316dcbe6-a95c-4965-a61e-0e60e45f530d: "What are you doing? What is this madness?"
f06e1c51-8d2d-4361-ac0e-9cc4b50ec03c: "Let us dispense with sentiment. Factotum, what precisely occurred on the agri-world of Janus?"
093cd756-66e5-48a9-8678-f3a0c482b520: {n}Izdrig steps back, shaking his head as he struggles to recover. The wounds on his body would have killed any other man, yet Batoshski stands in spite of it all, simply refusing to stop. And yet... He raises his hands.{/n}
a73391fd-b797-49ff-a148-c255e35a3f83: "Risks of a fatal outcome? The risks are negligible when what is at stake is crossing the limit of death itself. Both of my attempts to create a Triasomata have failed, and so it is necessary to apply a different methodology." {n}He turns his clouded gaze to you.{/n} "Your thoughts on this matter interest me greatly, my colleague — so let them flow freely."
cde33ef3-3962-40e1-8979-c7292d2609db: "A good start, but it's too early to draw any conclusions. Look more closely around the outpost. It seems a lowly pawn was involved. And an ally like that is bound to leave evidence."
733244d9-c7da-407f-99df-a526e4f63e16: {n}The old man's frantic gaze latches on to you and he begins wailing piteously:{/n} "Lord Captain, please help me! These beasts want to cut off my legs! I was a senior gunner on a macro-cannon. I lost my legs in my youth — it was only thanks to my implant I was able to fulfil my duty, guiding teams of haulers! And now they say that that's it, I have to give my legs back, but how can I give them back? Butchers!"
be9a1f51-3ded-4f4d-802f-b0fbd6486342: "At least I had the authority to arrest Camilla Drivestem. Though we could do with that old schemer's advice just about now."{n}Something changes in Laria's face: she clearly likes this new idea.{/n}
60ae9bd7-829e-4c2e-a229-4baab58e6ca8: {n}Ferrenz approaches you quickly and, from under the deck, pulls out a package the same size and shape as the augment you gave him.{/n} "The efficiency of this machine has been restored. The record of tech-heresy within the Sector Bionica system has been deleted."
79ee3b03-9d4b-4c1f-bfcb-35b9cebeeeff: {n}Technological horror has completely subjugated all life on this world. More than anything, the planet resembles a void station of grandiose proportions, twisted and disfigured by the {g|Encyclopedia:XenosGlossary}xenos{/g}' repulsive tech-sorcery. The colossal towers, obelisks, and pyramids, arranged in strict and lifeless symmetry, can be seen even from orbit and appear like black bones protruding from the carcass of an ancient creature.{/n}
bedbfa60-783a-4f74-9652-a4cc471179e4: {n}The vox-station looks well-maintained: you do not see even a speck of dust on the surface.{/n}
92d58c57-c3ed-4ade-8460-94986b2c55b8: The worker is huddled on the floor, the imprint of a mechanical hand on his shattered shoulder. Someone seized him with great force and threw him against the wall.
fa992161-656a-47f6-bfc1-29ac279e9a46: "Killing one's allies in the midst of a siege is detestably low, whatever the reason. The... enforcer will be detained immediately." {n}Laria glowers in the direction of the infirmary.{/n}
52307ba3-9234-4f39-a126-1d69a11b68f4: {n}You realise that the fragment you discovered is only a small part of something immeasurably larger. The mechanism extends deep underground, beneath the flawless recreation of the palace bridge, entwined with its very foundations. If this device were disabled, the repercussions of the outage would reverberate throughout the entire structure.{/n}
bdb9aba3-eaf5-4981-8301-a32e8f1d134d: {n}Seberian glances at the Warrant, but no angry outbursts, curses, or prayers erupt from his vox-synthesiser.{/n} "The dynasty is saved. Such an obscenity is a small price to pay for the fate of the von Valancius." {n}His voice sounds thin, almost mechanical.{/n}
fe9af355-6eb6-42af-9c92-02942337e8b3: "The scope of the Rogue Trader's vision is quite inspiring. I never imagined I would be able to study a live xenos, albeit not entirely in its natural environment. What do you think, Nuncius?"
9b2b2632-1728-4368-b7c7-f51d8fafe969: On hit, increases {g|Encyclopedia:FireDamage}fire{/g} and {g|Encyclopedia:EnergyDamage}energy damage{/g} taken by the target by +30% until the end of combat. Does not stack.
f2994279-03b0-489d-b24f-dc865a1737cc: "I wished to do this swiftly and without bloodshed. But a civil war aboard the flagship? I shall not become like the accursed Goge Vandire..." {n}Seberian falls silent, lowering his head with the whirring of his mechanical spine, before mustering his resolve.{/n} "I withdraw my claim, {name}. We shall settle this matter later, once the crisis has passed. And on that, I shall swear an oath. Before the Warrant itself." {n}Yet he voices even his capitulation and request as both a verdict and a command.{/n}
e111add8-e700-4765-804a-8eb022cc6082: "I beg you, save my son! You can save him, can't you? Will the boy survive?"
c3ec1c33-b585-425b-ae16-ba0814755690: {n}A few seconds later, you hear the heavy footsteps of people running. A breathless Laria Voigtvir, escorted by enforcers, is rapidly ascending the wall.{/n} "In the name of the God-Emperor, what is going on here?"
a714a9d1-06b5-48f8-b60e-48694c5ccc8e: {n}The criminal sneers, glaring at you. Another spasm contorts his face but does not reach his hands: the muzzle of the plasma pistol remains fixed on the Genetor's head. The {g|Encyclopedia:TechPriest}Tech-Priest{/g} emits a binharic trill.{/n}
683daaef-14b7-4d5f-b057-b50597a7fd18: "What is this 'Final Order' you speak of?"
eedf579c-3d89-43ce-abe3-18b1c0417379: "I have done much to strengthen these holdings. Your reproaches are without foundation."
cf03e138-1525-4cda-b885-9bc7d965cf40: "Lord Captain! There you are. That's good. I will show you myself what's going on in this vile place." {n}The man's face twitches slightly, as if with a persistent tic. The area where the implant connects to his head has turned red and is oozing pus.{/n}
3af8b848-1be1-403b-9ba8-4fe69dd1a562: When the {g|f:ef7fcc36126f4f28a79482830aec55ae}Oppressive Shriek{/g} ability is used, the wearer does not lose resolve and can still use {g|Encyclopedia:CareerLeaderGlossary}Officer{/g} abilities, if any.
6e91d81d-680d-4f62-8a5c-329665136713: A thick volume bound in time-darkened human skin covered in unholy runes.
d1aa6cdd-8e75-4618-9d4e-4b8080d17500: "Master Ravor, course from orbit into the void. I must give this some thought."
69f67e4b-e27d-42f7-8d69-519d3a3a3484: {n}A faint smile plays on Eogunn's lips.{/n} "Declined with gratitude and due reverence. The tech-unit Nuncius is designated as a personal instrument of the quest for the divine, which confers the privilege of privacy. That includes privacy from intrusion by entities of equal standing."
738c8af3-5101-447f-adfc-875c11106c21: "One moment." {n}Juvenna stares intently at your face.{/n} "Yes, I think you'll do. I require a worthy companion for an outing beyond the gates — I wish to ascertain the condition of my family's altar. It is located in an alcove to the north, not far from here."
20fb1edd-73a9-46b6-8374-6630eef7443f: "That's nothing, mate. Just the first batch! To the void with it, today all my thrones are going on a proper feast for the family. We'll drink to the von Valancius. Bring yourself along, and the little ones too."
82b421a8-2b07-4ee6-9e9b-ea0c742b39c9: The Imperial Navy seeks a planet within the sector whose orbit would support a waystation, well-equipped with weaponry and void docks. The Rogue Trader will graciously offer Zarkov's orbit, effectively placing the planet under the shield of the Navis Imperialis.
9d189c90-3358-4701-9afa-8e6524a76159: "Choose someone who did not wish to leave Zarkov — such a person would take care of their home better than anyone else, I am sure."
cdc445d4-5615-4960-947f-942fdfe8ee7e: "We cannot. The regiment's traditions are sacred. Even if they mean nothing to you, they mean everything to me and my people."
7b696056-7894-483b-aeea-425aaa438850: "Oh, come. Drowning your sorrows in a bottle is just as effective as ever. A few rounds of something stronger will take your sorrows away! At least until morning."
be03655f-c988-4d81-bb1c-2e13a6aada70: {n}Cassia studies the canvas thoughtfully, barely touching the scrollwork of the heavy frame with her slender fingers.{/n} "The work of a skilled and undoubtedly talented painter, Lord Captain. The execution is so exquisite that glimpses of the model's own emotions flicker before my gaze. As if... As if the master has captured not only the man's likeness upon the canvas, but his very soul."
a3d5d3f7-9c86-488b-b1d0-14232513c5a5: {n}Trazyn lets out a self-satisfied laugh.{/n} "Wise. Short-lived species should not trifle with secrets hidden in the darkness of antiquity."
237ed3c4-f346-4611-82fc-18b3fe8381e5: After each attack, the user must pass a {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerToughness}Toughness{/g} {g|Encyclopedia:ResistanceTest}resistance test{/g} with a −{uip|DC|9240ed9f123d4fa69f44e9c24d6e1b35} penalty or gain +{uip|Toxin|9240ed9f123d4fa69f44e9c24d6e1b35} levels of {g|Encyclopedia:ToxinEffect}toxin{/g} and immediately suffer an extra instance of damage from it.
799a24a9-935f-4ac5-9489-8998baaaf475: {n}...the broad, sumptuously ornate deck of a voidship stretches out beneath your feet. The sight both fascinates you and fills you with a strange horror — as if you have seen all this before, as if the very sight before you presages the arrival of a monstrous, endless nightmare....{/n}
3c8d4b06-7740-4cfb-8543-3365eb6d7c46: "I never imagined that a specimen of one of the most dangerous galactic species would voluntarily join the Rogue Trader's retinue. Nuncius, you are familiar with the concise xenological characterisation of the Drukhari, yes? Their morphology is poorly understood, and this presents a rare opportunity to observe a member of that race at close quarters."
96746139-d543-4331-b4c4-01a3be9ca4a5: "The xenoheresy referred to by its followers as the 'anchor-core.'" My fallen comrades said that the traitor Decabalus had desecrated the station's sacred energy patterns. This Omnissiah-damned device was powering the central drill complex and stabilisation mechanisms with the Motive Force. Without it, the machine spirit of Tirkuaz-4 would shut down and find peace."
8736cfbf-d838-4199-8048-215061129d5b: "Interesting, quite interesting... the only item of note in this worthless heap. But what is the genesis? The originating cause? A definitive answer would require more thorough research... but the testimony of even a primitive creature will serve. You!" {n}The figure turns to you and nonchalantly gestures toward one of the trophies on the wall.{/n} "Where on the ship did this object come from? What do you know of its provenance?"
0013af90-b999-498c-bd4e-63307f712db7: "I never thought I would find you alive. We are caught in a trap, and we must flee."
dfd75e21-d72e-466c-a6ca-f98c5997455d: "You are right. Vanquishing death will be the triumph of reason and the triumph of being, born not by the will of chance but by the will of humanity."
7efba0c9-0b98-40b0-bd53-c391ea89351f: {n}Your fist slams into the metal plate in Eogunn's skull, leaving him unharmed. Tervantias watches you with utter indifference.{/n} "If you thought this would amuse me, specimen, you have failed to do so."
1d9e9159-a9d3-4dbe-aac3-5406b1206770: The user deals +5% more damage and suffers −5% less damage for each allied character within a 3-cell radius that is still in combat.
02b30a29-2773-41c7-8d93-f7dc87077ae6: "The {g|Encyclopedia:OmnissiahGlossary}Omnissiah{/g}'s blessings be upon you! The null hypothesis of the fulfilment of Pact TR-3 was met in accordance with mathematical expectations. The {g|Encyclopedia:AdeptusMechanicusGlossary}Priesthood of Mars{/g} praises the commitment of Rogue Trader." {n}Logis Tetria-Lemma hums a short binharic prayer.{/n} "The darkness of ignorance no longer confuses our data regarding Tirkuaz-4."
5038d0eb-bed4-4428-975b-55071b6b993d: "The Undying One whispered Domin'Seberian's name many cycles ago, and being near him was... excruciating. But your name remains with you, Amic'Abelard, and it is too soon for you to sorrow."
e5068f49-d10c-42ca-9479-a61c6c076af6: "You said that the shoots are armoured?"
554ac949-67a5-4857-9b33-f5ad5e010cf6: "This is our home. Bitter, wild, and deadly. Just like us."
ba588507-5ed9-4abb-b9a0-f270b9c4ba84: "While disputations with the autochthonous inhabitants are most engaging, I wish to return to the Sector Bionica at the earliest opportunity. What can Nuncius and I do to bring that moment closer?"
b78cecf0-1351-403d-ae70-3222cfaadd0a: {n}Seberian's organic eye bores into you.{/n} "None in our lineage has been blessed with that."
30204faf-8e5c-4b35-b2c3-677d5f51120e: "Why is the implantation throne so important to you?"
60da32bd-8538-4bcd-a2a7-e77230921d04: "Decabalus is not what he claims to be! In the name of the God-Emperor, Pask, kill the heretic!"
bbdfd4e0-0b8d-48d2-ba2c-715d5a85b269: "The artefact. Nuncius and I had a disagreement — I was frustrated by the lack of progress, and I wanted to take a more radical approach to studying it. I wanted to experiment on myself. Nuncius was vehemently opposed — he argued that it was reckless, with insufficient data and unpredictable outcomes. Of course, he was right. In my stubbornness and arrogance, I did not heed him. I removed the artefact from the stasis sacellum and reached for it with a mechadendrite. Nuncius tried to intercept it and inadvertently touched the artefact. That moment has haunted me for the past fifty years."
a6b7d2a9-ec95-4489-b57f-a835e8b007ea: {n}You have never heard of such xenos.{/n}
e90dbd03-7c3e-4737-9691-f18842b8cf73: The ancient buildings show signs of recent repairs.
1850ea69-1fe4-4abe-bf16-94c0eca8d278: "What? This is inconceivable! Ferrenz must have violated the integrity of the logs. Nuncius, what has he done to you?"
82d07021-df7b-48f0-a358-68dc0cfe8254: Genetor Ferrenz, left in charge of the Sector Bionica's chirurgeons, laid down his life to bring back replacement arm implants for his maimed partner. Utterly obsessed with finding the perfect augment, he fell into tech-heresy and was destroyed.
d03ff9ec-0c0f-443e-9e63-e854673f3637: "I will not tolerate such treatment again."
a12f00e4-27d2-45ca-afa6-98cf40ccad60: "Or they are hiding it..." {n}Solomorne surveys those present with a sceptical eye.{/n} "Unlikely, though. That would require far too much subtlety for such rabble."
51486d46-863f-454e-9123-b89ac848818f: Extremis Genetor Eogunn Februs has invited the Rogue Trader to the Sector Bionica to participate in an experiment. His goal is to master death and calculate the formula for righteousness.
82e0df30-5484-4020-97bf-7c6bef9b155a: "God-Emperor... have mercy." {n}The Seneschal's face is drained of all colour, and his gaze, fixed upon the empty resting place of the Warrant, holds a soul-crushing despair. In all the time Abelard has accompanied you, he has never seemed closer to his heart giving out than now.{/n}
8a1bcfd8-22aa-4122-8f51-e7ef6905a23c: "I will put an end to you and your false utterances once and for all." {n}The Manipulus gives you a grateful nod and leans over the console of the restraining machine, signalling for you to step back. A few minutes later, streams of sizzling plasma burst forth from the heart of the machine, leaving nothing behind of the prisoner but a puddle of bubbling metal.{/n}
b7898a74-6737-4038-9356-b3ee5e046cfa: Whenever this character hits a target with an arc rifle, they gain +1 {g|f:e0905afb8bc4403797f2b3a667c707e5}residual charge{/g} stack. When reaching 10 stacks, they immediately gain an additional attack with the arc rifle. Once the effect is triggered, these stacks are removed.
0d93b930-f7b4-4a9e-a141-a8f3734f596b: "...of the Yekhmet dynasty, which once reigned over this region. The Great Sleep crippled their minds and left them defective, vulnerable to distortions. Of course, I felt it my duty to remove objects of historical value from their possession."
89349a6b-c63f-4909-8a20-8eff14d0e8ee: {n}Ulfar lets out a short growl.{/n} "Were this not a gift from the hands of an honourable warrior, I would think this another xenos-made fake." {n}He bares his teeth in disapproval as he examines the weapon's glowing edge.{/n} "There is no surety in steel forged from xenos cunning, Aett-Vater."
f87ae81b-cdf6-4d4d-855f-7577592d015a: "What is this tomb that it refers to?"
ff97143e-a184-4214-a3f6-b460fb3553cc: {n}The colonel deflects the first strike, the second, and the third. But you are faster, and he misses the fourth one by a fraction of a second.{/n}
bc18cebe-f62c-4697-859d-638aeb84e02a: {n}Eogunn contemplates the half-dead man lying stretched out on the galvanised surface.{/n} "The choice has been made, but will you deign to answer my question — why him? I must know your thought process."
41616a9b-2279-49f1-b648-8afdf268a16a: {n}With a graceful motion, Kibellah draws her weapon from its sheath.{/n} "I am ready to hear the name that the Undying One will whisper."
bd51dc56-f91e-4cea-ab4f-4a6e2d107cf6: "Augmetics, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. In our songs, it is said your ancestor possessed so many gifts that he could share them with others. I don't know if this is true. But that the Rogue Trader supplied the entire regiment with prosthetics is certain. We continue his work as best we can." {n}Vedka demonstratively clenches her metal hand, which is tipped with claws of bone.{/n}
1367fcc4-a224-4278-ac82-6eeac56d65ce: "Corporal Danchek, activate the lift to the excavation trail."
be4f36b1-321c-4bdf-bf55-12203b8666d0: "I do not. And as it happens, I am perfectly uninterested in the question at present." {n}With a short click, Camilla moves a bead on the necklace in her hands.{/n}
bc78fee1-9f94-4ef3-b626-9b9e70b5b66b: "The only one in my data-index is the enforcer captain Isimond Sauerback. He has been honoured with the blessing of a dynasty plasma pistol. Other weapons are used outside the palace system."
f0203220-e4a9-4abe-8d7f-78babfbb0f1a: {n}Dunawski frowns, causing the stone in her eye socket to shift slightly.{/n} "I thought things would be handled differently in the Imperium. But here, it seems, it's just like back on Zarkov. Well... at least we're used to it."
a4c8aecc-8f7d-41a9-a4ab-8af3267349ac:
Using combat stimulants does not inflict {g|Encyclopedia:NegativeEffectTypesGlossary}negative effects{/g}.
If the user chooses themselves as the target of a combat stimulant, they gain the {g|f:670419fc312d4ee2b2298d03af22e0c2}elusive{/g} effect for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}.
43edb3f1-6fab-4a30-8e5e-c55ed39c3b03: The Angel of the Emperor has also been taken prisoner by the xenos. Powerless to avert his fate, he nonetheless yearns to continue his service. Now, his life rests in the Lord Captain's hands.
fcce8caf-2e5f-45bd-935e-760eb3476d75: {n}Zorael gestures to one of the {g|Encyclopedia:HardLight}hard-light{/g} statues.{/n} "These platforms transport objects to the upper galleries. You may reach them by ascending one of these. Go forth and do battle with valour, human."
3865eac0-26ba-4809-97c8-bac141365248: "You know, I think I'll decline. It's not worth the risk."
be0614df-a80f-41c1-bef4-e88899d2aa83: {n}The Lexmechanic utters these final words with distinct envy, keeping his eyes locked on both Magos Haneumann and Genetor Februs.{/n}
76505ea3-797b-4e97-b1c4-a8dc43070bef: A strange xenos construction resembling a throne.
8daaa528-a1ba-46a3-969e-d7a4368aa2e2: The von Valancius coat of arms is engraved on the solid-looking ring. Its flawless lustre shows how fervently its wearer reveres the dynasty.
9a044b29-f22c-4888-9c40-2c32d76c67e4: "The functions of the life form 'Martinax' have been restored. Critical blood loss was quickly replenished by the multitude of lesser-status life forms from the lower decks who presented themselves for urgent blood-letting. Supposition: the rabble did this out of loyalty to that particular medicae officer. The life form 'Martinax' also wishes to thank you."
9ea72e60-3679-48db-85e2-76587c303731: "Unfinished service is sufficient reason to extend life, even if that life is marked by a flaw. Hypothetically, the stigma of psykerism is correctable... unlike death."
f1e26557-e119-4cf1-a905-dd35b1d15eb1: "The Omnissiah knows all, comprehends all. Acquisition of knowledge of the blessed archeotech corrects a priori probability and increases the accuracy of predicting gracious events." {n}The Tech-Priest emits a long, vibrating binharic tech-litany, causing the vox-transmitter to rattle.{/n}
9cb294df-a908-4f6f-b7ba-9d6f2acb9e51: {n}The disdain in her eyes speaks more eloquently than any direct answer. Juvenna stares at you in silence, as if you were dressed in the fetid rags of a raving vagrant.{/n}
4eba9d72-a08a-4e23-8568-10952e7e8965: "I know that the Inquisition is a dangerous enemy. If House Gaprak's reinforcements have not yet arrived... I fear we shall have to rely on our forces alone."
e744f5f2-9842-4a26-bb15-f38f4ab4f66a: "The sacred process of my Great Work requires a blessed influx of Motive Force, and it is of greater importance than the crew's minor functional needs met by the disabled systems. A pair of compartments can endure a dozen watches without heating in the name of knowledge."
ccf14c22-4e9e-42a4-b24c-8ac8ca1c0b14: For each stack of this effect, Eogunn deals +{unit_stat|WarhammerIntelligence|bonus}% more {g|Encyclopedia:DamageGlossary}damage{/g}.
a7008726-9dbe-46bf-a96a-19425592b69b:
If the user moved during their {g|Encyclopedia:Turn}turn{/g}, 1 Tactical Simulacrum appears in the cell where they started their turn and remains for 1 {g|Encyclopedia:Round}round{/g}. For every 10 points of the user's {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerPerception}Perception{/g} bonus, +1 more Tactical Simulacrum appears in a random cell adjacent to their current position.
A Tactical Simulacrum has {g|Encyclopedia:HitPoints}wounds{/g} equal to 1 and cannot {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeGlossary}dodge{/g}, {g|Encyclopedia:Parry}parry{/g}, or block attacks or take {g|Encyclopedia:DetailsOfTheCoverGlossary}cover{/g}.
The user's Tactical Simulacrums become {g|Encyclopedia:PriorityTarget}priority targets{/g} for all enemies.
a3ad3467-5a7a-4b25-aa29-e17f4642d278: "Curious. I am versed in practices for suppressing memories. Could this be evidence of the use of one of them?"
6d814032-9583-40f0-824e-ddbfa91fe987: {n}The {g|Encyclopedia:ServoSkulls}servo-skull{/g} hovers haughtily without uttering a word. It is clear he thinks you need no introduction.{/n}
597ff071-84e1-4481-8aab-41679c417c4f: "O wisest of men, a couple of artefacts have come into my possession that may interest you. Out of great respect and goodwill, I am willing to donate them to you for a very reasonable price!"
9cc9bb8c-5fdf-4aea-b7e7-82204018a724: [Address the patient] "I have heard that a good story can dull the pain. Would you like me to tell you about my adventures? On one occasion..."
9afa8c68-5b48-40a2-a6a6-1e956c208889: "Things have improved under the new Lord Captain. Wherever you look, everything's spick and span, and we've got new beds again..."
8ac40259-388b-43b3-8175-87c57692962d: "Attach no importance to that. Juvenna is always worrying, it is her nature. The girl grew up among the nobility and is accustomed to thinking in schemes and doubting everything. On this occasion, she is convinced that I should strike a deal with af Irramon for the sake of the dynasty. But Juvenna does not understand that you never negotiate with an aggressor. Sometimes the answer is obvious, and today that answer is war! Her recent fascination with Saint Elana's biography must be confusing her, filling her head with idealistic thoughts."
1ec3f3c8-c4a1-4a35-90d1-1e71bf625390: "I know you designed the rules of this game yourself. I know your every move — and not just in the game."
2c44a711-4edd-4c1e-9760-6eb2d455a311: "You are standing in my palace. Whom else did you expect to see here?" {n}Despite the overwhelming degree of augmentation, the voice coming through the vox-grille is not without a touch of irritation.{/n} "Seneschal, if you will."
43576383-88c8-4200-a572-72cfc6772c9d: Thanks to the Rogue Trader, the scales tipped in favour of Juvenna von Valancius.
287a5cd8-2c17-4ba6-9bad-c09f6dd7e7fb: The ancient obelisks of black stone are inscribed with some kind of xenos glyphs.
a39d0809-d01c-4922-9475-443d6b16cb23: "I admire your sense of family duty, shereen! If I had an elderly relative buzzing in my ear and trying to claim my place, I wouldn't hesitate to send him back to where he crawled out of."
b06f37c0-9c6d-4018-b1c1-c96e2bedce5f: "The machines can be purified and recommissioned, but the losses among the servants of the Cult Mechanicus are irrecoverable. I grieve, not for the machines, but for the knowledgeable minds that have perished."
da0bf2a0-897d-4dcf-b2b4-c06ce71a5f5c: {n}What appeared to be merely a jumble of parts next to the prisoner suddenly stirs into motion. The Elucidator servitor, miraculously surviving amidst the perpetual ice, creakingly begins to translate the otherworldly sounds into Low Gothic:{/n} "Now thrice as many... lowly have come... as before."
2b6cb839-d27d-4950-a27e-6b65d1c0c883: "Do not point your finger at me."
7a9cfe0b-dbfe-408c-8b45-73368ae2eabe: "Your affairs? Shouldn't that be Elana's affairs?"
40422808-27d2-4c0a-8f23-c9ee2644f702: "Hm." {n}In the colonel's grumbling, you think you catch a hint of approval.{/n}
06cbc9c7-b01f-4a82-9b3f-e9543507efef: {n}The pages are filled with cramped writing, along with blots, crossings-out, and sweeping underlines that reveal the owner's passionate nature. The ink is as faded and decayed as the pages themselves, the thin parchment crumbling to dust at any careless touch.{/n}
4d336851-9fad-488b-b51a-4c7ed4051daa: "I swear on the Holy Throne, we saw nothing! Why would we do such a thing? We don't want to die before our time, and we'd be nothing but delighted to see any reinforcements. You'd better talk to the Seneschal — she's a sensible woman, she'll know what's what in a heartbeat."
f258f837-a559-40e0-b205-8d19415979f7: "In other words, allow Nuncius and I to remain on the ship and accompany you. We are well-versed in augmetics, genetics, alchemy, the anatomy of humans and abominable {g|Encyclopedia:XenosGlossary}xenos{/g} alike, and the mysteries of the Motive Force and of sacred engineering. These fields of knowledge may also be employed to eliminate units whose views are not conducive to productive debate."
e6402579-3d0f-4ad3-ae7b-42dcebdda8d0: "Very well. Let's get this over with quickly."
ad2bbac7-3c42-4594-9df1-1bfccc424024: "...All I can be certain of is that the portion of my designs that most of my retinue would consider overly bold will not leave the confines of these chambers. It is rather amusing that this personal laboratory has ultimately become a secret sanctuary, to whose walls I can tell most of my ideas and research. At times, I sense that the esteemed Exalt-117 is entirely aware of its existence, but the Archmagos has always possessed that rare combination of human qualities: a sense of tact coupled with the ability to keep others' secrets..." {n}The stream of thought is cut short — the next page of the diary has been ripped out.{/n}
7712324d-cb37-4657-8ebc-23d1dbd22abd: "A collection of heresies assembled by another heresy... I have been to many places in my life, but this is the foulest corner of the galaxy I have ever had the misfortune of visiting."
415d1037-8e73-4262-92af-5527981de93c:
You can gain a better understanding of what transpired in combat by checking the combat log. All actions performed by your character, their companions, and enemies are recorded in the combat log in the bottom right corner of the screen.
Click {mouse_icon|RightMouse} on an event or an action to learn more about it.
5a97239e-6dbb-4621-85d3-c93994cae3fe:
There is a trap up ahead! Order your character to disable it. If a character enters a trapped area or fails the {g|Encyclopedia:Test}test{/g} to disarm it, the trap will trigger, dealing {g|Encyclopedia:DamageTypesGlossary}damage{/g} and inflicting {g|Encyclopedia:Injury}injuries{/g} on all characters within its {g|Encyclopedia:AreaOfEffect}area of effect{/g}.
Sometimes you will see a trap marked with a red area and a connecting dotted line. This line leads to the mechanism that controls the trap. Hover your cursor over the mechanism — if the cursor changes to a trap icon, the trap can be disarmed by interacting with the mechanism. Click {mouse_icon|LeftMouse} on the mechanism to attempt the disarm test. If you have your entire party selected, the character with the highest relevant {g|Encyclopedia:Skills}skill{/g} score will go to disarm the trap. If a trap is marked with a red area without a dotted line, simply approach the trap and click on it to attempt the disarm test.
Different skills are used to disarm different types of traps.
ba55adda-83ae-43f0-8212-2ce8866845e7: "An inquisitive mind is a rare thing in a layperson. And a layperson who dares to apply their mind to the mysteries of xenos and retains their vital functions is a statistical miracle. Nuncius and I invite you to a discussion for the purposes of mutual enrichment, Mistress Heydari."
256cdae9-1d51-4ef5-8acd-25b3397b71df: Destroy the rebel Tech-Priests of the Adeptus Mechanicus
8d19ebe6-0ad3-47c9-83c7-0b0e263c3bb0: {n}Xenos may not know fatigue, but nor do they know the world they seek to conquer. The Merovians kept their distance, luring the enemy onto fragile roots and using the planet itself as a weapon. These creatures may have once ruled the planet — but its new masters have proved that those times are long gone.{/n}
65244b80-52b9-432b-af2d-b8e2b3d7edd3: Seberian's retinue is too frightened of the Crimson Dance of Khaine to go against him.
fd833801-9dd0-4565-a57f-1df0537262b1: The nightmares that ambushed us were an outrage against the very nature of reality. Giant segmented bodies wriggled silently through the air, disappearing and reappearing as lasgun shots passed through them. Their incorporeal appearance was deceptive; the long claws that pierced one man after another left no doubt that they were quite real. Nothing we had could match those technological monstrosities. The only option left was...
260129cb-8bde-45d9-ae3b-aadf0dd53799: "I found this trinket on a frontier world. It must be restored to working order."
06e1b374-0897-4c6c-8c2b-490e617a2062: "I will give you Nomos in exchange for your help."
02997a69-ed58-4ba5-a02c-5dd900c8412a: As heiress, Juvenna's voice carries particular weight. A way must be found to remove her from the equation.
631985b0-d40a-4c0e-b2cc-2b96e270be64: "The enforcers await your orders." {n}The ring of steel in Jocasta Sauerback's tone eloquently signals her readiness for battle.{/n} "Give the order, and all those troublemakers will be immediately subdued."
1089a164-a9a8-4272-aadf-4ad73fbf0342: {n}Izdrig lunges forward, his trusted knife in hand, then suddenly stops.{/n} "Damn me... Seberian..."
dfcc1525-883a-4ad1-8f85-6acf0246ceac: "I thought entrenching ourselves here in the inner palace would be the best course of action, but... We should listen to the others, Your Lordship. My approach is not suitable." {n}Laria {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_DL_LariaTraitor}forces the words out{/d}, not looking away from you. You observe a gamut of emotions in her eyes: self-contempt, shame, despair... hope.{/n}
2047b77a-ff34-4921-8daf-24da52fa1679: "A young and primitive civilisation, diligently exploring the region for the past several centuries. Rarely do they achieve anything of substance, and yet even such unremarkable creatures can at times leave a mark on history."
f04a5491-c986-4ec0-a6be-864c08c43eb1: The wearer gains +10 {g|Encyclopedia:WarhammerWillpower}Willpower{/g} and +10% {g|Encyclopedia:DodgeGlossary}dodge{/g} while in {g|Encyclopedia:DetailsOfTheCoverGlossary}cover{/g}. Death world characters gain +20 Willpower and +20% dodge while in cover instead.
77bb0af2-1a2c-4664-9cda-7bf8188465d1:
{g|Encyclopedia:DeadeyeShot}Deadeye shots{/g} fired by this rifle deal an additional +5 base damage per enemy size class, i.e.:
-<indent=5%>Hulking creatures (2×2 cells) suffer +5 damage.</indent>
-<indent=5%>Enormous creatures (3×3 cells) suffer +10 damage.</indent>
b87a0f96-0503-4f7b-81db-424b4b029c13: "I dare say that you must have misinterpreted the context of Nuncius's statement." {n}The Manipulus opens and closes his mechadendrites distractedly.{/n} "At any rate, my colleague certainly did not utter anything of the sort."
78316510-c3b6-459a-ba06-63ebc96b0d57: "What do you intend to do now?"
382dedf8-7e42-4075-94d7-c26a682b0a85: "Presumably, we should add the phrase 'if they are committed without the authorisation of Magos Haneumann.' Have I adequately grasped the essence of your argument, colleague?"
52b89f58-1d5a-4c2c-9892-6ca060e920e9: "Of course, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. To give this ancient relic to you is a great honour."
e6885d58-c9ab-44d2-bc12-e4b5085e8521: "Stan-dard... mmmhhh... ser-vo-sku-ll mo-del one-sev-en... four-ze-ro-ze-ro... one. Sys-tem status: stand-by. Err-rr-orr. Re-ques-ess-ting re-pair rit-u-al." {n}The skull twitches, trying to take flight, but clamps keep it securely bound to the flesh.{/n}
ad88f4ad-c4bc-4557-bb72-0ec4b587e85f: "Leave the old man alone. Let him live out his last years in peace."
bdac3253-7382-4aab-8c50-48bebe32ac4e: "High Factotum, I, the Lord Captain, do not permit you to take your leave."
5f84a3d6-a484-4ed6-aa00-1872aecede0f: "Make arrangements for the ceremony and return the Warrant of Trade to its proper place."
0f6ef0d3-25ca-405f-bf78-5ee582bdeb14: "Lady Voigtvir is {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_DL_KamillaTraitor}entirely correct{/d}." {n}Camilla Drivestem's voice sounds surprisingly firm.{/n} "Her competence in the art of war is undeniable."
5c9de6a6-93e7-45ea-a624-af3e75e929a0: {n}The Manipulus smiles mischievously.{/n} "{name}, let me tell you a secret. Not every fact declared to be incontrovertible is actually so. Let us test the robustness of your claim together, as befits two colleagues."
bfa71104-b821-41d7-88c1-0c5ca7556dc2: A low rumble came from somewhere below. Monotonous and growing louder, it sounded like the operation of some monstrous machine awoken from centuries of slumber. The sound intensified, permeating everything, resonating painfully in our bones.
578ff318-f3f9-4058-b17b-73b71bcdae77: {n}His face grim, Heinrix gives an almost imperceptible shrug as if to say, "I warned you."{/n}
81c4eb5d-26ee-4e6b-84bc-a6440781639b: You kept the discovery of the psyker's journal a secret from Izdrig Batoshski.
f7b1181f-b199-4275-a325-1dc2862f1340: {n}Juvenna holds up the index finger on her impeccably manicured hand.{/n} "One: my father must be forced into meeting with Inquisitor af Irramon face to face. Under the pretext of securing a peace treaty, for instance. Only Seberian's retinue could persuade him to do such a thing, so my father's associates must be loyal to us or dead. Preferably the former."
89570fbf-22cd-487b-9860-98174bec4553: "Merely informing you. You might wish to collect it."
8f1ea127-cec3-4717-89a8-33b4c1e79514: "Hmm... The Sector Bionica has become more attentive to the augmentations of senior officers. After my last ocular lens revision, my nightly headaches have practically disappeared."
3a0612a5-f5c9-4e44-8843-0fff7bcb30aa: "Thank you for the advice, Your {mf|Lordship|Ladyship}. It's something to think about."
f97d5543-d3e6-41f5-be78-31b5d9802c74: "You would really call Tervantias an esteemed colleague?"
8840655f-0f53-4124-9bc0-1ef75845cd62: {n}The roots that have broken through the walls are tightly entwined around a desiccated corpse. You can make out that the mummy is wearing rusted armour bearing the Von Valancius emblem and has an {g|Encyclopedia:AugmentationGlossary}augmentation{/g} in place of one arm. You have never seen an implant of this kind before, and it has been crafted with incredible skill.{/n}
c54c6bf6-264f-4338-8ab5-ca982d69d6c5: "This is where I must take my leave."
2e6102b6-d0a1-43c1-96b1-90feb07f9331: "And, all the same, my promenade culminates in the palace."
136ea01a-498a-4722-ad99-448565613dd3: "Elaborate on your thesis, Eogunn. Why is vanquishing death so important to you?"
d43a25b6-54a1-46d5-b661-2f9d3c98e9c2: The Crimson Dance of Khaine must be acquired. This will require the aid of someone with a thief's dexterity. Fortunately, the palace teems with Merovian penal conscripts.
cfcc46af-01e8-47df-895c-8acb9a7e7e80: "You must be wondering about my progress on the weapon I promised you. Given the opportunity to focus on studying the artefact, I was able to modify a plasma gun, after discovering its true potential with the help of... unconventional methods. I hope you find this creation to your liking."
fb23a29a-8a2a-44b7-817e-d7515c251bf3: "Every few months, I once again spontaneously deduce that I am insane, as I have just done. And I arrive at the same conclusion as before."
544a36d7-89f2-44ab-ac89-b974bba1c4ef: {n}Dunawski nods to herself and turns to you.{/n} "It's a good thing you sent him back. Janur is a good soldier, but he can be too hard-headed. I have faith that our victory is certain, but Zarkov teaches you to always have a backup plan."
14438b3e-5801-4d63-b1e0-fdf2b53d3d62: "I am registering a high level of sound judgement in the stated decision." {n}Pasqal bows almost imperceptibly.{/n} "The consequences of Genetor Februs's malfunction and irrelevant decisions will be rectified immediately, and the functioning of the ship's machine spirits will be optimised according to sacred protocols."
1f91acbf-7b70-4f95-bdee-b7729b17305d: "May the Emperor guide you, Lord Captain."
404fe9da-930b-4665-8a6a-2b20bcff5b9e: "Damned bloodhound. Can he sniff out a scheme at twenty paces?" {n}The Merovian clicks his tongue in irritation.{/n} "Well, to the void with him."
92616f1f-e8c5-4df7-8f37-7d0a0ac31ea6: "{name}, I know psykers are dangerous, of course — but look at me. I'm hardly a waste of the ship's oxygen, am I? Psykers with more experience can tear through the veil of the Immaterium with precision, like a medicae officer opening up a wound — then the echoes will attract {g|Encyclopedia:XenosGlossary}xenos{/g} for a long time to come. And the most talented psykers... well, I'm sure you could find a use for them in the protectorate."
6653470a-101c-498e-aa95-b3b25d54a253: The value is determined by the selected game difficulty setting.
9cf7d9bb-f3d9-4673-b9a4-2232327b9b78: Thanks to the Rogue Trader, the scales tipped in favour of Laria Voigtvir.
27771f5c-da4f-4996-a24a-a8e39afebda9: "I had my suspicions, but... {g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}God-Emperor{/g} as my witness, I still hope you are wrong. Lead the way," {n}she says in a lifeless tone, as if she were the one being led to the gallows.{/n}
4ee6d9a7-a595-4b35-bf4f-50dfcae581f6: "Only with much blood of the {g|Encyclopedia:EmperorGlossary}Undying One{/g}'s enemies shall we be cleansed of the taint of this sacrilegious trampling of the relic."
d53a77fa-db4b-4309-8367-879d5d545466:
This plasma drive provides 14 {g|Encyclopedia:SpeedSpace}speed{/g} along with average {g|Encyclopedia:AccelerationBrakingSpace}acceleration and braking{/g}.
If the {g|Encyclopedia:FlagshipSpace}flagship{/g} has 75% {g|Encyclopedia:HullIntegritySpace}hull integrity{/g} or less, its speed is increased by +2 {g|Encyclopedia:MovementPointsSpace}VMP{/g}.
3354c325-111a-4415-a7f9-d5c41dcc81d3: "So, it fears fire? Don't you have incendiary weapons?"
0ead9df1-1ff0-4784-99e3-dd2a28e86522: {n}Near an overturned {g|Encyclopedia:DrukhariGlossary}Drukhari{/g} aircraft, a woman is curled up, desperately trying to stop the blood gushing from the terrible wounds on her body. She barely has the strength to lift her head as you approach. Laria Voigtvir smiles with greyish lips upon seeing you and Seberian.{/n} "Father. You came. You returned after all."
8cab3c44-1f3f-4a74-b7b0-1fa60a15d4a5: "I have no intention of damaging anything. I give you my word."
8887e546-6836-4c40-a6e1-3d19d56f943a: "You are not mistaken, layperson. This machine is the centre of Tirkuaz-4. But it is also its greatest violation. A most grievous heresy and blasphemy of the highest degree."
f818ebe8-6638-4762-8385-ef3cac6f3f1c: "I will inform the enforcers of your decision. They will ensure the people are monitored. Should you wish to personally honour the procession with your presence, please speak to me on the bridge."
